They have 7.7 m PAH EW of the order of 4.6 m, smaller than that of the normal galaxy NGC 701, but still a factor of 10 larger than Sf1's. NGC 1667 was observed with ASCA by Turner et al. ( ) who report luminosities of 0.8 10^ 40 erg s^ 1 and 2.6 10^ 40 erg s^ 1 over the 2--10 keV and 0.5--2 keV range, respectively, well within the range of inactive galaxies. Furthermore, according to these authors, 68 of the X-ray flux in this galaxy can be accounted for by a thermal component due to the integrated stellar emission from a starburst. Mrk 673 appears in Burstein et al. ( ) with an upper limit L_ x 41.2 to its 0.5--4.5 keV luminosity. Mrk 673 also coincides with the ROSAT PSPC source WGA J1417.3+2651 with a 0.5--2 keV luminosity L_ x = 40.8 , again consistent with a mostly stellar origin. Finally, NGC 5674 may represent the first Sf2 where one starts seeing through the torus, since its X-ray absorbing column could be measured with Ginga, 0.8 10^ 23 cm^ 2 (Smith Done ).

sources.summary: The paper analyzes X-ray spectra of NGC 1667, describing its best-fit components, Fe K emission or flux behavior, and comparison with previous modeling.

quotes.quote: NGC 1667 The XMM-Newton spectra were best fit with a single absorbed power law plus a thermal component. To constrain the Fe K EW in the local continuum fit (i.e. 3 - 8 keV), the spectra were binned by a minimum of 2 counts versus the 15 counts used for the global fit; C-stat was utilized in this local fit.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 1667's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 1667 is a low-inclination infrared-luminous spiral galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0152 ( D 67.8 Mpc) with a Sy 2 nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 , which is also detected as compact core in radio (e.g., ho_radio_2001 ). After its detection in , NGC 1667 was measured in the N band but uncertainly detected with the MMT maiolino_new_1995 . The nucleus remained undetected in the subarcsecond-resolution N band imaging with Palomar 5 m/MIRLIN gorjian_10_2004 .

gmw_00000174.name: NGC 1808
gmw_00000174.overview: NGC 1808 is a barred spiral Seyfert 2 galaxy [arXiv:1202.2713, arXiv:1811.00984] that hosts a circumnuclear starburst [arXiv:1212.5368, arXiv:1804.07311] and a variable hard X-ray source [arXiv:1508.05065]. The galaxy features a nuclear spiral [arXiv:2011.09133], a starburst ring [arXiv:1201.3281], and a starburst-driven biconical dust outflow [arXiv:2403.02427].

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 1808 has strong nuclear PAH emission, an extended PAH feature, a circumnuclear starburst plus AGN, and MIR emission not dominated by the AGN.

quotes.quote: The 11.3 m PAH feature is only clearly detected in the nuclear spectra of two AGN, while it is more common in the Spitzer data. For those two objects the AGN emission in NGC 7130 accounts for more than 80 of the MIR continuum at 12 m while in the case of NGC 1808 the AGN is not dominating the MIR emission. This is confirmed by the correlation between the MIR and X-ray continuum luminosities.

A strong PAH feature at 11.3 m is present only in NGC 1808 and NGC 7130 in the T-ReCS spectra. A hint of the PAH feature is also present in other 4 AGN (NGC 1386, NGC 5643, NGC 7172, and NGC 7582). Thus, for 14 out of 20 sources their high spatial resolution T-ReCS spectra are free of signs of star-formation in the MIR, as traced by the PAHs. From visual inspection of the 2D T-ReCS spectra of these sources, the PAH feature is spatially extended for NGC 1808 and coincident with the nuclear emission for NGC 7130. Moreover, the central UV continuum of NGC 7130 consists of several knots of star-formation distributed in an asymmetric ring within 1 arcsec, not spatially resolved by our data Gonzalez-Delgado98 . Thus, the PAH features detected in NGC 7130 could be associated to this ring.

NGC 1808 shows a circumnuclear starburst region e.g. Collison94 together with an AGN Veron-Cetty85 . The 3.3 m PAH emission of NGC 1808 was studied by Tacconi-Garman05 using high spatial resolution images obtained with the ISAAC (VLT) instrument. They found PAH emission coincident with the nucleus supporting these results.

sources.summary: The paper presents SINFONI observations of NGC 1808's inner 600 pc, describing its barred spiral nucleus, young circumnuclear ring clusters, large molecular reservoir, non-circular gas motions, and weak AGN signs.

quotes.quote: NGC 1808 is a nearby barred spiral galaxy which hosts young stellar clusters in a patchy circumnuclear ring with a radius of 240 pc . In order to study the gaseous and stellar kinematics and the star formation properties of the clusters, we perform seeing-limited H+K band near-infrared integral-field spectroscopy with SINFONI of the inner 600 pc . From the M_ BH _* relation, we find a black hole mass of a few 10^7 M_ . We estimate the age of the young stellar clusters in the circumnuclear ring to be 10 Myr . No age gradient along the ring is visible.

However, the starburst age is comparable to the travel time along the ring, indicating that the clusters almost completed a full orbit along the ring during their life time. In the central 600 pc , we find a hot molecular gas mass of 730 M_ which, with standard conversion factors, corresponds to a large cold molecular gas reservoir of several 10^8 M_ , in accordance with CO measurements from the literature. The gaseous and stellar kinematics show several deviations from pure disc motion, including a circumnuclear disc and signs of a nuclear bar potential.

sources.summary: The paper models PAH survival near NGC 1808, finding pyrene half-lives at 20-80 pc too short unless unrealistically high X-ray optical depths are assumed.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:tau (bottom) shows the half-life estimates of pyrene (C _ 16 H _ 10 ) for distances between 20 and 80 pc from the nuclear region of NGC 1808. Each curve represents a different value for the X-ray optical depth ( 0.0 _x 10.0 ). For comparison, injection timescale estimates ( 2.5 10^9 yr, see Jones1994 ) assuming carbon-rich AGB stars as the primary source of PAHs are also shown. For _x = 0.0 , the half-lives span from merely 1.8 10^4 to 2.8 10^5 yr, which are at best four orders of magnitude shorter than the PAH injection estimates.

The half-life estimates are still unsatisfactory even if _x= 4-6 values are considered. For _x = 4.45 , which is related to the upper limit value of N_ H_2 = 2 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 for NGC 1808 Salak2018 , the half-lives are in the range of 1.5 10^ 6 yr to 2.5 10^ 7 yr. For _x = 5.0 ( N_ H_2 = 2.2 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 ), the half-lives span from 2.6 10^6 to 4.2 10^7 yr, while for _x = 6.0 ( N_ H_2 = 2.7 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 ) they range from 7.1 10^6 to 1.1 10^8 yr.

The survival time of pyrene towards the inner region of NGC 1808 is only compared to the PAH injection timescale of if very high X-ray optical depth values, such as _x = 9.0 ( N_ H_2 = 4.0 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 ) and _x = 10.0 ( N_ H_2 = 4.5 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 ), are considered. For _x = 9.0 , the half-lives vary from 1.4 10^8 to 2.3 10^9 yr, while for _x = 10.0 these values span from 3.9 10^8 to 6.2 10^9 yr.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1808 as a barred starburst Seyfert 2 with large-scale starburst outflows, active ring star formation, a nuclear spiral, and a compact misaligned torus.

quotes.quote: NGC 1808 is a barred starburst containing a Seyfert 2 nucleus. Optically, it is possible to see the dust lanes expelled perpendicular to the major axis. There is evidence of large-scale (1 kpc) outflows, likely due to the starburst, and NGC 1808 has been classified as a superwind galaxy, similar to M82 Dahlem1990, Dahlem1994 . The outflow is also seen in HI Koribalski1993 .

Star formation is very active through hot spots aligned on a ring of radius 10 = 450 pc Koribalski1996 . Inside this ring, also contrasted in CO Salak2016 , there is a circumnuclear disk or ring of radius 200 pc. Within this circumnuclear disk it is possible to see a trailing spiral in the CO (figure fig:1808 ), which is even more contrasted in the dense gas tracers: HCO ^+ (4-3), CS. The continuum is a point source inside the nuclear spiral, and may also correspond to a torus of radius 0.13 = 6 pc.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1808 as having a trailing nuclear spiral and nuclear inflow evidence, while ALMA finds no parsec-scale CO outflow despite a known larger-scale outflow.

quotes.quote: Such trailing nuclear spirals have been found also in NGC 613 Audibert2019 and in NGC 1808 (Audibert et al. 2020, in prep.), as can be seen in Fig. fig1 . The nuclear spiral is conspicuous in the CO(3-2) emission line, but also in the dense gas tracers like HCN, HCO ^+ or CS. The rotation curves of these galaxies, derived both from the stellar potential (traced by near-infrared images) and the observed kinematics of the gas, confirm that the nuclear gas is falling within the sphere of influence of the black holes. The gravity torques have been measured on the deprojected images of the molecular gas, with the method described in , and the torques are indeed negative Audibert2019 .

In NGC 1808, an outflow has been known for a long time, at large scale, from ionized gas and dust ejected perpendicular to the plane, creating an extra-planar medium Busch2017 . However, at parsec scale, the CO emission from ALMA does not reveal any outflow. In that case, it can be concluded that the outflow is driven by supernovae feedback, but not from the AGN. In that NGC 1808 galaxy, the starburst and AGN contributions can be distinguished by the diagnostics of line ratios between HCN and HCO ^+ or CS.

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 1808's central kiloparsec with ALMA, finding warm dense gas, enhanced atomic carbon in the circumnuclear disk, radial line-ratio trends, and starburst-driven outflow context.

quotes.quote: We present [ C 1 ] ^3 P _1 ^3 P _0 , ^ 12 CO, ^ 13 CO, and C ^ 18 O ( J=2 1 ) observations of the central region (radius 1 kpc) of the starburst galaxy NGC 1808 at 30--50 pc resolution conducted with Atacama Large Millimeter/submillimeter Array. Radiative transfer analysis of multiline data indicates warm ( T_ k 40 80 K) and dense ( n_ H_2 10^ 3 4 cm ^ 3 ) molecular gas with high column density of atomic carbon ( N_ CI 3 10^ 18 cm ^ 2 ) in the circumnuclear disk (central 100 pc). The C 1 /H _2 abundance in the central 1 kpc is 3 7 10^ 5 , consistent with the values in luminous infrared galaxies. The intensity ratios of [ C 1 ] /CO(1--0) and [ C 1 ] /CO(3--2), respectively, decrease and increase with radius in the central 1 kpc, whereas [ C 1 ] /CO(2--1) is uniform within statistical errors.

The case-study object (Table tab:gal , Figure fig:gal ) is a nearby barred galaxy with vigorous star formation in its central 1 kpc region, as revealed by the presence of H 2 regions, young star clusters, and supernova remnants detected at various wavelengths (e.g., Dah90,Sai90,Col94,Kot96,TG05,GA08,Bus17 ). The galaxy has been classified as a starburst/Seyfert composite VV85 , but the activity appears to be dominated by star formation feedback, with a total star formation rate in the central 1 kpc of 4 M_ yr ^ 1 Sal17 . On kpc scale, the most striking feature in optical images is the presence of polar dust lanes that appear to emerge from the central 1 kpc disk (e.g., VV85,Phi93 ; Figure fig:gal ). Observations of neutral gas suggest that the feature is a starburst-driven outflow from the central region Phi93 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1808 as an H II/Seyfert galaxy with ALMA-resolved molecular rings, nuclear spiral structure, decoupled kinematics, dense-gas enhancement, and evidence for inflow toward the nucleus.

quotes.quote: The ``hot spot'' H ii /Sy galaxy NGC 1808 was studied using ALMA Cycle 3 observations at 12 pc spatial resolution. The CO(3-2) is distributed in a patchy ring at a radius 350 pc, that is most prominent in the south part and another broken ring at 180 pc. They are connected by multiple spiral arms. Inside the star-forming ring, a 2-arm spiral structure is clearly detected at 50 pc radius (left panel of Fig. sub ), as presented in . The nuclear spiral region corresponds to the peak of the velocity dispersions ( 100 km/s).

We find that the nuclear region of NGC 1808 presents line ratios that indicate excitation conditions typical of X-ray dominated regions in the vicinity of AGN (Fig. sub ). What is remarkable in our observations, is that the nuclear trailing spiral is even more contrasted in the dense gas tracers. The two-arm spiral structure is also detected in the residual maps in the NIR by busch17 , supporting the scenario of gas inflow towards the nucleus of NGC 1808.

sources.summary: ALMA observations of NGC 1808 reveal a nuclear spiral, a compact circumnuclear molecular disk or torus, dense-gas excitation linked to the AGN, and barred-potential torques feeding the nucleus.

quotes.quote: We report on Atacama Large Millimeter Array (ALMA) observations of CO(3-2) emission in the Seyfert2/starburst galaxy NGC 1808, at a spatial resolution of 4 pc. Our aim is to investigate the morphology and dynamics of the gas inside the central 0.5 kpc and to probe the nuclear feeding and feedback phenomena. We discovered a nuclear spiral of radius 1 '' = 45 pc. Within it, we found a decoupled circumnuclear disk or molecular torus of a radius of 0.13'' = 6 pc.

The torques exerted on the molecular nuclear spiral by the barred stellar potential reveal that the gas is feeding the nucleus, on a timescale of five rotations -- or on an average timescale of 60 Myr at these radii. Inside the nuclear spiral, the molecular gas appears kinematically decoupled in a tilted circumnuclear disk of radius 0.13'' = 6 pc, which is traced in CO(3-2) and also all dense gas tracers. This molecular disk or torus has HCN and HCO+ line ratios compatible with an excitation from the AGN. According to the distance from the center, the excitation changes progressively from AGN-like to starburst-like.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 1808’s dusty circumnuclear starburst with ALMA recombination-line and free-free emission, deriving a 3.1 solar-mass-per-year SFR and comparing it with other tracers.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we report on the ALMA detections of 85.69 and 99.02 GHz continuum emission and H42 and H40 lines emission from the centre of NGC 1808. This object is a nearby (9.5 2.0 Mpc; Tully2016 ) barred spiral galaxy deVaucouleurs1991 with a starburst nucleus. The galaxy is known for its peculiar hot spots in its dusty central region Morgan1958,Sersic1965 where there are supernova remnants e.g., Saikia1990,Forbes1992,Collison1994 and young star clusters e.g., Krabbe1994,Kotilainen1996,Tacconi-Garman1996,Galliano2005,Galliano2008 . It is unclear whether the galaxy contains an active galactic nucleus (AGN) at the centre, but even if an AGN is present, it may be a relatively weak energy source e.g., Phillips1993,Krabbe2001,Jimenez-Bail2005,Dopita2015,Busch2017 . Salak2016,Salak2017,Salak2018,Salak2019 and Audibert2021 have studied this galaxy using ALMA data, but their analysis has primarily focused on the molecular gas.

The star formation rate (SFR) derived from the ALMA free-free emission is 3.1 0.3 M _ yr ^ 1 . This is comparable to the SFRs measured from the infrared emission, mainly because most of the bolometric energy from the heavily obscured region is emitted as infrared emission. The radio 1.5 GHz emission yields a SFR 25 per cent lower than the ALMA value, probably because of the diffusion of the electrons producing the synchrotron emission beyond the star-forming regions.

sources.summary: The paper presents ALMA observations of barred starburst NGC 1808, identifying a molecular torus, central dust/CO source, dense-gas radial gradients, shock-tracing SiO, and starburst/AGN-like nuclear chemistry.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we present our recent high-resolution ( 1'' or 50 pc) ALMA observations of the nearby, barred starburst galaxy NGC 1808, and highlight some of the main findings: (1) the discovery of a double peak revealed in the distributions of CO (3-2) and CS (2-1) in the circumnuclear disk indicating the presence of a molecular gas torus with a radius of 30 pc; inside the torus, we found a compact source of dust continuum at 0.87 mm and CO, coincident with the location of the low-luminosity AGN revealed by X-ray observations; (2) the excitation of CO gas, traced by the line intensity ratio of CO (3-2) to CO (1-0) is elevated to 1 in the star-forming disk; the ratio is between 0.3 and 1 in the 500 pc ring; (3) the ratio of HCN (1-0) to HCO ^ + (1-0) in the central 1 kpc exhibits a radial gradient: while the ratio decreases from 1.5 in the center to 0.9 in the 500 pc disk, it exhibits another peak of 1.2 in a ring-like structure at a galactocentric radius of 300 pc. The detection of SiO (2-1) in the circumnuclear disk suggests the presence of shocks, likely generated by intense nuclear star formation (supernova explosions and winds from massive stars), as well as inflows and outflows of turbulent gas in the central 100 pc.

The target is NGC 1808, a nearby (11 Mpc), barred starburst galaxy with peculiar polar dust lanes and gas outflow emerging from the nucleus (Fig. 1a) . In this presentation, we outline the observations and focus on: (1) the discovery of a molecular gas torus in the galactic center (central 100 pc) traced with ^ 12 CO ( J=3-2 ) and CS (2-1), (2) the intensity ratio of the CO (3-2) and CO (1-0) lines at 100-pc scale, (3) the distribution of dense gas tracers HCN (1-0) and HCO ^ + (1-0), whose intensity ratio exhibits a radial gradient in the central 500 pc disk, and (4) the first detection of SiO (2-1) in NGC 1808.

NGC 1808 will remain an interesting target galaxy for further studies with ALMA. These first high-resolution, multi-line observations of NGC 1808 have given us an insight into the SF process and its feedback in a nearby starburst galaxy, demonstrating the revolutionary capabilities of ALMA. While the achieved high resolution yielded a fascinating view on the nuclear spiral pattern, distribution of individual GMCs, and the resolved intensity ratio of different molecular lines, the high sensitivity allowed detections of weak lines such as SiO (2-1), essential to understand the physical conditions of molecular gas.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 1808 multi-wavelength data, reporting biconical extraplanar dust filaments and favoring a starburst-driven outflow that lifts dust from the disk.

quotes.quote: Giant Biconical Dust Filaments in NGC 1808 Kane Veilleux ./ figures/ Giant Biconical Dust Filaments in the Starburst Galaxy NGC 1808 0009-0009-1074-3696 Rohan Kane Department of Astronomy, University of Maryland, College Park, MD 20742, USA 0000-0002-3158-6820 Sylvain Veilleux Department of Astronomy, University of Maryland, College Park, MD 20742, USA Joint Space-Science Institute, University of Maryland, College Park, MD 20742, USA We present the results from an analysis of multi-wavelength archival data on the multi-phase outflow in the starburst galaxy . We report the detection at 70 and 100 \ of dust filaments that extend up to 13 kpc from the galactic mid-plane and trace an edge-brightened biconical structure along the minor axis of the galaxy.

We adopt a distance of 12.3 Mpc for NGC 1808 Tull09, Tull13 , corresponding to a scale of 59 pc per arcsecond, for the remainder of this paper. A summary of the archival data used in the present study in given in Table tab:data . We use archival observations of NGC 1808 taken with the \ Photodetector Array Camera and Spectrometer (PACS) at 70 , 100 , and 160 \ under program ID KPGT esturm 1.

In the end, we therefore favor an outflow origin for the extraplanar dust filaments, heated by the starburst light and radiative shocks (see Fig. \ fig:cartoon for an illustration of the geometry). However, this interpretation requires the outflow to inject sufficient energy into the ISM on a reasonable timescale to lift the material to the observed heights.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC1808 as a young starburst with MIR hot spots, a model PAH underestimate, and no detected silicate absorption features.

quotes.quote: NGC1808 This starburst is claimed to be young (Krabbe et al. 1994). In high resolution MIR images, several hot spots are detected which coincide with the most intense radio sources (Galliano et al. 2005). Our fit (Fig. sed2.ps ) somewhat underestimates the PAH emission.

So one may have to increase the PAH abundance (as was done in the model of Siebenmorgen et al. 2001) which is set constant in the computations of our SED library. Piovan et al. (2006) predict silicate absorption features at 10 and 18 m which, however, are not detected. The dip at 10 m is due to the wings of neighboring PAH bands. It is not caused by silicate self--absorption which would require much higher optical depths.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC1808's central starburst, finding deeply embedded MIR/radio clusters, intense star formation in the inner region, and young clusters traced by nebular emission.

quotes.quote: NGC1808 is located at a distance of 10.9 Mpc Koribalski96 , which corresponds to a scale of 53 pc per arcsec. Its redshift is 0.003319 Koribalski04 . The central region (inner 750 pc) of this galaxy is undergoing an intense episode of star formation, discussed in e.g. Tacconi-Garman96,Tacconi-Garman05 . With ISAAC at VLT/UT1, we collected NIR images and spectra of the circumnuclear MIR sources discovered in NGC1808 Galliano05a .
Hence, there are two types of embedded clusters in NGC1808: one bright in the MIR, bright in the radio and faint in the NIR, and the other bright in the NIR and faint in the MIR and the radio.
For three of the sources, namely M3, M6 and M8, we have collected NIR spectra: they display intense nebular emission lines, confirming that the sources are young embedded clusters.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 1808 as a Seyfert 2 with central AGN and star formation activity, possible interaction-driven star formation from NGC 1792, and BLAST detections of its core and disk.

quotes.quote: NGC 1808 NGC 1808, presented in Fig. fig:ngc1808 , is a Seyfert 2 galaxy jimenez-bailon2005 . Subsequent measurement seems to indicate that a large part of the radiation emanating from the central 1 kpc of the galaxy is from an active galactic nucleus as well as a high rate of star formation foerster_schreiber2003,maiolino2003 . Using , junkes1995 estimate a star formation rate of between 5 and 13 M _ yr ^ 1 , and a supernova event rate of 0.09 yr ^ 1 . A companion galaxy, not observed by BLAST, NGC 1792 is located 40 \ from NGC 1808 (130 kpc at a distance of 10.9 Mpc) and may be responsible for an accelerated star formation rate in NGC 1808 jimenez-bailon2005 .
BLAST made four observations of a 1 deg ^2 area centered on NGC 1808, with a total integration time of 179 minutes. BLAST detects both the core and disk of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1808 as a high-quality starburst case, finding a Br starburst ring, more extended [FeII] near the nucleus, and a diffuse supernova-rate ring.

quotes.quote: To demonstrate the morphological correlation between the [ FeII ] and SNrate, a side-by-side comparison of the K band continuum, extinction corrected Br flux, extinction corrected [ FeII ] _ 1.26 flux, and SNrate is shown in Figure fig:snr for NGC 4536, NGC 1808, and NGC 7552. These galaxies were selected for presentation because of the high quality of the observations. Visually comparing the morphologies of the four different maps, we find that the SNrate most closely resembles the [ FeII ] emission.

NGC 1808 displays a clear starburst ring in Br surrounding the bright nucleus. The [ FeII ] reveals a much less prominent ring configuration with more extended emission around the nucleus spanning the south-east to north-west plane. The SNrate map shows more ring structure than the [ FeII ] but it is quite diffuse and lacks the strength of the Br peak in the north-east region of the ring.

sources.summary: The paper observes NGC 1808 as a Sy2 peculiar Sbc galaxy with a low-luminosity AGN, central starburst emission, weak polarization, and complex Faraday structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 1808 is identified as a Sy2 galaxy Tacconi-Garman1996,Galliano2008 with a low luminosity AGN Veron-Cetty1985,Dahlem1990,Gonzalez-Martin2013,Esquej2014 . It was classified as a peculiar Sbc galaxy Veron-Cetty1985 . It also has a star-burst region in the inner 750 pc dominating the infra-red emission Sengupta2006 .
NGC 1808 : Displays a Faraday spectrum with three Faraday-thin components -- Figure NGC1808-rmclean . We detect three prominent components. The source is Faraday complex with _ = 49.4 rad m ^ 2 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1808 as a circumnuclear starburst with compact star formation, a radio halo slightly larger than its star-forming disk, and unusually energetic outflows.

quotes.quote: In these galaxies, the vast difference between r_ 25 and r_ SF (see entries for NGC 1808 and M 82 in Table tab:cuts ) illustrates clearly that r_ 25 is not a suitable tracer of currently ongoing SF activity. This, in turn, supports the notion that the observed gaseous halos are created by processes related to the young stellar populations (I), not the old ones (II).

A direct comparison of the values of r_ SF and r_ halo (Table par:data ) suggests that for circumnuclear starbursts (NGC 1808, M 82) r_ halo is slightly larger than r_ SF . This is caused by the fact that the starburst-driven outflow cones widen beyond the disk planes in the arche-typical hour-glass shape (e.g. Veilleux et al. 2005 and references therein).

Again, there is a clear difference between proto-typical starburst galaxies (NGC 1808, M 82) and others with more widespread SF. The energy injection rates in circumnuclear starbursts, , are orders of magnitude higher than in galaxies with widespread SF, leading to much more energetic outflows which can be detected at high distances above the disk planes, of up to 5--10 kpc (Dahlem et al. 1998, Lehnert et al. 1999, Strickland et al. 2004a,b, T ullmann et al. 2006).

sources.summary: NGC 1808 is described as a Sy2 galaxy with intense central star formation; the paper warns that stellar contamination makes its infrared SED fit unreliable.

quotes.quote: NGC 1808. The Sy2 galaxy NGC 1808 is undergoing an intense episode of star formation in its central 750 pc Tacconi96,Tacconi05,Galliano08 . The near-IR fluxes were obtained using ISAAC/VLT, and we measured the mid-IR flux from 10.7 and 18.2 OSCIR/CTIO images. Consequently, NGC 1808 has relatively low spatial resolution for our sample (the angular resolution of the ISAAC images is 0.6 and that of the OSCIR image at CTIO is 0.9-1 ). The near-IR SED shape is flat and peculiar, and star formation may contaminate these measurements (according to the argument of stellar_contamination ), making the fit not very reliable.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1808 as a starburst galaxy with a variable hard X-ray source and central ULX or low-luminosity AGN, but MIR emission remains star-formation dominated.

quotes.quote: NGC 1808 A long-term variable hard X-ray source in the starburst galaxy NGC 1808 was first detected by using . then used and to resolve the individual sources in this galaxy and find a central ultra luminous X-ray source or low-luminosity AGN without significant obscuration. analysed only the data and find a slightly more powerful and moderately obscured AGN. In the MIR, a compact but resolved source was detected at subarcsecond scales. As indicated by the corresponding MIR spectrum, the emission is still star formation dominated at these scales (see also gonzalez-martin_dust_2013 ). This explains the offset position of NGC 1808 in the MIR--X-ray plane close to pure starburst galaxies. Conversely, the predicted intrinsic X-ray luminosity from the MIR--X-ray correlation of = 41.85 and obscuring column density = 24.31 have to be regarded as upper limits for the AGN in NGC 1808.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1808 as a transition-object galaxy with a nuclear starburst, outflow, and robust emission-line classification.

quotes.quote: NGC 1808 is an (R)SAB(s)a galaxy at a distance of 9.3 Mpc. Similarly to NGC 253, NGC 1808 also shows a nuclear starburst, with an outflow coming from the central kpc. Such an outflow was detected via its [ N ii ] , H and Na I lines phi93 , and its CO molecular emission sal16 .
In addition, one interesting point to be discussed is the fact that two of these objects, NGC 253 and NGC 1808, show significant outflows associated with nuclear starbursts.

The emission-line ratios of NGC 1808 and NGC 253, on the other hand, are much more precise and, as consequence, there is no doubt about the classification of the nuclear emission-line spectra of these two objects.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 1808 as a starburst ring galaxy with HI along its central stellar component and outer pseudo-ring plus strongly warped outer kinematics.

quotes.quote: NGC 1808: The bottom right panels in Fig. fig:mosaic3 show the optical and 1 velocity map for this galaxy. The optical image shows a bright central linear disc-like structure with two diffuse low surface-brightness outer spiral arms that wind around tightly to form an outer pseudo-ring. The 1 gas is seen to trace the narrow linear bright central stellar distribution as well as the outer pseudo-ring. From the 1 velocity map it is evident that the rotation in the inner regions is different to the that in the outer regions, with very large kinematic warps observed in the outskirts. This is also an interesting galaxy in our sample, as it is the only one showing clear co-location of 1 gas along the narrow central stellar component. NGC 1808 is a starburst galaxy as indicated by a number of previous studies (REF).

sources.summary: The paper reports a new radio recombination-line detection in NGC 1808 and derives ionized-gas and star-formation properties for its starburst nucleus.

quotes.quote: We detected for the first time RRLs from the starburst nuclei in the Circinus galaxy and NGC 1808.
NGC 1808 was detected in RRL emission at 3.9 with a line strength of 0.47 mJy. No radio recombination lines were detected from the other galaxies surveyed to a 3 limit of 0.3 mJy to 1.4 mJy.

The detected line strengths infer ionized gas masses of 3000 M_ to 10^ 6 M_ in the Circinus galaxy and 10^4 M_ in NGC 1808, and corresponding star formation rates of 0.2 M_ yr ^ 1 to 6 M_ yr ^ 1 and 0.3 M_ yr ^ 1 to 0.6 M_ yr ^ 1 , depending on the model conditions.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1808 as the most massive member of a small group, with a super-wind, compact radio sources, molecular ring, and debated Seyfert nucleus.

quotes.quote: NGC 1808 is the most massive member of a small group of galaxies. The galactic center has a super-wind, compact radio sources, and a molecular ring 1996A&A...313..771K . 1985A&A...145..425V first suspected that NGC 1808 has a faint Seyfert nucleus. This has since been refuted by many 1992MNRAS.259..293F,1993AJ....105..486P,1994ApJ...425...72K , yet there is recent hard- X-ray evidence to support the Seyfert nucleus Awaki1993221 .

sources.summary: The S7 notes describe NGC 1808 as an active starburst galaxy with a starburst ring, warped inner disk, deep Na D absorption, a galaxy-wide outflow, and catalogued HII regions.

quotes.quote: NGC 1808: An active starburst galaxy with a starburst ring, a warped inner disk, and deep Na D absorption. There is a prominent galaxy-wide outflow that will be the subject of a detailed analysis by Rupke et al. (2017, in prep.). The nucleus has enhanced but no other sign of being a Sy 2. The \ regions in this galaxy have been catalogued by Tsvetanov95 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1808's starburst as comparable in size to M 82, with PAH emission along the major axis, 15 m emission following it, and off-nuclear [ Ar ii ].

quotes.quote: NGC 1808 The starburst in NGC 1808 covers a region of comparable physical size to that of M 82 but being three times more distant, less structural details are resolved by ISOCAM. The PAH emission appears the most extended and oriented parallel to the major axis of the galaxy ( P.A. 140^ ), peaking at the nucleus. The 15 m emission follows closely the PAH emission. The [ Ar ii ] 6.99 m line emission is the most distinct in that the peak clearly is off-nucleus, about 5 \ to the southeast, and the centroid of the emission region lies 2.5 \ southeast of that for the 15 m continuum and PAH emission.

sources.summary: NGC 1808 is described as an LGG 127 spiral with an outer ring-like structure, extended multiple arms, consistent measured magnitudes, and possible past-interaction bar/arm signatures.

quotes.quote: NGC 1792 and ESO362-019 have a bulge-less flocculent appearance, while NGC 1808 has an outer ring-like structure and an extended multiple-arms structure. For the three galaxies in our sample (NGC 1800, NGC 1808 and NGC 3522) in common with the sample of dePaz07 , the measured magnitudes are consistent within errors with those measured by dePaz07 in d _ 25 . A possible signature of past interaction events is the presence of a bar structure, seen in LGG 127's, NGC 1744 and ESO305-009 which show in addition open arms, and multiple arms, as NGC 1808 in the same group.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 1808 as a pseudobulge galaxy, citing The bulge has a very high surface brightness flocculent spiral; n_b=0.8 , and the bulges is actively forming stars with M_ 3.6 M_ 8.0 =3.3 mag.

quotes.quote: NGC 1808 RSXS1../Sbc pec Pseudobulge. The bulge has a very high surface brightness flocculent spiral. The decomposition yields n_b=0.8 , and the bulges is actively forming stars with M_ 3.6 M_ 8.0 =3.3 mag.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 1808's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 1808 is an inclined peculiar spiral galaxy at a distance of D= 12.3 2.2 Mpc tully_extragalactic_2009 with an active nucleus containing a prominent starburst an possibly an AGN (see e.g., krabbe_near-infrared_1994 ). Optically, the nucleus has been classified as a Sy 2 veron-cetty_ngc_1985 or as an H II region yuan_role_2010 . The detection of an obscured long-term variable hard X-ray source supports the AGN scenario awaki_asca_1996,jimenez-bailon_x-ray_2005 .

sources.summary: The appendix describes NGC 1808 as an inclined spiral Sy2 galaxy with a prominent starburst and MIR emission dominated by star-forming regions within 200 pc.

quotes.quote: NGC 1808 is an inclined spiral galaxy with a Sy2 nucleus and a prominent starburst Krabbe94,Veron85 . Yuan10 considered the possibility that the nucleus is a H II region. Asmus14 found that star-forming regions dominate the MIR emission within 200 pc.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 1808 as both an AGN/Seyfert and starburst-nucleus galaxy, with low-resolution CN data and a central CN/HCN increase likely driven by its starburst.

quotes.quote: Our final sample of 9 galaxies (Table table:physical ) is quite heterogeneous, and includes galaxies with a prominent active galactic nucleus (AGN) or Seyfert nucleus (Circinus, NGC1808, NGC3627, NGC 7469), luminous and ultraluminous infrared galaxies (U/LIRGs: IRAS 13120-5453, NGC3256, VV114), galaxies with a starburst nucleus (M83, NGC 1808), and spiral galaxies (NGC3627, NGC3351).

When only low spectral resolution data were available for CN (NGC 1808, NGC 3351) or HCN (NGC 3256), that line was imaged in channel space with no binning; the other two lines were then imaged using velocity widths matched to the output from the low spectral resolution cube.

Finally, NGC 1808 also shows an increase (20 ) in the CN/HCN ratio in its center; its relatively faint AGN combined with the extent of the area of the enhanced ratio (Fig. fig:appendixfig3 ) may imply that the enhancement is driven by the central starburst rather than the AGN.

gmw_00000175.name: NGC 2110
gmw_00000175.overview: NGC 2110 is a lenticular SAB0 Seyfert 2 galaxy [arXiv:2111.10036, arXiv:2210.15376] hosting an active nucleus with a hidden, disk-like broad-line region [arXiv:0708.3221]. The galaxy features a dusty circumnuclear disk [arXiv:1905.00994], a rotating stellar velocity field [arXiv:1705.06949], and a bent, S-shaped radio jet that drives a compact nuclear outflow [arXiv:1006.4635, arXiv:2305.06366].

sources.summary: The paper reports that NGC 2110 is a Seyfert 2/S0-E galaxy with a hidden disk-like broad-line region, transient double-peaked H emission, and obscured type-1-like nucleus.

quotes.quote: We have discovered an extremely broad, double-peaked H emission line in the polarized flux spectrum of NGC 2110, establishing that this well-studied Seyfert 2 galaxy contains a disk-like hidden broad-line region (BLR). Several properties of NGC 2110 suggest that it is an obscured twin of Arp 102B, the prototypical double-peaked emission-line active galactic nucleus (AGN). A comparison between our data and previous spectra of NGC 2110 indicates that the double-peaked H feature is transient. The presence of a disk-like BLR in NGC 2110 has important implications for AGNs: it expands the range of properties exhibited by Seyfert 2 galaxies, and the fact that the BLR is obscured by a torus-like structure provides the first evidence that double-peaked emitters and classical Seyfert nuclei may have the same basic parsec-scale geometry.

Our results establish that NGC 2110 possesses a disk-like BLR, which expands the range of properties exhibited by Seyfert 2s. In addition, NGC 2110 presents some opportunities for interesting follow-up study. As noted above, the double-peaked feature is transient and variable, and it would be worthwhile to monitor its behavior. The observed variations may trace changes in the structure of the BLR or in the properties of the scattering medium.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 2110's Seyfert nuclear outflow, finding an accelerated NLR structure, a circumnuclear dusty disk shaping the NLR, and disk-related bending of the radio jet.

quotes.quote: We present a HST/STIS spectroscopic and optical/radio imaging study of the Seyfert NGC 2110 aiming to measure the dynamics and understand the nature of the nuclear outflow in the galaxy. Previous HST studies have revealed the presence of a linear structure in the Narrow-Line Region (NLR) aligned with the radio jet. We show that this structure is strongly accelerated, probably by the jet, but is unlikely to be entrained in the jet flow.

The circumnuclear environment of NGC 2110 consists of several components which each play a role in shaping the structure and dynamics of the region and the NLR. We explore the outflow using well-resolved kinematics from STIS spectroscopy and constrain the influence of the circumnuclear components using high resolution optical and radio imaging. Summarising our results: A circumnuclear dusty disk plays a major part in shaping the appearance of the NLR. We propose a model that explains the angular offset between the axis of the inner radio jet and the EELR in terms of the biconical illumination of this dusty gaseous disk. We also argue that the strong, symmetric bending of the jet is a consequence of the anisotropic mass and pressure distribution from the disk.

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 2110 has low-sigma patches and an h3 map anti-correlated with velocity, while its stellar velocity field is well reproduced by rotation.

quotes.quote: Patches of low- are seen for Mrk 607, NGC 2110, NGC 3516, NGC 4051, NGC 4235 and NGC 5899, while three objects (NGC 1052, NGC 3227 and NGC 4388) show a centrally peaked distribution. These rings and partial rings are located at distances from the nucleus ranging between 150 and 250 pc. Nuclear values for each galaxy are presented in the last column of Table tab_mod , obtained by fitting the nuclear spectrum integrated within a circular aperture with radius of 75 pc. Most galaxies show low h_ 3 values ( 0.10 <h_ 3 < 0.10), suggesting that their LOSVDs are well represented by a Gaussian velocity distribution. Exceptions are Mrk 607, Mrk1066 and NGC 4051 that show h_ 3 values larger than 0.15. For 8 galaxies (NGC 1052, NGC 2110, NGC 3227, NGC 3516, NGC 4051, NGC 5506, Mrk 607 and Mrk 1066), the h_ 3 map is anti-correlated with the velocity field, with positive h_ 3 values seen at locations where rotation velocities are observed in blueshifts and negative h_ 3 values associated to regions where the rotation velocities are observed in redshifts.

The velocity dispersion maps show low- ( 50-80 ) rings for 4 galaxies (Mrk 1066, Mrk 1157, NGC 5929 and NGC 788) or ``patches" of low- structures (for Mrk 607, NGC 2110, NGC 3516, NGC 4051, NGC 4235 and NGC 5899) at typical distances of 200 pc, interpreted as being originated in young/intermediate age stellar populations. Centrally peaked maps are observed for three galaxies (NGC 1052, NGC 3227 and NGC 4388). The h_ 3 moment is anti-correlated with the velocity field for 8 galaxies (NGC 1052, NGC 2110, NGC 3227, NGC 3516, NGC 4051, NGC 5506, Mrk 607 and Mrk 1066) -- positive h_ 3 values seen at locations where the velocity field shows blueshifts and h_ 3 <0 for locations where the velocity field shows redshifts. The presence of these wings are attributed to the contribution of stars from the galaxy bulge that present lower rotation velocities. The observed velocity fields are well reproduced by a rotating disk model, with deprojected velocity amplitudes in the range 60--340 .

sources.summary: NIFS mapping of NGC 2110 finds young and old stellar components, rotating and outflowing gas, jet-driven shocks, and weak kinetic feedback in the inner 200 pc.

quotes.quote: We present a two-dimensional mapping of stellar population age components, emission-line fluxes, gas excitation and kinematics within the inner 200 pc of the Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 2110. We used the Gemini North Integral Field Spectrograph (NIFS) in the J and K bands at a spatial resolution of 22 pc. The unresolved nuclear continuum is originated in combined contributions of young stellar population (SP; age 100 Myr), a featureless AGN continuum and hot dust emission.

The young-intermediate SP ( 100< age 700 Myr) is distributed in a ring-shaped structure at 140 pc from the nucleus, which is roughly coincident with the lowest values of the stellar velocity dispersion. In the inner 115 pc the old SP (age >2 Gyr) is dominant. The emission-line flux distribution is correlated with the radio emission and its kinematics comprise two components, one from gas rotating in the galaxy plane and another from gas in outflow within a bicone oriented along north--south.

These outflows seem to originate in the interaction of the radio jet with the ambient gas producing shocks that are the main excitation mechanism of the \ emission. We estimate: (1) an ionized gas mass outflow rate of 0.5 \ at 70 pc from the nucleus; and (2) a kinetic power for the outflow of only 0.05 of the AGN bolometric luminosity implying weak feedback effect on the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2110 as a nearby S0 Seyfert 2 with variable X-ray emission, prominent Fe K line, and a best-fitting Compton-thin torus reflector.

quotes.quote: NGC 2110* NGC 2110* is a nearby S0 galaxy, 1991devaucouleur Seyfert 2 AGN. NGC 2110* shows a prominent Fe K line accompanied by variable intrinsic emission 1996hayashi . 2014marinucci report the X-ray spectral analysis of NGC 2110* observed by NuSTAR in 2012, when the source was at the highest flux level ever observed, and in 2013, when the source had more typical flux levels.

We re-examine the 2013 observations together with the 2003 observation in the overlapping 3--10 keV band, finding a consistent slope and variable normalization. We therefore fit jointly the broad band spectra with untied normalization for the cutoff power-law component between instruments. Modelling the reflection with PEXMON gives R _ f =1.01 _ 0.91 ^ 1.12 . Modeling the reflection with a torus instead, using borus02 we found a Compton thin reflector with (N_ H,refl ) =23.49 _ 23.03 ^ 23.76 covering 13 of the sky, consistent with .
The torus reflector produces the best fit.

sources.summary: The paper interprets NGC 2110 as a Seyfert 2 with patchy absorption, neutral fluorescent Fe and Si lines, tentative ionized emission near extended X-rays, and absorber geometry compatible with a small radio jet.

quotes.quote: We present results from Chandra HETGS (250 ks over two epochs) and XMM-Newton EPIC and RGS (60 ks) observations of NGC 2110, which has been historically classified as a Narrow Emission Line Galaxy galaxy. Our results support the interpretation that the source is a Seyfert 2 viewed through a patchy absorber. The nuclear X-ray spectrum of the source is best described by a power law of photon index 1.7 , modified by absorption from multiple layers of neutral material at a large distance from the central supermassive black hole. We report the strong detections of Fe K and Si K lines, which are marginally resolved with the HETGS, and we constrain the emission radius of the fluorescing material to 1 pc. There is some evidence for modest additional broadening at the base of the narrow Fe K core with a velocity 4500 km s ^ 1 .

We find tentative evidence for ionized emission (O viii Ly , an O viii RRC feature, and possibly a Ne ix forbidden line) in the MEG and RGS spectra, which could be associated with the known extended X-ray emission that lies 160 pc from the nucleus. We suggest that the 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 partially covering absorber originates in broad-line region clouds in the vicinity of the AGN, and that the 3 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 coverer is likely to have a more distant origin and have a flattened geometry in order to allow the small-scale radio jet to escape.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 2110 as a peculiar nearby S0/Seyfert galaxy with both radio-loud and radio-quiet traits, using INTEGRAL and Swift X-ray data to test unified AGN models.

quotes.quote: NGC 2110 is a nearby ( z=0.0078 , D=34.5 Mpc) S0 galaxy first discovered in the X-rays in the late 1970s. It was first classified as a Seyfert 1 galaxy particularly because of its X-ray luminosity and its timescale variability. But e.g. Malaguti et al. suggested it to be classified as a Narrow Emission Line Galaxy (NELG), a transition between Seyfert 1 and Seyfert 2, because of its moderately flat ( 1.7 ) powerlaw spectrum in the X-rays and narrow emission lines in the optical spectrum.

Thus on one hand NGC 2110 presents simultaneously characteristics of a radio quiet (high X-ray luminosity, emission lines) and a radio loud (radio structure, spectral energy distribution overview) galaxy. On the other hand, even inside the radio quiet class, this galaxy shows both type 1 (e.g. flat continuum) and type 2 (e.g. narrow emission lines only) features. Those elements suggest that NGC 2110 could be a peculiar galaxy, inconsistent with the unified models or belonging to a transition class between radio quiet and radio loud galaxies. The aim of this work is to test the unified model through the investigation of this peculiarity in the X-rays, providing a direct view on the central engine emission mostly free of absorption effects.

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 2110 with Gemini NIFS, describing its Seyfert nucleus, molecular and ionized gas distributions, stellar rotation, and compact warm-molecular outflow.

quotes.quote: We present a two-dimensional mapping of the gas flux distributions, as well as of the gas and stellar kinematics in the inner 220 pc of the Seyfert galaxy NGC 2110, using K-band integral field spectroscopy obtained with the Gemini NIFS at a spatial resolution of 24 pc and spectral resolution of 40 . The 2 2.1218 m emission extends over the whole field-of-view and is attributed to heating by X-rays from the AGN and/or by shocks, while the Br emission is restricted to a bi-polar region extending along the South-East--North-West direction.

NGC 2110 is an early type S0 galaxy which hosts a Seyfert 2 nucleus, located at a distance of 30.2 Mpc, assuming a redshift z=0.007789 (NED NASA/IPAC extragalactic database ), where 1'' corresponds to 146 pc at the galaxy. Due to its proximity, it has been already observed in several wavebands, from radio to X-rays.

For NGC 2110 we proposed that the outflow is a result from gas in the plane of the galaxy being pushed by clouds outflowing from the nucleus. The same hypothesis was put forth by allan , where the authors observed a compact outflow from the nucleus of NGC2110, suggesting a spherical geometry, using optical integral field spectroscopy. In the case of 2, most of the gas is concentrated at the disk of the galaxy, and thus the interaction of the expanding spherical bubble is seen only at locations where the 2 is present, forming the bipolar structure seen in the residual map.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2110 as a mildly obscured type 2 Seyfert with a featureless hard X-ray spectrum, narrow Fe K line, and broad torus-parameter degeneracies.

quotes.quote: NGC 2110 NGC 2110 is mildly obscured by =(4.1 0.2) 10^ 22 , which is detectable as an exponential roll-off of the power-law continuum ( =1.628 0.007 ) at the lower end of the \ band. Its spectrum is remarkably featureless, except for a narrow Fe K line with an equivalent width of 33 6 eV. Stepping through the range of \ between 22.0 and 25.5 we find that acceptable model include tori with very small covering factors, <0.2 for >23.5 , as well as tori with high covering but low . The difference in the best-fit ^2 over the whole range is very small ( < 10 , for 819 d.o.f.). A broad minimum in ^2 at 22.5< <23.5 covers nearly the full range of covering factors (0.1--1.0).
The best fit, with ^2/ d.o.f. =860.4/818 , is found for 23.3 . With \ fixed at this value, <0.24 and >0.28 .

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 2110 molecular gas and argues its AGN locally suppresses CO emission while warm molecular and ionized gas fill a resolved nuclear lacuna.

quotes.quote: We report on a high-resolution multi-phase study of the nuclear environment in the nearby Seyfert galaxy NGC 2110 using the Atacama Large Millimeter Array (ALMA), Hubble and Spitzer Space Telescopes, and the Very Large Telescope/SINFONI. We identify a region that is markedly weak in low-excitation CO 2 1 emission from cold molecular gas, but appears to be filled with ionised and warm molecular gas, which indicates that the AGN is directly influencing the properties of the molecular material. Using multiple molecular gas tracers, we demonstrate that, despite the lack of CO line emission, the surface densities and kinematics of molecular gas vary smoothly across the region.

Here we present evidence for localised transformation of molecular gas through direct impact from the AGN's radiation field in the nearby Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 2110 (luminosity distance D_ L = 34 Mpc, cz=2335 ).

Figure contextfigure a shows the ALMA \ map in the centre of NGC 2110. This emission is distributed in an inhomogeneous spiral pattern suggestive of a circum-nuclear disc. Many of the bright arms of the CO disc are aligned with dark dust lanes seen in the HST color map (Figure contextfigure ).

sources.summary: The paper maps ionized gas in NGC 2110 and argues that its moderate-power radio jet likely drives a compact outflow, producing high dispersion, shocks, and non-circular motions.

quotes.quote: We present a spatially-resolved study of the ionised gas in the central 2 kpc of the Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 2110 and investigate the role of its moderate luminosity radio jet (kinetic radio power of P_ jet = 2.3 10^ 43 \ erg s^ 1 ).

The outflow component has typical values of 700 km s ^ 1 and is confined to the central 2.5 400 pc, which is coincident with linear part of the radio jet detected in NGC 2110. At the AGN position, the [ O III ] 5007 line shows high velocity components reaching at least 1000 km s ^ 1 . This and the high velocity dispersions indicate the presence of outflowing gas outside the galaxy plane.

NGC 2110 is an early type spiral galaxy (SAB0) hosting a Seyfert 2 nucleus McClintock1979 , observed at an intermediate inclination i=42-65^ see e.g., and Fig. fig:photo Wilson1985,GonzalezDelgado2002,Kawamuro2020 . It contains a radio jet, whose `S'-shaped jet was proposed to be the result of its bending by the ram pressure of the host galaxy rotating gas Ulvestad1983 . The jet appears brighter on the north side, which allowed to estimate that it is at an intermediate orientation with respect to the plane of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper reports a peculiar [ FeII ] line-to-continuum distribution in NGC 2110, co-rotating disk gas, and a centrally peaked velocity-dispersion profile across its central kiloparsec.

quotes.quote: The H _2 line-to-continuum ratio is constant or drops significantly towards the nucleus in most galaxies, except NGC 2273, NGC 5273, and NGC 7450. For Br , in contrast, the ratio peaks towards the nucleus in many galaxies, consistent with stronger ionization mechanisms towards the active nucleus. These two results both explain the observed central drop of the H _2 /Br ratio. Of the three emission lines, the [ FeII ] line-to-continuum ratio shows the most distinct radial variations. Its profile is centrally peaked in Mrk 1, Mrk 3, NGC 262, NGC 1320, NGC 2273, NGC 3081, NGC 5273, and NGC 7450. Several sources show two maxima (or a central drop, e.g. NGC 1667, NGC 3185, NGC 4151, NGC 4253, NGC 4388, and NGC 5506). Finally, Mrk 1066, NGC 931, NGC 2110, NGC 2992, and NGC 4051 show peculiar distributions, e.g. the distribution in NGC 2110 shows three separate peaks.

However, the gas velocity curves are similar to those of the stars, which define the galaxy disk. This is the case in Mrk 1, Mrk 1066, NGC 262, NGC 931, NGC 1667, NGC 2110, NGC 2273, NGC 3081, NGC 3185, NGC 4388, NGC 5273, and NGC 5506. In these galaxies, all three emission lines display a velocity curve that follows that of the stars and is reminiscent of rotation, thus strongly implying that the line-emitting gas is located in the disk of the galaxy and rotating with the disk. Other galaxies (Mrk 3, NGC 1320, NGC 2992, NGC 4051, NGC 4151, NGC 4253, and NGC 7450) display velocity curves different from the stellar velocity curve in one or more emission lines, which may be a signature of non-rotational motion, either in- or outflows or other non-planar motion of the gas.

The radial dispersion profiles in Mrk 3, Mrk 1066, NGC 2110, NGC 2273, NGC 4051, NGC 4151, NGC 4253, NGC 5273, and NGC 5506 have a central peak, which indicates a higher kinetic gas temperature in the nucleus. Either the ISM is more turbulent (possibly caused by a jet- or wind-driven outflow), and/or more intensely heated by the incident radiation field. In either case, a peaked dispersion distribution is a strong indication for the AGN governing the physical state of the gas at the nucleus. The dispersion peak is most often present in [ FeII ] (indicating dust destruction close to the nucleus), and/or in Br (indicating a strong radiation field), while the H _2 line shows the least-pronounced central rise. Examples for flat velocity dispersion profiles are NGC 3081, NGC 3185, and NGC 4388, which show no significant change over up to 1.2 kpc in diameter. The velocity dispersion in all three sources is of order 100 km/s, well within the range observed for star forming regions, which is consistent with star formation being the dominant the gas excitation mechanism. Finally, the dispersion profiles in Mrk 1, NGC 262, NGC 931, NGC 1320, NGC 1667, NGC 2992, and NGC 7450 show marked differences between the three emission lines, with variations of 100 km/s or more at a given radius. This indicates that the ionized atomic and molecular gas is responding differently to the central AGN in these sources, perhaps due to the presences of asymmetric energy input (a jet or shocks) together with a stratified medium, e.g. a thin molecular disk in a thick ionized medium.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2110 as a well-known Seyfert 2 galaxy whose polarized spectrum shows a double-peaked broad H line that constrains the scattering geometry.

quotes.quote: In the last dataset for our project, we observed the well-known Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 2110. For some reason, this object had been passed over in other surveys at H . As Figure 6 illustrates, we have detected a spectacular double-peaked broad H line in the polarized-flux spectrum of this object (Moran et al. \ 2007).

The line profile is similar to those of other double-peaked emitters (Eracleous Halpern 2003). The remarkable thing is that the line profile has not been smeared out in the scattering process. The H emission originates in a disk, so most of the scattering must be done by a medium that sees that disk over a pretty narrow range of inclination angles.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 2110 as a near-IR-variable Seyfert with nuclear dust features, comparable fits with or without the AGN component, and constrained moderate-silicate torus parameters.

quotes.quote: Nine out of the 13 galaxies in our sample have been reported to show variability in the near-IR: NGC 7674 (Quillen et al. 2000), NGC 1068, IC 4329A, NGC 2110, MCG 5-23-16, NGC 5506, and NGC 7469 (Glass 2004), NGC 3227 (Suganuma et al. 2006), and NGC 4151 (Koshida et al. 2009). There are no reports on mid-IR variability of our sources. The typical variability in the near-IR is 40 on average, with amplitude variations around the median of between 10-30 for our sample. For all these galaxies the 1-2.2 m data, when available, were taken simultaneously. The LM fluxes in some cases were not simultaneous with the shorter wavelength fluxes. However, in most cases the LM fluxes are taken as upper limits as there was no estimate of the unresolved emission. Glass (2004) showed that the L band variability is typically less than 20 around the median flux, which is within the photometric and unresolved emission uncertainties. We can therefore assume that variability in the near-IR does not affect the compiled SED within the above discussed uncertainties of the unresolved measurements. Published ground-based mid-IR spectroscopy The published mid-IR spectroscopy used in this work (see Table 2) was obtained with four different instruments: (i) The Thermal-Region Camera Spectrograph (T-ReCS; Telesco et al. 1998) on the Gemini-South Telescope. The T-ReCS data were obtained with the low-resolution grating, which provides a spectral resolution of R 100 . The slit widths were 0.67 (Young et al. 2007) and 0.36 (Roche et al. 2006, 2007). (ii) The Michelle instrument (Glasse et al. 1997) on the Gemini-North telescope. The Michelle observations (Mason et al. 2006; 2009 and Section 2.2) were obtained with a 0.36 slit width and a spectral resolution of R 200 in the N band, and a 0.54 slit and R 100 in the Q band. (iii) VISIR, the mid-IR imager and spectrograph mounted on the 8.2m UT3 telescope at the ESO/Paranal observatory in Chile. The VISIR observations (H onig et al. 2010) were obtained in low-spectral resolution mode ( R 300) with a slit width of 0.75 . (iv) MIDI (Leinert et al. 2003), the mid-IR interferometer at the VLTI at the ESO/Paranal observatory. The MIDI observations (Tristram et al. 2007) were taken with a 0.6 width slit and R 30 . The data used here are a "total flux spectrum", where 4 spectra are obtained for this, two for each telescope of the interferometer and 2 for each "window" in MIDI. The spectrum was extracted in a 6 pixel wide mask or 0.516 , and calibrated individually for the windows and telescopes. Because the VISIR ground-based mid-IR spectra have slightly higher spectral resolution than the other spectra, we rebinned them to contain approximately 150 spectral points. The MIDI, T-ReCS and Michelle spectra contain between 100 and 200 spectral points. We did not attempt to remove any emission lines (e.g., [ S iv ] 10.51 m) or broad features (e.g., PAH features) in the spectra. The final step was to scale the spectra to the photometric points. This was mostly necessary for the spectra observed with the narrowest slits (see Table 2) because of slit losses. As an example, in Figure 3 we show the original N and Q band spectra (Mason et al. 2006 and Section 2.1, respectively) of NGC 1068 together with the mid-IR photometric points of Tomono et al. (2001) for a circular aperture of 0.4 diameter, and the photometry through a rectangular 0.29 0.15 aperture after deconvolution of the data. To match the angular resolutions of the imaging and spectroscopic data, the NGC 1068 N and Q band spectra were scaled to their corresponding 0.4 photometric points. Finally, for all the N band spectra in our sample we added in quadrature the intrinsic error of the spectra and the 15 error of the imaging data (see Section 2.3) used for scaling the data, for each spectroscopic point. For the Q band spectrum of NGC 1068 the associated error for scaling the spectrum is 25 . These errors were added for the fitting. Clumpy Torus Models In this work we use an interpolated version (see Section 3.3) of the clumpy dusty torus models of Nenkova et al. (2002, 2008a,b) including the corrections for the previously erroneous AGN scaling factor (see the erratum by Nenkova et al. 2010). The CLUMPY models are described by the six parameters listed in Table 3 that we explain in the following (see also Figure 1). An AGN with a bolometric luminosity L_ bol ( AGN ) is surrounded by a torus of dusty clouds and all the clouds have the same optical depth _V , which is defined in the optical V band. The torus clouds are located between the inner radius of the torus R_ d and the outer radius of the torus R_ o , and the torus radial thickness is defined as Y = R_ o /R_ d . The inner radius of the torus is set by the dust sublimation temperature ( T_ sub 1500 K), R_ d = 0.4 1500 K T_ sub ^ 2.6 L_ bol ( AGN ) 10^ 45 erg s^ 1 ^ 0.5 pc . The angular distribution of the clouds is assumed to have a smooth boundary and it is described as a Gaussian with a width parameter _ torus . The radial distribution is a declining power law with index q ( ^ q ). The mean number of clouds along radial equatorial rays is N_0 . The number of clouds along the line of sight (LOS) at a viewing angle i (measured from the polar direction, see Figure 1) is, N_ LOS (i) = N_0 e^ (90-i)^2/ _ torus ^2 . In a clumpy dust distribution the classification of an object as a type 1 or type 2 AGN is not truly a matter of the viewing angle but of the probability for direct view of the AGN (see Figure 1, and also Elitzur 2008). The probability for an AGN-produced photon to escape through the torus along a viewing angle i when all the clouds are optically thick ( _V>1 ) is, P_ esc e^ (-N_ LOS ) . In the models the radiative transfer equations are solved for each clump and thus the solutions depend mainly on the location of each clump within the torus, its optical depth, and the chosen dust composition. We adopt a dust extinction profile corresponding to the OHMc dust (i.e., the standard cold oxygen-rich ISM dust of Ossenkopf et al. 1992). The total torus emission is calculated by integrating the source function of the total number of clumps convolved with the radiation propagation probability along the torus (Nenkova et al. 2002). For Type 1 and intermediate type Seyferts where there is an unobscured view of the AGN, it is also possible to include its contribution to the resulting SED. The AGN continuum emission in these models is characterized with a piecewise power law distribution (see Nenkova et al. 2008a for details). We finally note that there is some evidence of the presence of an extra hot dust component, originating very close to the AGN. Reverberation mapping (see discussion by Kishimoto et al. 2007) and near-IR interferometric observations (e.g., Kishimoto et al. 2009 and references therein) of Seyfert galaxies have shown that the sublimation radii appear to be smaller than expected from Equation 1 (similar to the Barvainis 1987 relation). However in this work we take the simplest approach for fitting the data assuming that all the near-IR emission is originating in the ¨classical¨ torus, with no additional hot dust components. Foreground dust components In this section we examine the possibility of having contributions to the observed IR SEDs and mid-IR spectra of Seyfert galaxies from dust emission and absorption unrelated to the AGN. There are several pieces of evidence we can look into, namely the inclination of the galaxies, and the presence of nuclear dust lanes and spatially resolved variations of the silicate feature. Approximately half of the galaxies in our sample have minor-to-major axis ratios of b/a<0.5 (inclined host galaxies, see Table 4), and as suggested by Deo et al. (2009), it is likely that dust in the host galaxy disk can contribute significantly to the observed silicate absorption and the long-wavelength continuum. The only exception appears to be IC 4329A, which is a highly inclined system, but does not show silicate absorption. Deo et al. (2009) concluded for this galaxy that it is likely that our line of sight does not intersect any dense clouds in the host galaxy. Using color maps Martini et al. (2003) showed that in general the dust in the nuclear regions of Seyfert galaxies is on physical scales not associated with those of the dusty torus. It is then likely that this dust is located in front of the nucleus (see also Regan Mulchaey 1999) and arises from the galactic ISM. In our sample, Circinus, IC 5063, NGC 5506, NGC 7582, NGC 2110, NGC 7172, and possibly NGC 7674 show dust features in the nuclear (central 1-2 ) regions (see e.g., Quillen et al. 1999; Maiolino et al. 2000; Martini et al. 2003).

For NGC 2110 we obtained comparable bolometric luminosities (and other torus model parameters) with and without the AGN component. This is probably due to the lack of photometric points in the near-IR for this galaxy.

For the three galaxies with moderate silicate features (MCG 5-23-16, NGC 2110, and NGC 7674, see Table 4) we are able to reproduce reasonably well the photometric SED points and the mid-IR spectroscopy, and the torus model parameters are mostly well constrained.

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 2110 observations with simulations, emphasizing its circumnuclear disc, young star clusters, shocked gas, outflows, and possible recent starburst-triggering instability.

quotes.quote: Using Keck/OSIRIS near-infrared (NIR) spectra, Durre_14 investigate the circumnuclear disc in NGC 2110 e. g. Veron_06,Rosario_10,Storchi-Bergmann_99 and find a total, nuclear star formation rate of 0.3 M _ yr ^ 1 . This SFR is dominated by four massive and young star clusters that are embedded into a rotating nuclear disc of shocked gas in the inner 100 pc surrounding the active nucleus see Fig. 2a, Durre_14 . The shocked intercluster medium is thought to be excited by strong outflows that do not appear to originate from the AGN, but rather are localized to the clusters. This is reminiscent of the morphology of the stellar surface density in our simulations in the phase following Toomre instability and clump merging, that shows a handful of clumps orbiting in the nuclear potential in our simulations (e. g. Fig. fig:galnuc_partdist a). These observed clusters could, therefore, be a sign of a recent event that triggered gravitational instability and the observed starburst and might have significant impact on the (ongoing and future) central activity Davies_07 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 2110 as an outflowing AGN target, measuring broad-component density and noting localized densities above 1000 cm^-3 in the outflow.

quotes.quote: Three of the targets, namely NGC 1068, NGC 1266 and NGC 2110, are classified as having an ionized outflow based on the presence of broad components in the 5007 profile of the S7 data. The broad components of the profiles in the three targets show fast outflows with FWHM > 500 km/s compared to the other targets which require only narrow components with FWHM < 200 km/s (See Sect. sect3.1 for more details on emission line fitting).

For the broad outflowing component in NGC 1068, NGC 1266 and NGC 2110, we get a density value of 726 90, 1120 295 and 237 78 cm ^ 3 respectively. The spectrum showing these broad components are shown in Fig. fig:sii_spectrum_outflow . Therefore, from the integrated spectrum analysis, the outflowing component has a significantly higher density compared to the non-outflowing components.

In targets with an active outflow inferred from the broad profiles such as NGC 1068 and NGC 2110, the density can reached values > 1000 cm ^ 3 in certain locations supporting a dense outflow scenario as predicted from the integrated spectrum analysis described earlier.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2110 as a Seyfert 2 Sa galaxy with a dusty circumnuclear disk, S-shaped jetlike emission, offset radio peak, and IFU feedback components.

quotes.quote: NGC 2110 is a well-known Sa Seyfert 2 galaxy Malkan1998 . The HST and + \ emission line images of the galaxy is characterised by a dusty circumnuclear disc and a long S-shaped jet Mulchaey1994 . A similar jetlike feature was reported by Nagar1999b that extends for about 4 \ along the north-south direction. Despite the prevalence of the extended emission along a similar position angle at both optical and radio wavebands, the emission regions are not causally-related. The peak of the radio emission is off-centred with no corresponding line emission.

sources.summary: The paper finds both modeled column densities for 2110 in the same regime, with narrow-line assumptions and previous partial-coverage results broadly matching its fits.

quotes.quote: 2110 For both obsIDs of this AGN tab-fit shows that both \ and \ are . In both cases the lower \ limit tends to zero and is fixed to the value quoted by evans2007 for HETG data, i.e., 20 eV. The laha2020 \ values reported for these obsIDs are in general agreement with our \ \ values. In particular, their separate partial-coverage \ results are in very good agreement with our \ results for each obsID. The \ value for obsID 100024010 reported by fukazawa2011a is of the same order, although somewhat larger, than our \ value.

sources.summary: NGC 2110 is an SAB0 Seyfert 2 galaxy with strong inner featureless continuum, young central stellar population, constant reddening and stellar properties, and mixed older circumnuclear populations.

quotes.quote: NGC 2110 NGC 2110 is classified as SAB0, harboring a Seyfert 2 nucleus. In this galaxy, we detected a significant FC contribution of up to 40 in the inner 0 6 region. This region is also dominated by a young stellar population, with a <t>_L of 0.1 Gyr and subsolar metallicity. According to our analysis, both the reddening and the stellar are constant within our FoV, except from border effects. We did not detect such rings, and the circumnuclear stellar populations within our FoV are mainly dominated by a mixture of intermediate-age and old SSPs.

sources.summary: The paper describes a core-like mm-wave component and halo in NGC 2110, arguing the halo is more consistent with AGN photo-ionization than the radio jet.

quotes.quote: 28: NGC 2110 A core-like component appears to be surrounded by a halo, whose major axis is oriented at a PA of 140 . In the adjacent cm-wave band, a jet in a north-south direction was clearly revealed up to 4 on each side e.g., Ulv83 . The difference in angle between the radio jet and the mm-wave halo would suggest that the mm-wave emission would not be dominated by the jet. Instead, ionization cones traced by optical ionization lines (e.g., [ O iii ] and [ S ii ] +H ) have similar angles to that of the halo e.g., Mul94 . Spatially resolved X-ray iron emission that could be due to X-ray irradiation of gas by the AGN also has a similar angle Kaw20,Kaw21 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 2110 as an isolated-sample AGN galaxy with extended strong emission lines, strong rotation, an off-center dispersion peak, and regular Hermite profiles.

quotes.quote: NGC 2110 The galaxy is a well known AGN (e.g. Pfefferkorn et al. 2001) and appears in the isolated galaxy sample of Colbert et al. (2001). Strong emission lines are seen, with a large spatial extension beyond the continuum. This may affect the kinematic values derived in the outer, lower S/N, region. Strong rotation is seen with a (off-centre) peaked velocity dispersion profile. Both hermite profiles appear to be fairly regular.

sources.summary: Suzaku found NGC 2110 to be the lone sample AGN lacking both a broad relativistic iron line and a detectable Compton reflection component.

quotes.quote: The Compton-thin Seyfert 2 NGC 2110 (at z=0.0078) was observed by Suzaku in September 2005, for a net exposure of 100 ks (Figure 11) and at a very high flux level, of 1.05 10^ 10 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 (2-10 keV) or 2.7 10^ 10 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 (15-100 keV). A weak unresolved iron line was detected by , at 6.40 0.01 keV, with an equivalent width of 44 3 eV. The stringent limit on the reflection component with HXD of R<0.1 implies an origin in Compton-thin matter, while the high energy cut-off is restricted to >200 keV. No diskline component appears to be present in this source, down to an upper-limit of 10 eV (assuming diskline parameters of 6.40 keV rest frame energy, R_ in =6R_ g , emissivity q=-3 and inclination 45 ). Thus NGC 2110 appears to be the only AGN here which does not show a broad / relativistic iron line, or show evidence of reflection.

sources.summary: NGC 2110 is described as optically narrow-line with complex stable and variable absorption, making Fe-band residuals difficult to distinguish from a relativistic Fe K line.

quotes.quote: This is a narrow-line object in the optical and is viewed through a relatively large absorption screen, which may be patchy (e.g., Evans+07 ), or ionized ( Nandra+07 ). Different observations with XMM-Newton , Chandra and SUZAKU demonstrated the presence of a relatively stable, full-covering absorber (column density of 3 10 ^ 22 cm ^ 2 ). Together with this, an additional absorber, likely variable in both column density and covering fraction, was required ( Rivers+14 ). The difficulties in modelling the absorber could produce residuals in the Fe band, which are difficult to disentangle from any relativistic Fe K line.

sources.summary: The S7 notes describe NGC 2110 as a Seyfert 2 galaxy with a two-sided curved extended narrow-line region across the WiFeS field, bipolar outflow, and no active star formation.

quotes.quote: NGC 2110: This Sy 2 galaxy has a very extended two-sided curved ENLR that extends across the WiFeS field (38 arcsec), emerging from the nuclear region at PA 0^ . The ENLR displays pronounced bi-polar outflow. The ENLR is much more extended than was revealed by HST \ imaging, which shows a narrow, 1-arcsec long jet-like feature at PA = 340^ , and a weaker feature at PA = 160^ Mulchaey94 . There is no evidence of active star formation or \ regions.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2110 as the radio-loud exception in the sample, with extended jet emission, a steep high-frequency spectrum, and no millimeter excess.

quotes.quote: NGC 2110, which is known to have an extended radio jet ulvestad89 has a Gaussian integrated flux of 17.8 mJy, as compared to 10.8 mJy peak flux, and 13 mJy in the 2 extraction, but it is the RL exception in this sample, rather than the rule. We find for 2MASX J0444+2813, Mrk 79, NGC 2110, and NGC 3516 slopes of = +0.08, -0.67, -2.4 , and +0.27, respectively. NGC 2110 is an extended radio loud source. In the present sample, except for NGC 2110, which is radio loud and has extended radio emission, strong jet scenarios are ruled out.

sources.summary: The paper identifies AT2019ppm with the Seyfert II galaxy NGC 2110 and interprets the ACT millimetre excess as a roughly month-long flare from the host galaxy AGN.

quotes.quote: AT2019ppm was reported on September 7, 2017 2019TNSTR1763....1N . Its position coincides with the galaxy NGC 2110, which is also a known Seyfert II galaxy. The transient processing pipeline that reported the event, AMPEL 2019A&A...631A.147N at the Zwicky Transient Facility 2019PASP..131g8001G , flags a discovery when it is associated to a ``Known SDSS and/or MILLIQUAS QSO/AGN''.
Since the AT2019ppm transient is associated with NGC 2110, we conclude that we have detected a flare in the host galaxy's AGN that lasted for approximately one month.

sources.summary: The paper presents sub-parsec VLBA imaging and radio SED results for NGC 2110, describing its central radio morphology and spectral shape in the FRAMEx AGN sample.

quotes.quote: NGC 2110 [ 5 ^ h 52 ^ m 11 3763, 07 27 22 519 ] : L, C, and X bands images revealed a bright point source at the center. No emission was detected in the K band image, so we used the nondetection upper limit for the SED. A GHz-peaked spectrum is found for NGC 2110 with a steeper spectrum in a higher frequency regime beyond 5 GHz. The C X band SED sub-plots are similar with much flatter indices ( > - 0.5) for peak intensities and integrated flux densities, suggesting a central point source.

sources.summary: NGC 2110 is described as the most uncertain broad-line case: its Brackett line is moderately wide, while comparison lines and Paschen evidence are inconclusive.

quotes.quote: The most uncertain case is NGC 2110 with its moderately wide Brackett line. The nondetection of [ Si IX ] and the absence of ISO spectroscopy makes a direct comparison to the forbidden lines impossible. Evidence on possible broad components to Paschen is mixed (Rix et al. ; Veilleux et al. ). We have listed this case as a narrow line, on the basis of the similarity of the Brackett line width with that of [ Fe II ] (Veilleux et al. ).

sources.summary: The atlas treats NGC 2110 as an individually observed active or starburst galaxy, summarizing its prior classification and near-infrared continuum, emission-line, and absorption properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 2110 . It was initially classified by bra78 as a narrow-line X-ray galaxy with sufficient column of dust to the nucleus to obscure the broad-line region, thus leading to a Seyfert 2 classification of the optical spectrum, but with an insufficient gas column to attenuate the 2-10 keV emission. In fact, its hard X-ray luminosity is comparable to those of Seyfert 1 galaxies wea95 . In the NIR, it has been the subject of numerous studies vgh97,sosa01,rkp03 . Our NIR spectrum reflects the low-ionization nature of this source.

sources.summary: The paper detects blurred reflection in NGC 2110 with Model SRC but not Model ION, requiring an emissivity index incompatible with q = 3.

quotes.quote: A significant detection of a blurred reflection component is made in 7/11 observations at a confidence level of 95 : MCG+8-11-11, Mrk 6, Mrk 110, NGC 7469(1), NGC 2110, HE 1143-1800 and NGC 5506(1).
NGC 2110, HE 1143-1800 and NGC 5506(1) are the three observations that give a significant detection of the blurred reflection with Model SRC, but not with Model ION.
For NGC 2110, MCG+8-11-11, Mrk 110 and HE 1143-1800, the uncertainty of the emissivity index is not compatible with q = 3 , which explains why reflection could not be detected with Model N07.
