The resulting value of _ p placed the inner ring near the I4R and the nuclear ring between two ILRs, consistent with the findings of Schwarz (1984a) and Simkin et al. (1980). Figure ngc3081-resonances shows how well NGC 3081 fits into barred spiral theory.

sources.summary: The paper estimates NGC 3081 X-ray luminosity from broad-band analyses, notes little variability, and compares prior N-band imaging with the new narrow-band VISIR flux.

quotes.quote: NGC 3081 beckmann07 find, from a combined \ and \ analysis, =1.8 0.2 with L_ 20-100 =8.1 10^ 42 erg s ^ 1 . They also find significant variability of a factor of a few in the BAT 14--195 keV lightcurve over a 15 month period. Fitting an absorbed PL above a few keV (the softer flux is fit by weak scattering or a blackbody) to spectra extracted from the Tartarus database gives = 68( 5) 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 with L_ 2-10 =4-6 10^ 42 erg s ^ 1 ; the variation being that between the \ SISs. Extending the de-absorbed PL to 200 keV gives L_ 14-195 =9-14 10^ 42 erg s ^ 1 , well matching the more recent values quoted by markwardt05 and ajello08_swift . This suggests that variability may not be very strong.

sources.summary: NGC 3081 is treated as a Sy2 galaxy with ringlike star formation; sparse SED data give model parameters, high reprocessing efficiency, and silicate absorption.

quotes.quote: NGC 3081. NGC 3081 is a Sy2 galaxy with intense stellar formation located in a series of nested ringlike features Buta04 . As in the case of NGC 1386, the SED of NGC 3081 is formed with only three data points, and the model results are similar, with the probability distributions of the parameters presenting the same shapes. The N_0 and _ V histograms have median values of N_0 = 10 3 and _ V = 47 _ 23 ^ 42 , respectively. We determine lower limits of > 52 , q > 1.2 , and i > 42 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3081 as a ringed, weakly barred Seyfert with an obscured nucleus, compact symmetric STIS line emission, and anisotropic coronal-line evidence.

quotes.quote: NGC 3081 is a galaxy with complex morphology showing multiple stellar and dust rings, and a weak bar, hosting a Seyfert type 2 nucleus (Buta 1990). A spectropolarimetric study by Moran et al. (2000) has established that it is a Seyfert 1, obscured by dense material at a few parsecs from the nucleus. The radio emission of this galaxy has been studied by Ulvestad Wilson (1989).

The STIS blue spectra of NGC 3081 show strong, very symmetric, single-peaked lines from low-ionisation (i.e. [ O ii ] , [ Ne iii ] , and [ O iii ] ) and high-ionisation species (i.e , , and ), as well as permitted hydrogen and helium lines. The most extended CL is , which can be traced up to 40 pc SW and 55 pc NE of the nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper highlights NGC3081 as a Seyfert host with distinct spiral arms resembling barred spirals and dust clumps that the unsharp-mask method detects well.

quotes.quote: In Figure fig:fourpanels , we provide images of two galaxies (MARK1330, NGC3081) that show distinct spiral arms. These features are easily distinguished from the ambient stellar light profile in the core of each image. In some galaxies, these arms are reminiscent of galactic scale spiral features, such as barred spirals (NGC3081). Some spiral arm like features are more unique. For example, MARK1330 has a single arm that appears to in-spiral and connect with the bright core of the galaxy.

We measured the contrast parameters for two representative galaxies in the sample, NGC3081 and NGC3608. The IUM technique appears to work very well in detecting the dust clumps in NGC3081, whereas NGC3608 was largely devoid of dust clumps according to the visual inspection.

sources.summary: The paper reports a centrally peaked [ FeII ] line-to-continuum profile in NGC 3081, co-rotating disk gas, and a flat velocity-dispersion profile across its central kiloparsec.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3081 as a Seyfert 2 SED fit with central-resolution data, a steep radial profile, constrained near-infrared fitting, and added Herschel far-infrared fluxes.

quotes.quote: The galaxies are NGC 1068, NGC 3081 and NGC 5643 (Sy2s) and NGC 1365, NGC 3227 and NGC 4151 (Sy1s) and they are at distances smaller than 19 Mpc, with the exception of NGC 3081 (see Table sources ). This implies that, at the average resolution of ground-based N-band observations ( 0.3-0.4 ), we are probing the central 20-30 parsecs ( 55 pc for NGC 3081). On the other hand, in the case of NGC 3081, we have a steep radial profile ( q>2 ), and we cannot constrain Y by including the spectroscopy (see Appendix appendixA ). The fit of the whole NIR+MIR dataset of NGC 3081, which is shown in the fourth row of Figure ngc3081 , produces posteriors much more typical of Sy2 galaxies ( Ramos11 ). In the case of NGC 3081, we also have nuclear FIR fluxes from the Herschel Space Observatory that we published in Ramos11b .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3081 as an observed weakly barred, flat-rotation-curve galaxy whose rings and disk properties match analytic resonant-orbit formulations.

quotes.quote: This formulation and supporting by N-body simulations are compared to Hubble Space Telescope observations of the rings in the galaxy NGC 3081 which has a weak bar and a flat rotation curve. This galaxy is close enough to see individual young star associations in the rings. The pointed oval shape of the inner () ring is produced by the analytic formulation as observed, as are the outer R1 and R2 rings with all turning at the bar pattern speed. The NGC 3081 disk surface density and relative importance of the dark matter halo can be deduced with the formulation. Some of the eccentric resonant stellar orbits described below resemble the shapes of the star forming rings in the NGC 3081 model.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3081 as an SB0/a Seyfert 2 galaxy with large and nuclear bars, resonance rings, nuclear spiral arms, star formation, and radio/IFU components.

quotes.quote: NGC 3081 is an SB0/a galaxy hosting a Seyfert 2 nucleus Malkan1998 . The galaxy has a weak large-scale bar and a nuclear bar Buta1990 followed by four resonance rings -- two outer rings, an inner and a nuclear ring Buta1998 . Inside the nuclear ring, two spiral arms are observed. A comparison between the continuum adjacent to the \ and to the + \ lines showed that the nuclear ring and nuclear spiral arms are sites of recent or ongoing star formation Ferruit2000 . Radio observations revealed a compact radio source oriented roughly along the north-south direction Nagar1999b .

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 3081's central ionized gas, describing its strong-emission region, velocity field, and signs of nuclear outflow, rotation, density structure, or inflow.

quotes.quote: This Sy 2 galaxy shows gas emission peaking at the nucleus, a SER elongated to the N-NW, approximately perpendicularly to the PMA. A nuclear bar is observed along the PMA, whose orientation is in approximate agreement with that of the KMA. A distorted rotation pattern, observed in [ N ii ] has been associated to inflows along the bar, combined with a compact nuclear outflow almost perpendicular to the bar -- as previously discussed in SchnorrMuller2016 . The outflow is approximately oriented along the SER, and is associated with an increase in the gas density. An increase in is observed surrounding the SER, probably due again to an equatorial outflow , or lateral displacement of the ambient gas by the outflow.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 3081 as a nearby obscured AGN with Compton-thin line-of-sight absorption, Compton-thick average obscuration, high torus covering factor, and diffuse soft X-ray emission.

quotes.quote: We found that NGC 3081 is best fitted by the MYTorus model in the de-coupled mode (edge-on configuration), while ESO 565-G019 is best fitted by the borus02 model. NGC 3081 is Compton-thin along the l.o.s., but with the obscuring material being, on average, Compton-thick.

NGC 3081 has C _ TOR =0.73_ 0.10 ^ +0.09 , suggesting that the torus contributes in a significant way to cover the central emission. Moreover, the ratio between l.o.s. and average column densities is typical of a clumpy scenario. Thanks to the presence of \ data, we were able to investigate the extended emission of NGC 3081 and ESO 565-G019.

sources.summary: NGC 3081 is an SAB0 Seyfert 2 galaxy with a distinct reddened, metal-rich nucleus, mostly old stellar populations, and a larger star-forming ring known outside the observed field.

quotes.quote: NGC 3081 NGC 3081 is classified as SAB0, with a Seyfert 2 nucleus. We found that the nucleus displays slightly different properties than the rest of the galaxy, exhibiting a higher reddening ( 0.8 mag), a higher stellar sigma ( 180 km s ^ 1 ), a higher metallicity ( 0.035 ), and a very faint ( 10 ) contribution of a FC. Regarding the stellar population, we detected a dominance of old SPs, with a very small contribution of intermediate-age SPs in the south-eastern edge of our FoV, co-spatial with a 10 contribution of a FC. These SP results are consistent with the typical central populations of S0 galaxies. They found a very large ring with a radius of 3 kpc, with SFR surface density up to 10^ 2 M_ yr^ 1 kpc^ 2 .

sources.summary: The paper reports central nuclear emission and a bright southern mm-wave component in NGC 3081, with the southern feature lacking significant radio emission.

quotes.quote: 41: NGC 3081 In addition to the central nuclear emission with 0.7 mJy beam ^ 1 ( a ), our image at a resolution of 0 5 unveils a comparably bright component ( b ) with 0.5 mJy beam ^ 1 2 \ away from the nucleus in the south. An 8.4 GHz image was obtained at a similar beam size of 0 4 by using VLA, and shows radio emission ( 1 mJy) spatially consistent with a . On the other hand, there is no significant radio emission around the south mm-wave component ( b ). The upper limit of the radio emission, corresponding to 3 rms, is estimated to be 0.05 mJy beam ^ 1 , leading to an upper limit of < - 0.7 for the spectral index. This is smaller than that for the central emission ( 0.7 ).

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 3081 as a barred spiral with extended N- and Q-band MIR emission, weak PAH and [Ne II], strong high-ionization lines, and nuclear activity dominating its MIR emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 3081 is a barred spiral galaxy which presents extended emission in the N- and Q-band images, being brighter in the Q-band. The emission is stronger towards the north, extending out to 450 pc from the southeast to the northwest (PA 160 deg). The Spitzer/IRS spectrum of this galaxy shows faint 6.2 and 11.3 m PAH emission, weak [ Ne II ] line emission and strong [ Ne III ] , [ S IV ] and [ Ne V ] features. Therefore, based on the lack of PAH features in the Spitzer/IRS spectrum, we can conclude that the MIR emission of NGC 3081 is mainly produced by nuclear activity.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 3081 has a compact coronal nucleus plus a detached [SiVII] blob coincident with optical and UV emission in its extended ionized structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 3081, the coronal emission resolves into a compact nuclear region and a detached faint blob at 120 pc north of it. The HST [ OIII ] 5007 and H images show rather collimated structure extending across the nucleus along the north-south direction over 300 pc radius (Ferruit et al. 2000). Besides the nucleus, the second brightest region in those lines coincides with the detached [ Si VII ] emission blob (Fig. 2d). At this same position, we also find UV emission in a HST/FOC image at 2100 .

sources.summary: The paper reports larger-radius gas knots in NGC 3081, unusually high O3N2/N2 differences, and notes it is the only Seyfert with SB1 abundances measured.

quotes.quote: For seven galaxies (NGC 1433, NGC 1530, NGC 1832, NGC 2665, NGC 2935, NGC 3081 and NGC 4314) we have also found knots of gas emission at larger radii, corresponding to one or several crowded \ regions.
But in two of them, NGC 3081 and NGC 2217, these differences are higher (0.4-0.5 dex), and with the peculiarity that become smaller (again 0.1-0.2 dex) for larger galactocentric distances.
NGC 3081 is the only Seyfert galaxy for which we have been able to calculate abundances with the SB1 method.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 3081's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 3081 is a low-inclination barred spiral galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.008 ( D 40.9 Mpc) with a Sy 2 nucleus phillips_nearby_1983,durret_narrow_1986 with polarized broad emission lines moran_frequency_2000 . It is an X-ray ``buried" AGN candidate eguchi_suzaku_2011 and belongs to the nine-month BAT AGN sample. The AGN very compact in radio possibly elongated with PA 164 ; nagar_radio_1999 and possesses a biconical NLR extending 4 780 pc along a similar PA ferruit_hubble_2000 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3081 as a low-inclination barred spiral Sy2 galaxy with polarized type 1 features and Spitzer evidence for weak silicate absorption, PAH emission, and forbidden lines.

quotes.quote: NGC 3081 is a low-inclination barred spiral galaxy with a Sy2 nucleus Phillips83, Asmus14 . However, Moran00 reported a type 1 optical spectrum in polarized light. Weaver10 found that the Spitzer spectrum exhibits a weak absorption by silicate at 10 m , a weak PAH emission, and prominent forbidden emission lines.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3081 as a Compton-thin Seyfert whose high-energy data favor a transmission model, with a large absorbing column and intermediate iron-line equivalent width.

quotes.quote: NGC 3081 The Compton thin, transmission model (N _H = 6.4 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 ) fits the high energy data better than the Compton thick models at a high confidence level (the latter ones have reduced _r^2 > 2 ). The absorbing column density is very large, and is responsible for making the observed EW(Fe K ) intermediate between typical Compton thin sour ces and Compton thick ones.

More specifically NGC2273, NGC3393, NGC4939, NGC5643 and MCG-05-18-002 were identified as Compton thick with N _H > 10^ 25 cm ^ 2 , NGC1386 is Compton thick with N _H > 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 , while NGC3081 and NGC 4941 were identified as Compton thin with N _H 5 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 .

gmw_00000179.name: NGC 3094
gmw_00000179.overview: NGC 3094 is a low-inclination barred spiral galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.008 [arXiv:1310.2770]. It hosts an obscured active galactic nucleus, which is conservatively classified as an uncertain type II active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1310.2770].

sources.summary: The paper shows NGC 3094's flux-calibrated MIR image and PSF-subtraction residual and notes its silicate absorption bottom is noisy, making the optical-depth measurement a lower limit.

quotes.quote: Fig. fig:Imaging (left) shows the resulting flux-calibrated image for NGC 3094.
The right panel in Fig. fig:Imaging shows the residual after the subtraction of the PSF (dashed contours) for NGC 3094.
Note that in NGC 3094 and NGC 7479 the bottom of the silicate absorption is very noisy so the determination of _ 9.7 is poorly constrained. Thus, our measurement can be considered as lower limit.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 3094's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 3094 is a low-inclination barred spiral galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.008 ( D 40.8 Mpc) with an obscured AGN armus_long-slit_1989,imanishi_3.4-m_2000 . Not much is known about this AGN, there are not X-ray measurements available, and only a low-resolution radio map showing extended emission condon_1.49_1990 . We conservatively treat this object as uncertain type II AGN.

gmw_00000180.name: NGC 3166
gmw_00000180.overview: NGC 3166 is an inclined S0/a or SB0 galaxy [arXiv:1907.07261, arXiv:astro-ph/0406494] that hosts a LINER nucleus [arXiv:1310.2770]. It belongs to a tight triplet of disk galaxies [arXiv:1907.07261] and is undergoing cold gas accretion [arXiv:1907.07261], which is evidenced by multiple twisted dust filaments and asymmetric ionized gas [arXiv:1907.07261].

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 3166 as an S0/a in a tight triplet undergoing cold gas accretion, with asymmetric ionized gas, polar central gas, and outer corotation only beyond 60 arcsec.

quotes.quote: NGC 3166. This S0/a galaxy belongs to a tight triplet of disk galaxies embedded into a common \ envelope haynes81 . Many signatures give evidences for the current cold gas accretion onto NGC 3166 from this huge intergalactic gaseous cloud: young age, 2 Gyr, of the nuclear stellar population sil3166 , large extended molecular-gas reservoir within the galaxy disk nro94 , strongly lopsided structure of the large-scale stellar disk bournaud05 , multiple twisted dust filaments within the central bulge-dominated part of the galaxy hst3166 . The map in emission line (Fig. fig_fpi ) shows also asymmetric distribution of the ionized gas, with a prominent arm at 100^ (11 ) to the west from the nucleus. The gas isovelocities turn in the very center of the galaxy into a polar orientation. In the outer parts the gas starts to strictly corotate the stars only at R>60^ (Fig. fig_ls ), where the large-scale stellar disk begins to dominate in the total surface brightness s0disks .

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3166 as a bar-strength outlier with SB0 morphology, a relatively large bulge, weak disk, and reduced torque-based bar strength.

quotes.quote: As an example, the two systems with the largest H band F_ bar for their Q_g values (the points on the bottom right of Fig. 2b) are NGC 3166 and NGC 4772. Both these galaxies have H band morphologies of SB0 (Eskridge et al. 2002), with relatively large bulges and weak disks. The F_ bar statistic is very good at describing such bars, but Q_g is not. This is due to the relatively strong bulges causing the effective torque from the bars to be small.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 3166's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 3166 is an inclined spiral galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0045 ( D 25.3 Mpc), forming a possibly interacting group with NGC 3165 and NGC 3169. It hosts a little-studied LINER nucleus ho_search_1997-1 , which remained undetected at radio wavelengths nagar_radio_2005 but is supported by the detection of in the MIR dudik_spitzer_2009 . After , it was observed with IRTF devereux_infrared_1987 , and with /IRAC, IRS and MIPS.

gmw_00000181.name: NGC 3169
gmw_00000181.overview: NGC 3169 is an early-type spiral galaxy [arXiv:2206.03316] classified as a LINER [arXiv:0801.4382, arXiv:1310.2770] that hosts a low-luminosity active galactic nucleus [arXiv:0801.4382, arXiv:1112.4640]. The galaxy exhibits a warped disk and peculiar morphology [arXiv:0705.0973] due to its ongoing gravitational interaction with the nearby companion galaxy NGC 3166 [arXiv:2206.03316, arXiv:1108.3662].

sources.summary: The paper studies spatially resolved star formation efficiency in NGC 3169's bulge, finding a star-forming ring and longer depletion times toward the center linked to AGN, bulge, and dynamics.

quotes.quote: Here, we use spatially-resolved observations of warm ionised gas emission lines (H , [ O iii ] 4959,5007, [ N ii ] 6548,6583, H and [ S ii ] 6716,6731) from the imaging Fourier transform spectrograph SITELLE at the Canada-France-Hawaii Telescope (CFHT) and cold molecular gas ( 12 CO(2-1)) from the Atacama Large Millimeter/sub-millimeter Array (ALMA) to study the SFE in the bulge of the AGN-host galaxy NGC 3169. After distinguishing star-forming regions from AGN-ionised regions using emission-line ratio diagnostics, we measure spatially-resolved molecular gas depletion times ( _ dep 1/SFE) with a spatial resolution of 100 pc within a galactocentric radius of 1.8 kpc. We identify a star-forming ring located at radii 1.25 0.6 kpc with an average _ dep of 0.3 Gyr. At radii <0.9 kpc, however, the molecular gas surface densities and depletion times increase with decreasing radius, the latter reaching approximately 2.3 Gyr at a radius 500 pc. Based on analyses of the gas kinematics and comparisons with simulations, we identify AGN feedback, bulge morphology and dynamics as the possible causes of the radial profile of SFE observed in the central region of NGC 3169.

NGC 3169 is an S(a) spiral galaxy at a distance D=18.7 Mpc, according to the NASA Extragalactic Database redshift-independent distance catalogue steer2016redshift . It has an inclination i=54^ , a position angle PA=45^ and a systemic heliocentric velocity V_ sys =1232 km s ^ 1 (from the H i 21 cm observations of lee2012pre , consistent with the best-fitting systemic velocity derived from our own CO data discussed in Section vfield ). NGC 3169 is representative of early-type spiral galaxies, while interacting with its close companion galaxy NGC 3166 sil2006central .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3169 as an Sa LINER galaxy with a hard nuclear X-ray source, VLBA radio core, obscuration, and strong evidence for a low-luminosity AGN.

quotes.quote: NGC 3169 This is a galaxy of type Sa, at a distance of 19.7 Mpc t88 , and its nucleus is classified as a LINER hfs97-3 . The \ observation of the nucleus of NGC 3169 was analyzed by tw03 and the nucleus identified as a low luminosity AGN, but the x-ray data are analyzed and presented here again for completeness. NGC 3169 was observed by \ for 2 ks and detected with 159 counts. Its hardness ratio HR = +0.86 makes it the hardest of the six sources discussed here and the only one to have a positive HR.

The combination of being a sub-parsec scale radio source as well as a luminous hard x-ray source points to the source being an AGN. Further evidence comes from the high luminosity. The source is obscured, as made clear from the x-ray spectrum.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3169 as a nearby Sa LINER whose hard nuclear X-ray source, absorbed spectrum, and coincident compact radio source make it almost certainly an AGN.

quotes.quote: NGC 3169: This is an Sa galaxy at 20 Mpc, classified optically as a low ionization nuclear emission line region (LINER). detected a nuclear hard X-ray source (Fig. fig:n31n41 ), indicative of an AGN. The X-ray spectrum is an absorbed power-law with slope 2 and column density N_H 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 . The AGN identification is clinched by the fact that the X-ray source coincides with a 7mJy mas-scale VLA radio source . Thus the source is almost certainly an AGN. NGC 4102: This is a Sb galaxy at 17 Mpc, classified optically as a HII nucleus. detected a hard point source in its nucleus with extended soft emission (Fig. fig:n31n41 ).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3169 as a LINER 2 with extended soft nuclear emission and a large absorbing column, making it one of the highly absorbed LLAGNs in the sample.

quotes.quote: The soft and hard band images of the nuclear regions of NGC 3169 and NGC 4278 are shown in Fig 1 as examples of extended emission. In the soft band image of NGC 3169, emission in the nuclear region extending 10 arcsec in diameter is clearly visible, while the nucleus itself is not detected (Table 2).

One galaxy (NGC 3169, a LINER 2) has a large absorption column ( = 1.1 10^ 23 ), while two galaxies (NGC 4548, a LINER 2, and NGC 3226, a LINER 1.9) show substantial absorption ( 1.6 10^ 22 , and 0.9 10^ 22 , respectively). Obscured LLAGNs In our sample, we found at least three highly absorbed LLAGNs (NGC 3169, NGC 3226, and NGC 4548). Among these absorbed objects, NGC 2787 is classified as a LINER 1.9, NGC 3169, NGC 4548, and NGC 6500 as LINER 2s, and NGC 5866 as a transition 2 object.

sources.summary: The paper flags NGC 3169 as an interacting close-pair galaxy with a warped disk, peculiar morphology, and nuclear structure resembling pseudobulge features.

quotes.quote: For example NGC 3169 and NGC 3166 form a close pair (separation is 50 kpc). note that NGC 3169 shows a warped disk and peculiar morphology. Both of these galaxies have nuclear structure that is similar to structure found in pseudobulges. However, given the presence of the companion, we cannot say whether the central structure represents a secularly-evolved pseudobulge or is due to short-term merger-induced gas inflow and star formation (see KJB04 ).

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 3169's outer profile as I and describes bar evidence, spiral structure, dust features shaping its measured surface-brightness structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 3169 (I; SA(s)a pec) : This is a peculiar galaxy, clearly interacting with NGC 3166 mollenhoff01 , as seen from the weak low brightness bridge. Its bulge is large, though with considerable dust, and its spiral structure is filamentary. In spite of its irregularities, the brightness profile is of Type I. eskridge02 did mention ``evidence for a weak bar'', but we do not find any sign of this, in agreement with laurikainen04 and menendez-delmestre07 . No break in the profile is seen out to at least 6 scalelengths.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 3169 lacks enough optical data to constrain the truncated disk, but a jet-dominated model fits the available data with particle-dominated post-shock energy.

quotes.quote: NGC 3169 As was the case of NGC 2681, for this LLAGN there are not enough optical observations to fit the truncated thin disk model and estimate the transition radius. The JD model accounts well for the available data. The inferred extreme values of _e and _B imply that essentially all the energy in the post-shock region of the jet is carried by the particles.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 3169's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 3169 is an inclined spiral galaxy at a distance of D= 18.7 3.4 Mpc (NED redshift-independent median) with a LINER nucleus ho_search_1997-1 . At radio wavelengths, the nucleus appears a compact source with biconical extended emission along a PA 120 on arcsecond-scales hummel_effects_1987,nagar_radio_2000 and as unresolved on subarcsecond scales nagar_radio_2005 . The extended NLR is cospatial with the arcsecond radio structure gonzalez_delgado_h_1997 .

gmw_00000182.name: NGC 3185
gmw_00000182.overview: NGC 3185 is an SBa-type barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0211453, arXiv:1601.04706] located in a compact group [arXiv:1302.5011, arXiv:astro-ph/0211453] that hosts a borderline Seyfert 2 or transition nucleus [arXiv:1310.2770, arXiv:astro-ph/9811342]. The galaxy features a pseudobulge [arXiv:1004.5393] and a circumgalactic ring of star-forming material [arXiv:1302.5011, arXiv:1004.5393].

sources.summary: The paper detects radio emission from NGC 3185, recovers diffuse multi-band structure, and suggests its morphology may trace circumnuclear star-formation rings.

quotes.quote: Initially, we detected NGC 3185 and NGC 3941 only in the L-Band (at 1.5 GHz), with un-tapered maps exhibiting diffuse radio emission (see Figs. fig:contourMaps3185 and fig:contourMaps3941 ). In order to further investigate this issue, the C, X and Ku datasets of these sources have been re-imaged with a uv-taper equal to the L-band clean beam size (via the parameter uvtaper with sub-parameter outertaper = [ '1.21arcsec','1.15arcsec','-74.63deg' ] in tclean ), applied to match their respective L-band observation. This has been done in order to be more sensitive to the extended radio emission. Then, the final uv-tapered images have been smoothed to the final resolution of the L-band beam (via the restoringbeam parameter of the casa task tclean ). In the case of NGC 3941, which is fainter with respect to NGC 3185, we used a briggs weighting with robust parameter equal to 2: this value ensures that the weighting is close to a natural weighting. Natural weighting guarantees a better surface brightness sensitivity but degraded resolution, and for this reason it is more appropriate when considering diffuse emission. We have thus recovered radio emission in C band for NGC 3185 and NGC 3941 at a level above 5 , and radio emission in NGC 3185 in X band at a level 3.5 (images are shown in Figs. fig:contourMaps3185 and fig:contourMaps3941 ). In the case of NGC 3185, in order to estimate source parameters we considered interactively defined boundaries via the casa viewer . The uncertainties associated to peak and integrated flux densities are calculated as _S = N (rms)^2 +(0.05 S)^2 , where N is the number of beam areas covered by a source of flux density S, and it is taken into account an uncertainty of 5 per cent in the absolute flux density scale.

Considering NGC 3185, the structure observed in L-Band has a size of 5 3.8 arcsec, which translates into a linear scale of 0.5 0.4 kpc If instead we use z-independent distance provided in the Nasa/Ipac Extra-galactic Database (NED) of 23.2 Mpc, then the linear scale would be 0.56 0.42 kpc, so our linear scales estimates are affected by a 10 per cent error, which do not affect our considerations. . Analogous considerations can be made for the C and X Bands, leading to linear scales of 0.38 0.36 kpc. There is evidence for radio emission spread over the host galaxy scale for a number of Seyferts when observed with adequate angular resolution and sensitivity Orienti2015 . In particular, circumnuclear starburst rings with knots of star formation are observed. OrientiPrieto2010 observed diffuse radio emission with knots of star formation for a number of Seyferts, on scales smaller than 1 kpc (NGC 5506, NGC 7469 and NGC 7582). Analogously, the morphology of the radio emission in the case of NGC 3185 as observed in our radio maps may be consistent with emission from circumnuclear rings of star formation. In order to check this hypothesis, we overlapped L-band and C-band radio contours to an archivial HST image Taken from the Hubble Legacy Archive (HLA), https://hla.stsci.edu/ for NGC 3185, as shown in Fig. fig:3185_HST . The radio contours overlap with optical emission, from which a nearly circular, ring-like structure emerges. However, further work is needed to understand the link between radio emission and star formation in the form of circumnuclear rings in this source. In particular, intermediate angular resolution scales radio observations (such as those of e-MERLIN) combined with H maps would allow us to confirm the star-formation hypothesis.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 3185 as an LLAGN host, measuring its X-ray luminosity and detecting a faint unresolved radio source coincident with its optical nucleus.

quotes.quote: NGC 3185 is located at 25.8 Mpc 2007A&A...465...71T . There are two observations of the source in the X-ray band. Spectra extracted from both observations were fitted with an absorbed power law model, with a photon index ranging between 1.9 and 2.1 (best fit value for and respectively).

The mean unabsorbed source luminosity is 3.61 10^ 39 erg s ^ 1 . We detect a faint radio source, coincident with the optical nucleus of NGC 3185, in both sub-bands. The source is unresolved to a beam size of 0.48 0.24 arcsec at 7.45 GHz.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3185 as a Seyfert with a circumgalactic star-forming ring, far-infrared disk regions, compact-group membership, and low specific star formation.

quotes.quote: NGC 3185: This Seyfert galaxy shows a circum-galactic ring of star-forming material in both UV and IR images. The DECOMPIR decomposition requires a 3 and a 12 contribution of the AGN to the total IR luminosity and 8 35 luminosity respectively. Its disk shows up in the FIR image as two bright regions at the opposite ends of the galaxy. It is the most distant member of the triplet it forms with NGC 3190 and NGC 3187 on the sky, but it and NGC 3190 have much closer recessional velocities than NGC 3187 (1217 km s ^ 1 and 1271 km s ^ 1 vs 1581 km s ^ 1 ).
NGC 3185 has a negative IRAC slope and its sSFR places it in the relatively quiescent population as expected by Tzanavaris (2010).

sources.summary: The paper reports a two-maximum or centrally depressed [ FeII ] profile in NGC 3185, co-rotating disk gas, and a flat velocity-dispersion profile across its central kiloparsec.

quotes.quote: The H _2 line-to-continuum ratio is constant or drops significantly towards the nucleus in most galaxies, except NGC 2273, NGC 5273, and NGC 7450. For Br , in contrast, the ratio peaks towards the nucleus in many galaxies, consistent with stronger ionization mechanisms towards the active nucleus. These two results both explain the observed central drop of the H _2 /Br ratio. Of the three emission lines, the [ FeII ] line-to-continuum ratio shows the most distinct radial variations. Its profile is centrally peaked in Mrk 1, Mrk 3, NGC 262, NGC 1320, NGC 2273, NGC 3081, NGC 5273, and NGC 7450. Several sources show two maxima (or a central drop, e.g. NGC 1667, NGC 3185, NGC 4151, NGC 4253, NGC 4388, and NGC 5506). Finally, Mrk 1066, NGC 931, NGC 2110, NGC 2992, and NGC 4051 show peculiar distributions, e.g. the distribution in NGC 2110 shows three separate peaks.

However, the gas velocity curves are similar to those of the stars, which define the galaxy disk. This is the case in Mrk 1, Mrk 1066, NGC 262, NGC 931, NGC 1667, NGC 2110, NGC 2273, NGC 3081, NGC 3185, NGC 4388, NGC 5273, and NGC 5506. In these galaxies, all three emission lines display a velocity curve that follows that of the stars and is reminiscent of rotation, thus strongly implying that the line-emitting gas is located in the disk of the galaxy and rotating with the disk. Other galaxies (Mrk 3, NGC 1320, NGC 2992, NGC 4051, NGC 4151, NGC 4253, and NGC 7450) display velocity curves different from the stellar velocity curve in one or more emission lines, which may be a signature of non-rotational motion, either in- or outflows or other non-planar motion of the gas.

The radial dispersion profiles in Mrk 3, Mrk 1066, NGC 2110, NGC 2273, NGC 4051, NGC 4151, NGC 4253, NGC 5273, and NGC 5506 have a central peak, which indicates a higher kinetic gas temperature in the nucleus. Either the ISM is more turbulent (possibly caused by a jet- or wind-driven outflow), and/or more intensely heated by the incident radiation field. In either case, a peaked dispersion distribution is a strong indication for the AGN governing the physical state of the gas at the nucleus. The dispersion peak is most often present in [ FeII ] (indicating dust destruction close to the nucleus), and/or in Br (indicating a strong radiation field), while the H _2 line shows the least-pronounced central rise. Examples for flat velocity dispersion profiles are NGC 3081, NGC 3185, and NGC 4388, which show no significant change over up to 1.2 kpc in diameter. The velocity dispersion in all three sources is of order 100 km/s, well within the range observed for star forming regions, which is consistent with star formation being the dominant the gas excitation mechanism. Finally, the dispersion profiles in Mrk 1, NGC 262, NGC 931, NGC 1320, NGC 1667, NGC 2992, and NGC 7450 show marked differences between the three emission lines, with variations of 100 km/s or more at a given radius. This indicates that the ionized atomic and molecular gas is responding differently to the central AGN in these sources, perhaps due to the presences of asymmetric energy input (a jet or shocks) together with a stratified medium, e.g. a thin molecular disk in a thick ionized medium.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 3185 as a transition SBa galaxy in a compact group, with smooth central flux, extracted nuclear and off-nuclear spectra, and no detected stellar-population gradient or dust dilution.

quotes.quote: NGC 3185 is a so-called transition object (having both Seyfert and HII region characteristics) with a host galaxy of SBa type located in a compact group including NGC 3190 (Gon alves et al., 1999). To our knowledge, there are no published results on the stellar content of the inner bulge of this galaxy. The radial flux profile along PA=0 of this galaxy is smooth without a strong nucleus (Fig. 2). In Figure 4a we show the spectra of the nucleus and that of an extended region obtained by averaging together similar data extracted from both sides of the nucleus, 1.5 arcsec away (1'' 80pc). No evidence is found either for a stellar population gradient or dilution by dust (Figure 4b and Table 4).

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 3185 hosts an optically borderline AGN with claimed Compton-thick X-ray evidence, while MIR nondetection prevents stronger conclusions.

quotes.quote: NGC 3185 The optically borderline AGN in NGC 3185 has so far only been observed with . The corresponding data were analysed in and who claim a Compton-thick source based on the data. It was not detected in the MIR at subarcsecond resolution but the upper limit on the intrinsic X-ray luminosity estimated from the MIR--X-ray correlation is consistent with the estimate in . From the current data no further conclusion about the AGN nature of NGC 3185 can be drawn.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 3185 as a pseudobulge galaxy and reports its central morphology, Sersic index, and mid-infrared star-formation state.

quotes.quote: NGC 3185 RSBR1../SBa(s) Pseudobulge. The bulge has a nuclear ring that has radius of about 2 , with spiral arms extending off the ring. The decomposition yields n_b=1.0 , and the bulge is actively forming stars with M_ 3.6 M_ 8.0 =0.6 mag.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 3185's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 3185 is an inclined spiral galaxy at a distance of D= 20.3 6.1 Mpc springob_erratum:_2009 with a possible AGN, optically classified as a borderline Sy 2/H II nucleus ho_search_1997-1,goncalves_agns_1999 . The nucleus is only marginally detected at radio wavelengths (e.g., ho_radio_2001 ). The nucleus of NGC 3185 was first observed with IRTF devereux_infrared_1987 and MMT maiolino_new_1995 , in which it was weakly detected at 20 mJy.

sources.summary: NGC 3185 has an old SFD population, a 37 percent expected unbarred-disk SF fraction, and the oldest population age in the sample despite detectable disk star formation.

quotes.quote: However, significant differences are evident in the ages revealed by our spectroscopic analysis, with the SFD regions in two galaxies (NGC 3185 and NGC 3351) having very old populations, while NGC 2543 and NGC 2712 have evidence for more recent truncation of SF, within the last 2 Gyr.

The resulting fractions are: for NGC 2543, 33 per cent; for NGC 2712, 26 per cent; for NGC 3185, 37 per cent; and for NGC 3351, 15 per cent, with the smaller value for the last galaxy being due to its shorter bar.

The oldest population age is found for NGC 3185, the one galaxy in this sample classified as SBa, and the one that shows weakest (but still clearly detected) disk SF in its H image (Fig. fig:N3185 , lower frame).

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 3185 as an SBa galaxy with power-law photoionization and separates its lines into star-forming and Seyfert components.

quotes.quote: NGC 3185 is a SBa galaxy (Sandage Bedke 1994). Its emission-line spectrum is power-law photoionized according to Stauffer (1982). Ho et al. (1997a) called it a Seyfert 2, although the published line ratios indicate a ``transition'' spectrum.

gmw_00000183.name: NGC 3227
gmw_00000183.overview: NGC 3227 is an interacting barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:0705.4162, arXiv:1804.07311] that hosts a Seyfert active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2203.04585, arXiv:2405.20162]. It exhibits a circumnuclear starburst [arXiv:1804.07311], a molecular nuclear ring [arXiv:0705.4162], and strong X-ray and ultraviolet variability [arXiv:0810.3777, arXiv:2004.03824].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3227 as an interacting barred Seyfert with a molecular nuclear ring, radio and emission-line outflows, and regular stellar rotation in its bar-aligned center.

quotes.quote: NGC 3227 is a well studied barred galaxy, interacting with the elliptical galaxy NGC 3326, and with an associated dwarf galaxy carole_3227_2 . The galactic disc has an inclination of 56 , with a outer photometric major-axis at a position angle PA=158 carole_3227 , coincident with the major-axis of the stellar bar. It is hosting a type 1.5 Seyfert nucleus Ho3 . The central region has been mapped in ^ 12 CO(1-0) and ^ 12 CO(2-1) by , who detect molecular gas very close to the nucleus ( 13  pc). The inner kpc regions host several complex features: a radio jet (PA 10 ; 3227_carole_95b ), an conical NLR outflow at a PA of about 15 carole_3227 , an \ outflow at PA  50 3227_outflowHa , and a molecular nuclear ring eva_3227 .

Our \ stellar continuum map (Fig.  pair0 ) presents an elongated structure (PA  = 153 ), aligned with the stellar bar. The stellar velocity field is regular (Fig.  pair0 ), the major-axis being parallel to the galactic disc orientation. Outside > 5 , the stars rotate rigidly (isovelocity contours parallel to each others). The stellar velocity dispersion rises towards the centre, reaching 200 km s ^ 1 in the inner 5 .

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 3227 as an AO/SINFONI black-hole-mass modeling example with old-bulge and young nuclear-disk components, correlated model parameters, and a reliable central mass estimate.

quotes.quote: The example of NGC 3227 dav:dav06 demonstrates several important issues that should be borne in mind. For this object, two stellar components were included, representing the bulge of older stars and a thick nuclear disk of younger stars. The resulting ^2 landscapes for the three parameters ( M_ BH , M/L_ bulge , and M/L_ nuclear ) is shown in Fig. dav:nearby:fig:n3227 .

The NGC 3227 model yielded M/L_ bulge = 27.5 M_ /L_ , within the range 25--35 M_ /L_ expected for bulges of spiral galaxies for03 . On the other hand, it suggested that M/L_ nuclear = 2.5 M_ /L_ , a factor of 5 larger than that derived from modelling the starburst using evolutionary synthesis code. The reason is that the mass of the thick nuclear disk includes a substantial contribution from molecular gas, so that its effective ratio is M/L_ nuclear = 1 -5 M_ /L_ . Thus the model result is consistent with expectations. It is such consistency checks that can give one confidence that the derived black hole mass -- in this case 1.5 10^7 M _ - is reliable.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3227 as a reddened Seyfert 1.5 with variable UV flux, significant variable X-ray absorption, and nearly unchanged SED parameters across two XMM observations.

quotes.quote: NGC 3227 This AGN is classed as a Seyfert 1.5 in NED. As already mentioned, it is also known to have significant optical and UV reddening 2001ApJ...555..633C and our SED fit to the OM data confirms this. For the observation designated NGC 3227 (2), the point in the UVM2 band ( 2310 ) is lower in F_ than the UVW1 point ( 2910 ), contrary to the increasing shape expected from the UV disc emission, implying that the far UV has been significantly reddened. The nucleus also seems to exhibit considerable variability in the UVW1 band, as the flux at this energy in observation (1) is a factor of 2 larger than that for observation (2).

Additionally, this object is known to have significant and possibly variable X-ray absorption from , who analyse one of the same sets of XMM observations used here. Our simple zwabs model for the intrinsic absorption yields values of log( N_ H / cm ^ 2 ) between 21.1--22.6, broadly consistent with the estimates of 21.3--22.0 from .The presence of variable absorption presents difficulties in constraining the intrinsic X-ray luminosity. Our value of L_ 2-10keV 3.2 10^ 41 erg s^ 1 is comparable to the value 2.2 10^ 41 erg s^ 1 from the more detailed modelling of .

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 3227 velocity-dependent H lags with other AGNs, finding its reversed velocities match the off-axis variability model better than an accelerating outflow.

quotes.quote: These theoretical curves can be compared with the four best examples of a strong velocity dependence in the H lag: the 1993-1995 monitoring of Mrk 6 sergeev+99 , the 2007 monitoring of NGC 3516 and NGC 3227 denney+09b , and the 2008 monitoring of Mrk 40 bentz+08 . NGC 3227 shows the opposite sign of the velocity dependence to the other three, so it has been plotted with the velocity scale reversed. The four AGNs have different line widths and total lags. To facilitate comparisons the lags of both observations and models have been normalized to a 15-day lag at zero velocity.

8 is that an accelerating outflowing wind model is a poor fit to the NGC 3227 lags. In the wind model the high-velocity red wing should show the largest lag, but the observed lag decreases in the wings. It can be seen instead that, after reversing the velocities, the velocity dependence of the NGC 3227 lags is completely consistent with the velocity dependence for the other three AGNs. This supports the idea of a common origin of the velocity dependence of the lags.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 3227 has centrally peaked velocity dispersion and h3 anti-correlation with the velocity field, attributed to slower bulge-star rotation.

quotes.quote: Patches of low- are seen for Mrk 607, NGC 2110, NGC 3516, NGC 4051, NGC 4235 and NGC 5899, while three objects (NGC 1052, NGC 3227 and NGC 4388) show a centrally peaked distribution. These rings and partial rings are located at distances from the nucleus ranging between 150 and 250 pc. Nuclear values for each galaxy are presented in the last column of Table tab_mod , obtained by fitting the nuclear spectrum integrated within a circular aperture with radius of 75 pc. Most galaxies show low h_ 3 values ( 0.10 <h_ 3 < 0.10), suggesting that their LOSVDs are well represented by a Gaussian velocity distribution. Exceptions are Mrk 607, Mrk1066 and NGC 4051 that show h_ 3 values larger than 0.15. For 8 galaxies (NGC 1052, NGC 2110, NGC 3227, NGC 3516, NGC 4051, NGC 5506, Mrk 607 and Mrk 1066), the h_ 3 map is anti-correlated with the velocity field, with positive h_ 3 values seen at locations where rotation velocities are observed in blueshifts and negative h_ 3 values associated to regions where the rotation velocities are observed in redshifts.

The velocity dispersion maps show low- ( 50-80 ) rings for 4 galaxies (Mrk 1066, Mrk 1157, NGC 5929 and NGC 788) or ``patches" of low- structures (for Mrk 607, NGC 2110, NGC 3516, NGC 4051, NGC 4235 and NGC 5899) at typical distances of 200 pc, interpreted as being originated in young/intermediate age stellar populations. Centrally peaked maps are observed for three galaxies (NGC 1052, NGC 3227 and NGC 4388). The h_ 3 moment is anti-correlated with the velocity field for 8 galaxies (NGC 1052, NGC 2110, NGC 3227, NGC 3516, NGC 4051, NGC 5506, Mrk 607 and Mrk 1066) -- positive h_ 3 values seen at locations where the velocity field shows blueshifts and h_ 3 <0 for locations where the velocity field shows redshifts. The presence of these wings are attributed to the contribution of stars from the galaxy bulge that present lower rotation velocities. The observed velocity fields are well reproduced by a rotating disk model, with deprojected velocity amplitudes in the range 60--340 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3227 as the spiral/Seyfert member of Arp 94 behind J1023+1952, with projected overlap, similar CO ratio, and a possible gaseous bridge to the TDG.

quotes.quote: J1023 + 1952 is a kinematically detached cloud in front of the spiral galaxy NGC 3227, in the interacting system Arp 94 1995MNRAS.277..641M . Fig. 1 shows our target in context, highlighting the large size of the Arp 94 system and its complexity in terms of tidal tails as revealed by and deep optical emission. Specifically, the Arp 94 system is composed of a spiral galaxy hosting a Seyfert 2 nucleus NGC 3227; 1995MNRAS.275...67M,2001ApJ...549..254S,2014ApJ...794....2N interacting with a ``green valley'' elliptical NGC 3226; 2014ApJ...797..117A . The TDG lies at the base of the northern tidal tail and partially overlaps in projection with the disc of NGC 3227. Since the TDG is obscuring stellar light from NGC 3227, it must be in front of the spiral galaxy 1995MNRAS.277..641M,2004ApJ...614..648M .

Moreover, there is some kinematic evidence for a gaseous ``bridge'' along the line of sight towards the North-East direction, which is mostly made up of compact structures (GMCs) and connects the TDG with the background galaxy NGC 3227. Overall, the velocity of the TDG along the line of sight is positive relative to the centre of NGC 3227, similar to the southern part of NGC 3227. Since extinction arguments confirm that the TDG is currently in front of the spiral galaxy 2004ApJ...614..648M , the TDG must be moving towards NGC 3227, as long as the tangential velocities (which we cannot measure) are not much larger than the radial velocity. That ``backward'' motion towards NGC 3227 would suggest that the TDG has been detached from its parent galaxy for a considerable amount of time; the timescales for such interactions are typically of the order of several hundred Myr or even 1 Gyr.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 3227 during a triggered transient X-ray obscuration event, constructing its broadband SED from NIR through hard X-rays using new and archival observations.

quotes.quote: From monitoring of a sample of active galactic nuclei (AGN) we found a transient X-ray obscuration event in Seyfert-1 galaxy NGC 3227, and thus triggered our joint , , and Hubble Space Telescope ( ) observations to study this event. Here in the first paper of our series we present the broadband continuum modelling of the spectral energy distribution (SED) for NGC 3227, extending from near infrared (NIR) to hard X-rays. We use our new spectra taken with , , and the HST Cosmic Origins Spectrograph (COS) in 2019, together with archival unobscured , , and HST Space Telescope Imaging Spectrograph (STIS) data, in order to disentangle various spectral components of and recover the underlying continuum.

In 2019 we discovered another obscuration event in the AGN NGC 3227 . Figure swift_lc shows the light curve of from March 2018 to end of 2019. In November 2019, we found an intense X-ray spectral hardening event that lasted for a few weeks until the end of the visibility window. During this period we successfully executed the triggering of our , , and /COS observations. Two sets of observations were taken, separated by about three weeks. Figure unobscured_obscured_fig compares an archival unobscured spectrum of taken on 5 December 2016 with the new obscured spectra taken on 15 November and 5 December 2019. Strong soft X-ray absorption is evident in the new spectra.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 3227 as a Seyfert AGN with multi-phase warm absorbers and obscuration events, finding BLR-scale obscurers distinct from longer-lived warm absorbers.

quotes.quote: NGC 3227 is a suitable target to study the properties of both WAs and obscurers, because it matches the following selection criteria: WAs in both ultraviolet (UV) and X-rays, suitably variable, bright in UV and X-rays, good archival spectra for comparing with the obscured spectra. To investigate WAs and obscurers of NGC 3227 in detail, we used a broadband spectral-energy-distribution model that is built in the first paper of our series and the photoionization code of SPEX software to fit the archival observational data taken by XMM-Newton and NuSTAR in 2006 and 2016. Using unobscured observations, we find four WA components with different ionization states ( \ \ [ erg \ cm \ s ^ 1 ] 1.0, \ 2.0, \ 2.5, \ 3.0 ).

We find an obscuration event in 2006, which was missed by previous studies. One obscurer component is required for this 2006 obscuration event. For the previously published obscuration event in 2016, we detect two obscurer components. A high-ionization obscurer component ( \ = 2.81_ 0.04 ^ +0.15 ; covering factor C_ f =0.29^ +0.04 _ 0.08 ) only appears in the 2016 observation, which has a column density around 10^ 23 \ cm ^ 2 , while both the 2006 and 2016 observations have a low-ionization obscurer component ( \ 1.0--1.9; C_ f 0.2-0.5 ), which has a lower column density ( 10^ 22 \ cm ^ 2 ) than the high-ionization obscurer component. Assuming that the variations of flux is caused by the transverse motion of obscurers across the line of sight, we estimate the locations of obscurers to be within the BLR.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 3227's 2019 X-ray obscuration event, finding a short-lived obscurer with uncertain location and no accompanying broad UV absorption troughs.

quotes.quote: NGC 3227 is a nearby z=0.003859 , dva91 Seyfert 1.5 galaxy, which contains a supermassive black hole M_ BH =5.96 10^6 M_ , ben15 . Multiple X-ray obscuration events have been reported in NGC 3227 from 2003 to 2016 lam03,mar14,beu15,tur18 . As described in Paper I hereafter meh21 , a Swift geh04 monitoring campaign was carried out in XMM- Newton Cycle 18 (PI: J. S. Kaastra) to catch transient X-ray obscuration events in a sample of type I AGN. In late 2019, an X-ray obscuration event in NGC 3227 was identified with the Swift monitoring. We triggered two joint target-of-opportunity observations with XMM- Newton jan01 , NuSTAR har13 , and HST Cosmic Origins Spectrograph COS, gre12 on 2019-11-15 and 2019-12-05, respectively.

Multiple X-ray obscuration events have been reported in the nearby Seyfert 1.5 galaxy NGC 3227 from 2000 to 2016. Another X-ray obscuration event was found in late 2019. Our photoionization modeling of the two X-ray observations (in mid-November and early December) requires distinct parameters of the obscurer, which cannot be explained by the same obscurer (whether it has one or two photoionized components) responding to the variable ionizing continuum.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3227 as a Seyfert with thermal molecular hydrogen emission, a compact molecular disk, circumnuclear star formation, and possible X-ray contribution to the gas excitation.

quotes.quote: NGC 3227 supports this scenario. Figure perfis a shows a comparison of the line profiles of 2 2.121 m and [ Fe ii ] 1.257 m. It can be seen that the latter line is much broader than the former although both are emitted from a region 70 pc in radius. Note that [ Fe ii ] 1.257 m is slightly asymmetric towards the blue, mainly in the wing of the line, but it is not possible to tell from the data if the asymmetry is related to 2 \ or it is due to He i 1.252 m. NICMOS imaging in 2 (1-0)S(1) 2.121 m carried out by Quillen et al. ( ) on this object shows that the molecular hydrogen emission originates in a 100 pc diameter disk that is not directly associated with either the [ O iii ] emission or the 18 cm radio emission.

Moreover, Rodr guez-Ardila Viegas ( ) detected 3.3 m PAH emission in the inner 120 pc of this object, implying circumnuclear star formation within that region probably related to the disk of molecular gas. The differences in line widths reported here supports the view that both the NLR and the molecular gas are co-spatial but emitted by different volumes of gas and most probably originating from different excitation mechanisms.
In four out of seven objects, the predicted 2 \ flux is negligible while in NGC 4151 and NGC 3227, X-rays may contribute for up to 15 of the measured 2.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 3227 has rotating stellar kinematics, modest residuals, asymmetric turnover, and a low-dispersion region matching CO emission and blue near-infrared colors.

quotes.quote: NGC 3227 Our radial velocity map (central right panel of Fig. painel-3227 ) also shows a rotation pattern, where the redshifted side is to SE of the nucleus, in agreement with the H i radial velocity data of SET00 . The line of nodes PA obtained from the model ( = 156.7 ) agrees within 4 with the values found by mun95 and SET00 . The residuals of the model fitting, shown in the bottom right panel of Fig. painel-3227 , are mostly below 15 km s ^ 1 , except for two blueshifted regions approximately along the line of nodes, which deviate from the model by more than 20 km s ^ 1 .

The rotation curve (upper right panel) suggests that to NW the turnover has been reached already at 150 pc from the nucleus, while this does not happen to the SE. The velocity dispersion map (bottom left panel of Fig. painel-3227 ) is characterized by a nuclear region with above 100 km s ^ 1 which drops in all directions but towards SE reaching values of 75 km s ^ 1 or less. The values increase again towards E, N and NW as evidenced in Fig. corte-3227 by two one-dimensional cuts of the map. The loci of low regions have a good correspondence to the loci of high CO emission reported by SET00 and referred to as a gas ring. In particular, the loci with the lowest velocity dispersions correspond to those with highest CO emission.

sources.summary: The paper uses Chandra HRC/LETG observations of Seyfert galaxy NGC 3227 to constrain radiative dark matter decay from its assumed NFW halo mass.

quotes.quote: We have studied a Chandra HRC/LETG observation of the Seyfert 1 galaxy NGC 3227 (observation id 1591), shown in Fig. fig:spectrum . NGC3227 has a redshift of z=0.004 , which corresponds to a luminosity distance of 16.7  Mpc. The data has been processed and analysed with CIAO version 3.4 using CALDB version 3.4.0 .
We have assumed an Navarro, Frenk White (NFW)  profile for the dark matter halo of NGC3227 with a scale radius of 15 kpc and a virial radius of 200 kpc.

The mass of NGC3227 has been taken to be 10^ 11 solar masses which is conservatively low based on the luminosity of the galaxy . The observational field of view is 25  kpc at the distance of NGC3227, reducing the observed mass to about a tenth of the total mass. This is probably an underestimate of the observed mass, but a larger observed mass will only improve the constraints.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 3227 as a nearby Seyfert galaxy, measuring high central HCN/CO ratios, circumnuclear-ring contrast, and X-ray ionisation as a plausible driver of enhanced nuclear HCN.

quotes.quote: In this paper we examine the specific case of the Seyfert galaxy NGC 3227, for which there are subarcsecond resolution data for the HCN (1-0) 88 GHz and CO (2-1) 230 GHz rotational lines, enabling us to spatially separate a circumnuclear ring at a radius of 140 pc and an inner nuclear region within 40 pc of the AGN. The HCN(1-0)/CO(2-1) flux ratio differs by more than an order of magnitude between these two regions. We carry out large velocity gradient (LVG) computations to determine the range of parameters (gas temperature and density, HCN/CO abundance ratio, column densities and velocity gradients) that yield physically plausible solutions for the observed flux ratio in the central 100 pc.

We present an LVG analysis of high-resolution observations of CO (2-1) and HCN (1-0) line emissions in the central regions of the Seyfert galaxy NGC 3227. We find that The HCN(1-0)/CO(2-1) ratio in NGC 3227 is an order of magnitude higher in the central 80 pc than in the circumnuclear ring at a radius of 140 pc. NGC 6951 and NGC 1068 have similarly high published ratios in their central 100 pc. The nuclear HCN/CO intensity ratio in NGC 3227 may reflect optically thick line emission in dense gas with only a weak constraint on the HCN/CO abundance ratio.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3227 as having unresolved 3 mm continuum but compact, resolved nuclear dense-gas emission with a large line width and little resolved-out flux.

quotes.quote: NGC 3227 A Gaussian fit to the 3 mm continuum (Fig fg:3227 , left panel) yields a size of 1.15 1.05 FWHM at a position angle of 47^ . This is very similar to the beam size and indicates the continuum is spatially unresolved. The 3 mm flux density from the integrated spectrum (Fig fg:sp3227 ) is 1.56 0.24 mJy, consistent with the 1.79 0.12 mJy obtained from the Gaussian fit to the continuum map.

Comparison with the previous measurement of 2.1 Jy km/s at 2.4 resolution sch00a suggests that very little of the line emission has been resolved out at our higher resolution, and that most of the \ in NGC 3227 does originate from the central compact source. The compact nature of the \ emission is in stark contrast to the CO(2-1) emission, as shown in Fig. fg:maps . Indeed the CO emission is distributed around the circumnuclear ring (which is also seen in the H-band stellar continuum, Fig 5 and dav06 ), and very little originates from the central arcsecond. On the other hand, the \ emission is dominated by the nucleus itself.

sources.summary: NGC 3227 is presented as a nearby Seyfert 1.5 interacting with NGC 3226, with recent nuclear star formation, high-ionization lines, and detected mid-J CO emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 3227 NGC 3227 is a nearby (14.4 Mpc) Seyfert 1.5 galaxy Ho1997 . It is interacting with the dwarf elliptical galaxy NGC 3226. The hard X-ray luminosity of the AGN is 1.9 10 ^ 41 erg s ^ 1 Gondoin2003 . In addition, recent ( 40 Myr) intense star-formation is observed in the nucleus. The luminosity produced by these young stars represents about half of the bolometric luminosity of the galaxy Davies2006 .

In the FTS spectrum of NGC 3227 we detected the CO lines from J_ up = 4 to J_ up = 11 . These CO emission lines are not resolved at the \ spectral resolution; thus we assumed the line width of the middle component of the 1 and J=2-1 transitions, 200 (see Section ss:groundCO_fitting ).

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 3227 as a reddened Seyfert 1 whose inclined disk constrains NLR emission, with asymmetric high-velocity H outflows attributed to bicone geometry.

quotes.quote: NGC 3227 This is a highly reddened Seyfert 1 galaxy with an inclined disk that constrains NLR emission to the northeast of the nucleus. The modeled kinematics provide a fair fit to the data, with less successful fits in slit positions 0.5'' from the nucleus. Modeling would not be possible using [ OIII ] spectra alone, as the single available G430L slit position does not return enough kinematic information to fit to a model, as shown in the online supplementary data. Fortunately, supplementary G750M spectra map out a large portion of the NLR, which allow us to see prominent doppler-shifted H emission northeast of the nucleus. Though corresponding [ O III ] emission is lacking, we can assume that the observed emission is due to outflows in the NLR versus ionization and rotation within the host disk as the kinematics are asymmetric, contain blueshifted velocities > 400 km s ^ 1 , and contain double-peaked profiles in the narrow H emission line (see also Wal08 ).

We find that the blue and redshifted kinematic components can instead be attributed to a single side of both cones, with the host disk extinguishing the other half of each cone, and that the bicone axis is near our line of sight. This alignment between bicone axis and host disk concurs with analysis in Cre02 which concludes that AGN reddening of NGC 3227 is due to lukewarm absorbers coplanar with the host disk.

sources.summary: NGC 3227 is treated as a reverberating dwarf Seyfert whose line-profile inner BLR radius follows the usual size-luminosity relation and agrees closely with its reverberation radius.

quotes.quote: In the present contribution, high quality spectra obtained with the Space Telescope Imaging Spectrograph (STIS) are used to constrain the size of the BLR in the dwarf Seyfert nuclei of M81, NGC 3998, NGC 4203, NGC 3227, NGC 4051, and NGC 3516.

There are presently six LLAGNs whose BLR size has been deduced by modeling the shape of the H Balmer lines including three non-reverberating dwarf Seyferts (M81, NGC 3998 and NGC 4203), and three reverberating ones (NGC 3227, NGC 3516 and NGC 4051). Some basic observables are listed in Table 1, and their BLR size-luminosity relationship is illustrated in Figure 1. The three non-reverberating dwarf Seyferts lie far from the correlation defined by reverberating AGNs. The other three LLAGN are reverberating, and their inner radii consistently lie on the correlation defined by other reverberating AGN. This latter result is important as it shows that the line profile fitting method does yield a size that agrees with the reverberation size.

In particular, the inner radius determined from line profile fitting coincides almost identically with the reverberation radius as demonstrated for NGC 3227 Dev13 , NGC 4051 DH13 and NGC 3516 (Devereux 2015, in prep). This coincidence between the reverberation radius and the inner radius determined from line profile fitting can be understood if both are measuring the gas closest to the BH.

sources.summary: The paper presents ALMA observations of Seyfert galaxy NGC 3227, finding barred large-scale molecular motions, extended nuclear continuum, a molecular outflow entrained by the AGN wind, and non-classical torus-like CO emission.

quotes.quote: We present ALMA observations of the CO(2--1) and CO(3--2) molecular gas transitions and associated (sub)-millimeter continua of the nearby Seyfert 1.5 galaxy NGC 3227 with angular resolutions 0.085-0.21 (7--15 pc). On large scales the cold molecular gas shows circular motions as well as streaming motions on scales of a few hundred parsecs associated with a large scale bar. We fitted the nuclear ALMA 1.3 mm emission with an unresolved component and an extended component. The 850 m emission shows at least two extended components, one along the major axis of the nuclear disk and the other along the axis of the ionization cone.
Assuming that the radial motions are in the plane of the galaxy, then we interpret them as a nuclear molecular outflow due to molecular gas in the host galaxy being entrained by the AGN wind.

The nuclear CO(2-1) and CO(3-2) molecular gas and sub-mm continuum emission of NGC 3227 do not resemble the classical compact torus. Rather, these emissions extend for several tens of parsecs and appear connected with the circumnuclear ring in the host galaxy disk, as found in other local AGN.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 3227 as a nearby Seyfert AGN with strong X-ray/UV variability, occultation-related spectral hardening, absorption signatures, and lags attributed to circumnuclear material.

quotes.quote: Here, we report on a large series of XMM-Newton + NuSTAR observations of NGC 3227, a nearby Seyfert 1.5 galaxy at a redshift of z = 0.003859 deVaucouleurs91 and an estimated luminosity distance of 20.3 Mpc Mould00 .

Summary In this paper, we have presented a series of X-ray variability results from a long XMM-Newton and NuSTAR observing campaign on the bright AGN NGC 3227. We find the source to exhibit strong variability in both the X-ray and UV bands. The typical trend is for the source to be steeper when brighter, consistent with other similar-class AGN. However, NGC 3227 does also undergo a period of significant spectral hardening due to an occultation event by a cloud of mildly ionized gas passing into the line of sight. This largely manifests itself as absorption signatures --- primarily from the Fe UTA - in the soft X-ray band.

Instead, given the well-established evidence for the existence of large quantities of circumnuclear photoelectrically-absorbing material a few tens to hundreds of r_ g from the black hole in NGC 3227, we instead consider that these lags may arise from this material via energy-dependent scattering.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 3227 as a Seyfert 1.5 spiral with radio components, outflows, a molecular ring, distorted gas rotation, and AGN/HLR excitation structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 3227 NGC 3227 is a spiral galaxy (SAB(s)a), located at a distance of 22 Mpc and with a Sy 1.5 nucleus ho97 . A full description of its NIR emission line spectrum is made in rogerio06 . Radio continuum observations at 18 cm show two main components separated by 0.4 arcsec, offset from the optical peak and located at PA 10^ . An outflow is observed in the [ O iii ] 5007 , but it is not aligned with the radio emission mundell95 .

schinnerer00 detected an asymmetric nuclear ring of cold molecular gas with 3 arcsec diameter, which is co-spatial with regions of high /Pa (of up to 4) and H _2 /Br (up to 10) values astor19 . The velocity fields of the hot molecular and ionised gas show a rotation pattern, but it is distorted indicating the presence of additional kinematic components astor19 . The flux maps for NGC 3227 (Fig. fig:N3227 ), show extended emission over most of the FoV, except for the , whose emission is restricted to the inner 1 arcsec radius.

sources.summary: The paper selects NGC 3227 as a PAH-containing intermediate Seyfert AGN and describes it as an interacting spiral with central molecular gas and hard X-ray variability.

quotes.quote: In order to compare our spectroscopic data with astrophysical objects, we selected three distinct PAH-containing AGNs, namely NGC 3227, NGC 7469, and NGC 7582, taken from . These AGNs are classified as intermediate Seyfert-type galaxies, with PAH bands identified in mid-IR and predominance of small, neutral, and pure PAH species MartinsFranco2021 . The X-ray luminosities in the range of 2-10 keV of these selected AGNs are shown in Table tab:agn .

NGC 3227 is a spiral-type galaxy interacting with a neighboring dwarf elliptical galaxy Delgado1997 . This object presents a large concentration of molecular gas in the central part, equivalent to 80 of the total CO emission that is detected in a radius of 1.2 kpc Delgado1997 . NGC 3227 is located at a distance of 22 Mpc, at a luminosity of log L _X = 42.14 0.21 erg s ^ 1 Jensen2017 . The galaxy also exhibits a short-term hard X-ray variability likely related to variation in the intrinsic continuum emission RamosAlmeida2009 .

sources.summary: The paper characterizes NGC 3227 as a Seyfert 1 galaxy with multi-phase AGN-driven outflows, biconical geometry, and a tidal interaction history with NGC 3226.

quotes.quote: We have characterized the ionized, neutral, and warm molecular gas kinematics in the Seyfert 1 galaxy NGC 3227 using observations from the Hubble Space Telescope Space Telescope Imaging Spectrograph, Apache Point Observatory's Kitt Peak Ohio State Multi-Object Spectrograph, Gemini-North's Near-Infrared Integral Field Spectrometer, and the Atacama Large Millimeter Array. We fit multiple Gaussians to several spatially-resolved emission lines observed with long-slit and integral-field spectroscopy and isolate the kinematics based on apparent rotational and outflowing motions. We use the kinematics to determine an orientation for the bicone along which the outflows travel, and find that the biconical structure has an inclination of 40 ^ +5 _ 4 \ from our line of sight, and a half-opening angle with an inner and outer boundary of 47 ^ +6 _ 2 and 68 ^ +1 _ 1 , respectively.

NGC 3227 ( z = 0.003859, pictured in Figure fig:ngc 3227 ) is a Seyfert 1, SAB(s)a type galaxy deVaucouleurs91 . It is located in the constellation Leo at a distance of 23.7 2.6 Mpc tonry01, blakeslee01 so that its transverse scale corresponds to 115 pc arcsec ^ 1 on the plane of the sky. NGC 3227 has a complex dynamical history through its interactions with its companion NGC 3226.
Overall, significant excursions from the rotation curve in all three components indicate outflows in the nuclear region of NGC 3227 that reach projected velocities up to 500 km s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: NGC 3227 is one of two low-luminosity AGN outliers in the BLR size-luminosity relation; extinction corrections improve but do not remove its offset.

quotes.quote: Two obvious outliers, which do not fit the general trends in any of the wavelength bands, are NGC 3227 and NGC 4051. These are the two-least luminous objects in all wavelength bands. This behavior could be due to major reddening or obscuration in these two objects, which decreases their measured luminosity (e.g., NGC 3227 - Crenshaw et al. 2001; NGC 4051 - Kaspi et al. 2004).

For NGC 3227, the extinction correction suggested by Crenshaw et al. (2001) increases the UV luminosity (at 1450 ) by a factor of 66 and the optical luminosity by a factor of 2 (see Figure mainfigure ). Although this would improve the agreement of this data point with the general trend, it would still remain an outlier. An alternative explanation can be that the BLR-size -- luminosity relation found at high luminosities breaks down at low luminosities. We will exclude these two objects from our fits to the BLR-size -- luminosity relation, noting the derived relation applies to AGNs with optical luminosity in the range 10^ 43 < L_ (5100 ) < 10^ 46 .

sources.summary: The paper reports that SINFONI data resolved young nuclear stars in NGC 3227 and enabled a stellar-dynamical black hole mass estimate of 5 million to 20 million solar masses.

quotes.quote: In the 5 cases we have analysed in detail so far (Mkn 231, NGC 7469, IRAS 05189-2524, Circinus, NGC 3227), the stellar population is young: the range of ages we find is 40--120 Myr The (young) stellar luminosity is comparable to that of the AGN on scales of 1 kpc (Mkn 231, IRAS 05189-2524); is 10--50 of the AGN on scales of 50--100 pc (NGC 7469, NGC 3227); and is a few percent of the AGN on scales of 10--20 pc (Circinus).

The high spatial resolution and integral field capability of SINFONI provide an ideal combination to do this, and we have successfully derived M_ BH in NGC 3227 from stellar kinematics -- the first time for a Seyfert 1 -- using Schwarzschild orbit superposition techniques. Details of the specific code, which is based on that used by the Nuker team, are given in Thomas et al. (2004) . While the inclination and mass-to-light ratios are often uncertain parameters, for NGC 3227 they are relatively well constrained. Nevertheless, we have explored the range of values which the modelling would permit and find it to be consistent with those expected, giving us confidence that the results are physically meaningful and reasonably robust. The resulting range of permissible black hole masses is M_ BH = 5 10^6 - 2 10^7 M_ .

sources.summary: The paper uses reverberation mapping for NGC 3227, fits its spectra with PLG, notes poor model fits at low fluxes, and discusses a possible absorption event.

quotes.quote: Data selection and reduction In Tab. tab:tab1 , we list the 10 AGN in our sample, some of their properties (distance, redshift and BH mass estimates), and details of the \ observations we used in this work. We used the petersonea:2004 estimates, from reverbaration mapping, for the BH mass (M _ BH ) in Fairall 9, NGC 4051, NGC 3227, NGC 5548, NGC 3783, and NGC 3516. In the case of Mrk 766 and MCG-6-30-15 we used the stellar velocity dispecrsion measurements of botteea:2004 and mchardyea:2005 , respectively, and the tremaineea:2002 M_ BH relation to estimate BH mass. The BH mass estimates for Ark 564 and NGC 5506 were taken from zhangwang:2006 and wangzhang:2007 , respectively.

Summary of the acceptable model fits = = 1. A simple PL model describes well the spectra of Fairall 9, Akn 564 and Mkn 766. 2. In NGC 4051, NGC 3227, and NGC 5548 a statistically acceptable global fit to the data is obtained with the PLG model. 3. In NGC 3783 and NGC 5506, the addition of an absorption edge with energy fixed at 7.1 keV is needed to achieve an acceptable model fit (ePLG). 4. In MCG 6-30-15 the best global fit is reached by the ePLG model but only when the edge energy is allowed to vary (the resulting mean edge energy is E _ edge 7.4 keV). 5. Finally, the same ePLG model provides an acceptable global fit to the NGC 3516 spectra but only when both the energy of the iron line and the energy of the absorption edge were left free to vary ( E _ line 6.1 , and E _ edge 7.95 ).

The disagreement between best model fit and data points is more pronounced in the case of NGC 3227, especially at low fluxes. This is a source where a possible absorption event, which lasted for a few months, has been observed in its \ light curve lamerea:2003 . In this case, one would expect _ obs to be harder than the model predictions at a given (low) flux, which is opposite to what is observed. However, such an event should also shift the model line to lower fluxes. Therefore it is difficult to reach final conclusions regarding the quality of the model fit to the NGC 3227 data.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3227 as an interacting nearby Seyfert whose compact coronal-line region is limited to the central tens of parsecs.

quotes.quote: NGC 3227 This nearby Seyfert 1 galaxy is classified as a SAB galaxy and is interacting with NGC 3226. It is known for having a compact (0.4 ) double radio source at PA 170 , and [ O iii ] emission extending 7 north-east of the nucleus, in PA 30 (Mundell et al. 1995), meaning that this object may represent another case in which the radio structure and the [ O iii ] are physically misaligned. Previous reports on high excitation gas in this object are scarce.

The optical spectrum displays weak nuclear emission of [ Ne v ] , [ Fe vii ] , and , not detected in any other aperture. At the distance of NGC 3727, this implies that the CLR is restricted to the central 15 pc, making this source the AGN of the sample with the smallest CLR. This result, moreover, puts a tighter constraint on the size of the [ Fe vii ] gas and agrees with the values reported by Rodr iguez-Ardila et al. (2006).

sources.summary: The paper argues that NGC 3227 lacks broad [O III] emission because ionization, not high density, shapes its BLR, and models a low-metallicity inflow photoionized by the central source.

quotes.quote: However, for NGC 3227, an equally valid alternative explanation is that O ^ 2+ is progressively ionized to O ^ 3+ as the central UV--X-ray source is approached. Observational evidence for such an ionization gradient is provided by spectra obtained with the Space Telescope Imaging Spectrograph . Modeling the rich UV--visible spectrum of NGC 3227 with the photoionization code Cloudy supports the interpretation that the absence of broad [ O iii ] forbidden emission lines is due to ionization, rather than high gas density, and further suggests that what we perceive as the BLR in NGC 3227 is just the illuminated portion of a much larger inflow.

The observational evidence includes an ionization gradient defined by a rich spectrum of UV--visible emission lines attributable to H, He, C, N, O, Mg, and S. Additionally, a comparison of emission line profile shapes reveals that high velocity permitted C iv 1549 line emission coincides with diminished [ O iii ] 5007 forbidden line emission as would be expected if O ^ 2+ is progressively ionized to O ^ 3+ . These observational results constrain a model in which the central UV-X-ray source in NGC 3227 photoionizes a steady-state inflow of low metallicity gas.

sources.summary: The paper presents an XMM-Newton/NuSTAR campaign on Seyfert NGC 3227, isolating a rapid absorption event and interpreting it as a clumpy BLR-associated occultation.

quotes.quote: NGC 3227 exhibits rapid flux and spectral variability in the X-ray band. To understand this behaviour we conducted a co-ordinated observing campaign using 320 ks of XMM-Newton exposures together with 160 ks of overlapping NuSTAR observations, spanning a month. Here, we present a rapid variability event that occurs toward the end of the campaign.
