NGC 3227 is a Seyfert 1.5 galaxy at z=0.003859, that has shown high unabsorbed e.g. lamer03a and absorbed low X-ray states in previous X-ray observations rivers11a,markowitz09a,markowitz14a . A study using XMM , Suzaku and Swift data beuchert15a found an outflowing warm absorber that is complex, with signatures from three zones of ionized gas with transit by one inhomogeneous clump of gas linking part of the absorber complex to the location of the optical broad-line region.

The observation of such in NGC 3227, along with its wide range of observed spectral states motivated the 2016 XMM / NuSTAR campaign comprising 320 ks of XMM-Newton time overlapping 160 ks of NuSTAR observations.

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 3227 gas kinematics, noting its barred Seyfert 1.5 nucleus, interaction with NGC 3226, shock-enhanced line ratios, and northeast outflow signatures.

quotes.quote: firstpage - lastpage We have used the Gemini Near-Infrared Integral Field Spectrograph (NIFS) in the J and K bands to map the distribution, excitation and kinematics of the ionized H ii and warm molecular gas H _2 , in the inner few 100 pc of 6 nearby active galaxies: NGC 788, Mrk 607, NGC 3227, NGC 3516, NGC 5506, NGC 5899.

In the case of NGC 3227, NGC 3516 and NGC 5506 we also fitted a broad component to the Pa and Br emission line profiles in the central region, as these galaxies host type 1 AGN. This was done through a modification of the PROFIT routine to fit the broad component and subtract its contribution from the profiles in order to generate a data cube only with the narrow components. The steps for such task were: ( i ) fit of only one Gaussian to the broad component, by masking strong narrow emission lines; ( ii ) its subtraction from the spectra in which it is present, and ( iii ) fit of the narrow components. We achieved very satisfactory fits in all cases, with no constraints placed for the fit. The spatial region within which the fit of a broad component was necessary is shown by a cyan square in the third panel of the first row in Figs. ngc3227 , ngc3516 and ngc5506 , respectively for NGC 3227, NGC 3516 and NGC 5506. As the broad components are from the unresolved Broad Line Region (BLR), during the fit we kept fixed the central wavelength and width of the broad components to the value measured from the integrated spectrum within the square mentioned above and allowed only the variation of its amplitude. From the measurements presented in Figs. ngc3227 , ngc3516 and ngc5506 , we conclude that the subtraction of the broad components was satisfactory, as we found no traces of them in the subtracted data cube. From the fits of the line profiles and resulting parameters we have constructed maps of: flux distributions, V _ LOS , velocity dispersions, h_3 and h_4 moments as well as of the reddening E(B-V) and emission line ratio maps, presented in Fig. ngc788 for NGC 788, Fig. mrk607 for Mrk 607, Fig. ngc3227 for NGC 3227, Fig. ngc3516 for NGC 3516, Fig. ngc5506 for NGC 5506 and Fig. ngc5899 for NGC 5899. We chose to show the results only for [ Fe ii ] 1.2570 m, Pa 1.2822 m (or Br 2.1661 m in one case) and H _ 2 2.1218 m because these are the strongest emission lines allowing the mapping of the ionized gas properties via the [ Fe ii ] and Pa (or Br ) emission lines, and the warm molecular gas properties through the H _2 emission line above. Although the nuclear spectra of some galaxies show further coronal lines, as [ S ix ] 1.2525 m and [ Ca viii ] 2.3211 m, their flux distributions are unresolved or barely resolved and are not shown here, leaving a detailed discussion about these lines together with that of the gas excitation to be presented in Paper B. In the next subsections we discuss separately the results for each map, where we have masked out pixels with bad line fits, flagged according to the following criteria, applied to all maps: (1) relative uncertainties in the line fluxes larger than 30 ; (2) uncertainties in velocity and velocity dispersions larger than 50 km s ^ 1 . Flux Distributions Flux maps are shown in the first row of Figs. ngc788 - ngc5899 , where we have drawn the Position Angle (PA) of the major axis of the galaxy as given in the HYPERLEDA database hyper over the continuum images (leftmost panel) and H _2 flux and velocity maps. In all emission-line maps, the peak flux is observed at the location of the continuum peak, defined as the origin of the coordinates and identified with the galaxy nucleus. The flux distributions for the selected emission lines cover most of the FOV, corresponding to maximum distances from the nucleus varying from 100 pc for NGC 3227 to 410 pc for NGC 788. The direction of the largest extent of the [ Fe ii ] 1.25 m and Pa flux distributions follows the orientation of the ionization axis, and which in many cases approximately coincides with the orientation of the major axis of the galaxy in our sample. Exceptions are the cases of NGC 788 where they are oriented at a small angle relative to the major axis, and NGC 5506, where the largest extent of the [ Fe ii ] emission is perpendicular to the major axis. Although also showing the highest emission levels following the major axis orientation, the H _2 2.12 m flux maps usually show emission more spread over all directions, being less collimated than the ionized gas emission. The only exception is NGC 788, for which the strongest H _2 emission is more extended perpendicularly to the major axis of the galaxy. Line-ratio maps Here we describe briefly the main features observed in the line-ratio maps; a more in-depth discussion of the gas excitation will be presented in Paper B. The reddening and line-ratio maps are shown in the first column of Figs. ngc788 - ngc5899 , below the galaxy continuum image. The E(B-V) map was obtained from: E(B-V)=4.74 5.88 F_ Pa /F_ Br , where F_ Pa and F_ Br are the corresponding line fluxes and we have adopted in the derivation of the expression above the cardelli extinction law and F_ Pa /F_ Br theoretical ratio of 5.88 osterbrock . The E(B-V) values are mostly in the range 1--3, reaching the highest values at the nucleus of NGC 5506 ( E(B-V) 5 ) and NGC 5899 ( E(B-V) 3 ) while for the other galaxies the highest values are observed outwards. The [ Fe ii ] 1.2570 m/Pa ratio maps can be used to investigate the excitation mechanism of [ Fe ii ] e.g. ardila05,riffel08b,rogerio09,storchi2009 , with typical values for Seyfert galaxies ranging from 0.6 to 2. Most values are observed within this range, usually increasing from the centre outwards. In the case of NGC 3227 its value increases to 4 in a region with the shape of an off-centered ring surrounding the nucleus in which an enhanced velocity dispersion in the [ Fe ii ] emission lines is observed. Higher values than 2 are also observed in NGC 3516 and NGC 5899, also in association with regions of higher velocity dispersion than the surroundings, suggesting the contribution of shocks for the gas emission. The [ Fe ii ] 1.2570 m/ [ P ii ] 1.8861 m line ratio can also be an indicator of shocks storchi2009 , if its value becomes larger than 2. The corresponding maps are compact, due to the small extent of the [ P ii ] emission, with typical values of 5 at the nucleus and increasing to 10 at 0 5 , thus supporting the presence of shocks. In the cases of NGC 3227 and NGC 5899, the off-nuclear values reach values larger than 10, also in association with high [ Fe ii ] values, supporting even stronger contribution from shocks. The H _2 2.1218 m/Br , used to investigate the origin of the H _2 excitation, is usually in the range 0.6 to 2 for Seyfert galaxies ardila05,riffel08b,rogerio09,storchi2009 . Most measured values are indeed in this range, with a general behavior of showing the lowest ratios at the nucleus and increasing outwards, that we attribute to the destruction of the H _2 molecule by the strong radiation field close to the nucleus. The lowest values -- lower than 2 -- are observed for NGC 788 and NGC 5506, while the highest, reaching values in the range 8--10, are seen again in NGC 3227, NGC 3516 and NGC 5899, at similar locations as those where an enhancement is seen in [ Fe ii ] /Pa and [ Fe ii ] values, supporting again a contribution from shocks. Line-of-sight Velocity Fields The line-of-sight velocity (V _ LOS ) fields are shown in the second line of Figs. ngc788 - ngc5899 , after the subtraction of the systemic velocity obtained through the fit of a rotating disk model to the H _2 velocity field. Again, here we just describe the general features of these velocity fields, as a proper discussion is deferred to Paper B. The most common characteristic of the velocity fields is the rotation pattern, that is clearer in the H _2 velocity maps (presenting the typical ``spider diagram'' structure gal_dynamics-book ). The H _2 velocity amplitudes is similar to those also seen in the [ Fe ii ] and Pa velocity fields, except in the case of NGC 788, for which the H _2 rotation amplitude is smaller. The rotation pattern can also be seen in the [ Fe ii ] and Pa velocity fields, but in these cases it is usually disturbed -- more in the case of [ Fe ii ] than in the case of Pa - indicating the presence of additional kinematic components, usually associated with enhanced values. Velocity dispersion maps These maps are shown in the third line of Figs. ngc788 - ngc5899 . The [ Fe ii ] maps usually show the highest values, reaching up to 150--300 km s ^ 1 , while the H _2 maps show the lowest values, in the range 40--90 km s ^ 1 . The Pa maps are similar to those of [ Fe ii ] although reaching somewhat overall lower values, in the range 40 km s ^ 1 to 250 km s ^ 1 . An exception is the case of NGC 5506 that shows regions of enhanced to 350 km s ^ 1 ) for all emission lines, extended perpendicularly to the major axis for [ Fe ii ] and Pa and in patches parallel to the major axis in H _2 ; such patches are also seen in [ Fe ii ] . h_3 and h_4 Gauss-Hermite moments The h_3 and h_4 maps show values ranging from --0.3 to 0.3 for all emission lines. An inverse correlation between the h_3 map and the velocity fields is observed for all emission-lines: positive values of h_3 are seen at the locations where negative velocities are observed in the velocity fields, while negative values are seen where positive velocities are observed. This means that red wings are observed in blueshifted emission lines, while blue wings are observed in redshifted emission lines. We also observe in some cases (e.g. for the three emission lines in NGC 3227 and some of the lines in the other galaxies) an inverse correlation between the h_4 and the velocity dispersion maps. Positive values and highest values of h_4 are seen at the locations showing the lowest velocity dispersion values, while values zero or most negative ones are seen in the regions with the highest velocity dispersion. Total gas mass and surface mass density distributions The measurement of gas masses and surface mass densities within the inner few hundred pc of the host galaxies of AGN can be used to evaluate the gas reservoir available to trigger and maintain the nuclear activity, as well as the formation of new stars in the circumnuclear region. The presence of recent star formation in the circumnuclear region of active galaxies has been evidenced via the observation of low stellar velocity dispersion structures (Riffel et al. 2017) and their association with young stars e.g. storchi2012 . We can also use gas mass estimates in the vicinity of AGN to look for a possible correlation between the amount of available gas and the power of the AGN. We have calculated the ionized gas masses, in units of solar masses (M _ ) using the following expression e.g. scoville,riffel08,storchi2009,astor2014,astor2017 : M_ HII 5.1 10^ 16 F_ Pa erg s^ 1 cm^ 2 D Mpc ^2 [ M_ ] , where F_ Pa is the flux in the Pa line and D is the distance to the galaxy. This equation was obtained from scoville assuming an electron temperature of T=10^4 K, electron density of N_e=100 cm ^ 3 and case B recombination osterbrock , values applicable to the inner kiloparsec of active galaxies. The flux of Br used in the original equation was replaced by the stronger Pa one assuming the theoretical ratio F_ Pa / F_ Br =5.88 osterbrock . For NGC 5506, we did use the flux of Br emission line instead of Pa , as the signal to noise in the J-band spectrum is worse than in the K-band one, particularly in the extranuclear regions. The mass of the warm molecular gas (in M _ ) was obtained using scoville : M_ H_2 5.0776 10^ 13 F_ H_ 2 2.1218 erg s^ 1 cm^ 2 D Mpc ^2 [ M_ ] , where F_ H_ 2 2.1218 is the flux for the corresponding emission line. In the derivation of the above equation by scoville it was assumed that the vibrational temperature is T=2000 K, indicating a thermalized gas which is valid for n_ H_2 >10^ 4.5 cm ^ 3 . Previous studies by members of our group have found that the assumption of thermal equilibrium is indeed applicable for nearby AGNs and the derived temperatures are very close to the above value storchi2009,diniz2015 . Uncertainties in the derived mass values were obtained considering that the mass derived from both Eq. mhii and mh2 are directly proportional to the line fluxes. All the mass uncertainties are thus lower than 30 , as we have masked out regions with flux uncertainties larger than this value. The actual uncertainties may be somewhat higher due to further uncertainties in the absolute flux calibration. In addition, we cannot derive precise physical parameters such as gas density and temperature, thus, we have to rely on the above assumptions even though they are just ``educated estimates" based on our previous similar studies. We show the surface mass density distributions of ionized and warm molecular gas -- _ HII and _ H_2 , respectively -- in the first two columns of panels of Fig. mass , and the corresponding azymuthally averaged profiles in the third column, in units of solar masses per parsec square. The surface mass densities were obtained by calculating the gas masses in each spaxel using the equations above and dividing them by the surface area of each spaxel, taking into account projection effects via the use of the disk inclinations quoted in riffel2018 . The resulting ionized gas surface mass density distributions are in some cases more compact when compared with that of the warm molecular gas, that seems to extend farther and more uniformly from the nucleus than the ionized gas. The azimuthaly-averaged profiles of _ HII and _ H_2 , shown in the last column of Fig. mass , reveal that typical ratios between the two surface mass densities within the inner 100 pc are of the order of 10 ^3 and that gradients are steeper for the ionized than for the molecular gas. In Table tabmass we show the integrated gas mass values (for the whole FOV) as well as the average surface mass densities (in units of M _ pc ^ 2 ) obtained as the ratio between the integrated masses and the area over which they are distributed, listed also in the Table. We also include in Table tabmass an estimate of the cold molecular gas mass. A number of studies have derived the ratio between the cold and warm H _2 gas masses by comparing the masses obtained using the cold CO molecular lines observed in millimetric wavelenghts with that of the warm H _2 observed in the near-IR. dale05 obtained ratios in the range 10^5 -10 ^7 , while ms2006 , using a larger sample of 16 luminous and ultra-luminous infrared galaxies, derived a ratio M _ cold /M _ warm = 1 - 5 10^6 . More recently, ma2013 compiled from the literature values of M _ cold derived from CO observations and H _2 (2.12 m) luminosities for a larger number of galaxies, covering a wider range of luminosities, morphological and nuclear activity types. From those data, they propose that an estimate of the cold H _2 gas mass can be obtained from the flux of the warm H _2 2.1218 line as: M_ H_2 cold 1174 L_ H_2 2.1218 L_ , where L_ H_2 2.1218 is the luminosity of the H _2 (2.12 m) line. The error associated to the factor = 1174 is 35 , according to ma2013 . Table tabmass shows that the integrated mass of warm H _2 gas (within typically 100 -- 400 pc from the nucleus) ranges from 106 M _ for Mrk 607 to 820 M _ for NGC 5506, while the estimated mass of cold molecular gas ranges from 8 10 ^7 M _ for Mrk 607 to 6 10 ^ 8 M _ for NGC 5506. The masses of ionized gas range from 1.8 10 ^5 M _ for NGC 3516 to 1.9 10 ^7 M _ for NGC 5506. We point out again that the uncertainties in the calculated masses, presented in Tab. tabmass , were obtained only by propagating the line flux uncertainties and are thus lower limits, considering that there are additional uncertainties in adopted physical properties -- e.g. on the M_ H_2 cold /M_ H_2 ratio, H _2 vibrational temperatures and electron densities. In this section we discuss individually each galaxy presenting a brief review of previous results from the literature, trying to relate them with ours . We also discuss and analyze the masses of ionized and molecular gas, as well as their surface mass density distributions and the global properties of the gas kinematics, leaving the kinematic modelling and the analysis of the gas kinematics and excitation to Paper B . NGC 788 NGC 788 (Fig. ngc788 ) was identified as a Seyfert galaxy by huchra1982 , and has been observed in the optical hamuy1987,wagner1988,kay1994,cruz1994 , radio ulvestad1989 , and millimetre heckman1989 wavebands. This galaxy is an early-type spiral with faint arms visible up to 30 from the nucleus evans1996 , most conspicuous to the north-west of the nucleus with a string of bright compact H ii regions, while a complex of fainter H ii regions is associated with a southern arm evans1996 . The kinematics major axis PA 125 ^ was determined from the V_ LOS fields (Fig. ngc788 ), with a value close to that obtained by riffel2017 for the stellar kinematics of 130 ^ . The velocity amplitude observed for [ Fe ii ] and Pa , of 150 km s ^ 1 is much higher than that for H _2 , of only 50 km s ^ 1 . These higher velocities are associated with a more collimated emission and higher velocity dispersions suggesting the presence of a bipolar outflow along the east-west direction, as suggested from the morphology of the gas emission in the [ Fe ii ] flux map. The E(B-V) map from Fig. ngc788 has the highest values to southwest of the nucleus, which coincide with a dust lane seen in the HST - F606W optical continuum image from . Mrk 607 Mrk 607 (Fig. mrk607 ) is an Sa galaxy hosting a Seyfert 2 nucleus. A continuum image from ferruit2000 shows a high ellipticity of 0.60, yielding an inclination i 67 ^ ), with major axis PA of 43^ , which agrees with our estimate of the kinematic major axis orientation of 38^ from the H _2 velocity field (Paper B). Our data reveal that the three velocity fields -- [ Fe ii ] , Pa and H _2 - show what seems to be a rotation pattern, very steep in the center in H _2 and less steep in the first two lines. Spiral dust lanes are clearly visible in the HST F547 continuum image of the inner 8 ^ (1.4 kpc) ferruit2000 . The high inclination of the galaxy may explain the large velocity dispersions observed. In the central 5 ^ (875 pc) it presents weak radio emission extended along PA 180 ^ colbert1996,nagar1999 which is not aligned with the axis of the most extended gas emission, which is observed along the galaxy major axis. The E(B-V) map from Fig. mrk607 has high values ( 5) to southeast/west of the nucleus, which coincide with a dust lane seen in the HST - F606W optical continuum image from . NGC 3227 NGC 3227 (Fig. ngc3227 ) is a well studied barred galaxy, with a Seyfert 1.5 nucleus ho1997 and in interaction with the dwarf elliptical galaxy NGC 3226 mundell2004 .

Regarding the presence of outflows, we have found signatures of them in most galaxies of our sample, most clearly observed in the [ Fe ii ] kinematics: (1) in NGC 788, along the east-west direction; (2) in NGC 3227, towards the northeast, as suggested by distortions in the velocity field and patterns in h_3 and h_4 ; (3) in NGC 3516, from the south-east to the north-west, as suggested by the disturbed velocity field and enhanced velocity dispersion; (4) in NGC 5506, along north-south, as suggested by the enhanced velocity dispersions; (5) and in NGC 5899, towards north and south, as indicated by the opposite velocity field between the ionized and molecular gas.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes seven NuSTAR observations of radio-quiet Seyfert 1.5 NGC 3227, finding variable X-ray emission, complex absorbers, and a positive Ecut-photon-index correlation.

quotes.quote: In Tab. tab:details we list the seven archival observations of NGC 3227. NGC 3227 shows highly variable X-ray emission and its absorption feature have been extensively investigated in literature. As an example, based on the and XMM-Newton observations, Turner_2018 found a rapid occultation event in NGC 3227 between two exposures of (60202002010 and 60202002012), with several absorber zones involved. However, as further shown in sec:fitting , our study on the high-energy cutoff in this work is barely influenced by these complex absorbers.
Obs. ID 60202002014 (No. 7) of NGC 3227 clearly deviate from other exposures in Fig. fig:all_HR .
A clear positive correlation between E_ cut and is seen in NGC 3227.

sources.summary: The paper finds bright polar mid-IR emission in NGC 3227, perpendicular to the ALMA torus, aligned with the optical cone, and accompanied by molecular outflow evidence.

quotes.quote: The PSF-subtracted images of six out of the twelve galaxies present bright extended mid-IR emission (Fig. fig:midIRimages ). These are NGC 1365, NGC 3227, NGC 5506, NGC 5643, NGC 6300, and NGC 7582. The contours show that the extended emission is detected up to at least a 20 _ bk level. For NGC 4388 we also detected extended mid-IR emission at a lower significance but in a similar orientation as that derived by using a different dataset. The sizes of the extended mid-IR emission range from approximately 50 pc in NGC 6300 to 190 pc in NGC 5506.

In NGC 1365, NGC 3227, and NGC 7582 as well as the inner regions of NGC 5506, the extended mid-IR emission appears to be approximately perpendicular (in projection) to the extended ALMA 870 m continuum emission. The latter for these four galaxies is identified as the torus. In NGC 5643, the orientation of the extended mid-IR emission is mostly along PA_ MIR = 60-70 ^ , which is nearly perpendicular (in projection) to the nearly north-south ALMA 870 m continuum emission. Finally, in NGC 6300 the extended mid- and far-IR emission show similar orientations, and thus the extended mid-IR emission is along the equatorial direction of the torus.

We find that the GATOS Seyferts with extended mid-IR emission in the (projected) polar direction (NGC 1365, NGC 3227, NGC 4388, and NGC 7582) as well as NGC 1068 and Circinus are located close to the region where polar dusty outflows are more likely to be launched. Among these galaxies, NGC 3227 and NGC 1068 present evidence of molecular outflows in CO transitions on the torus scales 20-40 pc, see AlonsoHerrero2019, GarciaBurillo2019 . The ALMA observations of Circinus show, on the other hand, a nuclear outflow with a modest velocity Izumi2018 .

sources.summary: The paper treats 3227 as a complex AGN whose observations show borderline global column densities, variable fitted columns, and disturbed interacting gas-rich surroundings.

quotes.quote: 3227 All obsIDs of this AGN have \ values that are in the \ regime. \ values for observations 703022040 and 703022060 are borderline \ within their upper limit 90 \ uncertainties. Although 10^ 24 \ is commonly used as the threshold for ness, strictly this is 1.24 24 , and even this is not without ambiguities See the \ manual ( http://mytorus.com/mytorus-manual- http://mytorus.com/mytorus-manual- ), Section 2.1, for a detailed discussion. .

There is considerable evidence across wavebands showing that 3227 is located in an environment that can affect the large scale distribution of neutral gas. It is in an overdense group environment garcia1993,crook2007 and is strongly interacting both with 3226 and a nearby gas-rich dwarf / \ cloud mundell1995,mundell2004,davies2014 , with evidence of large-scale neutral hydrogen streaming motions over several to hundreds of parsecs. It is reported to have a large-scale bar mulchaey1997,alonso-herrero2019 .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes multiple NuSTAR epochs of NGC 3227 and finds coronal temperature variation, no flux correlation with several spectral parameters, and an anticorrelation between optical depth and temperature.

quotes.quote: In this work we aimed to investigate the variation in kT_ e in three AGN, namely NGC 3227, NGC 5548 and MR 2251 178 using more than one epoch of data on a source from NuSTAR . We carried out spectral analysis of multiple epochs of data acquired using NuSTAR on the three sources including a few new observations not published so far. By fitting Comptonization model to the data we determined the temperature of the corona and also investigated changes in kT_ e if any in these sources.

In NGC 3227, we found evidence for variation in kT_ e . We found no correlation of kT_ e , photon index ( ), reflection fraction ( R ) and optical depth ( ) with flux, while, is found to anti-correlate with kT_ e . These could be due to more than one physical process at work in the source that causes the change in kT_ e .

sources.summary: The paper detects 3.3 micron PAH emission in NGC 3227, constraining a circumnuclear starburst to the inner 60 pc and linking it to a molecular disk.

quotes.quote: The 3.3 m PAH feature is clearly present in the QSO Mrk 478 and in the two Seyfert 1 galaxies Mrk 766 and NGC 3227. For Mrk 478 and Mrk 766 it is the first time that the 3.3 m PAH emission is detected. The peak of the PAH feature as determined from the Gaussian fit was 3.29 m for Mrk 478 and 3.30 m for NGC 3227 and Mrk 766, in agreement with the values reported in the literature for starburst and Seyfert 2 galaxies ( mi ; ID00; IM02). The FWHM of the 3.3 m PAH emission are rather similar: 550 , 460 \ and 450 \ for Mrk 478, Mrk 766 and NGC 3227, respectively.

Our 3.3 m PAH detection in NGC 3227 is in total agreement with the above results and confirms the presence of a circumnuclear starburst in this galaxy. Moreover, the size of the emitting PAH region, constrained to the central 60 pc, is coincident with the size of the CO molecular disk, suggesting a close connection between these two features.

sources.summary: NGC 3227 is treated as a Sy1.5 nucleus whose SED is reproduced by clumpy models including extinguished direct AGN emission, with broad parameter distributions.

quotes.quote: NGC 3227. This active nucleus is usually classified as a Sy1.5. XMM-Newton observations show short term hard X-ray variability likely related to variation in the intrinsic continuum emission Gondoin03 . The clumpy models reproduce the SED well, including the direct though extinguished AGN emission (using the Calzetti00 law), as in the case of NGC 4151. The resulting histograms show broad Gaussian distributions of the , N_0 , and _ V parameters, with median values of 33 _ 12 ^ 20 , 6 _ 4 ^ 5 , and 116 _ 55 ^ 56 , respectively.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3227 as an adaptive-optics science case, describing stellar-kinematic black-hole modeling and noting its barred-galaxy status and low placement on the black-hole relation.

quotes.quote: NGC 3227 was the first Seyfert 1 in which Schwarzschild orbit superposition modelling was applied to the stellar kinematics . The result (see Fig. fig:science1 ) was a 1 range for the BH mass of (0.7--2.0) 10 ^7 M _ . This is a robust result, although the range is rather large because of the limited signal-to-noise and also from degeneracies between the mass of the various components in the model. It is a little lower than, although just consistent with, masses derived from other methods based on reverberation mapping and X-ray variability; and suggests that NGC 3227 may lie a little below the M_ BH ^* relation. This is intriguing because NGC 3227 is a barred galaxy, and there is some evidence that barred galaxies as a class may preferentially lie below the relation, perhaps hinting that the relations between black holes and their host galaxies are more complex than first thought.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3227 as a nearby Seyfert with a truncated coronal-line spectrum, weak high-ionization detections, and a hard continuum deficient in high-energy EUV photons.

quotes.quote: Examples of this among the nearest objects in the sample are Mrk 1066, NGC 5728 - both lacking lines with IP > 166 eV or NGC 3227 and NGC 7682 - both lacking lines of IP > 280eV (see Table tab70 ). A similar argument applies to some of the objects studied by m10 on HST/STIS spectra: some of their objects, including NGC 3227, display [ Ne v ] and [ Fe vii ] , both with IP 100 eV, but not higher optical ionization lines. The results for NGC 3227 are further supported by b50 , who report on the non detection of [ Fe xi ] 7892 \ ( IP=260 eV).

NGC 3227 has a harder spectrum (see both figures), meaning that it has a deficiency in high energy EUV photons when compared to sources with above 2. It is interesting to note that its CL spectrum has also a clear threshold in IPs, as CLs with IP > 166 eV are barely detected (see Fig. fig-lay2 and Sect 3).

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3227 as the absolute calibration anchor for the AGN luminosity-distance method, citing its SBF-based distance and calibration uncertainty.

quotes.quote: Currently only NGC 3227 and NGC 4051 have direct distance estimates. The Tully-Fisher distance to NGC 4051 is the less accurate of the two. We therefore calibrated / F to the luminosity distance to the galaxy NGC 3227 based on a distance modulus of m-M=31.86 0.24 determined using the surface brightness fluctuations (SBF) method to its companion galaxy NGC 3226 2001ApJ...546..681T . Due to the occurrence of a supernova, 2002bo, in another member of the Leo III group to which NGC 3227 belongs, the SBF distance quoted above has been examined in detail and seems likely to be correct within the quoted uncertainty 2004AJ....128.3034K . However, the uncertainty in this calibration is relatively large, and we use it here only to determine an initial estimate of the luminosity distances.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3227 as a low-luminosity reverberation-mapped AGN whose near-IR continuum is dominated by host-galaxy emission but still follows the near-IR black-hole scaling relations.

quotes.quote: Four AGN in our sample, namely, Mrk 590, NGC 3227, NGC 3516 and NGC 4051, were found to be in a very low luminosity state with a continuum SED dominated by host galaxy emission (see Fig. SEDgal , top panels, and L11a , their Fig. 6). Therefore, in these cases the observed 1 m continuum luminosity represents only an upper limit to the AGN value. As Fig. lag shows, these sources (shown as green filled circles) populate indeed the low end of the near-IR luminosity distribution of our sample. However, they follow not only the near-IR R - L relationship remarkably well, but also the so-called black hole mass-luminosity ( M L ) relationship Kaspi00, Pet04 , which we plot in Fig. masslum for the near-IR. Therefore, the AGN 1 m continuum luminosity of these sources cannot be significantly (more than a factor of 2-3 ) lower than that of the host galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3227 as a resolved Seyfert comparison object, estimating its ionizing luminosity, averaging its measurements by radius, and noting a flatter observed ratio trend than expected.

quotes.quote: Resolved observations of the n_ f vs. relation We compare eq. eq: n vs with \ observations of two Seyferts in the literature, NGC 4151 which was observed by , and NGC 3227 which was observed by . We find L_ i,43 =15 and L_ i,43 =0.78 for NGC 4151 and NGC 3227, respectively, where =10^ 43 L_ i,43 .

In NGC 3227, we average over all position angles in bins of 0.1 dex in . We note that the central measurement in NGC 3227 is somewhat uncertain due to spectral decomposition issues, and due to geometric rectification issues during the data reduction (J. Walsh, private communication).
In NGC 3227, the observed ratio vs. relation is somewhat flatter than expected from RPC, and in NGC 4151, the observed ratios are typically higher than expected.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3227 as a Seyfert 1.5 galaxy, compares prior neutral and warm absorption measurements, and finds a similar neutral column plus three warm absorber components.

quotes.quote: NGC 3227- This is a Seyfert 1.5 galaxy and have been studied by . The = 1.57 and the intrinsic neutral absorption column is = 3 10^ 21 . There are two WA components. =1.2 , =1 10^ 21 , and =2.9 , =2 10^ 21 . We also detect a similar neutral absorption column but we find three discrete WA components.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 3227 consistent with independent soft-excess and power-law components, while noting alternative multiple-power-law models could reproduce its principal components.

quotes.quote: NGC 3227 A recent analysis of the variability in the Seyfert 1.5 NGC 3227 by Arevalo14 showed the spectral variations could be described by a two component soft excess plus power law model, where both components vary independently. This is consistent with the separate soft and hard components returned by our analysis, although the signal is weak (particularly in the second component). However, this object does appear to be particularly unusual (or the state in which it was observed was unusual), so these conclusions may not be universally applicable. An alternative model was proposed by Noda14 , where the variability arises from multiple power law components, one hard and one soft, with a constant distant reflection component. It is possible that such an arrangement of power laws could reproduce the observed PCs, and there are some similarities between the 2nd PC found here and that simulated for the two power law model in Fig. sims_twopls .

sources.summary: The paper records NGC 3227's adopted photometric profile or decomposition, giving bulge/scale parameters and comparing those choices with alternative literature fits for the structural analysis.

quotes.quote: NGC 3227: The bulge component is determined in Davies2006 from a high-resolution SINFONI image, giving R_e=3.1'' . The 'classical fit' of Laesker2014 delivers R_e=1.4'' , the 'best fit' R_e=0.5'' , both results are probably affected by insufficient resolution (FWHM= 0.9'' ). Sani2011 quote R_e=58'' , which is definitely too large. The K- band M/L scaled to the distance of KormendyHo2013 is given in the unusual units quoted by Davies2006 . We mark the 'peculiarity' of this definition by referring to the band as 'Kp' in Table tab_ml and by not plotting the galaxy in Fig. fig_ML .

sources.summary: The appendix describes NGC 3227 as an interacting barred spiral with a Sy1.5 nucleus, circumnuclear starburst, nearby star-forming regions, and MIR emission dominated by star formation.

quotes.quote: NGC 3227 is a low-inclination barred spiral galaxy. This source is in interaction with NGC 3226. The nucleus is classified as Sy1.5 and it is surrounded by circumnuclear starburst Veron10 . Rodriguez03 and Davies06 found star-forming regions at 70 pc from the nucleus. Asmus14 also found that the MIR emission is dominated by star-forming regions (at arcsecond-scale).

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3227 as a suspected buckling-bar case whose trapezoidal isophotes and outer-bar spurs may instead be distorted by interaction with NGC 3226.

quotes.quote: First, three galaxies of intermediate morphological types with trapezoidal inner isophotes (corresponding to the main buckling region) are suspected of having the ongoing buckling Erwin_Debattista2017,Li_etal2017 . It is worth noting, however, that one of the galaxies NGC 3227 is interacting with the dwarf elliptical galaxy NGC 3226. Moreover, both galaxies are embedded in a common cocoon of stars and warm gas, which is supposed to be the result of merging of the third galaxy Appleton_etal2014 . Thus, the shape of the isophotes of NGC 3227 can be greatly distorted due to this interaction. And it is not surprising that one of the two outer-bar spurs of NGC 3227 at the long side of the trapezoid is pointed out exactly to the satellite NGC 3226, which may create a false impression of the position of the spurs.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 3227 has a strongly asymmetric H I profile, possibly from interaction with NGC 3226 but not contaminated by its emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 3227: The blueshifted side of the profile of NGC 3227 is significantly weaker in flux than the redshifted side, resulting in a dramatically asymmetric shape (see Fig. \ batmen ). It is interacting with its neighboring galaxy NGC 3226 tonry2001 , which is an elliptical galaxy, and in the resolved HI study by , they detected no HI emission from it. So while the interaction might be a possible explanation as to NGC 3227's skewed profile shape, NGC 3226 most likely does not contribute to the emission profile we have detected. The spectral resolution of the previously published spectra range from 6.6 km s ^ 1 dr1978 from observations which used the 91 m telescope at the NRAO to 30 km s ^ 1 bf1979 for observations with the 305 m Arecibo telescope. The V _ R measurement of 1284 9 km s ^ 1 reported by presents the biggest discrepancy with our V _ R measurement of 1144.74 ^ +4.33 _ 0.82 km s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper reverberation-maps NGC 3227, measuring H-beta lags and a velocity-delay map whose blueshifted-gas delays indicate a broad-line disk with some inflow.

quotes.quote: We report the results of reverberation mapping three bright Seyfert galaxies, Mrk 79, NGC 3227, and Mrk 841, from a campaign conducted from December 2016 to May 2017 with the Wyoming Infrared Observatory (WIRO) 2.3-meter telescope. All three of these targets have shown asymmetric broad H emission lines in the past, although their emission lines were relatively symmetric during our observations. We measured \ time lags for all three targets and estimated masses of their black holes -- for the first time in the case of Mrk 841.
NGC 3227 shows the largest time lags for blueshifted gas and the two-dimensional velocity-delay map suggests a disk with some inflow.
During 2007, NGC 3227 possessed an \ profile with a strong red asymmetry and a double-peaked profile in its rms spectrum denney2009 .

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 3227 has an off-center intermediate-age stellar ring matching gas and emission structures, widespread old stars, nuclear hot dust, and sub-2 Gyr populations.

quotes.quote: NGC 3227: Very small fractions of the two younger SPC are detected. An off-centred (x=60 pc,y=-20 pc) ring-like structure of intermediate young ( xiy ) stars can be seen as well as a significant fraction of xio SPC is detected over the entire FoV. This structure has a very good agreement with the off-centre, ring-like structure found in CO(2-1) Atacama Large Millimeter/submillimeter Array (ALMA) observations by Alonso-Herrero+19,Alonso-Herrero+20 , as well as with a low velocity dispersion stellar ring Barbosa+09 and in Pa_ emission Bianchin+22 , indicating that this gas reservoir is forming stars. We note that a vertical stripe is seen to the W side of the FoV and that this feature should be treated with caution. The old SPC is also detected over the entire FoV contributing 25 percent, except for the unresolved nucleus ( 15 pc), which is dominated by hot dust emission (75 percent) together with 10 percent of FC emission.

sources.summary: The paper summarizes NGC 3227's central mm-wave continuum as two components, one aligned with an ionization cone and radio jet.

quotes.quote: 43: NGC 3227 Mm-wave continuum emission in the central 1 1 \ region of the object was discussed in detail by , who used the same ALMA data as we used. Here, we give a brief summary of their discussion, and those who are interested in more details should check and references therein. The authors suggested that there exist two mm-wave components with different PAs of 35 \ and 20 , indicated in Figure fig:img4notes . The centrally concentrated component with a PA 35 \ may be relevant to an ionization cone and a radio jet. By HST observations of [ N ii ] and H , an ionization cone was mapped at a similar PA.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 3227 as NGC 3226's Seyfert companion, with a saturated unresolved nucleus, dusty spiral arm, compact star-forming clumps, and a northeast ionized-gas cone.

quotes.quote: The core as well as the northern spiral arm of NGC 3227 are also located on our broad- and narrow-band ACS frames. The unresolved Seyfert nucleus is saturated in our I band frame. The spiral arm exhibits a complicated and chaotic network of dust filaments, in accord with the WFPC2 structure map of Pogge Martini (2002). The redshifted H + [ N II ] lines of NGC 3227 fall within the wavelength range of the F658N filter used to image NGC 3226. The derived ionization map shows a large number of luminous and compact clumps imbedded in diffuse regions in the spiral arm, likely the sites of active star formation.

sources.summary: The review discusses apparent outflow signatures in NGC 3227, but treats them as provisional because the campaign was short and its mass estimate remains normal.

quotes.quote: I think that reverberation mapping and spectropolarimetry (see, for example, axon+08 ) are going to provide the best answers. kollatschny03 found marginal evidence for some outflow in Mrk 110, and denney+09b have found a clearer signature of an apparent outflow component in velocity-resolved reverberation mapping of NGC 3227. Since the denney+09b results are from a single short observing campaign, I do not think that NGC 3227 presents a major problem yet for the general picture presented here.

The red-wing/blue-wing lag varies from year to year by more than the formal errors welsh+07 , but the NGC 5548 BLR is probably not changing direction at the end of every observing seasonA strong reason for believing that the NGC 3227 kinematics are not unusual is that, as denney+09b point out, the mass estimate lies on the M_ - _* relationship. If follow-up observing campaigns confirm the signature of outflow in NGC 3227 then this would be a significant challenge to the model favored here.

sources.summary: The paper reports EVN observations of NGC 3227, finding transatlantic-baseline phase coherence and a compact 5 GHz radio source at least as small as 0.19 by 0.09 pc.

quotes.quote: At a first look, based on pipeline calibrated data, three sources are clearly detected in at least one of the two bands, and namely NGC 3982, NGC 3227, NGC 4138. We now show in Fig. f.vplot the visibility amplitudes and phases on baselines to Arecibo for one of the newly observed sources (NGC 3227). Phase coherence is clearly present in these transatlantic baselines, which proves that the target is truely compact. For instance, in the case of NGC 3227 at 5 GHz, a preliminary visibility model fit indicates that the source is at least as compact as 0.19 0.09 pc.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3227 as one of five poorly fit Seyfert/BLRG spectra with high signal-to-noise Suzaku data and strong O 7/O 8 absorption requiring more detailed warm-absorber modeling.

quotes.quote: The majority of sources have spectra that are well-fit with this model. Five sources, however, are not well-fit, with reduced ^2 > 2.0 . These include NGC 3227, NGC 3516, NGC 4051, NGC 4151, and MR 2251-178. Each of these sources have high signal-to-noise Suzaku spectra with exposures from 79--275 ks in length and have strong detections of O 7 and O 8 absorption. The complexity in the soft X-ray emission is not fully accounted for with the simple addition of the absorption edges. This is clear in the bottom panels of Figure fig-basemodel , where the ratio of data/model for three of these sources clearly shows structure that is not accounted for with our model.

sources.summary: NGC 3227 has robust Fe K absorption: two-line absorption unlikely to be edge contamination, multiple high-significance detections, and transient lines varying on roughly two-week timescales.

quotes.quote: Of the sources included in the sample CBS 126, Mrk 766 (OBSIDs 701035010, 701035020), NGC 3227 (OBSIDs 703022010, 703022020, 703022030), NGC 4051 (OBSIDs 700004010, 703023010) and NGC 4151 all have at least one significantly detected absorption line at a rest-frame energy which is consistent at the 90 level with the neutral Fe K-shell edge from the reflection component. The absorption in Mrk 766, NGC 3227, and NGC 4051 is manifested by two lines with a common velocity shift equal to that expected for and and are therefore unlikely to be affected by the presence of an edge.

Even so, given that in the case of NGC 3227 the Fe K absorption lines are detected at P_ MC >99.9 significance in 3 separate observations, and in MCG 6-30-15 the and absorption lines present are both statistically resolved at P_ MC >99.9 confidence, the line detections alone still imply very robust absorber detections in both of these sources.
In NGC 3227 transient Fe K absorption lines are detected in 3 of the 6 epochs, with the line parameters varying on a t 2 week time-scale.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3227 as an example of strong X-ray spectral variability, linking changing warm absorption and ionization states to varying irradiation.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:ngc3227 shows an example of strong spectral variability in NGC 3227 with particularly changing warm absorption properties. Warm or (partially) gas still has a rich number of high- Z elements able to absorb soft X-ray photons. In addition to column density variations of such ionized gas, the ionization states are also changing between the XMM-Newton observations. These changes are interpreted to be due to the varying irradiation expressed by a varying power-law norm. As the ionization parameter = L/n_ e ^ 2 is proportional to the luminosity, it should behave directly proportional to it. This seems indeed to be the case for NGC 3227.

sources.summary: The paper gives K-band decomposition notes for NGC3227, describing its morphology, fitted components, residual structures, and why the adopted model is preferred over simpler alternatives.

quotes.quote: NGC3227 (Fig. fig:NGC3227 ) poses a challenge to decomposition not only due to the presence of a two-armed spiral structure, but also because of a close neighbour, elliptical galaxy NGC3226, with which it probably interacts. The proximity, size and brightness of NGC3226 prevents masking, thus requiring its inclusion in the model (as a single- \ profile), unfortunately causing a degeneracy with NGC3227's outer disk. The disk profile consists of two parts, having approximately the same scale radius but different surface brightness.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 3227 as an interacting Seyfert 1.5, adjusts contaminated infrared fluxes, and uses its well-sampled SED shape in the torus-parameter tests.

quotes.quote: Thus, we selected three Sy1 and three Sy2 galaxies from Alonso11 and Gonzalez13 hosted in nearly face-on galaxies (inclination 45 ) without prominent nuclear dust lanes and which are not part of merging systems The only exception is the Sy1.5 NGC 3227, which is interacting with its companion galaxy NGC 3226 Mundell04 . For the galaxies NGC 1068, NGC 3081, NGC 3227 and NGC 4151, the data are the same reported in Alonso11 and Ramos11 , and for NGC 1365, in Alonso12 , with the following exceptions: In the case of NGC 3227, we discarded the 8.99 VISIR/VLT flux from Honig10 and the 11.2 MICHELLE/Gemini North flux from Ramos09 because they appear contaminated by [ Ar III ] and PAH 11.3 emission, respectively. The MICHELLE spectra are from Mason06 and Alonso11 and the VISIR spectrum of NGC 3227 is from Honig10 . In particular, we have chosen NGC 3227 and NGC 4151, whose SED shapes are different between them and well-sampled (see Figure sy1sy2 ).

sources.summary: The paper fits ten Swift spectra of NGC 3227 with a power law plus ionized absorber and finds photon index positively correlated with intrinsic luminosity.

quotes.quote: NGC 3227 Sufficient Swift data of NGC 3227 was available to produce 10 flux-binned spectra. The best-fitting model to the Swift data consisted of a power law and an ionised absorber, with free to vary but all parameters of the absorber tied ( ^2_R = 1.07 ), which is statistically superior to a neutral absorber ( f = 16.3 and p=1.1 10^ 7 ). Previous spectral modelling of NGC 3227 has also shown its spectrum to be absorbed by an ionised absorber George1998,Ptak1994 , though some more recent studies have used more complex models involving multiple ionised absorbers and a reflection component e.g. Beuchert2015 . The parameters of our best-fitting model show a positive correlation between the photon index and the intrinsic luminosity of the source, consistent with Sobolewska2009 , who also found the same correlation and range of photon indices ( = 1.43-1.65 ) in the 2-10 keV band.

sources.summary: The paper fits NGC 3227's RMS H profile with a disk model, finding a double-peaked component plus an additional broader red-wing component likely closer to the SMBH.

quotes.quote: We show in Fig. n3227 the result of our attempt to fit the RMS H profile of NGC 3227 using our disk model and data from Denney10 : although the profiles are not always double-peaked, the RMS spectrum is. The parameters of the fit are listed in Table n1097 . Fig. n3227 shows that the model does fit the double-peaked component although there is, in addition, an even broader component, revealed by the extended red wing that could not be fitted by our model. This component probably originates at gas at higher velocities closer to the SMBH and/or in inflow or outflow, for example.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3227 to compare nuclear star-cluster and AGN luminosities, estimating stars contribute about 20 percent of the central radiation field.

quotes.quote: NGC 3227. davies2006 report a total luminosity of the NSC in NGC 3227 of L_ NSC ( NGC 3227 ) 1 10^ 43  erg s ^ 1 and measured in a 0.8 arcsec aperture. This corresponds to an intrinsic aperture radius of 3300 r_ sub . In addition, they estimate the AGN luminosity as L_ AGN ( NGC 3227 ) 4 10^ 43  erg s ^ 1 . Thus, we estimate that the stars contribute about 20 per cent to the total radiation field within 3300 r_ sub of the nucleus of NGC 3227.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3227 as a high signal-to-noise Seyfert 2 example whose coronal-parameter contours are smooth, weakly covariant, and robustly fitted.

quotes.quote: In Figure fig:NGC3227_conts we plot the contours for the Seyfert 2 NGC 3227. For this object, the contours are well-behaved, appearing as ellipses with relatively smooth levels. There is only slight covariance between the sets of parameters. Additionally, the sizes of the error bars on this source are small, indicating a robust and good fit for this high S/N observation.

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 3227's central ionized gas, describing its strong-emission region, velocity field, and signs of nuclear outflow, rotation, density structure, or inflow.

quotes.quote: The SER in this Sy 1 galaxy is very compact, but at fainter levels of gas emission is most extended towards the NE, making an angle of 54 ^ with the PMA. The highest blueshifts -- seeming to extend beyond our FoV are also observed in this direction. increases to values of the order of 1000 km s ^ 1 towards the NE (P.A. 27^ ), where blueshifts are observed and can be attributed to an outflow , which is also supported by [ Fe ii ] emission in the near-IR schoenell2019 . The velocity fields are distorted, dominated by blueshifts in the case of [ O iii ] and showing also a rotation component in the [ N ii ] kinematics, with a KMA approximately along the PMA.

sources.summary: NGC 3227 is treated as an AGN with heavy intrinsic far-UV reddening; the paper excludes it from photoionization-model comparison and flags its Balmer-jump diffuse-continuum estimate as affected by host extinction.

quotes.quote: The far-UV spectrum of NGC 3227 (both continuum at 1215 \ and Ly ) is heavily reddened by small dust grains intrinsic to the AGN Crenshaw01,Mehdipour21 , especially at wavelengths below 2000 , and we thus exclude it in our comparison with photoionization models.
Aside from the anomalous case of NGC 3227 which is impacted by heavy extinction, the ratios are L_ DC, 5200 /L_ 1215 0.1 0.6, L_ DC, 5200 /L_ Ly 1 5, and L_ H /L_ DC, 5200 0.1 0.3, respectively.

For the case of NGC 3227, the extrapolated DC flux just below the Balmer jump is essentially equal to the total observed continuum flux. However, NGC 3227 has substantial internal extinction within the AGN host that has not been corrected in the STIS data, and this extinction suppresses the observed flux at blue wavelengths.

sources.summary: NGC 3227 shows three occultations; its eclipsing clouds have BLR-like radii, with estimates dependent on the adopted black-hole mass.

quotes.quote: We detect three occultations in NGC 3227 and one occultation in NGC 3783, NGC 7314, and NGC 3516, together with the well-known occultations in Mrk 766.

The eclipsing clouds in Mrk 766, NGC 3227, NGC 7314, and NGC 3516 with distances from the X-ray source of (0.3-3.6) 10 ^4 R_g are moving at Keplerian velocities > 1122 km s ^ 1 , typical parameters of broad-line region clouds, while the eclipsing cloud in NGC 3783 is likely located in the dusty torus.

We find r_ values within (1-2) 10 ^4 R_ g for NGC 3227, which are lower than those of (0.7-2) 10 ^5 R_ g found for X-ray absorbing clouds located in the dusty torus of this galaxy 2014MNRAS.439.1403M . We would obtain lower distances around 10^3 R_ g for the eclipsing clouds in NGC 3227 if we consider the high SMBH mass of 2 10 ^7 M _ used in our analysis in Section Spectral_analysis , which are lower than the BLR cloud distances within (0.5-7) 10 ^4 R_ g measured for NGC 3227 2014MNRAS.439.1403M .

sources.summary: The paper favors two warm absorbers for NGC 3227, aligning with previous models that also included soft excess, cold gas absorption, partial covering, and reflection.

quotes.quote: NGC 3227 In our analysis, the results favour the model with 2 warm absorbers. This result is consistent with the work by Mar2009 , where the best fit model finds 2 warm absorbers, besides a strong soft excess also absorbed by cold gas. Moreover, by estimating a maximum distance, they place the ionized absorber outside the BLR. N2021 also finds 2 warm absorbers, as well as a partially covered power law and a reflection component.

sources.summary: The paper notes NGC 3227 has weak active continuum, strong Ca II triplet absorption, Paschen absorption from young stars, and an unexpectedly small, faint bulge affected by dusty nuclear regions.

quotes.quote: NGC 3227 -- The active continuum is weak, and the Ca II triplet lines are seen at nearly full strength nw04 . Note the presence of Paschen absorption lines in figure fig:fcqspec (particularly P14 8598.4 between the two stronger \ lines) indicating the presence of a young stellar population. An F5 III star was used for a velocity template in this case. The bulge of this galaxy is surprisingly small and faint given its Hubble type SABa rc3 and , perhaps as a result of the dusty nuclear regions malkan98 .

sources.summary: The atlas treats NGC 3227 as an individually observed active or starburst galaxy, summarizing its prior classification and near-infrared continuum, emission-line, and absorption properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 3227 . Because of its proximity (15.6 Mpc), NGC 3227 is a well-known and studied Seyfert 1/1.5 galaxy in virtually all wavelengths intervals. It displays all the possible ingredients found in an AGN: variability in its nucleus both in the line and continuum win95 , a radio jet kuk95 , an ionization cone in [ O iii ] mun95 , a circumnuclear starburst gdp97 , strong X-ray emission rei85 , an inner warp molecular disk qui99 . The NIR images of this galaxy reveal an unresolved nuclear source in the K band and a nuclear stellar cluster that is slightly resolved in the J and H bands, this cluster contributes to about 40-65 of the total emission continuum schinnerer01 . The NIR properties of this object have been studied, among others, by rkp03 , schinnerer01 and qui99 .

sources.summary: The model uses NGC 3227 as a nearby Seyfert with a past nuclear starburst, an opaque massive torus, and one of the sample highest mass-accretion rates.

quotes.quote: This scenario is corroborated by a closer look at the nucleus of NGC 3227 where Davies et al. (2006) found signs of a past starburst ( 40 Myr ago) and a presently quiescent gas torus with a Toomre parameter Q>1 .

Moreover, 3 AGNs (Circinus, NGC 3227, and NGC 1068) have opaque tori ( < 2 ), 2 AGNs (NGC 1097 and NGC 7469) show moderately transparent tori ( 3-4 ), and 1 AGN (NGC 3783) has a transparent torus ( > 5 ).
The highest mass accretion rates (NGC 3227 and NGC 1068) are several ten times higher than that of NGC 3783.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 3227 among Seyfert 1 torus fits, noting likely extra nuclear contribution, high cloud counts in fits, and an almost featureless silicate region.

quotes.quote: It is then likely that the mid-IR nuclear fluxes and spectroscopy of NGC 7469 contain a contribution from star formation. A similar situation may be the case for NGC 3227.

In some cases (e.g., NGC 3227, NGC 7469) it appears as if to compensate for limited values of _V , the fits are achieved with more clouds along radial equatorial rays, close to the maximum value allowed by the models of N_0=15 .

Note also that the SED alone fits of some pure type 1 Seyferts always tend to produce the 9.7 m silicate feature in emission (see Figure 4), while the observations show an almost featureless spectrum (IC 4329A, NGC 3227) or the feature slightly in emission (NGC 4151).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3227 as paired with NGC 3226, observed with UVOT, showing Seyfert-like [Ne V] emission and the largest AGN infrared contribution among its DECOMPIR fits.

quotes.quote: NGC 3227: Along with its smaller companion NGC 3226, NGC 3227 was not observed by GALEX , so we use UVOT data instead. Its IRS spectrum shows [ Ne v ] emission consistent with a Seyfert nucleus. Its DECOMPIR fits are the worst of the nine galaxies, but have the highest AGN contribution of 15 25 to the total IR luminosity and 45 60 of the MIR luminosity.

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 3227 as a type 1 Seyfert whose VLA nuclear flux is resolved into VLBI jet-like substructure, with point-like X-rays and kpc-scale jet evidence.

quotes.quote: In general, the majority of the VLA nuclear flux density is resolved in jet-like complex sub-structures at VLBI resolution, as, e.g., in the Compton thick NGC 1068 (G04) and in the type 1 NGC 3227 (B12).

On the other hand, unobscured type 1 AGN like NGC 3227 and NGC 4151 (X-ray point-like nuclei), placed in the top-right part of the right panel in Figure  ra_x , suggest that the diffuse radio emission does not necessarily correlate with diffuse X-ray emission.

We find evidence or hints of the presence of a jet/outflow feature in the majority of the VLBI detected sources, either extending up to kpc scales as in NGC 1068 (G04), NGC 3227 (B12), and NGC 4258 (Cecil et al. 2000), or being confined to the inner pc/sub-pc regions (NGC 3079, NGC 4151, NGC 4395), possibly because of the jet interaction with the inter stellar medium (e.g., Ulvestad et al. 2005, Wrobel Ho 2006, Middelberg et al. 2007).

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 3227 with model outcomes, noting uncertain gas mass estimates and relating its black-hole, stellar, and disk mass ratios to the simulations.

quotes.quote: For NGC 3227 the comparison with observational data is more difficult, as the gas mass estimate is connected with great uncertainties of 2-20 10^7 M_ where the stellar mass itself is estimated to be 2 10^7 M_ Davies2006 .
Still, the black hole mass of NGC 3227 as well as the stellar mass are both of the same order as the final masses of model S4 (see Table tab:s-u-masses_comp ).
For the smallest disk mass estimate the mass ratio is comparable to that for NGC 3227, which would be inconsistent with our model.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 3227's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 3227 is a low-inclination barred spiral galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0039 ( D 22.1 Mpc) interacting with NGC 3226. It hosts a circum-nuclear starburst and a well-studied variable Sy 1.5 nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 that belongs to the nine-month BAT AGN sample. In addition, NGC 3227 possesses a complex extended NLR with emission extending to the north-east (PA 30 ), which does not align with the double lobe radio emission along a PA 10 mundell_anisotropic_1995 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3227 as a highly X-ray-variable radio-quiet AGN with archival high-frequency radio data and a large 95 GHz excess over the low-frequency slope.

quotes.quote: Six of the targets, namely NGC 3783, NGC 5548, NGC 7469, NGC 3227, Ark 564, and Mrk 766, are highly variable in the X-rays according to the extensive monitoring of markowitz04 .
The excess is large ( 4 - 7 ) for NGC 3783, ARK 564, and NGC 3227, and smaller (but still > 1 ) for MR 2251-178, NGC 5506, and NGC 7469.
In the present sample, the sources that have archival data above 10 GHz are only NGC 5506, NGC 7469, and NGC 3227.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3227 as a Seyfert example where ALMA CO observations give an inner-15-pc molecular outflow rate consistent with the dusty-wind model.

quotes.quote: As an example, Alo19 determine the mass outflow rate in NGC 3227 from ALMA CO observations within the inner 15 pc around the AGN. They find M _w 0.6-5 M_ /yr for a typical Seyfert AGN with a luminosity of L_ AGN 2 10^ 43 erg/s. The expected outflow rate from eq. ( eq:mdotwind ) would be M _w 0.7 M_ /yr.

sources.summary: The paper uses warm-absorber data for NGC 3227 in a Seyfert sample to infer ambient circumnuclear medium density profiles of roughly n ^ 1.7 to n ^ 2.15.

quotes.quote: We study the correlation between the outflow velocity and ionization parameter of WAs in each of the five Seyfert 1 galaxies (NGC 3227, NGC 3783, NGC 4051, NGC 4593, and NGC 5548), inferring that the density profile of the ACM is between n ^ 1.7 and n ^ 2.15 ( n is number density and is distance) from 0.01 pc to pc scales in these five AGNs.

The density profile indexes k of the ACM in the five Seyfert 1 galaxies of our sample (NGC 3227, NGC 3783, NGC 4051, NGC 4593, and NGC 5548) are within the range of k for the ACM in TDEs between 1 and 2.5 ; Alexander2020 (see Figure fig:kdis ).
According to the fitting results of the v_ out relation, we infer that the density profile of the ACM is between n ^ 1.7 and n ^ 2.15 from 0.01 pc to pc scales in these five AGNs.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 3227 prefers a hybrid reflection model, has a high fitted iron abundance, and belongs among the lowest-Eddington-ratio hybrid-model sources.

quotes.quote: We find that 12 out of the 18 objects (Mrk 335, Fairall 9, Mrk 1040, NGC 1365, Ark 120, NGC 3227, NGC 3783, NGC 4051, NGC 4151, IRAS 13197-1627, NGC 5548, and NGC 7469) prefer the hybrid model (i.e. Note that we found high Iron abundance for NGC 3227 ( 2.76 ^ 0.46 _ 0.41 ), NGC 3783 ( 1.84 ^ 0.12 _ 0.09 ), and NGC 4593 ( 2.75 ^ 1.51 _ 1.06 ). The lower Eddington ratios are observed for the sources that prefer the hybrid model, being Mrk 140, NGC 3227, NGC 4151 and IRAS 13197-1627 the sources with the lowest Eddington ratios, even considering their uncertainties.

sources.summary: The paper cites near-infrared speckle imaging of NGC 3227, noting a compact stellar cluster within 300 pc and a denser unresolved core dominating cluster mass.

quotes.quote: In the paper of Schinnerer et al. (2001) the near-infrared high angular resolution speckle imaging of the central region of the Seyfert galaxy NGC 3227 was performed. The authors showed that the CSC do exist in the inner 300 pc, and that in the innermost (unresolved, <70 pc) part there is the even more dense core of the stellar cluster, which contributes less to the luminosity, but contains 85 of the mass of the cluster. This result supports our leading idea of large enough masses of CSCs in AGNs.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3227 as one of two reverberation-mapped AGNs whose black-hole influence radius may be HST-resolvable, making it a stellar-dynamical mass candidate despite nuclear-light contamination.

quotes.quote: Of all the reverberation-mapped AGNs, only NGC 3227 and NGC 4151 might be expected to have a black hole radius of influence that is spatially resolvable with HST . These two AGNs are thus good candidates for measuring their black hole masses by stellar dynamical methods. A serious problem is that the AGN nuclear light swamps the stellar light, making the stellar absorption features difficult to detect.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3227 as an interacting nearby Seyfert, notes PAH emission in its inner 60 pc, and treats its cooler infrared color as star-formation evidence.

quotes.quote: NGC 3227, This object is a nearby Seyfert galaxy interacting with a gas-poor companion. A clear 3.3 m polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon (PAH) emission feature, which is usually associated with starformation activity, was identified in its inner 60pc ( EW_ 3.3 =52 10 , Rodriguez-Ardila Viegas 2003).
As a first inspection, both NGC 3227 and Mark 766 have much cooler (60,25) than does 1H 1934-063.

gmw_00000184.name: NGC 3281
gmw_00000184.overview: NGC 3281 is a highly inclined spiral galaxy hosting a Compton-thick Seyfert 2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1506.06143, arXiv:1804.07311]. The nucleus is heavily obscured by a compact, dusty torus [arXiv:1106.5731, arXiv:1403.2266] and drives a molecular outflow that extends up to 2.6 kiloparsecs [arXiv:2304.04004].

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 3281 as a Compton-thick Seyfert II in Antlia, detected strongly in H I absorption; the absorption has three velocity components around the galaxy's systemic velocity.

quotes.quote: NGC 3281 is the brightest object detected in WISE and is projected at 460 kpc from NGC 3268 (Figure ngc3281w ). The galaxy hosts one of the most nearby obscured, Compton thick active galactic nuclei (AGN; N_H>10^ 24 cm ^ 2 ), and has been a target of X-ray and infrared observations to investigate the nature of the obscuring torus StorchiBergmann92,Simpson98,Vignali02,Sales11 . The 3.4-4.6 m color is at the low end of the typical W1-W2=0.8 color cut indicative of AGN activity Jarrett11,Stern12 , but its 6dF emission line spectrum indicate it is a Seyfert II, and Compton thick AGN are known to span a range of WISE colors (Figure 4; Gandhi14 ).

Redshift measurements in the literature from optical emission lines give an average systemic velocity for the galaxy of 3427 26 Martin76,Sandage78,Rubin85,deVaucouleurs91,Jones09 . We find no \ emission associated with the galaxy, but we detect it strongly in absorption (Figure ngc3281 ). The flux of the continuum source is 78.8 mJy/beam, and the peak of the absorption is -13.4 mJy/beam corresponding to a peak optical depth of 0.186. By integrating the optical depth over the width of the line and using N_ HI =1.82 10^ 18 T_ spin /f (v)dv Wolfe75 we calculate an \ column density of 5.05 10^ 19 \ T_ spin /f cm ^ 2 . The absorption spans 230 \ and appears to be composed of three velocity components, two of which are blue-shifted with respect to the systemic velocity of the galaxy and the third of which is redshifted.

sources.summary: ALMA observations of NGC 3281 reveal rotating disk gas plus a molecular outflow encasing ionised gas, with low kinetic coupling unlikely to affect host evolution.

quotes.quote: We present ALMA observations of the Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 3281 at 100 pc spatial resolution. This galaxy was previously known to present a bi-conical ionised gas outflow extending to 2 kpc from the nucleus. The analysis of the CO moment and channel maps, as well as kinematic modelling reveals two main components in the molecular gas: one rotating in the galaxy plane and another outflowing and extending up to 1.8 - 2.6 kpc from the nucleus, partially encasing the ionised component. The mass of the outflowing molecular gas component is M_ , = (2.5 1.6) 10^ 6 , representing 1.7 - 2 of the total molecular gas seen in emission within the inner 2.3 kpc.

To gauge the impact that the molecular outflow may have on the evolution of NGC 3281, we calculated the percent of AGN luminosity ( ) that couples kinetically with the molecular gas via outflows: the kinetic coupling efficiency _ f, = E _ , / L_ AGN . For this, we used a luminosity of = 10^ 44.3 0.4 (see Section sec:intro ), and since the method 1 and 2 returned similar values (Section sec:met1 and sec:met2 ), we used the peak value from the radial profile E _ , as the kinetic power. Therefore, the resulting _ f, of the outflowing molecular gas ranges between 10^ 4 and 0.02 . This value is, at least, one order of magnitude lower than the 0.5 value required in models e.g. hopkins_elvis10,zubovas18 to produce a significant impact in the galaxy and influence its evolution.

sources.summary: The paper presents mid-infrared spectra of the Compton-thick Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 3281 and models its nucleus as a dusty torus producing deep silicate absorption.

quotes.quote: We present mid infrared (Mid-IR) spectra of the Compton-thick Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 3281, obtained with the Thermal-Region Camera Spectrograph (T-ReCS) at the Gemini South telescope. The spectra present a very deep silicate absorption at 9.7 m, and [ S iv ] 10.5 m and [ Ne ii ] 12.7 m ionic lines, but no evidence of PAH emission. We find that the nuclear optical extinction is in the range 24 A _ V 83 mag. A temperature T = 300 K was found for the black-body dust continuum component of the unresolved 65 pc nucleus and at 130 pc SE, while the region at 130 pc reveals a colder temperature (200 K).

We describe the nuclear spectrum of NGC 3281 using a clumpy torus model that suggests that the nucleus of this galaxy hosts a dusty toroidal structure. According to this model, the ratio between the inner and outer radius of the torus in NGC 3281 is R_0/R_d = 20, with 14 clouds in the equatorial radius with optical depth of _ V = 40 mag. We would be looking in the direction of the torus equatorial radius ( i = 60 ^ ), which has outer radius of R _ 0 11 pc.

sources.summary: The paper extracts NGC 3281 to include extended emission, finds model disagreement over torus column-density scenarios, but concludes all models agree the source is N_H variable.

quotes.quote: NGC 3281 Data reduction/fitting: The data was extracted using a circle of radius 10 (background region, annulus 11 20 ) to include all the extended emission (thus, making the comparison with the data easier). An additional observation exists that was not public at the moment this analysis took place.

Analysis of results: The data is well-fitted by all models, although they are not in strong agreement: favors a low- N_ H,av scenario, while favors a high- N_ H,av one. Both models are able to find an equivalent scenario to the best fit of the other, although with worse statistics ( ^2_ red =1.14 for a configuration with high N_ H,av , and ^2_ red =1.09 for a one with low N_ H,av ). Our best-fit is consistent with the results of Zhao2020 . The models show significant disagreement in the best-fit values of N_ H,los , probably arising from different disentanglements of the degeneracy with and N_ H,av . and show the best agreement, although the observation is significantly more obscured in the best fit. , on the other hand, generally prefers higher obscuration. However, the observation is compatible with the determination. Overall, this results in painting a much more variable picture of the source. In any case, all models agree that the source is indeed ` N_ H,los variable', and we thus classify it as such.

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 3281 with Mrk 3, finding similar Compton-thick Seyfert 2 torus geometries and a more compact, optically obscuring dust torus in NGC 3281.

quotes.quote: Following that line, sales11 represented the torus model geometry of Compton-thick Sy 2 NGC 3281 using the clumpy models, finding evidence that the matter responsible for the silicate absorption at 9.7 m could also be responsible for the X-ray scattering, with a column density of N _ H 1.2 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 . We also constrained parameters of the CLUMPY model to the torus geometry of Mrk 3 (see Section sec:torus ), which might indicate a similar correlation between the dusty material associated with the silicate feature and the X-ray scattering region in this galaxy. However, we should note that this conclusion may be subject of some concerns as it has been explored by goulding12 and need to be further addressed. The physical parameters of each torus are listed in Tab. tab:torus_ngc3281_mrk3 .

From our analysis, we can conclude that the torus model geometry derived to NGC 3281 and Mrk 3 are quite similar, which have only differences in the visual optical depth of clump's clouds as well as the total number of clouds in the torus equatorial radius suggesting that the dust torus in NGC 3281 is more compact than Mrk 3 (Tab. tab:torus_ngc3281_mrk3 ). If we take A ^ obs _V = 1.086 N_0 _V e^ (i - 90)^2 ^2 as an estimate of the optical extinction along the line of sight, we obtain A ^ obs _V = 506 mag for NGC 3281, and A ^ obs _V = 393 mag for Mrk 3. The difference in the optical obscuration generated by the two tori may explain the depth of the silicate profiles as the spectrum of NGC 3281 shows a deeper silicate feature than that of Mrk 3 see Fig. 2 in sales11 .

sources.summary: NGC 3281 is discussed as a highly obscured Sy2 galaxy whose clumpy-torus model fits most data and predicts shallow silicate absorption and line-of-sight extinction.

quotes.quote: NGC 3281. This Sy2 galaxy suffers large extinction ( A_ V = 22 11 mag to the IR-emitting and N_H = 2 10^ 24 cm^ 2 to the X-ray emitting regions; Simpson98,Vignali02 ). The clumpy models fit the data well, except for the 18.3 point, which the models underestimate slightly. The probability distributions resulting from the fit show that large values of and i are more probable than others ( > 68 and > 49 , respectively) and establish an upper limit for the optical depth per cloud of _ V < 12 at a 68 confidence level. The number of clumps N_0 presents a Gaussian distribution with a tail towards large values, with median value N_0 = 6 _ 2 ^ 4 , as the q histogram (median value of q = 1.3 _ 0.8 ^ 1.1 ).

sources.summary: The S7 notes describe NGC 3281 as a Seyfert 2 with an extended V-shaped narrow-line region, bubble-like counterstructure, outflow-like dynamics, and no visible HII regions.

quotes.quote: NGC 3281: This Sy 2 galaxy was studied by Storchi-Bergmann92 , and imaged by HST Schmitt03 . Our data show a very extended V-shaped ENLR extending north from the centre with an opening angle of 40^ , and a more diffuse bubble-like structure on the other side. The ENLR extent is greater than the 38 arcsec field of WiFeS. The internal dynamics are suggestive of an outflow. No \ regions are visible.

sources.summary: The paper finds a NW-to-SE mm-wave linear structure in NGC 3281 and says radio and optical data do not reveal matching counterparts.

quotes.quote: 44: NGC 3281 A NW-to-SE linear structure, parallel to a PA of 100 -130 , can be seen. In contrast, reported only unresolved radio (8.46 GHz) emission with a size upper limit of < 60 pc based on a spatial resolution of 0 2. This is better than that achieved by the ALMA observation (0 5). As a spatially extended signature of the AGN activity, a narrow line region was mapped by using optical [ O III ] emission in , and this extends toward the NE. Eventually, from the radio and optical observations, we cannot find mm-wave counterparts.

sources.summary: NGC 3281 is treated as mildly Compton-thick; its X-ray model and lower high-resolution VISIR flux suggest extended N-band emission beyond the nuclear core.

quotes.quote: NGC 3281 vignalicomastri02 identified this source as being mildly Compton-thick. Using their model () which corrects for Compton-scattering in a physically-motivated way =>L_ 2-10 =1.7 10^ 43 and L_ 20-100 =2.1 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 in our cosmology. Using this model to infer the 20--40 keV and 14--195 keV fluxes in order to compare with the recent studies of bird07 and markwardt05 suggests log L_ 2-10 =43.30 0.15 . krabbe01 detected the presence of extended N band emission beyond their resolution limit of 1 \ in this source.

sources.summary: The paper characterizes NGC 3281 as a highly inclined Sy2 spiral whose T-ReCS spectrum shows deep silicate absorption, forbidden lines, and heavy obscuration from concentrated dust within 200 pc.

quotes.quote: NGC 3281 is an highly--inclined spiral galaxy with a Sy2 nucleus Veron10 . Ramos09 and Sales11 presented observations of this source with T-ReCS with the broad N and Qa bands. They found that the spectrum of NGC 3281 shows only a very deep silicate absorption at 9.7 m , and some forbidden emission lines (e.g., [ SIV ] at 10.5 m ). They conclude that NGC 3281 is a heavily obscured source, due to concentrated dust within a radius of 200 pc.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 3281's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 3281 is an highly-inclined spiral galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0107 ( D 52.8 Mpc) with a Sy 2 nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 that belongs to the nine-month BAT AGN sample. It features an extended cone-like NLR with a total length of 6 1.5 kpc along the north-south direction (PA 20 ; storchi-bergmann_ionization_1992,schmitt_hubble_2003 ), while the nucleus is unresolved at radio wavelengths (e.g., ulvestad_radio_1989,schmitt_jet_2001 ). After the discovery of its MIR brightness by , NGC 3281 was imaged in N band by and who detected a compact MIR nucleus.

gmw_00000185.name: NGC 3312
gmw_00000185.overview: NGC 3312 is a highly inclined, disturbed spiral galaxy [arXiv:1310.2770, arXiv:2107.04486] located near the center of the Hydra I cluster [arXiv:2105.06014, arXiv:2112.00231] that hosts a LINER nucleus [arXiv:1310.2770]. The galaxy is undergoing ram pressure stripping [arXiv:2209.05605, arXiv:2112.00231], which has truncated its neutral hydrogen disk [arXiv:2105.06014, arXiv:2112.00231] and produced star-forming stripped gas tails [arXiv:2209.05605].

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 3312 is undergoing ram pressure stripping, with compressed gas contours, star-forming stripped gas, a low wind angle, swept-back outskirts, and 5.0e8 solar masses in its stripped component.
