quotes.quote: We present a multi-wavelength study of the center of the Hydra I galaxy cluster, including exquisite new MeerKAT \ and DECam H imaging which reveal conclusive evidence for ram pressure stripping in NGC 3312, NGC 3314a and NGC 3314b through compressed \ contours, well-defined \ tails, and ongoing star formation in the stripped gas. In particular, we quantify the stripped material in NGC 3312 and NGC 3314a, which makes up between 8 and 35 of the gas still in the disk, is forming stars at 0.5 \ yr ^ 1 , and extends 30-60 kpc from the main disk.

NGC 3312 appears to be experiencing a relatively low wind angle (nearly face-on). The central \ disk appears largely unimpacted by the ram pressure, with the inner disk having a relatively flat \ distribution at 10^ 21 , while the galaxy outskirts are swept back (Figure fig:fdp24 ). The \ tail off the southern edge of the galaxy is longer than the northern edge.

For NGC 3312, we estimate 5.0 10^8 \ or 8 1 of the measured gas mas is in the stripped component. The \ on the southwest edge of the galaxy, which also features star forming streams, extends to about 30 kpc from the disk, as measured from perpendicular to the kinematic major axis.

sources.summary: The paper identifies J103702 273359 as a highly asymmetric Hydra I galaxy near the cluster centre, likely affected by ram-pressure stripping.

quotes.quote: The high asymmetry of J103702 273359 (first panel of Figure fig:5beam_galaxies ) is likely to be caused by ram pressure stripping, based on the truncation of the H i disc within the optical radius on the Northern side and compression (puffing out) of the H i on the Eastern (Western) side. This is further supported by J103702 273359 being located near the cluster centre (red contour near the star, which indicates the centre of the Hydra I cluster in Figure fig:sky_map ; /R_ 200 <0.1 , red square in Figure fig:phase_space ).

J103523 281855 and J103702 273359 (pink triangle and red square, respectively) lie near the border between the regions that statistically contain galaxies that are virialised or recent infalls (below and above dashed black line, respectively). J103523 281855 (pink triangle) has similar properties (fairly symmetric with a more extended H i disc relative to its optical disc) to J103335 272717 and J104016 274630. J104059 270456 (filled blue diamond) stands out as residing in a distinctly different part of phase-space with the largest projected distance from the cluster centre ( /R_ 200 0.8 ) and has a normalised velocity difference near the cluster escape velocity (solid black curve). Of these five galaxies, J104059 270456 appears to be the at the earliest stage of infalling into the cluster and yet has an asymmetry similar to J103702 273359, which is at a cluster centric distance of /R_ 200 <0.1 and has likely spent more time in the cluster.

sources.summary: The paper singles out NGC 3312 as a Hydra I galaxy with the smallest HI-to-optical diameter ratio, a truncated HI disc, and ram pressure stripping signatures.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:single_plate shows the PanSTARRS band and GALEX NUV images with overlaid ellipses indicating the band and NUV disc diameters (solid red and dashed blue, respectively) and the purple contour showing the H i surface density of 1 M _ pc ^ 2 of the galaxy with the smallest d_ HI /d_ opt =0.8 : WALLABY J103702 273359 (hereafter referred to by its NGC number: NGC 3312). The projected distance of NGC 3312 from the centre of Hydra I is <0.1R_ 200 . For NGC 3312, ram pressure stripping is likely the dominant mechanism responsible for the truncated H i disc due to the coincident NUV emission and compressed H i column density contours on the left (eastern) side of the galaxy. This interpretation is in agreement with the results of , who also find that NGC 3312 to be experiencing ram pressure stripping.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 3312's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 3312 is a highly-inclined disturbed spiral galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0096 ( D 48.4 Mpc) with a little-studied LINER nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 . No observations are available. In the WISE images, a compact MIR nucleus embedded within spiral-like host emission is visible.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 3312 as a giant spiral near NGC 3314A in velocity, potentially sharing a weakly interacting group, with disturbed disk morphology and southwest protruding stellar filaments.

quotes.quote: To explain the asymmetries found in the HI distribution, the authors suggested that NGC 3314A and the giant spiral galaxy NGC 3312 could be members of a weakly interacting group in the foreground of the Hydra I cluster. NGC 3312 is a cluster member at a similar radial velocity as NGC 3314A ( V_ hel =35 km s ^ 1 ), located SE of the cluster core (see Fig. fig:mosaic ). VST images show that the disk morphology of NGC 3312 is also quite disturbed: protruding stellar filaments are detected SW of the disk (see Fig. fig:mosaic ) .

gmw_00000186.name: NGC 3368
gmw_00000186.overview: NGC 3368 is a double-barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:1410.4339, arXiv:2303.00763] located in the Leo I group [arXiv:1208.5054, arXiv:2303.00763] that hosts a central supermassive black hole [arXiv:2210.04916, arXiv:2303.00763] and a weak LINER active nucleus [arXiv:1310.2770, arXiv:2104.10168]. The galaxy features an old, metal-poor outer stellar halo [arXiv:2401.03985] and is directly connected to the Leo Ring [arXiv:2209.10994], likely due to a past head-on collision with NGC 3384 [arXiv:1005.4208, arXiv:2202.08193].

sources.summary: The paper describes M96 as a massive late-type galaxy with offset outer spiral structure, possible HI-ring accretion, a smooth extended disk, and mild color asymmetry.

quotes.quote: M96 (NGC 3368) is one of two massive late-type galaxies in the group. The galaxy has long been known to have a faint outer ring or set of spiral arms originating at roughly 90 \ e.g. schanberg73 , which we clearly resolve as part of an outer disk with a position angle offset by more than 20 ^ from the high surface brightness inner disk. These outer spiral arms primarily have long made it (along with NGC 3384) a favored candidate in the proposed collisional origin of the HI ring rood84, md10 , as this level of asymmetry is difficult to produce via secular evolution processes. The galaxy's connection to the Ring was apparent from the Ring's initial discovery by schneider83 onward, with schneider89 noting that, since most of its HI content exists in its outer disk, M96 may actually be accreting matter from the Ring.

Beyond 10 kpc, there is no evidence for a disk break in the outer disk, which shows a smooth exponential profile out to 25 kpc (7.5 scale lengths, as measured using the profile beyond 200 ). Again, the integrated (uncorrected) color of B-V=0.85 that we measure is in good agreement with the RC3 color of (B-V)_ T =0.86 .

The northeast/southwest asymmetry in our color profiles may reflect this process: the northeast side, which shows bluer colors at 85 \ and 200 , is the direction from which the HI appears to be accreting schneider89 . That these colors are only mildly bluer ( B-V = 0.7 , compared to 0.8 in the remaining angular wedges) and spatially localized (little azimuthal mixing even at the innermost radius, 85 ) would imply that such star formation, if occurring, would be rather weak and somewhat recent, as it has not had time to azimuthally mix.

sources.summary: The paper describes M96 as the other spiral near Clouds 1-4, with a warp, optical asymmetry, possible cloud connection, and direct connection to the Leo Ring.

quotes.quote: Clouds 1-4 are midway between the giant spiral galaxies M95 and M96, which have a projected separation of approximately 0.7 degrees, equivalent to 136 kpc. The clouds are also at similar velocities to the two spirals (M95 is at 779 , M96 is at 888 ), as shown by the PV-diagram in figure fig:pvclouds . Cloud 1 is however somewhat deviant from the trend in P-V space seen from clouds 1-4.

In contrast, M96 does show evidence of a warp (this is much easier to see in the online 3D tool than the renzogram or PV map), as well as being asymmetrical in its optical appearance, but not any larger extension that could be identified as a tail that would indicate gas removal. One possibility is that the clouds in this region are the brightest peaks in a lower-density bridge of material connecting the two galaxies, as suggested for the M31-M33 clouds ( wolfe ). If so, the large-scale feature expected in tidal encounters ( me16 , me17 ) would exist but happen to be below our column density sensitivity. We investigate this in figure fig:m95renzo (see figure fig:wholeleo for a map of the whole survey region). The figure shows renzogram contours at the 3.5 level from the standard AGES cube, and also thinner contours at 4 from the cube after additional Hanning smoothing (width 15 channels, equivalent to 40 resolution) of the spectral axis - spectral smoothing has the advantage of increasing sensitivity without degrading spatial resolution. The smoothing reduces the rms to 0.3 mJy, about half that of the standard cube. Note that due to the lower spectral resolution, the sensitivity to total mass present, i.e. column density, is degraded to N _ = 3.0 10 ^ 17 cm ^ 2 at 1 smoothing improves the sensitivity to the average mass present per unit velocity, which is the relevant factor when searching for diffuse emission. Interestingly, this extra smoothing does not alter the appearance of M96, M95 or cloud 1, but the other clouds do appear more extended. Cloud 2 shows a tentative connection to M96. Somewhat surprisingly, cloud 4 appears to be marginally resolved, spanning approximately two beams - given that it has an optical counterpart, we would expect it to have a higher column density than the other features and so be more compact, yet this is not the case. However, this is a further indication that our size estimates in section sec:size are unlikely to be wide of the mark. Cloud 3 becomes significantly more extended, spanning four beams (45 kpc) with a clear velocity gradient across the whole feature (the velocity difference between the most extreme contours where the cloud is visible is 67 ), though it does not appear to directly connect with the disc of M96. The extra Hanning smoothing also reveals a tentative additional detection here labelled as cloud 7. In the standard cube this is just visible but appears as an extension of cloud 2. Owing to its small size (the contours do not even span a whole Arecibo beam) and low S/N (peak 5.4, integrated 6.25), we treat this detection with caution. If real, it would have an mass of 3.5 10 ^ 6 , a W50 of 52 and a W20 value of 80 . Despite the detection of extended features and a possible additional cloud, the extra processing does not reveal any large-scale bridge of material linking M95 and M96 - at this sensitivity level, there is no evidence that the clouds are peaks in a larger structure. While cloud 2 does resemble some of the clouds discussed in (where features which appeared as discrete at a lower sensitivity were revealed as being connected to the disc of M33 at a higher sensitivity), the smoothing does not suggest the disc itself is likely to be much larger than it appears in the standard cube. This does not rule out a tidal origin for the clouds, but does not fit the general expectation of tidal stripping. Of course, the elephant in the room is the Leo Ring - M96 is directly connected to the main body of the Ring The impression one gets from inspecting the data is that the of M96 is superimposed on that from the Ring. This is somewhat subjective, but clearly there is nothing like the classical tail and counter-tail structure predicted in most tidal encounters ( tooms ). , and it is possible M95 was also involved in the creation of this feature. Potentially M96 could be interacting with the other major galaxies in the Ring (e.g. M105, NGC 3384) as well as M95. It is interesting to note that the clouds 2, 3 and 4 are close in velocity to the warp seen in the of M96 -- again, in PV space these clouds do resemble a bridge, but this is not seen in PP space.

sources.summary: The paper argues NGC 3368 is a bona fide Leo I member; its published TRGB tip is likely too bright, while velocity, Cepheid distance, and H I maps support the group distance.

quotes.quote: The galaxies NGC 3368 and NGC 3627 are bona fide members of the Leo I group e.g. Humason:etal:56,Huchra:Geller:82 , yet their published m^ * _ I magnitudes of 25.61 Mould:Sakai:09a and 25.82 Mould:Sakai:09b , respectively, are brighter by 0.6 than the mean TRGB magnitude m^ * _ I =26.34 0.08 of four unquestioned group members (NGC 3351, 3377, 3379, and 3384; see Sakai:etal:04,Rizzi:etal:07,Mould:Sakai:09a,Mould:Sakai:09b ). The proposed tip of NGC 3368 is based on (too) few stars, and the genuine tip of NGC 3627 may well be buried in a dense cloud of stars. The edge detection functions of both galaxies show secondary peaks at m^ * _ I 26.4 that presumably reflect the true TRGBs. Also the velocities of v_ 220 =720 and 428 ( v_ 220 is the velocity corrected for a self-consistent Virgocentric infall model with a local infall 220 ) of NGC 3368 and NGC 3627 compared with the mean velocity of v_ 220 =497 ( =159 ) from 8 bright group members make it unlikely that they could be foreground galaxies.

Moreover, we note that the Cepheid distances of NGC 3368 and NGC 3627 of (m-M)=30.34 and (m-M)=30.50 Saha:etal:06 are in very good agreement with the adopted mean TRGB distance of the Leo I group of m-M =30.39 0.08 . Finally convincing evidence for the group membership comes from the HI maps of the Leo I group Figs. 3 and 4 Stierwalt:etal:09 . For these arguments NGC 3368 and 3627 are unquestionable members of the Leo I group; as such they are treated in the following.

sources.summary: NGC 3368 is described as a double-barred Sab galaxy whose nuclear disc appears smaller than the simulated one, with an embedded secondary bar and an 11 percent stellar-mass fraction.

quotes.quote: Comparison to NGC 3368 NGC 3368 is a double-barred Sab galaxy in the Leo Group with a Cepheid distance of 10.05 Mpc freedman01 . Although it is only slightly less inclined than NGC 3945 ( i 50 , for an outer-disc ellipticity of 0.34), its bar is at a more intermediate position angle (bar PA = 67 ).

The ND in NGC 3368 shows up in the ellipticity profile as a slight bump in ellipticity at a 400 -500 pc, and in the position-angle profile as the local maximum in the same semi-major axis range Note that the ``inner disc'' identified by erwin04 is actually the projected B/P structure. . NGC 3368 has a much more prominent B/P structure and a significantly smaller ND (with an embedded secondary bar) than is the case for the simulation. Erwin2014b estimates a ND mass of 7.1 10^9 \ for NGC 3368, or 11 per cent of the total stellar mass, about a factor of two lower than in the model at 10 Gyr. The size of the ND is 0.12 relative to the deprojected radius of maximum ellipticity. As with NGC 4371 and NGC 3945, the ND in the model is about twice as large as in this galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3368 to test orbit-model selection, describing its disk, bulges, weak LINER activity, photometry, kinematics, and inclination recovery.

quotes.quote: This galaxy is part of the SINFONI black hole survey cf. 2016ApJ...818...47S and was chosen by us as follow-up of a previous analysis by 2010MNRAS.403..646N . Due to the fact that NGC 3368 is a disk galaxy, its inclination can be estimated independently of the dynamical modelling. It is therefore a useful testbed for the inclination recovery.

NGC 3368 is a double-barred spiral galaxy with a composite bulge consisting of a larger pseudobulge and a smaller classical bulge cf. 2004A&A...415..941E . The galaxy is classified as a LINER2 1997ApJS..112..315H . The activity is so weak that we do not expect it to influence the dynamical modelling results. We assume that NGC 3368 lies at a distance of d=10.4 Mpc 2001ApJ...546..681T where 1 corresponds to 50.4 pc .

sources.summary: The paper describes M96-DF6 as an ultra-diffuse dwarf in the Leo-I group, likely hosting the newly found ultra-faint satellite dw1046+1244 at a projected 5.4 kpc.

quotes.quote: For PGC 4689210, a dwarf galaxy in the M96 group -- also known as Leo I 09 2002MNRAS.335..712T , or NGC3384-DF6, M96-DF6 2018ApJ...868...96C - we detected a faint stellar over-density, hereafter called dw1046+1244, close to the galaxy (see Fig. fig:pv ). It has a similar color and surface brightness granulation, indicating that it may be at the same distance as M96-DF6. In the HST image, M96-DF6 is barely resolved into stars. M96-DF6 is an ultra-diffuse galaxy in the Leo-I group with a tip of the red giant branch (TRGB) distance of 10.2 0.3 Mpc 2018ApJ...868...96C .

The ultra-diffuse galaxy M96-DF6 has a stellar luminosity of 2 10 ^7 L _ . This is 100 more luminous than its presumed satellite galaxy. It is separated from dw1046+1244 by only 1.75 arcmin, which corresponds to 5.4 kpc at their measured distance.

sources.summary: NGC 3368 is described as a double-barred spiral whose stellar bars, spiral arms, and gas distribution produce alternating inflow and outflow, including central inflow toward the AGN.

quotes.quote: NGC 3368 has two stellar bars, an inner one with a radius of 1.0 kpc and an outer one with a radius of 3.2 kpc, which have a small offset of 25 between their position angles (see Fig. fig_torq_dpj ). Two spiral arms are connected to the ends of the outer bar. The atomic gas distribution shows two gaseous spiral arms which are coincident with the stellar ones and an additional gaseous ring around the outer bar.

On the basis of our radial torque profiles we found gas inflow for the outer disk region (7-10 kpc) with an average angular momentum transfer d L/L of 10 of the total angular momentum per rotational period at a given radius, while on intermediate disk scales (2-7 kpc) gas outflow is present (see Fig. fig_torque ). Because of the switch from out- to inflow, gas is expected to accumulate at this radius which is confirmed by our observed atomic gas distribution (see Fig. fig_torque_mass ).
For the center we found a large gas inflow from 2 - 0.08 kpc, that seems to transport the gas towards the AGN.

sources.summary: The paper identifies M96 as a nearby spiral SN Ia host in Leo I, measures its TRGB distance modulus, and uses it to recalibrate SN 1998bu.

quotes.quote: M66 and M96 in the Leo I Group are nearby spiral galaxies hosting Type Ia Supernovae (SNe Ia). We estimate the distances to these galaxies from the luminosity of the tip of the red giant branch (TRGB). We obtain VI photometry of resolved stars in these galaxies from F555W and F814W images in the Hubble Space Telescope archive.

From the luminosity function of these red giants we find the TRGB I band magnitude to be I_ TRGB =26.20 0.03 for M66 and 26.21 0.03 for M96. These values yield distance modulus (m-M)_0=30.12 0.03 ( random ) 0.12 ( systematic ) for M66 and (m-M)_0=30.15 0.03 ( random ) 0.12 ( systematic ) for M96.
M66 (NGC 3627, SAB(s)b) and M96 (NGC 3368, SAB(rs)ab) are nearby bright spiral galaxies hosting SNe Ia: SN 1989B in M66 eva89,wel94 and SN 1998bu in M96 vil98,sun99,jha99,her00,spy04 .

sources.summary: M 96-DF6 lies near the center of the M96/Leo-I group with an HST distance; the paper estimates 276 kpc separation from M96 and predicts a MOND dispersion of 9.5 km/s.

quotes.quote: Of those, the two elliptical galaxies M 105 and NGC 3384 have separations of 53 kpc and 67 kpc from M 96-DF6, respectively. The large spiral galaxy M 96 has a projected separation of 172 kpc. This UDG sits almost at the center of this massive group, complicating accurate predictions. Fortunately, for M 96-DF6, HST distance measurements are available D=10.2 0.3 Mpc 2018ApJ...868...96C , indicating that this UDG is at the nominal distance of M 96 D=10.4 0.3 Mpc 2013AJ....145..101K , and farther separated from M 105 D=11.3 0.1 Mpc 2013AJ....145..101K and NGC 3384 D=9.4 0.1 Mpc 2013AJ....145..101K .

For M 96-DF6, the three dimensional separation between M 96 and M 96-DF6 is 276 kpc, with a distance uncertainty for M 96-DF6 of 300 kpc. This gives a typical MONDian velocity dispersion of =9.5_ 1.0 ^ +1.0 km s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper discusses M96 as a bright Leo I subgroup galaxy and possible interaction partner, linking its disks, the HI ring scenario, and a southern stream toward NGC 3384.

quotes.quote: This is a very loose group composed, at least, of 11 bright galaxies where seven out of the total, including NGC 3379 (BGG) and NGC 3384, are associated with the subgroup NGC 3368 (M96) centered on the BGG.

Sil'chenko2003ApJ...591..185S found that the ages and kinematics of both the circumnuclear stellar and gaseous disks in NGC 3384, NGC 3379 and M96 are consistent with a formation age on the Gyr scale. The primordial formation of the HI ring, however, is hindered by stability problems over large time scales, a fundamental inconsistency which suggests that a close encounter between disk galaxies is the most likely scenario for the formation of the ring. They might be M96 and NGC 3384, since the S0 NGC 3384 is the only galaxy of the group having the disk major axis aligned with the ring.

Since they are elongated in the direction of the two nearby galaxies M96 and NGC 3338, they could be evidence of gravitational interactions with the pair. In particular, the stream in the direction of M96 could result from a head-on collision between LEO group members NGC 3384 and M96 Dansac2010ApJ...717L.143M .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 3368 (M 96) as a spiral target, estimating its globular-cluster count, distance, SMBH mass, luminosity, and globular-cluster specific frequency.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we present new measurements and analyses of the globular cluster systems of the Sab galaxies NGC 3368 (M 96), NGC 4736 (M 94) and NGC 4826 (M 64), and the Sm galaxy NGC 4395. Their surface brightness fluctuations (SBF) distances are 10.4 ^ +1.1 _ 1.0 Mpc for NGC 3368, 5.0 0.4 Mpc for NGC 4736, and 7.3 0.7 Mpc for NGC 4826 tonr01,blak10 . NGC 4395 is completely devoid of a bulge, and hence lacks an SBF measurement; we adopt a Cepheid distance of 4.3 0.4 Mpc thim04 . The masses of their central supermassive black holes are (3.6 1.1) 10^5 \ M_ for NGC 4395 pete05 ; (7.5 1.5) 10^6 \ M_ for NGC 3368; 6.8 ^ +1.5 _ 1.6 10^6 \ M_ for NGC 4736, and (1.6 0.4) 10^6 \ M_ for NGC 4826 korm13a .
Hence, we get N_ GC = 35^ +20 _ 16 for NGC 3368, N_ GC = 15 7 for NGC 4395, N_ GC = 46 18 for NGC 4736, and N_ GC = 69^ +31 _ 28 for NGC 4826.

From the distance to NGC 3368 and the values of B_ T,0 = 9.80 15 mag and ( B \ V ) _ T,0 = 0.79 0.01 given in rc3 , we find M_V = -21.08 0.36 mag, and a specific frequency S_N = N_ GC 10^ 0.4 M_V + 15 = 0.13 0.06 , if we consider random errors only, and S_N = 0.13 0.07 , if we include the uncertainty in the number of obscured clusters.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3368/M96 as a complex double-barred Leo I primary with MW-like halo mass, a central supermassive black hole, and five assigned satellite candidates.

quotes.quote: NGC 3368 (M96) is a double-barred spiral galaxy with complex morphology, hosting a supermassive black hole in the central bulge erwin_double-barred_2004,2010MNRAS.403..646N . As the primary of the Leo I Group, it has a similar mass to the MW ( M_ Halo 1.3 10^ 12 M_ from abundance matching), but is not an ideal MW analog because of the complex Leo I group environment. We assign five satellite candidates to M96, four of which have intermediate (AGC 200512=LeG 06, UGC 5812, AGC 202035) to high (AGC 202024=LeG 13, the only R_ 200 object of the five) likelihood of association. UGC 5812 is an Sdm galaxy karachentsev_updated_2013 in M95's projected R_ 200 field and outside of M96's, but is assigned to M96 because of a lower . The remaining candidate, UGC 6014, although identified as an M96 Group object by S09, is likely a background object given its large positive velocity separation and projected distance.

sources.summary: The paper detects M96's old, metal-poor outer halo in the BST1047 field, estimating its projected radius and metallicity and separating it from BST1047 populations.

quotes.quote: Finally, in the environment surrounding BST1047, our imaging also reveals the old, metal-poor ( [ M/H ] =-1.3 0.2 ) stellar halo of M96 at a projected radius of 50 kpc.

Of course, the background region itself is not a pure background. As BST1047 resides within the Leo I group, and also sits projected only 15 \ (48 kpc) northeast of the luminous spiral galaxy M96, our ACS pointing samples not only background sources, but also stars in M96's extended stellar halo, as well as any potential Leo I intragroup stars watkins14, ragusa22 .

Looking at the background region, which likely includes populations in M96's outer stellar halo, the red sequence seen there is well-matched by old RGB tracks with metallicity [ M/H ] in the range 1 to 1.5 . Presuming this is M96's halo we are seeing, and adopting a typical halo alpha abundance of [ / Fe ] =+0.3 , the metallicity corresponds to [ Fe/H ]   1.2 to 1.7 salaris93,streich14 . These metallicities are similar to those found in the outer halos of nearby spirals in the GHOSTS project monachesi16 , again arguing these stars belong to the old halo population of M96.

sources.summary: The paper finds that NGC 3368 has a gaseous disk whose dynamical position angle differs strongly from the stellar disk, suggesting externally acquired gas rotating in another plane.

quotes.quote: The dynamical position angle of the outer gaseous disk, PA_ dyn =170^ , at =30-200'' , differs strongly from the orientation of the line of nodes of the stellar disk, PA_0=135^ .

The orientation of the ionized gas disk PA_ dyn =170-175^ is in good agreement with the outer part of the neutral hydrogen velocity field (Schneider, ) and with CO kinematics at <15'' (see Fig. 1 in Sakamoto et al., ). Thus, we suggest that the gas (ionized/neutral) and the stars in this galaxy rotate in different planes. All the gas in this galaxy, which is the brightest member of the western half of Leo group, has an external origin, in agreement with Schneider ( ).

sources.summary: The paper links M96 to the Leo Ring through a warped disk and HI spur, while noting its bar and dust ring orientation as evidence in competing interaction and primordial-ring interpretations.

quotes.quote: A spur connects the gas cloud with the warped disk of the M96 spiral galaxy, suggesting the gas may have been swept out after an interaction involving NGC 3384 and M96 otherringref . However, the central bars and dust rings of the three brightest nearby galaxies (NGC 3384, M96 M105) which all have similar spatial orientations to the Ring ringref2 and the Ring's Keplerian rotation M96multi together suggest the gas could instead be primordial and left over from the formation of the galaxy group.

The most massive contribution to the Ring, containing the spur connecting to M96, as well as the additional structures to the north, east, and west found in the map are all recovered by the ALFALFA dataset, and no new significant structures are found despite the much larger sky coverage of the ALFALFA map. Both the map and the ALFALFA one show a velocity gradient with lower velocities found for the more scattered clumps to the northeast and higher velocities belonging to the larger portions in the southwest. The ALFALFA survey map of the Ring covering 708 to 1046 is shown in Figure ringcont overlaid on a mosaic of SDSS -band images. The largest optical galaxies in the M96 group are NGC 3384 and NGC 3379=M105 at the center of the Ring, NGC 3368=M96 which is connected to the Ring by a spur of HI, and NGC 3351 to the west.

sources.summary: The paper adds NGC3368 to its sample and treats it as a pseudobulge galaxy; it is identified as an outlier in one scaling relation and deviates in later comparisons.

quotes.quote: To increase the number of objects with low , we add to the sample NGC3368 and NGC3489 whose \ is estimated in Nowak et al. (2010).

Figure fg:mbh illustrates that 4 out of 9 pseudobulges (NGC3489, NGC3998, NGC4258, NGC4594) are consistent with the correlation for classical bulges, while the others (CIRCINUS, IC2560, NGC1068, NGC3079, NGC3368) are outliers, at more than 4 below the LINMIX ERR linear regression.
Of these, CIRCINUS, IC2560, NGC1068, and NGC3398 lie below all the \ scaling relations; NGC3368 deviates from , - but not from , while the contrary happens for NGC3998.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 3368 as a double-barred spiral with a large pseudobulge, weak nuclear activity, and a measured central SMBH mass of 7.5 x 10^6 solar masses.

quotes.quote: The spiral galaxy NGC 3368 is double-barred and hosts a large pseudobulge with a tiny classical bulge component at the very centre.
Both galaxies show weak nuclear activity in the optical, indicative of the presence of a supermassive black hole.
The SMBH mass of NGC 3368, averaged over the four quadrants, is M_ =7.5 10^ 6 M _ with an error of 1.5 10^ 6 M _ , which mostly comes from the non-axisymmetry in the data.

sources.summary: The paper identifies M96 as the massive interloper in a head-on collision with NGC 3384, leaving an H i bridge and helping form the Leo ring.

quotes.quote: In our model, NGC 3384 is the target disk of a head-on collision with the massive galaxy M96. M96 appeared to be the most likely interloper, assuming that the \ bridge linking it to the ring was left over after the collision. Note that the key role played by M96 in the formation of the ring had already been envisioned by .

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 3368's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 3368 is a low-inclination spiral galaxy at a distance of D= 10.6 1.6 Mpc (NED redshift-independent median) with an active nucleus optically classified as a LINER ho_search_1997-1 . No compact radio core could be detect in the centre of this galaxy nagar_radio_2002 , although a compact UV and X-ray source was detected satyapal_joint_2004, chiaberge_hubble_2005,maoz_murmur_2005 . The first N band photometry of NGC 3368 was obtained with IRTF cizdziel_multiaperture_1985 .

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 3368 as a LINER with no detected radio core, no observed UV variability, but a higher 2500 flux than earlier HST measurements.

quotes.quote: NGC 3368 - Nagar et al. (2000) report a 3 limit of 0.6 mJy at 2 cm on any radio core at VLA resolution ( 1'' ). MO5 found no UV variations between the two epochs, in 2002 and 2003, at which this LINER 2 was observed. However, the 2500 flux ( 22 10^ 17 ). was a factor of 4.5 higher than the 2300 flux measured in 1993 with HST/FOC by Maoz et al. (1996; 5 10^ 17 ).

gmw_00000187.name: NGC 3393
gmw_00000187.overview: NGC 3393 is a face-on, early-type barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:1206.3060, arXiv:2101.11625] classified as a Seyfert 2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1206.3060, arXiv:1505.01166]. It features a Compton-thick central engine [arXiv:1505.01166, arXiv:1604.02065], large-scale and nuclear bars [arXiv:1304.4254, arXiv:1806.02756], and an S-shaped narrow-line region aligned with radio jets [arXiv:1304.4254, arXiv:1806.02756].

sources.summary: The paper interprets NGC 3393 as a merging Seyfert 2 with two central obscured AGNs, dense shocked gas, altered abundances, and possible collision-triggered star formation.

quotes.quote: Two obscured AGNs appear in the central regions of NGC 3393. They are most probably powered by mass accretion. The lower limits of the black hole mass are 8. 10 ^5 and 10 ^6 for the NE and SW sources, respectively (Fabbiano et al. 2011). Simulation results show that the massive black hole separation and the appearance of the spiral arms created by the merger, could resemble those of NGC 3393 after about five billion years from collision (Mayer et al. 2007). The new discovery turned NGC 3393 into an interesting target to test merging phenomena.

NGC 3393 is a close (z = 0.0125), bright (m _B = 13.1; de Vaucouleurs et al. 1991) Seyfert 2, classified as an early type barred galaxy, appearing face-on. It is located in the outskirts of the Hydra cluster (de Vaucouleurs et al. 1991), covering more than an arcminute on the sky. Cooke et al (2000) derived from the redshift a scale of 180 h ^ 1 pc arcsec ^ 1 (h = H _0 /100 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 ).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3393 as a Seyfert 2 with an S-shaped NLR, turbulent knot kinematics, and possible obscuration affecting the inferred kinematic center.

quotes.quote: NGC 3393 NGC 3393 is another Seyfert 2 that contains an S-shaped NLR, shown in Figure ngc3393 that shows turbulent kinematics across several knots of emission, similar to Mrk 573 and IRAS 11058-1131. Though the NLR is well resolved (distinct knots visible over several arcseconds), it is unclear whether any distinct kinematic components are present. Two sets of high velocities exist to the southwest of the nucleus, one blueshifted component peaking at 0.75 '' and one redshifted component peaking at 1.25 '' , which for a Seyfert 2 AGN would be two components for one cone to the southwest, inclined close to the plane of the sky. This is unlikely as imaging shows symmetric lobes of emission on either side of the nucleus which would not be replicated in the kinematic data.

An alternative method to fitting the kinematics is to move the central position of the nucleus southwest 0.9 '' . This would imply that the location of the continuum peak in the spectral image would not suffice as the true position of the AGN, possibly due to obscuration and reflection of the central engine. Peaks in flux to either side of the new center support the possibility of obscuration over the AGN. At this new position, the two previous high velocity peaks are now on each side of the center, combined with the extended, near-systemic velocities further out to provide four kinematic components to model.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC3393 as a face-on late-type galaxy with two prominent rings, asymmetric star-forming spiral arms, and central bar-like light associated with bulge/ring modeling.

quotes.quote: NGC 3393 NGC3393 (Figures fig:NGC3393-1 and fig:NGC3393-2 ) is a late-type galaxy that contains two prominent rings. It is probably seen face-on based on the round outer disk, which extends to 80'' ( 21 ). The large-scale disk is slightly lopsided towards 50 E of N, and features two asymmetric spiral arms with prominent star-forming regions. The arms barely connect to the galaxy center and broadly resemble a ring, which is clearly indicated by the peak in the SB profile at 40'' / 10 . The inner ring at intermediate radii ( SMA =13 / 3.5 ) is much more elongated and has a lower axis ratio than the outer disk.

The ring might be the star-forming boundary of a large-scale bar. The innermost region ( 2'' / 0.5 ) appears to be dominated by the bulge, i.e. a steep increase in surface brightness and round isophotes. It harbors yet another bar-like light distribution, with a PA 140 E of N, which is misaligned by 20 from the major axis defined by the inner ring.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 3393 as a nearby Seyfert 2 with bars and radio jets, finding rotating disk gas, jet-driven and equatorial outflows, disturbed circumnuclear spirals, and bar-driven kinematic perturbations.

quotes.quote: firstpage - lastpage NGC 3393, a nearby Seyfert 2 galaxy with nuclear radio jets, large-scale and nuclear bars, and a posited secondary super massive black hole, provides an interesting laboratory to test the physics of inflows and outflows. Here we present and analyse the molecular gas (ALMA observations of CO J:2-1 emission over a field of view (FOV) of 45 45 , at 0 56 (143 pc) spatial and 5 km/s spectral resolution), ionised gas and stars (GEMINI-GMOS/IFU; over a FOV of 4 5 , at 0 62 (159 pc) spatial and 23 km/s spectral resolution) in NGC 3393. The ionised gas emission, detected over the complete GEMINI-GMOS FOV, has three identifiable kinematic components. A narrow ( < 115 km/s) component present in the complete FOV, which is consistent with rotation in the galaxy disk. A broad ( > 115 km/s) redshifted component, detected near the NE and SW radio lobes; which we interpret as a radio jet driven outflow.

And a broad ( > 115 km/s) blueshifted component that shows high velocities in a region perpendicular to the radio jet axis; we interpret this as an equatorial outflow. The CO J:2-1 emission is detected in spiral arms on 5 20 scales, and in two disturbed circumnuclear regions. The molecular kinematics in the spiral arms can be explained by rotation.

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 3393 with HST, describing its nearby Seyfert nucleus, water maser disk, jet-ENLR interactions, Seyfert-like bicones, and newly identified LINER-like cocoon.

quotes.quote: Here we investigate the bright m_B=13.1 ; deV91 nearby face-on spiral galaxy NGC 3393 as part of CHandra survey of Extended Emission line Regions in nearby Seyfert galaxies ( CHEERS ; PI: J. Wang). NGC 3393 is a low-luminosity L_X [ 0.5-10 keV ] 10^ 41 ; Levenson06 type 2 Seyfert galaxy at z=0.0125 D=53 Mpc; Theureau98 . NGC 3393 has a water maser disk Kondratko08 , X-rays show it is Compton-thick Maiolino98 , and various studies imply L_ bol 10^ 44 \ within an order of magnitude; see Kondratko08,Winter12,Baumgartner13,Koss15 . The host galaxy has long been known to exhibit evidence for complicated jet-NLR interactions, with a significant ENLR covering much of the galaxy, dominated by S-shaped emission line ``arms" which wrap around compact sub-kpc radio jet lobes Cooke00 .

Using narrow-band HST images, we have produced a resolved BPT diagram and BPT map of a 7 7 ( 1.8 kpc square) region covering the nucleus and inner ionization cones of NGC 3393. We have shown that the regions within the bicones are typically consistent with Seyfert galaxies, consistent with previous classification of the galaxy based on the nucleus alone, while the line emission outside these regions has line surface brightness too low to categorize at full resolution. The surprise is that we find a thin LINER-like `cocoon' bounding the AGN-like region.

sources.summary: The paper adds PDS coverage for NGC 3393 and argues that its high-energy data, unacceptable flat transmission solution, and large iron-line equivalent width favor a Compton-thick interpretation.

quotes.quote: NGC 3393 The MECS data of this source were already presented in Salvati et al. ( ), in this paper we include the PDS detection in the 20--100 keV range. As discussed in Salvati et al. ( ), statistically the Compton thick, cold reflection model is only marginally better than the transmission model: the latter (N _H = 3.7 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 ) fits the data with a ^2 =1 with respect to the former, at the expense of only one extra degree of freedom. However, the data above 10 keV are reasonably consistent with cold reflection, while the transmission model falls short of accounting for the high energy data. If in the transmission model the photon index is thawed (see Appendix) the best fitting value of is negative, that is certainly not acceptable for the intrinsic spectrum.

The EW(Fe K ) is large even when the continuum is corrected for the absorbing column deduced by the transmission model. Both findings are in favor of the Compton thick interpretation (with N _H > 10^ 25 cm ^ 2 ). The warm scattering model is ruled out at a confidence level larger than 90 and, in particular, falls short of accouting for the PDS flux.

sources.summary: The paper says NGC3393 has well-constrained masses in the linear approach, low-dispersion data, failed nonlinear triplet fitting, and a disk mass similar to or above the black-hole mass.

quotes.quote: We have applied the model to 7 AGN disks whose rotation has already been probed through water maser emission. For NGC3393 and UGC3789, the masses seem well constrained through the linear approach.

Finally, for IC1481, UGC3789 and NGC3393, dispersion is less than 10 , so both mass components should be obtained with ``accuracy''. It was not possible to derive a triplet ( , , s ) for all these systems through the non linear approach (negative mass or non convergence of the fitting procedure), even for UGC3789 and NGC3393 whose data, like for IC1481, are spread over a large radial domain and little dispersed. In two cases (NGC4945 and Circinus), the method is inappropriate: no correlation really exists in the position-dynamical mass and inferred parameters are non physical (in particular, <0 for NGC4945).

In contrast, results obtained through the linear approach are satisfactory for UGC3786, NGC3393, and NGC1068 where disk mass is similar to, or higher than the mass of the central black hole. For these objects, the massive disk should be prone to instabilities.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 3393 has off-nuclear [Fe vii] excitation peaks within clumpy bicone emission, with radio-jet cavities implicated in the excitation of the hot spots.

quotes.quote: The sample employed in this work is composed of five local Seyfert 2 AGN ( z < 0.02 , where z is the redshift), widely known for displaying a low-luminosity radio-jet and a narrow line region (NLR) characterized by a bi-conical structure, previously mapped through the [ O iii ] 5007 emission. This is the case of the Circinus Galaxy, NGC 5728, IC 5063, NGC 3393 and NGC 5643. They all display [ Fe vii ] 6087 in the nuclear spectrum and a NLR that extends to at least 2 kpc from the AGN.

In NGC 3393, in addition to the nucleus, two strong peaks of emission are observed at 1 kpc NE and SW of the AGN. In IC 5063, an increase of the gas excitation is evident outside the nuclear region, at 500 pc NW and SE of the AGN. The strong relationship with the radio jet is evident in Figure fig:gals . Radio emission from the jet traces cavities that enhances [ Fe vii ] /H , suggesting a role in the excitation of these hot spots.

sources.summary: NGC 3393 has uncertain intrinsic X-ray luminosity because most broad-band flux is suppressed, though line and Fe-based corrections give roughly consistent estimates.

quotes.quote: NGC 3393 Corrections based on the Fe line and on the flux of forbidden optical \ emission yield L_ 2-10 7.2-17 10^ 42 erg s ^ 1 levenson06 . Combining \ analysis with previous \ data, guainazzi05 find a decreased column density ( 4 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 ) with respect to the complete Compton-thick suppression ( > 10^ 25 cm ^ 2 ) inferred by maiolino98 . Because only a small part of the broad-band X-ray flux is transmitted, correcting the observed continuum to estimate the intrinsic luminosity is highly uncertain given the current data quality on this source. Still, levenson06 find reasonable agreement when extrapolating the \ high-energy data to their \ bandpasses with 2 . \ may provide better estimates in the near future.

sources.summary: The paper notes that deconvolution artifacts limit detailed analysis of NGC 3393, but scaling the [O III] image removes the brightest filaments while stronger scaling leaves negative residuals.

quotes.quote: NGC 3393 (Sy2) The [ OIII ] image of this galaxy was obtained with WF/PC before COSTAR, so it had to be deconvolved. The resulting image is quite clumpy, an artifact of the deconvolution process. The artifact structures remains after convolving with the ACS PSF. So a detailed analysis, as we do with the other objects, will not be possible for this galaxy. In this case we compare the images directly, without convolving first with the PSF of the other band, taking into account that the resolution for the two images is quite similar.

sources.summary: The paper lists NGC 3393 as a reported X-ray dual-AGN system, but its Subaru AO imaging shows no secondary nucleus at the reported separation.

quotes.quote: Infrared properties of X-ray dual AGNs Among the 29 observed infrared luminous merging systems, signatures of dual AGNs with separations of > 0 5 were previously reported from X-ray observations for the following five sources: Mrk 463 bia08 , Mrk 739 kos11 , NGC 3393 fab11 , NGC 6240 kom03 , and IRAS 20210 + 1121 pic10 . Using our methods, we detect double nuclear emission in Mrk 463, Mrk 739, NGC 6240, and IRAS 20210 + 1121, but not in NGC 3393. In NGC 3393, we see no signature of a secondary nucleus at 0 6 away from the primary nucleus fab11 , although our AO images should be able to resolve both nuclei spatially.

However, no X-ray spectra are shown in this source, and the origin of the detected X-ray emission is less clear than NGC 3393. If the detected X-ray emission from both nuclei originates in luminous AGNs, one nucleus in Mrk 463, Mrk 739, NGC 3393, and IRAS 20210 + 1121 could be a hot-dust-deficient AGN, in which case the contribution from AGN-heated hot dust emission to the observed L' band flux would be small, and the observed infrared K and L' band fluxes would be dominated by nuclear stellar-origin emission.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3393 as a type 2 Seyfert with double radio structure, nuclear spiral aligned with soft X-ray emission, hard point source, and Compton-thick absorption.

quotes.quote: NGC 3393 NGC 3393 was optically classified as a type 2 Seyfert see an optical spectrum in Appendix multiimages diaz1988 . A radio counterpart was found using VLA data, the galaxy showing a double structure morganti1999 . The HST image shows a nuclear spiral structure aligned with the soft X-ray emission, where the spiral structure can also be appreciated; this emission is perpendicular to the disc emission, observed at optical wavelenghts and aligned with the IR emission (see Appendix multiimages ). A point-like source is observed at hard X-rays (see Appendix multiimages ). It is a Compton -thick object observed by BeppoSAX N_H > 10^ 25 cm^ 2 , comastri2004 .

sources.summary: The S7 notes describe NGC 3393 as a Seyfert 2 whose compact HST narrow-line region is part of a larger X-shaped ionization cone embedded in a rotating HII-region ring.

quotes.quote: NGC 3393: The HST \ image of this Seyfert 2 galaxy by Schmitt03 shows an S-shaped ENLR, extended by 5.3 arcsec (1410 pc) along P.A. = 65 deg and 2.8 arcsec (740 pc) along the perpendicular direction. In our WiFeS \ image, this is simply the inner part of a much more extended X-shaped double-sided ionization cone with enhanced intensity along the major axis. This can be traced across the full 38 25 arcsec WiFeS field. The ENLR is embedded in a faint ring of \ regions with rotation. The \ regions in this galaxy have been catalogued by Tsvetanov95 .

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 3393's ALMA components with three known radio spots, noting a missing NE mm-wave counterpart and different nuclear emission behavior.

quotes.quote: 45: NGC 3393 This object is known to have three radio spots in NE, nuclear, and SW areas e.g., Coo00,Kos15,Mak17,Fin18 , and our ALMA image at a resolution of 0 3 shows two significant components: a in the nucleus and b in the SW direction. Thus, mm-wave emission spatially corresponding to the NE radio spot is undetected. At a similar resolution of 0 2, estimated the 8.5 GHz flux densities for the SW, nuclear, and NE components to be 1 mJy, 1 mJy, and 8 mJy, respectively. On the other hand, their mm-wave flux densities per beam are 0.1 mJy beam ^ 1 ( 5 _ mm upper limit), 0.2 mJy beam ^ 1 , and 0.4 mJy beam ^ 1 , respectively. If the SW and NE radio components have similar spectral indices of 0.7--0.9, as inferred from a study of , the non-detection of the NE component in the ALMA image is reasonable.

sources.summary: BETIS finds NGC3393 has the showcase sample’s highest DIG fraction, Seyfert-like line ratios, and emission consistent with fast shocks and AGN outflows.

quotes.quote: The subsample includes 3 known AGNs: NGC863 and NGC3393 are Seyfert 2 1977ARA&A..15...69W, 1988SoSAO..55....5L , and NGC692 which is a Low-Luminosity AGN (LLAGN; 2019ApJ...872..134Z ). NGC3393 exhibits the highest DIG fraction for both lower and upper limits (0.69-0.87), presenting a surface brightness that surpasses the regions, throughout the entire galaxy, resulting in a maximum difference of around 1 dex. However, when performing this diagnosis for individual galaxies, only NGC863 and NGC3393, both Seyfert-2 AGNs, exhibit line ratios characteristic of AGN emission.

In the case of NGC3393, the ratios observed corresponds to fast-shock without precursor with v _s between 200 and 1000 km/s and B between 0.0001 and 10. Furthermore, the fact that the ionisation bi-cone of NGC3393 and its continuum emission are uncoupled 2016ApJ...829...46M , along with the emission line ratios in the BPT diagram indicative of ionisation by fast shocks, suggests that we are tracing gas outflows rather than DIG emission 2020AJ....159..167L .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3393 as a barred Seyfert 2 galaxy with radio jets, possible nuclear complexity, patchy molecular gas, and continuum sources tied to the nucleus and jet.

quotes.quote: NGC 3393 NGC 3393 is an SBa galaxy 1991rc3..book.....D at an adopted distance D=49 8 Mpc.This distance was again estimated from the Local Group-centric redshift 1996AJ....111..794K by Kormendy+2013ARAA51.511 and Saglia+2016ApJ818.47 assuming the cosmology derived from WMAP 5-year data 2009ApJS..180..330K . NGC 3393 has a large-scale stellar bar ( PA_ mor 160 ), extended radio jets e.g. Cooke+2000ApJS129.517 and a Seyfert 2 nucleus Baumgartner+2013ApJS207.19 . A nuclear bar ( PA_ mor 145 ) has also been posited e.g. Lasker+2016ApJ825.3 . The presence of two compact X-ray sources separated by 130 pc suggests there are two SMBHs in the nuclear region Fabbiano+2011Nature477.431 , but these may be due to noise Koss+2015ApJ807.149 and subsequent radio, near-infrared, optical, UV and hard X-ray observations are all consistent with a single point source 2014ApJ...780..106I, Koss+2015ApJ807.149 . H _2 O megamasers were discovered using the National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA) Deep Space Network Kondratko+2006ApJ638.100 , and mapped with very long baseline interferometry (VLBI) to infer a central SMBH mass M_ BH =(3.0 0.2) 10^7 M _ at our adopted distance above Kondratko+2008ApJ678.87 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3393 as heavily obscured, with thermal components in its nuclear and central spectra and unusually distributed soft X-ray flux.

quotes.quote: They fit the 1--80 keV spectrum of NGC 3393 to find N_H > 10^ 25 .

The best-fitting models of the NGC 3393 nuclear and central spectra each include a thermal component. At this temperature, ( kT 1.6 keV), the collisional excitation results in a number of emission lines around 1 keV, where Fe L transitions are also strong. We considered adding to the unresolved lines a broad Gaussian emission feature, to account for the many Fe L lines, instead of the thermal component. The model with the thermal component fits the data much better than this alternate purely descriptive model.
NGC 3393 is exceptional; here less than 40 of the soft flux is contained within the nucleus, and the larger ( 1700 770 pc) central area accounts for the plurality of this galaxy's soft emission.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3393 as one of three anomalous AGN with unusually high [CII]/PAH ratios, reliable [CII] detections, and weak PAH emission not explained by measurement error.

quotes.quote: Among all of the sources in Figures 5 and 6, only three appear anomalous. These are all AGN and have the largest ratios f( [ CII ] )/f(PAH) - sources 3C 120, Mrk 590, and NGC 3393 in Table 1 - with f( [ CII ] )/f(PAH) about a factor of ten greater than the median. All have strong and reliable [ CII ] detections with no unusual profiles or evidence of spatial mismatches or source extension. The PAH 11.3 feature is weak in all three, but independent CASSIS spectra yield fluxes consistent to within 30 , so there is no indication that the anomalous weakness of PAH is a measurement error.

sources.summary: NGC 3393 is an SBa Seyfert 2 galaxy dominated by old populations, with spiral arms containing young and old stars, scattered or young featureless-continuum-like light, and enhanced inner reddening and metallicity.

quotes.quote: NGC 3393 This galaxy is classified as SBa, harboring a Seyfert 2 nucleus. According to our analysis, this object is dominated by old SPs, with two very distinct spiral arms composed of a combination of old and young SPs. In the same region of these arms, a slight contribution from a FC ( 10 ) was detected, which may be associated with light emitted by the AGN and scattered by the arms, or may be actually due to the contribution from very young SPs, whose spectrum is similar to that from an FC. Also, the inner 1 5 has a higher dust reddening ( 1.2 mag), stellar ( 180 km s ^ 1 ) metallicity ( <Z> 0.03 ), if compared to the rest of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 3393 has an S-shaped narrow-line region, a bar and possible ring, a nuclear bar, and alignment with the radio jet and megamaser axis.

quotes.quote: As an extreme example, the famous S-shaped emission region in NGC 3393 is detected weakly in the F160W image, but has no impact on the measured PA, which is nearly orthogonal to the orientation of the narrow-line region.
NGC 3393 also shows a bar and maybe a ring on 20 \ scales, and then has what appears to be a nuclear bar on 3 \ scales.

However, from the masked (grey) pixels in Figure fig:disk , we can see that the spectacular S-shaped narrow-line region in NGC 3393 does align with the radio jet and the megamaser rotation axis e.g., cookeetal2000,kondratkoetal2008 , and this is seen in Circinus as well greenhilletal2003 .

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 3393's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 3393 is a face-on barred spiral galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0125 ( D 61.6 Mpc) with a close binary AGN (separation 0.5 150 pc; PA 40 ; fabbiano_close_2011 ), which optically appears as a Sy 2 veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 . The binary AGN is presumably the result of a minor merger, which did not significantly affect the host morphology (see also contini_evidence_2012 ). Both are surrounded by an S-shaped NLR extending 1.8 0.4 kpc on either side along a PA 55 schmitt_comparison_1996,cooke_narrow-line_2000 coinciding with a double-sided jet along a PA 50 morganti_radio_1999,schmitt_jet_2001 .

sources.summary: The paper records NGC 3393's adopted photometric profile or decomposition, giving bulge/scale parameters and comparing those choices with alternative literature fits for the structural analysis.

quotes.quote: NGC 3393: We perfomed an Imfit bulge (function Sersic ) plus bar (function Sersic GenEllipse ) plus disk (function Exponential ) decomposition (see Fig. fig_ngc3393 and fig_imfitiso ) on an I- band image observed with the 0.9 m CTIO telescope by Schmitt2000 . The parameters of the decomposition are given in Table tab:3393decomp . The M/L is computed as in Appendix C to match the velocity dispersion of Greene2010 within an effective aperture of radius of 1.6'' , using the distance of KormendyHo2013 .

sources.summary: The spectropolarimetry paper treats NGC 3393 as a previously uncertain NHBLR object and reports its first polarized spectrum, revealing clear broad Balmer components.

quotes.quote: The origin of the classification of NGC 3393 as a NHBLR is uncertain, and indeed Kay02 reported the detection of scattered broad H , but did not publish the data either.

For NGC 3393, here we report the first polarized spectrum of the galaxy, which reveals clear broad H and H components of 5000 km s ^ 1 . This broad component is also detected in H , but the emission line is too faint to obtain a reliable fit (see Fig. fig1 ).

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 3393 as an SBab Compton-thick AGN with radio lobes and ionization cones, finding elongated cone-region morphology and extended hard X-ray emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 3393 NGC 3393 is an SBab type galaxy at RA=10:48:23.46 (162.098 ), Dec=-25:09:43.4 (-25.162 ), and z=0.01251 (D _ lum 61 Mpc), with a triple-lobed radio source and extended ionization cones oriented along the north-east direction (e.g., Coo00 ).
NGC 3393 NGC 3393 is elongated in the ``cone'' region (Figure fig:grid:3393 ) and continues to have an azimuthal dependence even in the hard X-rays, although to a lesser extent.
This is further evidence of extended hard X-ray emission in NGC 3393, as first suggested in Mak17 and later in Mak19 , and consistent with wind-driven shocks and collisionally excited gas.

gmw_00000188.name: NGC 3486
gmw_00000188.overview: NGC 3486 is an intermediate-mass spiral galaxy [arXiv:1605.01638, arXiv:2303.00763] hosting a borderline Seyfert 2 or LINER nucleus [arXiv:1310.2770, arXiv:1508.05065]. Its structure features stream-like neutral hydrogen extensions [arXiv:1605.01638], a central gas depression [arXiv:1605.01638], and an unusually high number of gas-containing satellite candidates [arXiv:2303.00763].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3486 as an apparently unabsorbed Seyfert 2 whose hard X-ray excess and reflection/transmission fits indicate Compton-thick obscuration despite no observed variability.

quotes.quote: NGC 3486 The XMM-Newton spectrum of NGC 3486 is well fitted by a power-law absorbed by the Galactic column only and so appears to be another unabsorbed Seyfert 2 galaxy. However, an excess at hard energies points to a different scenario. The spectrum is also well fitted by the addition of a Compton reflection model ( pexmon , ) with an underlying thermal component ( raymond ), so this could in fact be a Compton thick Seyfert 2 which looks unabsorbed.

We also attempt to add a strongly absorbed transmission component to the model, which also improves the fit from a simple power-law. However, the column density of this component is poorly constrained, so we fix this to an arbitrary log( N_ H ) = 24.5. Estimates of the intrinsic L_ HX from the reflection component predict a L_ Bol which agrees well with L_ Bol estimated from L_ O III , suggesting that NGC 3486 is under-luminous in X-rays due to Compton thick obscuration. Finally, there is no evidence for variability of the X-ray flux in the XMM-Newton observation.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 3486 as an LLAGN host, fitting its absorbed X-ray spectrum and reporting a marginal point-like radio counterpart coincident with the optical core.

quotes.quote: NGC 3486 is found at a distance of 16.7 Mpc 2007A&A...465...71T . Again the source spectrum was fitted with an absorbed power law. The source unabsorbed luminosity was 1.01 10^ 39 erg s ^ 1 in the 0.5-10 keV range.

A marginal 4 detection is obtained for the radio counterpart of NGC 3486 by combining the two frequency bands and using natural weighting. The source is point-like and coincident with the optical core of the galaxy. We measure a flux density of 26.6 6.4 Jy.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3486 as a borderline Seyfert/LINER nucleus with uncertain power source and inconsistent X-ray luminosity estimates, making the Compton-thick scenario doubtful.

quotes.quote: NGC 3486 NGC 3486 hosts a borderline Sy 2/LINER nucleus with uncertain power source. The nucleus remaine undetected in a Chandra snapshot survey ho_detection_2001 and only appears in the much deeper data cappi_x-ray_2006 . While favours a Compton-thick obscuration scenario for the source, provide only an unobscured fit to the data without further discussion. The luminosity estimates range from 39.63 41.5 depending on the assumed model. The subarcsecond scale MIR upper limit together with the MIR--X-ray correlation predicts 40.47 , which is inconsistent with the CT scenario if NGC 3486 is indeed AGN powered.

sources.summary: The paper reports stream-like HI features, central gas depression, compact molecular gas, warped neutral-gas kinematics, and disc-dominated stellar light in the SABc galaxy NGC 3486.

quotes.quote: NGC 3486 New VLA HI observations of the SABc galaxy NGC 3486 presented in Fig.  fig:n3486sum show stream-like features extending from the northwest and southeast sides of the HI disc. These features are low column-density, but appear to follow the rotation curve of the disc. There is no obvious nearby companion or signature of a recent merger. In the central region, the HI mass surface density shows a depression in the central 40 arcsec, then remains constant before decreasing exponentially at 180 arcsec. The molecular gas distribution is confined to the central 40 arcsec, but is only dominant within 6 arcsec, as inferred from a CO map from the CARMA STING survey.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3486 as an intermediate-mass active spiral with unusually many gas-containing satellite candidates, though several lack clear optical counterparts.

quotes.quote: NGC 3486 NGC 3486 is an SAB() galaxy hosting an x-ray faint active nucleus annuar_nustar_2020 , also classified as a Type 2 Seyfert ho_search_1997 . With a halo mass M_ Halo 4 10^ 11 M_ , it is of intermediate mass in our host sample, a factor of three less massive compared with the MW. NGC 3486 appears unique in its abundance of gas-containing satellites (Figure fig:count_comprehensive ). It has six candidates within R_ 200 that are close to the host in both the velocity and position space with relatively high F_ d_ 3D . But we identify this high R_ 200 satellite number as a potential outlier because 4/6 of the R_ 200 candidates are missing clear optical counterparts -- AGC 208569, 722731, 219369, and 212945.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 3486's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 3486 is a face-on spiral galaxy at a distance of D= 13.7 2.7 Mpc tully_extragalactic_2009 with a borderline Sy 2.0/LINER nucleus, which might be powered by a compact central star cluster instead of an accreting SMBH maoz_low-luminosity_2007 . It has not been detected at radio wavelengths ho_detection_2001 but in the X-rays, where it appears rather heavily absorbed brightman_nature_2008 . The first attempt to detect NGC 3486 in the MIR failed rieke_10_1978 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3486 as a borderline Seyfert/LINER, noting radio and X-ray nondetections, nonvariable UV flux, and possible AGN-dominated UV emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 3486 - In this bordeline Seyfert/LINER nucleus, no radio cores at 6 cm and 20 cm were detected at a VLA resolution of 1'' by Ho Ulvestad (2001), to a 3 limit of 0.12 mJy beam ^ 1 . In the UV monitoring by M05, this source displayed neither short-term changes between the two, closely spaced (by 1 month), epochs in which it was observed, nor long-term variations when comparing the 2500 flux to a 2300 measurement in 1993 with the HST/FOC by Maoz et al. (1996). The 2500 and 3300 flux levels are 10.8 10^ 17 , and 18.2 10^ 17 , respectively.

gmw_00000189.name: NGC 3521
gmw_00000189.overview: NGC 3521 is a highly inclined, flocculent spiral galaxy [arXiv:2203.02522, arXiv:1003.1751] classified optically as a borderline H II/LINER active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1310.2770]. It features a weakly barred morphology [arXiv:2302.14800], a massive diffuse halo of slow-rotating gas [arXiv:1312.2399], and a stellar mass comparable to the Milky Way [arXiv:2306.09854].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3521 as an anomalous inclined spiral with unusually scattered pixel SEDs, complex dust attenuation and scattering, and mixed star-formation, age, and dust effects.

quotes.quote: The case of NGC 3521 is opposite and much more complicated. First of all, the scatter in SED from pixel to pixel is evidently larger than in any other galaxy. The two main PCs of this galaxy contribute very similar amount of variance (54 and 40 respectively), at odds with the fact that PC1 is clearly the dominant contributor to the variance in all other galaxies. PC1 appears to be more closely correlated to optical colours than PC2, which, as opposed, has stronger correlations with the IR bands; however PC1 and PC2 do not separate optical and IR in a clear way. The spatial distribution of PC1 and PC2 intensity (Fig. fig_PCA_N03521 ) shows a surprising result.

In the inner parts PC1 is approximately axissymmetric and reflects in part the spiral structure of the galaxy; beyond 50 arcsec, the symmetry is broken, with the western part of the galaxy displaying more intense, positive PC1, and the eastern part having mainly negative PC1. By looking at the colour-composite giH image of this galaxy (see ZCR09, Fig. 5), we see that this reflects the differential attenuation and the scattering of light in the inclined disc. Thick dust lanes on the near side (to the west) strongly attenuate all optical wavelengths, and even attenuate the H band as well, meaning that our normalization will be biased for all these pixels.

sources.summary: NGC 3521 is presented as a neighboring Sbc galaxy with dwarf satellites, a shallow potential well, low dark-halo mass, and a declining outer rotation curve.

quotes.quote: As can be seen from the last row of Table 3, a bright ( B = 9.8 mag) neighboring Sbc galaxy NGC 3521 with a radial velocity V_h = 737 km s ^ 1 is located at a projected separation of 17 deg or 3.0 Mpc from NGC 3115. The radial distances of NGC 3521 (10.7 Mpc) and NGC 3115 (10.2 Mpc) practically coincide with each other within their measurement errors. According to Karachentsev Kashibadze (2021), NGC 3521 has 6 assumed satellites, of which radial velocities were measured for three. We searched for new dwarf satellites using DECaLS data (Dey et al. 2019). In an area of 4 x 4 degrees (i.e. 750 x 750 kpc), we found four new likely satellites of low surface brightness.

The average projected separation of the satellites, R_p = 160 38 kpc, and their rms velocity relative to NGC 3521, V^2 ^ 1/2 = 55 km s ^ 1 , turn out to be significantly less than that of the NGC 3115 satellites. The average estimate of the total mass M_T = (0.90 0.42) 10^ 12 M_ and the ratio M_T/L_K = (7 3) M_ /L_ indicate a shallow potential well of this galaxy. The rotation curve of NGC 3521, which decreases with distance at periphery (Casertano van Gorkom 1991), also indicates a low mass of the dark halo of this spiral galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3521 as a well-observed spiral SINGS case study, fitting its UV-to-infrared SED and placing it at the low-activity end of the comparison sequence.

quotes.quote: To investigate the implications of these fits for the determination of physical parameters, we focus on three galaxies spanning different dust properties along the / versus / colour-colour relation in Fig. irx_beta a, for which a wide set of observations are available: NGC 3521, a spiral (SABbc) galaxy at a distance of 9 Mpc; NGC 337, a spiral (SBd) galaxy at a distance of 24.7 Mpc; and Mrk 33, a dwarf irregular (Im) starburst galaxy at a distance of 21.7 Mpc. For all three galaxies, observations from GALEX ( FUV and NUV ), 2MASS ( JHKs ), Spitzer (IRAC and MIPS), IRAS and SCUBA are available, along with integrated \ and \ spectroscopy from . For NGC 3521 and NGC 337, additional constraints are available from RC3 ( UBV ) and ISO (ISOCAM 6.75 and 15 ) photometry. Fig. seds2 shows the models (in black) providing the best fits to the observed ultraviolet, optical, \ and , and infrared spectral energy distributions of these three galaxies (in red). The quality of the fits is remarkable, as quantified by the residuals shown at the bottom of each spectrum.

We find that, as anticipated from Fig. seds2 , the transition from NGC 3521 to NGC 337 to Mrk 33 corresponds to a combined rise in specific star formation rate ( ) and dust temperature ( \ and ), a larger contribution to the total infrared luminosity by warm dust ( ) relative to cold dust ( ), and by stellar birth clouds relative to the ambient ISM ( ), and a weakening of PAH features ( ). These results are consistent with the finding by , on the basis of a more sophisticated physical dust model, that the intensity of the dust-heating starlight increases, and the fraction of dust mass contributed by PAHs decreases, from NGC 3521 to NGC 337 to Mrk 33.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3521 as the closest Milky Way twin candidate, with similar optical radius, stellar mass, black-hole mass, rotation curve, and radial oxygen abundance distribution.

quotes.quote: The galaxy NGC 3521 is an SABb galaxy (morphological type code T = 4.0 0.2). It is included in The H i Nearby Galaxy Survey (THINGS) list Walter2008 . We adopted the following parameters of NGC 3521 used or obtained by the THINGS survey: distance d = 10.7 Mpc; \ optical radius of 4.16 arcmin; physical optical radius R_ 25 = 12.94 kpc Walter2008 ; inclination angle i = 73 ; position angle of the major axis PA = 340 deBlok2008 ; stellar mass M_ = 5.01 10 ^ 10 M _ , or log( M_ /M_ ) = 10.70 Leroy2008 ; and rotation velocity V_ rot = 227 km s ^ 1 deBlok2008,Leroy2008 . The black hole mass in NGC 3521 is log( M_ BH /M_ ) = 6.85 0.58 Davis2014 .

Thus, the values of the optical radii of NGC 3521 ( R_ 25 = 12.9 kpc) and the Milky Way ( R_ 25 = 12.0 kpc) are close to each other. The value of the stellar mass of the NGC 3521 (log( M_ /M_ ) = 10.7) is close to the value of the stellar mass of the Milky Way (log( M_ /M_ ) = 10.716). The mass of the black hole at the centre of NGC 3521 (log( M_ BH /M_ ) = 6.85) is close to that in the Milky Way (log( M_ BH /M_ ) = 6.62).

sources.summary: The paper keeps NGC 3521 in its analysis despite perturbed kinematics, noting a regular morphology, disc-dominated central rotation, and reduced dark matter within the inner disc.

quotes.quote: NGC 3521 is a spiral galaxy in the constellation Leo. The rotation curve of the galaxy NGC 3521 shows a general decrease in the velocity of about 40 km/s (from 235 km/s at  155 arcsec to 200 km/s at  600 arcsec). This drop is created mainly by a sharp jump at about R 320  arcsec. The gap may be related to a very perturbed and asymmetric kinematics (e.g. Casertano_vanGorkom1991 ). This is confirmed by other authors deBlok+2008,DiTeodoro_Peek2021 and by our major-axis position-velocity diagram.
Casertano_vanGorkom1991 suggested that this galaxy experienced some interaction. However, the galaxy has the regular morphology.

sources.summary: The paper notes NGC 3521 has out-of-model color pixels, possible blue-halo systematics, and dusty-disk reflection that creates asymmetry in its reconstructed mass map.

quotes.quote: Few pixels have colours are not covered by models, in NGC 4552, NGC 3521 and NGC 5713. Their M/L ratio must be derived via extrapolation (minimum surface curvature fitting).
We can speculate that large-angle PSF wings from scattering in i (and g , but not in H ) band cause the ``blue'' i-H halo around NGC 4552 (and possibly the blue ``halo'' around NGC 3521 and 5713).

Although this assumption is generally reasonable, there are cases where it fails, such as NGC 3521 (Fig. fig_sample_RGB ): it is immediate to see that the far part of the dusty disk (on the East side) reflects back the light of the bright inner regions of the small bulge and the disk. This results in the artificial asymmetry of the reconstructed mass map of Fig.

sources.summary: The paper derives center estimates and harmonic-decomposition results for NGC 3521, describing its disk morphology, non-circular motions, and whether the inferred potential is consistent with being round.

quotes.quote: NGC 3521 NGC 3521 (Fig. fig:ngc-3521 ) a) Center estimates [ 1.5ex ] NGC 3521 appears in the 3.6 m IRAC image as well as in the \ map as a multi-armed, flocculent spiral galaxy. The IRAC image shows an inner disk, in which a strong nuclear point source (without a counterpart in the radio continuum) is embedded. We use the position of the nuclear point source ( = 8 8, = 5 7) as input for a \ fit with all parameters left free. The top row of Fig.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3521 as a highly inclined flocculent spiral with a ring-like structure, high gas mass, extended disk, and JCMT CO observations of its molecular gas.

quotes.quote: NGC 3521 is a highly inclined flocculent spiral galaxy (type SAB(rs)bc) that shows a ring-like structure hel03 . It is associated with the nearby Leo Triplet (of which another of our galaxies, NGC 3627, is a member). It has a moderately high mass 8 10^9 , wal08 , the highest of these three galaxies, and the disk extends well beyond the stellar disk. The adopted distance to NGC 3521 is 10.7 Mpc Hubble flow distance using the velocity listed on NED wal08 .

The HARP-B line (rest frequency 345.79 GHz) observations for the three galaxies we are examining here, NGC 628, NGC 3521, and NGC 3627, were taken over multiple runs between November 2007 and March 2008 as part of the JLS program.

sources.summary: The paper places this THINGS galaxy in Group B, where BDM fits outperform NFW, imply r_c roughly comparable to r_s, and favor a strong dark-matter core.

quotes.quote: In the second case, Group B, (IC 2574 (B1), NGC 2366 (B2), NGC 2903 (B3), NGC 2976 (B4), NGC 3198 (B5), NGC 3521 (B6), and NGC 925 (B7)), we get a fitted value of r_c r_s and BDM profile fits better than NFW. In fact, the NFW profile in some cases over-predicts the velocity in the inner parts of the galaxy or does fit with unphysical values for r_s > O (10^4) or _0 < O (10^3) M_ /kpc^ 3 , see Table tab:onlydm_bdm and tab:onlydm_nfw . The BDM profile gives r_c r_s for all stellar mass models, having an average r_c value of 6 and 5 kpc for Min.Disk and Min.Disk+Gas, respectively. Since r_c r_s , implying that our BDM profile is of the type (r_c + )^ 3 and hence having a strong core behavior. For this set of galaxies the Burkert and ISO profiles, as well as the BDM, fit much better than NFW, indicating that a core is needed.

sources.summary: The paper decomposes NGC 3521 HI into regular and anomalous components, finding a massive slow-rotating diffuse halo component likely connected to a galactic fountain.

quotes.quote: A study is presented of \ line observations of the nearby spiral galaxy NGC 3521 observed with the VLA as part of The \ Nearby Galaxy Survey. Clearly evident in the \ data cube is the presence of an anomalous \ component that is both diffuse and slow-rotating. The data cube is dynamically decomposed into regular and anomalous \ components. A mass of M_ HI =1.5 10^9 \ is estimated for the anomalous \ 20 per cent of the total \ mass. Standard \ data products and rotation curves are produced for each dynamical component.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 3521 contains substantial molecular gas, uses THINGS and HERACLES/THINGS rotation curves, and obtains improved halo fits when molecular gas is included.

quotes.quote: NGC 3521 In Figures fig:N3521-NFW and fig:N3521-ISO we plot the predicted, model and best fitting rotation curves for NGC 3521. For NGC 3521 we use a single component stellar decomposition. The HERACLES rotation curve is steeper within 4 . We therefore consider two observed rotation curves when fitting ISO and NFW models - a THINGS rotation curve and a HERACLES/THINGS rotation where we use the HERACLES rotation curve within 4 and the THINGS rotation curves for larger radii. NGC 3521 contains a considerable amount of molecular gas in comparison to the other galaxies in this sample.

sources.summary: The model gives NGC 3521 the sample’s highest value because CO(2-1) emission is underestimated; its outer-disk velocity dispersion rises sharply and disagrees strongly with observed profiles.

quotes.quote: With a value of 270 , NGC 3521 shows the highest (including only ) of the whole galaxy sample. This is mainly due to an underestimation of the model CO(2-1) integrated line emission.
The model velocity dispersion profiles are approximately flat except for those of NGC 3521 and NGC 7331 where the velocity dispersion suddenly increases by 8 km s ^ 1 in the outer disk.

We observe the strongest discrepancy between our velocity dispersion profiles and the observed profiles for NGC 3521 and NGC 7331. In these galaxies, the velocity dispersion increases sharply at R 6 7 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 3521 has ordered spiral magnetic fields, a polarized-intensity minimum in its inner disk, and star formation distributed more uniformly through the center.

quotes.quote: To check this hypothesis we observed two flocculent spirals, NGC 3521 and NGC 5055, in the radio continuum (total power and polarization) at 10.55 GHz with a resolution of 1 13 . A search for traces of optical spiral patterns has also been made by observing them in the H line and by filtering their available blue images. NGC 3521 and NGC 5055 were found to possess a mean degree of magnetic field ordering similar to that in grand-design spirals. However, the polarized emission fills the central region of NGC 5055 while a minimum of polarized intensity was observed in the inner disk of NGC 3521. This can be explained by a more uniform star formation distribution in the centre of NGC 3521, while a higher concentration of star-forming activity in the nuclear region and in the rudimentary spiral ``armlets'' of NGC 5055 leaves broader interarm regions with unperturbed regular magnetic fields.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3521 as a highly tilted Sbc galaxy with nuclear H absorption, possible past starburst activity, central CO depletion, and flocculent radio structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 3521 . This highly tilted Sbc galaxy shows strong and broad H absorption around the nucleus. This absorption component is most likely caused by a stellar absorption line of the central bulge component, characteristic of A-type stars. In order to have such a bright A-type star cluster, a large-scale starburst may have occurred during the last 10^9 yr sofue98 . The high-resolution CO image shows a pronounced lack of emission at the center. Our high-resolution radio image (Fig. figura1a ) shows the spiral galaxy with its flocculent structure, and a strong background radio source to the southwest.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 3521 prefers a distance below the measured mean, while still allowing sensible M/L and constraining the stellar scale-height near 0.25 kpc.

quotes.quote: NGC 3521 For NGC 3521, the preference is for a much lower distance than measured, which can be seen in the contour plot of Fig fig:com3521 . This is not necessarily out of the question since the error on its distance is 30 , but the ^2 minimum is actually further from the mean than this. The parameters of the two models plotted against the observed rotation curve in the top left panel are given in table tab:par and correspond to a distance more than 1 lower than the mean and roughly 0.5 lower than the mean. Both require sensible M/L and the stellar scale-height is constrained to be close to 0.25 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper finds that NGC 3521 still disagrees with MOND when the EFE is free, although a sizable external field improves the rotation-curve fit.

quotes.quote: (6) NGC 3521 : Given no significant change in the reduced ^2 , the disagreement between the MOND rotation curve and the observed one remained even when the external field is taken as a free parameter in the fitting process.
