But as is shown in Figure 2 and Table 1 the existence of an external field with a magnitude of about 0.645^ +0.760 _ 0.645 a_0 gives us a better RC compared to the fit without an EFE. SDSS J110440.08+000329.6 with luminosity of L_V=2.8 ( 0.58) 10^ 7 L_ n3521-jj at an angular distance of 0.1707^0 and a corresponding radial distance of about 1.2 Mpc (maximum 9.8 Mpc and minimum 21.5 kpc) to NGC 3521 n3031-dm, n3521-p, n3521-lu, n3521-md is a good candidate to be the source of the external field. This galaxy can produce an external field of a_e=3.37 10^ 3 a_0 with a maximum and minimum value of a_e=1.82 10^ 2 a_0 and a_e=4.12 10^ 5 a_0 at the location of NGC 3521.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 3521 has many bimodal line profiles and a declining fitted rotation curve, but its axisymmetric fit leaves coherent residuals.

quotes.quote: NGC 3521 Large parts of the velocity map of this galaxy have bimodal line profiles, possibly suggesting that emission is both from the disc and a stream of extra-planar gas. We have excluded pixels having bimodal line profiles and fit an axisymmetric model that has a declining rotation curve. Our fit, Figure  fig.N3521 , is in reasonable agreement with that from dB08, but our uncertainties are smaller, probably because we excluded pixels having bimodal profiles. The axisymmetric fit is hardly satisfactory because the velocity difference maps reveal areas of large coherent residuals, although we were unable to discern a coherent pattern to them.

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC 3521 as an extreme high-h3/h4 case once suspected to host counter-rotation, but spectral decomposition showed co-rotating bulge and disk components and a resolution artifact.

quotes.quote: The most extreme case is NGC 3521, which shows |h_3| and |h_4| as large as 0.24 and 0.35, respectively see also zeilingerNGC3521Stellar2001 . For this reason it is thought to host a counter-rotating component; however, by applying a spectral decomposition, coccatoSpectroscopicDecompositionNGC2018 found that the two distinct components are actually a bulge and disk in co-rotation. They explained the counter-rotation as an artifact due to spurious signals that are generated in the Fourier space when the data have a poor spectral resolution. zeilingerNGC3521Stellar2001 and fabriciusKINEMATICSIGNATURESBULGES2012 used the Fourier correlation quotient method benderUnravelingKinematicsEarlytype1990,bender1994 for the LOSVD recovery of NGC 3521.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3521 as a flocculent weakly barred spiral comparable to NGC 5194; SCUBA-2 recovers extended structure but not clear spiral arms.

quotes.quote: NGC 3521 NGC 3521, shown in Figure fig:ngc3521 is a flocculent, weakly-barred spiral galaxy with a tightly-wound two-arm pattern liu2011 . NGC 3521 is considered comparable to NGC 5194 (discussed below), as both are metal-rich and quiescently star-forming liu2011 . We recover a significant amount of the extended structure of NGC 3521, although the spiral arms are not clearly visible in Figure fig:ngc3521 . HARP ^ 12 CO J=3 2 observations of NGC 3521 were presented by wilson2012 .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 3521 as a flocculent spiral with small geometric fluctuations, low inner radial velocity, and rough anisotropic streaming motions in the outer disk.

quotes.quote: NGC 3521 NGC 3521 is a flocculent spiral galaxy . The TRM measurements of the inclination angle and P.A. show small fluctuations, <10^ , across the disk (see Fig. NGC3521 ). Due to these moderate variations, we find that v_t(R) v_c(R) .

sources.summary: The paper estimates infrared dust-heating parameters for NGC 3521 and notes that including SCUBA flux makes simultaneous MIPS and SCUBA photometry difficult to reproduce.

quotes.quote: NGC 3521: From Figure fig:IRAC4/MIPS band ratios g, we find 4.6 . From Figure fig:MIPS band ratios g, we obtain 1.1 and 0.007 . SCUBA fluxes are also available for this galaxy Dale+Bendo+Engelbracht_etal_2005 ; when the 850 \ flux is used to constrain the grain model, Draine+Dale+Bendo_etal_2006 find that it is difficult to simultaneously reproduce the MIPS and SCUBA photometry. The overall best fit is found for =2 , =0.01 .

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 3521's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 3521 is a nearby highly-inclined spiral galaxy at a distance of D= 11.5 2.8 Mpc (NED redshift-independent median) with a little-studied active nucleus, classified optically as borderline H II/LINER ho_search_1997-1 . A nuclear compact source was detected in X-rays zhang_census_2009 , while no subarcsecond-resolution radio observations have been published. The first observed in the MIR by but not detected, while report a marginal detection.

sources.summary: The decomposition comparison flags NGC3521 for discrepant u-band measurements, a high fitted bulge index among Sb-Sc galaxies, and high-inclination dust structure that complicates two-component fitting.

quotes.quote: Our results are similar to tex:M04 , with M < 0.5 mag, except for the u-band measurements of NGC2841, NGC3521, NGC4274 and NGC4303.
The five Sb--Sc galaxies with n_ b >4 are NGC5430, NGC2841, NGC3521, NGC3642 and NGC4698, with n_ b = 4.2 , 5.7 , 6.7 , 6.8 and 7.1 , respectively.
NGC3521 SAB(rs)bc A high-inclination galaxy with strong dust features that make it extremely difficult to fit a two-component model.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 3521 as an uncertain LINER AGN with only limited X-ray information and an MIR-X-ray upper limit on any intrinsic AGN luminosity.

quotes.quote: NGC 3521 list the observed 0.3-8 keV flux for the uncertain LINER AGN in NGC 3521 using the deeper of the two observations, unfortunately without providing any further details on the X-ray analysis. We assume a power-law with = 1.7 to calculate an = 38.67 . The upper limit on the intrinsic X-ray luminosity of any AGN in NGC 3521 is 40.61 as predicted from the MIR--X-ray correlation.

sources.summary: The paper excludes NGC 3521 from bar and arm environmental subpopulations and finds its cloud mass distribution strongly favors a truncated power law with an unusually high completeness limit.

quotes.quote: GMCs from galaxies without arms or bars (NGC 3521, NGC 3621, NGC 4826) are not included in any of these sub-populations of GMCs.
The BIC indicates a very strong preference BIC >10 , bayes-factor for the truncated power-law mass distribution in NGC 0628, NGC 2903, NGC 3521, and NGC 3627.

In NGC 3521, the truncated power-law shows a completeness limit 30 times larger than the truncation mass M_c , a byproduct of the model finding a solution to explain the large mass range where the PDF is flat.

sources.summary: NGC 3521 is described as an inclined, flocculent barred spiral whose H II-region gradients are well represented by a single radial fit.

quotes.quote: The TYPHOON Survey The six galaxies in this study --- NGC 1566, NGC 2835, NGC 3521, NGC 5068, NGC 5236, and NGC 7793 --- are drawn from the TYPHOON survey. The H signal to noise image of each galaxy are shown in Figure fig:galaxies . The TYPHOON data are constructed using a Progressive Integral Step Method (PrISM), establishing a 3D data cube where each spatial pixel (spaxel) occupies a spectrum for that pixel. A long-slit spectrum is captured and then the slit is moved along spatially, generating multiple slices to form into a cube. Details about the TYPHOON Program and long-slit data reduction techniques will be presented in a forthcoming paper (M. Seibert et al., in preparation). Here, we provide a short summary, focusing on aspects immediately relevant to this study. The TYPHOON galaxies (PI and Team Leader, B.F. Madore) were observed using the Wide-Field CCD imaging spectrograph on the 2.5m du Pont telescope, located at the Las Campanas Observatory in Chile. The WFCCD has a field of view of 25' ; the TYPHOON 3D datacubes are constructed using a custom long-slit (18' × 1.65; 0.5 square arcminute). The slit was positioned as either north-south or east-west, moved after each 600s to scan the data in the perpendicular direction of each galaxy. This was performed until the entire optical disk of each galaxy had been observed. The observations were taken over the course of observing runs from 2011 through 2018. Observations were performed only when the seeing was less than the slit width of 1 '' 65. By choosing nearby galaxies ( z 0.005 ), TYPHOON, and the galaxies in this study, is able to achieve seeing-limited resolutions of up to 5 pc. Standard spectro-photometric flux calibrations were taken each observing night. The data are reduced using standard long-slit data reduction techniques, summarized in ho17 and to be described in detail in a forthcoming paper (M. Seibert et al., in preparation). We briefly summarize here the most important details. The wavelength calibration has a typical root-mean-square value 0.05 \ for the entire data set. Flux calibration is accurate to 2 at the spaxel scale over the range of 4500--7500 . The reduced long-slit 2D spectra are tiled together to form the 3D data cube. The final reduced data cube covers a wavelength range of 3650--8150 , with spectral and spatial samplings of 1.5 \ and 1.65'', respectively. The instrumental dispersion is approximately 3.5 , corresponding to a spectral resolution R 850 at 7000 . We typically reach a 3 surface brightness sensitivity for H of 4.2 10^ 17 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 arcsec ^ 2 . We note that as all main results in this paper (metallicity, ionization, and pressure diagnostics) are inferred from line ratios, the results depend only on the relative flux calibration and are insensitive to the absolute flux calibration. The TYPHOON Galaxies The six galaxies selected for this study are all the spiral galaxies from the TYPHOON survey that have undergone the full data reduction pipeline at the time of writing, and additionally, were not previously published by ho17, ho18 . A summary of properties of the galaxies are listed in Table tab:1 along with the spatial resolution of the observations and the number of \ regions in each galaxy. NGC 1566 The most distant galaxy in the sample at 17.9 Mpc, NGC 1566 is a face-on spiral galaxy with an intermediate-strength bar type, classified as a SAB(s)bc. NGC 1566 has a nuclear gas disk which presents as a star-forming ring smajic15 and an outer pseudo-ring that winds anti-parallel to the bar ends buta15 . NGC 1566 hosts a low-luminosity active galactic nuclei (AGN) that does not show evidence of an outflow nor AGN feedback combes14 . The moderate to loosely wound arms are consistent with being formed through bar-driven spiral density waves shabani18 . The largest \ regions in NGC 1566 of 600 pc (Figure fig:size ) are consistent with the maximum size of star-forming complexes of 508 179 pc as traced by the young stellar population grasha17b . NGC 2835 NGC 2835 is a highly inclined star-forming galaxy with a reported shallow radial metallicity gradient of 0.096 dex/kpc using the dopita16 metallicity calibration kreckel19 . The PHANGS-MUSE data from kreckel19 have a spatial resolution of 51 pc and thus are able to observe \ regions that are 42 smaller than the native resolution of the TYPHOON data. ryder95 use the R_ 23 diagnostic pagel79, kobulnicky04 to report a gradient of 0.041 dex/kpc. pilyugin14 used the data from ryder95 to provide homogeneous abundance determinations for \ regions using T _e based abundances, reporting a negative metallicity gradient of 0.032 dex/kpc. NGC 3521 NGC 3521 is a highly inclined, flocculent spiral galaxy with a barred and inner ring morphology that hosts a stellar counter-rotating component induced by the bar zeilinger01 .
The running median shows good agreement with the linear fit for NGC 3521, NGC 5068, and NGC 7793, indicating that the gradients are well-described with a single fit in these systems.

gmw_00000190.name: NGC 3607
gmw_00000190.overview: NGC 3607 is a fast-rotating early-type galaxy [arXiv:1609.03559] located at the center of the Leo II group [arXiv:astro-ph/0612348]. It features a central LINER or Seyfert 2 nucleus [arXiv:2110.07373, arXiv:1310.2770], a magnesium-enhanced kinematically distinct core [arXiv:astro-ph/0612348], and a regular molecular disc [arXiv:1609.03559] surrounded by a broad dust ring [arXiv:astro-ph/0612348].

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 3607's metallicity gradients, stellar populations, and emission lines, finding multiple star-formation episodes, a LINER center, and merger evidence from dynamical mass and Mg2 gradients.

quotes.quote: In this work we derive clues to the formation scenario and ionisation source of the lenticular galaxy NGC 3607 by means of metallicity gradients, stellar population and emission lines properties. We work with long-slit spectroscopy from which we (i) study the radial distribution of the equivalent widths of conspicuous metallic absorption features, (ii) infer on the star-formation history (with a stellar population synthesis algorithm), and (iii) investigate the ionisation source responsible for a few strong emission lines. Negative radial gradients are observed for most of the absorption features of NGC 3607. Compared to the external parts, the central region has a deficiency of alpha elements and higher metallicity, which implies different star-formation histories in both regions. At least three star formation episodes are detected, with ages within 1-13 Gyr.

The dynamical mass and the Mg_2 gradient slope are consistent with mergers being important contributors to the formation mechanism of NGC 3607. Emission-line ratios indicate the presence of a LINER at the centre of NGC 3607. Contribution of hot, old stars to the gas ionisation outside the central region is detected.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 3607 as a central Leo II lenticular with hot gas, an extended ionized-gas disk, dust ring, and a magnesium-enhanced kinematically distinct core.

quotes.quote: NGC 3607 is a giant lenticular galaxy located in the center of the Leo II group. Hot X-ray gas is detected inside this galaxy xrayasca , as well as around it xray82 - all three galaxies in the center of the group are embedded into the common envelope of hot gas. An X-ray atlas of xray02 demonstrates two maxima of the hot gas concentration -- one at NGC 3607 and another at NGC 3608 -- which fact forces authors to suggest a recent assembly of the Leo II group. Also NGC 3607, though of early type, is known to have a rather extended ionized-gas disk shields, macetal with a radius of 15 ( 1.5 kpc) containing a broad dust ring between R=8 4 and R=13 2 n3607gas .

After a thorough study of the central regions of the brightest galaxies of the Leo II group, the nearest to the center of the group, NGC 3607 and NGC 3608, we note a kinematically distinct area in the center of the former and confirm a presence of the counterrotating core in the latter. The presence of the counterrotating stellar core in NGC 3608 had been reported by js88 ; a fall of the rotation velocity after the circumnuclear maximum at R=6 in NGC 3607 was noted by fisher97 . Both kinematically decoupled cores have appeared to be also distinguished by a higher magnesium absorption-line strength, but do not differ from their outskirts as concerning Fe5270 ( Fe ) or H .

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 3607 as U376’s brightest member, reproduced by a 2:1 merger whose simulated photometry, morphology, star-formation history, and stellar age agree with observations.

quotes.quote: NGC 3607.       The simulated B band magnitude (Table table3 ), the SED (Figure figure1 ), and the morphology (Figure figure3 ) of NGC 3607, the brightest member of U376 are well matched by the merging of two triaxial collapsing systems with a mass ratio of 2:1 and a total mass almost 10 times larger than that of NGC 3605 (Table table2 ). This is a direct encounter with a first pericenter separation, p , corresponding to 1/4 of the semi-major axis of the primary system. The stellar systems merged into a unique configuration about 8 Gyr ago. The current age of the galaxy is 13.8 Gyr. The SFR proceeds about 6.5 Gyr before the merger and contributes to about 1/5 of the total SFR.

sources.summary: The paper records NGC 3607's adopted photometric profile or decomposition, giving bulge/scale parameters and comparing those choices with alternative literature fits for the structural analysis.

quotes.quote: NGC 3607: We calibrate the g- band SDSS image to the V- band using the (B-V) color from Hyperleda and the (g-V) transformation of Jordi2006 . McConnellMa2013 and KormendyHo2013 consider the galaxy a core elliptical; we accept their classification, although strong nuclear dust makes it difficult to determine its core properties see discussion in Rusli2013b . From the profile we measure R_e=45.0'' , in fair agreement with Beifiori2012 who measure R_e=56.34'' from a Sersic fit to the SDSS i- band image. In contrast, Laurikainen2010 perform a bulge plus disk decomposition, deriving R_e=6.1'' with B/T=0.32 . We do not think that this is a good description of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3607 as the most luminous Leo II group member, with the sample's lowest inner dark-matter fraction but a steep rise to a dark-matter-dominated outer halo.

quotes.quote: Our study also includes the two most dominant members of the Leo II group (NGC 3607 and NGC 3608) with intriguing f_ DM measurements. The most luminous member of the group, NGC 3607 ( M_ K =-24.96 ) has f_ DM 0.3 within 5  . NGC 3607 has the lowest f_ DM within 5  in our sample even when the DM content is maximised with a logarithmic potential, regardless of the adopted stellar M/L_ K . However, beyond 5  , the f_ DM in NGC 3607 increases steeply up to 0.8 , again showing that the outer halo is dominated by DM.
Within 5  , NGC 3607 has an average dark matter density of _ DM 6.2 \ kpc^ 3 , the lowest in our sample, unlike NGC 3608 with a denser DM halo with _ DM 7.2 \ kpc^ 3 .

sources.summary: NGC3607 is described as a Leo II fast-rotating early-type galaxy with a regular molecular disc and a flat Kroupa/Chabrier-like IMF mismatch profile.

quotes.quote: NGC3607 is a fast-rotating ETG with a central dust disc that is part of the Leo II Group of galaxies. The molecular disc is regular and relaxed at our resolution, with no sign of the disturbed structures present in the dust distribution. Its surface brightness profile is well fitted with an exponential disc model. The stellar population M/L of this (fast-rotating) elliptical has a mild negative gradient, which is consistent with that predicted dynamically from our CO observations. The IMF mismatch parameter in this source seems thus to be flat with radius, with a Kroupa/Chabrier type normalisation.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3607 as a nearby unbarred spiral/Seyfert system in the Leo II Group with a central black hole, star-formation lines, and LINER-like infrared spectrum.

quotes.quote: NGC 3607 (LEDA 34426, XBSJ111654.8+180304) is an unbarred spiral galaxy (SA0 ^0 ) at z= 0.00314, that forms part of the Leo II Group (NGC 3607 Group) of galaxies. Its central black hole has a mass of about (1.2 0.4) 10^8 M_ . NGC 3607 shows narrow emission lines in near-IR and optical spectrum suggestive of star-formation. NGC 3607 has an IR spectrum qualitatively similar to those of LINER 2-type nucleus . XBSJ111654.8+180304 in SIMBAD is classified as quasar, its alternative classification, for example, in NED is Seyfert 2 galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 3607's outer profile as I and describes dust features, profile breaks shaping its measured surface-brightness structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 3607 (I; SA(s)0 ^0 ) : The circular dust lane does not appear to have a significant influence on the brightness profile. The central region of the bulge is structured, and may contain an internal disk. However this is not resolvable in our image. No break in the profile is seen out to at least 4 scalelengths.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC3607 with intermediate kinetic temperature, a CO SLED turnover near CO(5-4), higher excitation, and Spitzer fluxes suggesting more active star formation or extra excitation.

quotes.quote: The kinetic temperatures obtained for the best-fit models are either low ( T_ K =10 20 K for IC1024, NGC1222, NGC1266, NGC2764, NGC3032, NGC3665, NGC4150, NGC4459, NGC4526, NGC4694, NGC4710 and NGC5866), intermediate ( 30 70 K for IC0676, NGC3607, PGC058114 and UGC09519) or high ( 120 K for NGC6014 and 150 K for NGC7465).

IC0676 and NGC3607 show CO SLED turnovers located around the ^ 12 CO(5-4) transition that, when also taking into account the best-fit model parameters ( T_ K , n (H _ 2 ) and N (CO)) for those sources, suggest a closer proximity to Maffei2's characteristics.

Since their CO SLEDs show higher excitation conditions, a few galaxies (IC0676, NGC1222, NGC3607, NGC6014 and NGC7465) may have slightly more active star formation or additional sources of excitation (AGN, cosmic ray-dominated regions, etc). Strong 24 , 60 and 160 m Spitzer fluxes in NGC3607 (compared to average values for ETGs) seem to confirm this hypothesis Temi09 .

sources.summary: NGC 3607 is used as a nearby early-type galaxy with an extended low-ionization emission-line region where He II 1640 constraints could test Type Ia progenitor scenarios.

quotes.quote: The nearby early-type galaxy NGC 3607 ( d 21 Mpc) has a mean stellar age of 3 -- 4 Gyr Annibali07 , and is known to host an extended low-ionization emission-line region. We can compute the expected 1640 \ line flux from the K-band luminosity 3 10^ 10 L _ K, within 14''x14'' from the 2MASS survey, 2MASS , and the appropriate M/ L_ K from . Assuming f_ = 1/2, and our prototypical case for the SD channel, the total luminosity reprocessed into He II 1640 \ emission is 3 10^ 39 erg/s. Using the GALEX Exposure Time Calculator (ETC) http://sherpa.caltech.edu/gips/tools/expcalc.html , we find that this should be detectable with S/N 5 given a 3 ksec observation Note that here we report 3 times the value given by the ETC, as recommended in the GALEX Spectroscopy Primer.

sources.summary: The paper finds no central point source in NGC 3607, argues against an AGN, and attributes its X-ray emission to point sources and diffuse stellar-process emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 3607 (L2, S0) has two point sources embedded in diffuse emission, but none of them is at the center. The brightest point source did not have enough counts to justify fitting models to its spectrum; its luminosity was estimated to be L_ 0.5-10 keV = 1.4 10^ 39 erg s ^ 1 by scaling the count rate to that expected from a power-law model. The absence of a central point source is a strong case against an AGN, in agreement with Terashima et al. (2002) who did not find evidence for an AGN from an X-ray spectrum obtained with ASCA. The point sources and the diffuse emission suggest that the X-ray emission is powered by stellar processes.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 3607's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 3607 is a low-inclination early-type spiral galaxy at a distance of D= 21.4 2.4 Mpc (NED redshift-independent median) with an AGN optically classified either as LINER ho_search_1997-1 or Sy 2.0 veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 . The nucleus is covered by a thick dust filament barth_search_1998 . It was detected at radio wavelengths nagar_radio_2005 but not in X-rays terashima_x-ray_2002,flohic_central_2006 , although propose that the nucleus could be Compton-thick obscured based on the X-ray to flux ratio.

gmw_00000191.name: NGC 3623
gmw_00000191.overview: NGC 3623 is a barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0603161] in the Leo Triplet [arXiv:0704.2839, arXiv:1303.5335] that hosts a LINER nucleus [arXiv:1310.2770]. Although its neighbors are tidally interacting [arXiv:1303.5335], NGC 3623 appears undisturbed [arXiv:astro-ph/0603161] with a relatively quiescent star formation history [arXiv:0906.2178].

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 3623 as similar to NGC 4274 but with a larger spheroidal bulge, weaker sigma-drop, high Sersic index, and different inner-disk to bulge ratio.

quotes.quote: Making the classical bulge a bit larger, and the pseudo-bulge smaller, one gets an object such as NGC 3623 (bottom of Fig. 3). Here the spheroidal bulge dominates the light, apart from the very inner regions. A sigma-drop is seen, but weaker than in NGC 4274 (Falc on-Barroso et al. 2006). The S ersic fit for the bulge gives n =3.4. Dust is associated with the central disks, but the SAURON maps show that the stellar populations in it are not different from the bulge outside it.
The comparison between NGC 3623 and NGC 4274 shows that both objects are very similar, but that the inner disk to elliptical bulge ratio in both galaxies is different.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3623, also M 65, as a large Leo Triplet member with generally old stellar populations and a central disc; its gradients differ from prior MPFS claims.

quotes.quote: NGC 3623 is a large member (M 65) of the Leo triplet. Its stellar populations are generally old, with its central disc, clearly seen in the stellar kinematics. Afanasiev Sil'chenko (2005) used our data for this galaxy, together with line strength maps from the MPFS instrument. They observe a central drop in , and also central drops in Fe 5270 and \ . We do not reproduce their result, and find relatively small radial gradients.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3623 as the quiescent Leo Triplet member, arguing from simulations and ALFALFA velocity data that it was not involved in perturbing NGC 3628.

quotes.quote: Despite the appearance of a bridge of gas connecting NGC 3623 to the perturbed NGC 3628, simulations of a collision that is prograde for NGC 3627 and retrograde for NGC 3628 but not involving NGC 3623 match best with the observations of the plume toomre, tripmodel, maptrip . NGC 3623 also has a relatively quiescent star formation history tripother , further suggesting that NGC 3623 was not involved in perturbing NGC 3628.

The ALFALFA data do not reveal any disturbance in the velocity field of NGC 3623 which may suggest it was not involved in the past encounter. The HI clump that appears to connect NGC 3623 with NGC 3628 is actually well separated in velocity from NGC 3623. Thus it is not a bridge between the two galaxies, but instead an extension of NGC 3628.

sources.summary: The paper detects NGC 3623 as the faintest Leo Triplet member in the 2.64 GHz map, corrects its contaminated flux downward, and finds its magnetic field weaker than the other spirals.

quotes.quote: The group consists of three tidally interacting spiral galaxies, NGC 3623, NGC 3627, and NGC 3628.

The third member of the Triplet, NGC 3623, is also visible, but is significantly weaker with an integrated flux of 19 2 mJy. It should be noted here that the large beam of our observations causes flux contamination by the background sources at R.A._ 2000 = 11^ h 19^ m 01^ s , Dec_ 2000 = +13^ 01' 47'' and R.A._ 2000 = 11^ h 18^ m 57^ s , Dec_ 2000 = +13^ 04' 08'' . An attempt to estimate the real value at 2.46 GHz is described in Sect.  intflux .
The magnetic field of NGC 3623 is weaker; however, this galaxy is smaller than the other two, which may explain the difference.

sources.summary: NGC 3623 is described as an undisturbed barred Sa member of the Leo Triplet, with a prominent dust lane and a twisted stellar velocity map.

quotes.quote: NGC 3623 is a barred Sa galaxy and a member of the Leo Triplet. While there are strong indications that the two other members of the triplet (NGC 3627 and NGC 3628) are interacting, NGC 3623 appears undisturbed cemd98 . This galaxy contains a prominent dust lane at 15 \ East of the nucleus. The stellar velocity map is twisted with respect to the reconstructed intensity image.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 3623's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 3623 is an inclined spiral galaxy in the Leo Triplet at a distance of D= 12.8 2.2 Mpc (NED redshift-independent median) with a LINER nucleus ho_search_1997-1 . The first N band observations of this object were performed by but the nucleus remained undetected. The /IRAC and MIPS images show an extended nucleus embedded in large-scale host emission.

gmw_00000192.name: NGC 3627
gmw_00000192.overview: NGC 3627 is an interacting barred spiral galaxy located in the Leo Triplet [arXiv:1207.1195, arXiv:1503.00280]. It hosts a LINER/Seyfert nucleus [arXiv:1503.00280, arXiv:2111.09545] and exhibits highly asymmetric spiral arms and dust lanes, likely distorted by a recent collision with a companion galaxy [arXiv:1207.1195, arXiv:1503.00280].

sources.summary: The paper uses XMM-Newton data to link NGC 3627 hot gas, radio polarization structures, ram pressure, and eastern-disk distortions to group motion and a possible dwarf collision.

quotes.quote: We use the XMM-Newton X-ray observations of NGC 3627 in the Leo Triplet galaxy group to explain peculiar features visible in the polarized radio maps. We analyzed soft X-ray (0.2-1 keV) emission from NGC 3627 to study the distribution of the hot gas and its temperature in different areas of the galaxy. Any change throughout the disk can reflect distortions visible in the radio polarized emission. We also studied two bright point sources that are probably tightly linked to the evolution of the galaxy. We find an increase in the temperature of the hot gas in the area of the polarized radio ridge in the western arm of the galaxy.

The polarized radio ridge in the western arm of NGC 3627 is most likely formed by ram-pressure effects caused by the movement of the galaxy through the intragroup medium. To explain the distortions visible in the eastern part of the disk in polarized radio maps, the asymmetry of the bar, and the distortion of the eastern arm, we propose a recent collision of NGC 3627 with a dwarf companion galaxy. Galaxies: groups: general -- galaxies: groups: individual (Leo Triplet) -- galaxies: individual: NGC 3627 -- Galaxies: NGC 3627 (M66) is a barred galaxy of SAb type at a distance of 11 Mpc (see Table astrdat ) in the Leo Triplet galaxy group.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3627 as an interacting barred LINER/Seyfert galaxy in the Leo Triplet, with asymmetric dusty spiral structure, recent-starburst X-ray properties, nuclear CO/radio peaks, and a roughly linear K-S relation.

quotes.quote: NGC 3627 (Messier 66) is an interacting e.g., vivi04 and barred galaxy classified as SAB(s)b at a distance of 10 Mpc, with signatures of a LINER/Seyfert 2 type nuclear activity ho97 . With NGC 3623 and NGC 3628, it forms the well-known Leo Triplet (M 66 Group, VV 308). Optical broad-band images of NGC 3627 reveal a pronounced and asymmetric spiral pattern with heavy dust lanes, indicating strong density wave action ptak06 .

While the western arm is accompanied by weak traces of SF visible in H , the eastern arm contains a star-forming segment in its inner part smith94,chemin03 . NGC 3627 also possesses X-ray properties of a galaxy with a recent starburst dahlem96 . Both the radio continuum 2.8 cm and 20 cm, urbanik85,paladino08 and the CO emissions e.g., regan01,paladino08,haan09,vivi11,watanabe11 show a nuclear peak, extending along the leading edges of the bar that forms two broad maxima at the bar ends, and then the spiral arms trail off from the bar ends.

In NGC 3627, we can only say that N_ fit 1.2 both with \ derived from ^ 12 CO(1--0) at 82 pc and from ^ 12 CO(2--1) at 36 pc. Neglecting the 200 pc-resolution cases, the Pearson correlation coefficient is 0.6 -- 0.7 and \ is 1.2 -- 1.3 Gyr for both the lowest ^ 12 CO transitions.

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 3627 contains a low-luminosity AGN candidate, but X-ray measurements and MIR flux are affected by off-nuclear or star-forming contamination.

quotes.quote: NGC 3627 NGC 3627 contains a low-luminosity AGN candidate, which remained undetected in the first snapshot observations ho_detection_2001 . Furthermore, argue that the data on this source is significantly contaminated by off-nuclear emission and thus not usable to extract information about the nucleus. They derive an upper limit on the observed X-ray luminosity of 38.25 . On the other hand, analyse a deeper observation from 2008 and find = 39.15 .

They attribute this 1 dex difference to the different model used but do not comment on the fact that the earlier value was also an upper limit. The nuclear MIR flux suggests an even higher intrinsic X-ray luminosity and heavy obscuration ( =40.25 0.39; = 24.2 0.71 ). However, in , we point out that even the subarcsecond measurement of the nuclear MIR flux is presumably contaminated by nuclear star formation (see also masegosa_nature_2011 ). While our results here are consistent with the presence of an AGN in NGC 3627, it presumably is not energetically important even on nuclear scales.

sources.summary: The paper presents SMA CO observations of NGC 3627, describing its nuclear peak, spiral-arm emission, velocity gradients, and interaction-disturbed barred spiral morphology within the Leo Triplet.

quotes.quote: We present moderate ( 5^ ) and high angular resolution ( 1^ ) observations of ^ 12 CO (J=2-1) emission toward nearby, interacting galaxy NGC 3627 taken with the Submillimeter Array (SMA). These SMA mosaic maps of NGC 3627 reveal a prominent nuclear peak, inter-arm regions, and diffuse, extended emission in the spiral arms. A velocity gradient of 400 - 450 km s ^ 1 is seen across the entire galaxy with velocity dispersions ranging from 80 km s ^ 1 toward the nuclear region to 15 km s ^ 1 in the spiral arms. We also detect unresolved ^ 13 CO (J=2-1) line emission toward the nuclear region, southern bar end, and in a relatively isolated clump in the southern portion of the galaxy, while no C ^ 18 O(J=2-1) line emission is detected at a 3 rms noise level of 42 mJy beam ^ 1 per 20 km s ^ 1 channel.

(M66) is a spiral galaxy [ RC3 type SAB(s)b; Vaucouleurs91 ] in the Leo Triplet galaxy group and displays signatures of a LINER/Seyfert 2-type nuclear activity in its spectrum Ho97, Peng98 . Optical broadband images of NGC 3627 reveal a weak optical bar, two prominent, asymmetric spiral arms, and large-scale dust lanes Arp66, Ptak06 . The perturbed morphology of the western arm, which appears to be displaced from the plane of the galaxy, provides evidence for recent interaction with neighboring galaxy NGC 3628 e.g., Rots78, Haynes79, Soida01 . The close proximity ( 11 Mpc ) and high inclination ( 61^ ) of NGC 3627 allow for an excellent view onto its spiral structure and pronounced dust patterns, making the galaxy an attractive candidate for investigating post-interaction galactic evolution.

sources.summary: The paper reconstructs NGC 3627 magnetic vectors, finding spiral-arm-aligned fields, mixed inward and outward directions, and a dominant bi-symmetric spiral mode.

quotes.quote: We analyzed the data of Stokes I , Q , and U in C- and X-bands and investigated the large-scale magnetic field structure of NGC 3627. The polarization intensity and angle in each band were derived using Stokes Q and U maps. The rotation measure was calculated using the polarization-angle maps. Moreover, the magnetic field strength was calculated by assuming energy equipartition with the cosmic ray electrons.

In this study, we focus on NGC 3627, which is located at (RA (J2000), Dec (J2000)) = (11h20m15.027s, +12d59m29.58s) and is classified as a molophological type of SAB(s)b 1991rc3..book.....D . In addition, its galaxy distance is 11.1 Mpc 2019ApJ...882..150H , at which the angular size of 1" is equivalent to the physical scale of 54 pc. The position angle and inclination of NGC 3627 are 176^ and 52^ , respectively 2007PASJ...59..117K .
Therefore, we found that the magnetic mode of m_ B =1 was dominant in NGC 3627.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC3627 (M66) as a barred Leo Triplet spiral with asymmetric arms likely disturbed by companions; its arms are marginally stable and not expected to fragment.

quotes.quote: NGC3627 NGC3627, also known as M66, is a member of the Leo Triplet. Its two grand-design arms are somewhat asymmetric, which may be indicative of disturbance by other group members. This galaxy has a bar structure, and the roots of the spiral arms are contiguous with the tips of the bar.

Although most of the spiral-arm regions are in marginally stable states with (S_ tot )>1 , some small areas indicate (S_ tot )<1 , e.g. at (R, ) (4 kpc ,0.25 ) . We estimate the wavelength of the most unstable perturbation to be _ MU 1 kpc in these areas. The size of these areas are smaller than the wavelength _ MU , and these areas are therefore actually expected to be stable. Moreover, these areas can indicate (S_ tot )>1 if we take into account the uncertainties of the CO-to-H _ 2 conversion factor (Section alphaCO ). We thus expect that the arms of NGC3627 are marginally stable and not on the way to fragment.

sources.summary: The paper maps dense gas tracers across barred spiral NGC 3627, finding HCN/CO tied to environment, less efficient central dense gas, and bar-end dynamics linked to enhanced star formation.

quotes.quote: We present new NOrthern Extended Millimeter Array (NOEMA) observations of a range of high critical density molecular tracers (HCN, HNC, HCO ^ + ) and CO isotopologues ( , C ^ 18 O) towards the nearby ( 11.3 Mpc), strongly barred galaxy NGC 3627. These observations represent the current highest angular resolution (1.85 ; 100 pc) map of dense gas tracers across a disc of a nearby spiral galaxy, which we use here to assess the properties of the dense molecular gas, and their variation as a function of galactocentric radius, molecular gas, and star formation. We find that the HCN /CO \ integrated intensity ratio does not correlate with the amount of recent star formation. Instead, the HCN /CO \ ratio depends on the galactic environment, with differences between the galaxy centre, bar, and bar end regions. The dense gas in the central 600 pc appears to produce stars less efficiently despite containing a higher fraction of dense molecular gas than the bar ends where the star formation is enhanced.

In assessing the dynamics of the dense gas, we find the HCN \ and \ emission lines showing multiple components towards regions in the bar ends that correspond to previously identified features in CO emission. These features are co-spatial with peaks of H emission, which highlights that the complex dynamics of this bar end region could be linked to local enhancements in the star formation.
NGC 3627 is a nearby, star-forming galaxy with a strong bar, part of the M66 group (Leo Triplet) garcia_1993 . It is also classified as LINER/ Seyfert type 2 galaxy ho_1997, filho_2000 .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3627 as the interaction-history barred-spiral analogue, emphasizing its distorted gas and arms, LINER/Seyfert nucleus, CO studies, and region-dependent star formation efficiency.

quotes.quote: Comparatively, NGC 3627 is a barred-spiral galaxy of type SABb according to the Third Reference Catalogue of Bright Galaxies DeVau1991 . Like NGC 4303, it has an active nucleus of the LINER/Seyfert 2 type AGN Ho1997 . It is widely considered to have experienced some interaction with nearby NGC 3623 and NGC 3628 at some point in the evolutionary history of both galaxies due in particular to the slight asymmetry in arm structure observable at optical wavelengths and a distorted HI gas distribution Haynes1979 . Additionally, significant distortion is also evident in the disc in polarised maps soft X-ray emission (0.2--1 keV), as well as a large asymmetry in hot gas temperature on the bar edges which implies a recent collision with a dwarf companion galaxy Wezgowiec2012 . The inclination angle of approximately 60 degrees has made NGC 3627 a relatively popular target for CO mapping and the study of molecular gas properties, as well as star formation activity Reuter1996,Helfer2003,Kuno2007 .

Additionally, there is also postulated to be a correlation between the kinetic temperature of gas in NGC 3627 and star formation efficiency Law2018 . Considering the relationship between molecular gas and star formation activity, NGC 3627 shows significant differences in the SFR from region to region but the bar itself has very low observable star formation efficiency Watanabe . The bar ends, however, show the most intense star formation of any region with SFE for the three regions Spiral Arm, Bar-End, and the Nuclear Region determined by Watanabe2019 to be 1.3 0.4 , 5.7 1.7 and 1.8 1.0 10^ 9 yr ^ 1 respectively.

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 3627 radio polarization, finding two magnetic-field components, distorted arm and interarm structure, discrepant magnetic pitch angles, and weak association with optical shock features.

quotes.quote: By observing the interacting galaxy NGC 3627 in radio polarization we try to answer the question to which degree the magnetic field follows the galactic gas flows. We obtained total power and polarized intensity maps at 8.46 GHz and 4.85 GHz using the VLA in its compact D-configuration. In order to overcome the zero-spacing problems, the interferometric data were combined with single-dish measurements obtained with the Effelsberg 100-m radio telescope. The observed magnetic field structure in NGC 3627 suggests that two field components are superposed. One component smoothly fills the interarm space and shows up also in the outermost disk regions, the other component follows a symmetric S-shaped structure.

We suggest that two distinct magnetic field components are present in NGC 3627: one component related to spiral arms and an interarm component. These components locally show dramatically discrepant magnetic pitch angles. The rapid changes of the magnetic field orientation are not clearly associated with optical features which could be attributed to density wave shocks.

sources.summary: Chandra imaging finds only a marginal weak nuclear X-ray source in NGC3627, while several off-nuclear sources dominate the galaxy's X-ray emission.

quotes.quote: In the case of NGC3627, an X-ray point source close to the radio nucleus (offset 2.6 arcsec) is marginally detected. Note that the astrometry error of the images is usually within 2 arcsec and therefore the nuclear X-ray source may be slightly offset from the radio nucleus. More specifically, the coordinates of the nucleus as derived from radio observations (Filho et al. 2001) are =11h20m15.0s , =+12d59m30s in J2000 while those of the X-ray source are =11h20m15.1s , =+12d59m28s (J2000).

We detect 15 counts from the nucleus translating to an X-ray luminosity of L_ 2-10 keV 4 10^ 37 ; we used a radius of 2 arcsec which encompasses over 90 of the light at 2 keV from an on-axis point source. For the conversion from counts to luminosities we use a power-law model with =1.9 absorbed by the Galactic column density. Note that Ho et al. (2001) derive an upper limit of L_ 2-10 keV 4 10^ 37 for the luminosity of the radio nucleus. At least 7 other X-ray point sources are apparently associated with NGC3627. The X-ray emission is dominated by an off-nuclear source (with J2000 coordinates =11h20m20.9s , =+12d58m45s ) with a luminosity of L_ 2-10 keV 10^ 39 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3627 as an interacting SAB Seyfert 2 in the Leo Triplet, with asymmetric gas motions, a molecular ring, radial motions, and twisted central kinematics.

quotes.quote: NGC 3627 is an SAB spiral galaxy hosting a Seyfert 2 nucleus. It is part of the Leo Triplet system, interacting with NGC 3623 and with evidence for a past interaction with NGC 3628 3627_interact . Previous radio and optical observations show asymmetric structures in the velocity field, non-circular gaseous motions and a molecular CO inner ring 3627_1 . More recently, IFS data obtained by showed evidence for strong gaseous radial motions.

The \ stellar continuum map shows an elongated structure along the photometric PA 170 (Fig.  pair2 ). The velocity field presents a regular rotation pattern, with a slight twist of the zero-velocity line in the very central parts ( < 3 ). The stellar rotation follows the orientation of the photometric major-axis. The stellar velocity dispersion rises towards the central regions up to 120 km s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: NGC 3627 has two offset stellar bars and spiral structure; the paper finds spiral-driven outer inflow/outflow, central gas outflow, and likely accumulation near 0.5 kpc.

quotes.quote: The stellar distribution of NGC 3627 exhibits two bars with a radius of 1.5 kpc and 0.2 kpc and two spiral arms at the ends of each of the bars. As the two bars have an offset between their PAs of 45 a possible decoupling of these two dynamical modes might influence the gas kinematics see also Gar08 . We found that the torque pattern reflects the shift of the PAs between these two bars.

For the outer disk the torques are dominated by the potential of the spiral arms with a CR radius at 2.5 kpc. Within this CR radius the gas is transported inwards, while moving outwards outside the CR radius, which is confirmed by our radial torque profile: A significant inflow (d L/L 0.3 ) from 2.8-0.7 kpc and outflow of gas (d L/L 0.3 ) from 2.8-5 kpc. For the central disk traced by our CO observation we found a large gas outflow (d L/L 0.4 ) from 0 - 0.5 kpc. Thus, gas accumulation might be possible at 0.5 kpc where the flow direction switches from out- to inflow, which is also visible as a small peak in the radial molecular gas density profile (see Fig. fig_torque_mass ).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3627 as an active asymmetric barred spiral in the Leo Triplet whose molecular gas is concentrated in its bar and spiral arms with distorted kinematics.

quotes.quote: NGC 3627 (M 66) is an active, asymmetric barred spiral galaxy (type SAB(s)b), a member of the Leo Triplet well known for its unusual kinematics that are influenced by both its bar and external interactions zha93,reu96,reg02,che03 . It is the most luminous and most active of these three galaxies, and has the highest molecular gas content of the three as deduced from observations M _ H_ 2 = (4.1 0.4) 10^ 9 , hel03 . In contrast, the mass is the lowest of the three galaxies, such that the molecular gas dominates the gas content of the galaxy. The adopted distance to NGC 3627 is 9.3 Mpc, derived by fre01 using Cepheid variables.

The integrated intensity maps of NGC 3627 (Fig. fig:ngc3627 a,b) shows a distinct pattern of an inclined barred spiral. Molecular gas is found throughout much of the optical disk, with detected right up to and sightly beyond the edges of our target field (the blue box in the upper right panel). It is mostly constrained to the bar and along the spiral arms, with no gas detected in the intervening regions.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3627 as an unquestionable Leo I group member, arguing its TRGB may be hidden by crowding and that velocities, Cepheids, and H I maps support the group distance.

quotes.quote: The galaxies NGC 3368 and NGC 3627 are bona fide members of the Leo I group e.g. Humason:etal:56,Huchra:Geller:82 , yet their published m^ * _ I magnitudes of 25.61 Mould:Sakai:09a and 25.82 Mould:Sakai:09b , respectively, are brighter by 0.6 than the mean TRGB magnitude m^ * _ I =26.34 0.08 of four unquestioned group members (NGC 3351, 3377, 3379, and 3384; see Sakai:etal:04,Rizzi:etal:07,Mould:Sakai:09a,Mould:Sakai:09b ). The proposed tip of NGC 3368 is based on (too) few stars, and the genuine tip of NGC 3627 may well be buried in a dense cloud of stars. The edge detection functions of both galaxies show secondary peaks at m^ * _ I 26.4 that presumably reflect the true TRGBs. Also the velocities of v_ 220 =720 and 428 ( v_ 220 is the velocity corrected for a self-consistent Virgocentric infall model with a local infall 220 ) of NGC 3368 and NGC 3627 compared with the mean velocity of v_ 220 =497 ( =159 ) from 8 bright group members make it unlikely that they could be foreground galaxies.

Moreover, we note that the Cepheid distances of NGC 3368 and NGC 3627 of (m-M)=30.34 and (m-M)=30.50 Saha:etal:06 are in very good agreement with the adopted mean TRGB distance of the Leo I group of m-M =30.39 0.08 . Finally convincing evidence for the group membership comes from the HI maps of the Leo I group Figs. 3 and 4 Stierwalt:etal:09 . For these arguments NGC 3368 and 3627 are unquestionable members of the Leo I group; as such they are treated in the following.

sources.summary: The paper identifies M66 as a nearby spiral SN Ia host in Leo I, measures its TRGB distance modulus, and uses that distance to recalibrate SN 1989B.

quotes.quote: M66 and M96 in the Leo I Group are nearby spiral galaxies hosting Type Ia Supernovae (SNe Ia). We estimate the distances to these galaxies from the luminosity of the tip of the red giant branch (TRGB). We obtain VI photometry of resolved stars in these galaxies from F555W and F814W images in the Hubble Space Telescope archive.

From the luminosity function of these red giants we find the TRGB I band magnitude to be I_ TRGB =26.20 0.03 for M66 and 26.21 0.03 for M96. These values yield distance modulus (m-M)_0=30.12 0.03 ( random ) 0.12 ( systematic ) for M66 and (m-M)_0=30.15 0.03 ( random ) 0.12 ( systematic ) for M96.

M66 (NGC 3627, SAB(s)b) and M96 (NGC 3368, SAB(rs)ab) are nearby bright spiral galaxies hosting SNe Ia: SN 1989B in M66 eva89,wel94 and SN 1998bu in M96 vil98,sun99,jha99,her00,spy04 . M66 has been host to other three SNe as well: SN II 1973R cia77 , SN imposter SN 1997bs van00 , and SN II-L 2009hd eli11 .

sources.summary: The paper presents a new ALMA GMC catalog for NGC 3627 and finds its GMC masses are consistent with a power law with weak cutoff evidence.

quotes.quote: New Catalogs We produce new catalogs of GMCs for NGC 3627 and NGC 3256, since no published catalogs currently exist for these galaxies. After downloading the pipeline calibrated datasets for NGC 3627 (project 2015.1.00956.S PI: A. Leroy) and NGC 3256 (project 2015.1.00714.S, PI: K. Silwa) from the ALMA archive, we perform our data reduction and imaging using the CASA software package CASA . We first perform continuum subtraction for the individual interferometric datasets and then combine the results from the 12-meter and 7-meter (ACA) arrays for each galaxy. To image the dataset, we use the non-interactive cleaning process in the tclean routine. We select the area of interest in the two galaxies, using the line-free regions in the datacube to estimate the noise, and then run the tclean process until it reaches a clean threshold of twice the measured rms noise. To obtain the final images for NGC 3627, we also run the feathering routine from CASA to incorporate the zero-spacing information from the available single-dish total power observations.

For M83 and NGC 3627, the 3 and 2 contours respectively, remain open up to the maximum tested value for M_* . In these cases, the GMC masses are also consistent with being drawn from a pure power law (not excluded at >3 significance), but with some weak evidence for a physical cutoff.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 3627 as a barred Leo-group galaxy whose 0.33 GHz map reveals higher flux and a diffuse halo absent from lower-sensitivity prior observations.

quotes.quote: (ii) NGC 3627: It has a highly prominent bar with wide open arms (galaxy type SAB) and is a member of the Leo group Dumas2007MNRAS . This galaxy has been observed before at 0.327 GHz with VLA with a resolution of 21.0 '' 16.6'' Paladino2009A&A and achieved an rms noise of 2 mJy. We have achieved an rms noise of 0.8 mJy/beam, with a beam size of 16 '' 11'' at 0.33 GHz. The measured flux density from our 0.33 GHz map is 1.7 0.1 Jy, which is almost double of 0.97 0.03 Jy measured by them. We note that there is a diffuse halo of size of about 6 ' seen around this galaxy, and the flux density for this object includes its contribution.

It is likely that due to a higher rms noise of the previous observation, the diffuse extended emission was not seen, which could partly explain the discrepancy. The L-band flux density from the archival VLA data is 0.33 0.01 Jy, which is consistent with what is presented by Paladino2009A&A .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3627/M66 as the most massive spiral host in the sample and a Leo Triplet primary with confirmed R200 satellites but uncertain outer candidates.

quotes.quote: NGC 3593 and 3627 (M66 Group) NGC 3593 and NGC 3627 (M66) have overlapping R_ 200 halos (Figure fig:virgo_field_sky_distribution ) and are at a derived three-dimensional distance of 547 kpc.

NGC 3627 (M66) is the most massive spiral in our host sample. At M_ Halo 2. 10^ 12 M_ , it is comparable to our adopted value for M31. All three of its R_ 200 objects, and the two we assign to NGC 3593, are confirmed by ELVES. Beyond R_ 200 , there are three more V_ esc candidates with low to intermediate F_ d_ 3D , and whose association is relatively unclear due to the complex group environment and potential contamination.

Other than NGC 3486, two more ALFALFA hosts, NGC 4559 and NGC 3627 (M66, group primary of Leo Triplet), appear to be physically explainable outliers with higher N_ sat values within 2R_ 200 . Both of these hosts have distinctively complicated group environments and are likely contaminated at these larger radii.

sources.summary: NGC 3627 is treated as a radio-bright Leo Triplet member whose continuum follows the bright stellar disk and H/CO/FIR emission, with central OH absorption and measured main-line ratios.

quotes.quote: This radio-bright galaxy belongs to the Leo triplet, together with NGC 3628 (see below) and NGC 3623 (see Zhang et al. for an overview). The bright radio continuum is co-extensive with the bright stellar disk as well as intense H (Chemin et al. ), CO (Regan et al. ), and FIR (Smith et al. ) emission. A multi-frequency radio continuum and polarisation study was presented by Soida et al. ( ).

Weak OH mainline absorption is detected against the brightest central radio continuum feature (offset by some 20 arcsec to the NE of the nuclear position) an shown in Fig. fig:hiohabs . Peak line depths at 1667 and 1665 MHz are -1.46 and -0.93 mJy/Beam relative to a continuum brightness of 31.6 mJy/Beam measured with the same PSF. The 1667/1665 line ratio of 1.6 is intermediate between the optically thin and optically thick cases. No corresponding H i absorption is detected.

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 3627 at 10.55 GHz, finding bar-end total-power peaks, no detectable central radio-continuum source, H-emission absorption asymmetry, and asymmetric polarized lobes aligned with optical arms.

quotes.quote: We present 10.55 GHz observations of the strong-density-wave spiral NGC 3627. Total power and polarization maps with a resolution of 1 13, sensitive to a weak extended emission were obtained. In the analysis we used also available data in the CO and H lines. The total power brightness distribution shows two equal ly bright sources close to the bar ends, coincident with similar peaks in CO and H . The strong central peak of the CO emission does not correspond to a detectable central source in radio continuum.
The H asymmetry is likely to be due to differences in absorption. The polarized emission has the form of two asymmetric lobes with B-vectors running parallel to the optical arms.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3627 as a barred interacting spiral whose bar and bright regions have flatter radio spectra, with integrated spectra better modeled by continuous injection than a simple power law.

quotes.quote: NGC 3627, the brightest member of the Leo Triplet, is an interacting spiral galaxy with a bar and two asymmetric spiral arms. The western arm is accompanied by weak traces of star formation, while the eastern arm contains a straight actively star-forming segment in its inner part soida01 . The spectral index image of NGC 3627 (Fig. N3627_spix ) clearly shows that the bar and its brights ending regions have a spectral index flatter than the underlying disk.

Bright regions, like the bar in NGC 3627 or the circumnuclear region in NGC 7331, are characterized by a flatter spectrum with respect to the underlying disk. The integrated spectra of galaxies NGC 3627 and NGC 7331 present deviations from the classical power law. In NGC 3627 the CI model fit do not requires synchrotron self-absorption.

sources.summary: M66 is described as a barred spiral Leo-group galaxy hosting FXRT 5 in its disk, with the transient region near the bar and strong H-emission knots.

quotes.quote: FXRT 5/XRT 080331 (cataloged as an X-ray source by Wang2016 and Sazonov2017 , although never classified as an FXRT) is located in the disk of the SAB(s)b galaxy M66 ( m_ = 9.6 AB mag, 11 Mpc), at an angular (projected) distance of 1 3 ( 4.3 kpc). M66 is a barred spiral galaxy in the Leo group. The probability of this occurring by chance is 2.8 10 ^ 6 based on its angular offset and the space density of m_ R < 9.6 AB mag galaxies, implying a highly probable association; the binomial probability that this FXRT is a background source is 3.9 10 ^ 3 , reinforcing an association with M66 (see Sect. sec:population for more details).

The FXRT candidate error region is located in a high extinction region of the disk, at the edge of the bar, with very few optical counterpart candidates ( 10 sources). However, the X-ray centroid is notably well aligned with two knots of strong H emission ( sources 1 and 2 in the HST/ACS-F658N image of Fig. fig:image_cutouts ) with m_ 658N 21.0 AB mag (or M_ 658N 9.2 AB mag). This suggests a link with a high-mass star formation region, while the 3 error circle encompasses at least ten fainter, unresolved candidate counterparts in the F110W and F160W images ( m_ 160W 22.5 or M_ 160W 7.7 AB mag).

sources.summary: The paper finds that NGC 3627 has CO-traced magnetic fields following its bar and asymmetric arms, with misalignments supporting collision-induced disk distortion and tidal inflow.

quotes.quote: NGC 3627 : For this interacting galaxy , the magnetic fields traced by ^ 12 CO (J = 2-1) closely follow the bar and asymmetric spiral structures, with FWHM 8.0'' (see Fig. ngc1068 ). By comparing with HAWC+ 154 m polarization (beam size 13.6'' ), we see a good alignment between the two measurements in the western arm and the bar. The misalignment appears in the southern bar end and the eastern arm. An unusual magnetic field component crossing the dust lane in the southeast (SE) disk was earlier found by radio polarization measurements . A recent collision with a dwarf galaxy may be responsible for the enhanced star formation in the eastern arm and the distortion of the SE region .
The tidal interaction with the neighbor galaxy NGC 3628 can significantly affect its subsequent dynamical evolution and trigger efficient radial inflow toward the nucleus .

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 3627 among four barred spirals with inner and outer CO-to-H2 conversion-factor and DGR measurements, separating center/bar regions from spiral arms and finding higher outer conversion factors and lower outer DGR.

quotes.quote: Finally, we obtained \ and DGR in the inner and outer regions of the following four barred spiral galaxies: IC 342, NGC 3627, NGC 5236, and NGC 6946, as listed in table tab:alpha_DGR_radial . The boundary of 0.2 R_ 25 in each four galaxies is shown in figure fig:R25_Vband . The inclination and the position angle were corrected.

In these galaxies, the inner region includes the center and the bar structure, while the outer region includes the spiral arms. The average and standard deviation of (inner) and DGR(inner) are 0.36 0.08 \ and 0.0199 0.0058 , while (outer) and DGR(outer) are 1.49 0.76 \ and 0.0084 0.0037 , respectively. In each galaxy, while (outer) is 2.3 to 5.3 times larger than (inner), DGR(outer) is 0.3 to 0.6 times smaller than that DGR(inner).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3627 as a low recent-SF-change LINER/Seyfert with decreasing activity and stronger gas depletion after earlier bar-related bursts.

quotes.quote: NGC 628, 4254, 4303, 4321, 4535, and 5068 belong to the high end of the distribution, while NGC 1365, 1512, 1566, 3351, and 3627 belong to its low end.This is also summarised in the last column of Table tab:yes_no . Looking at the distribution across the galactic disks in the 3 ^ rd column of Figure fig:all_maps , we observe large areas of increasing recent SF in NGC 628, 4254, 4303, and 4321, while NGC 1512, 1566, and 3627 exhibit recent SF activity that ranges from roughly unchanged to decreasing.

AGN feedback Among AGN hosts in the sample, there are Seyfert galaxies NGC 1365 veron_06 and NGC 1566 Shobbrook_66 , low ionization nuclear emission region (LINER)/Seyfert type 2 galaxies NGC 4303 and NGC 3627 agn_3627 , and the starburst NGC 4321 which is another LINER galaxy Wozniak_99 .

simulated interacting and isolated bars to match NGC 3627 and NGC 4303, respectively. They found that the presence of a bar boosts SF activity in both cases, however, occurring gravitational interactions cause an additional burst-like period of the SF, likely due to the consolidation of the spiral arm before the bar forms. Indeed, we see that decreases more in NGC 3627 than in NGC 4303, which could be due to a stronger gas depletion in NGC 3627 during the earlier SF bursts.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 3627's bar velocity gradients are offset from CO peaks, switching between leading and trailing sides and becoming disconnected around 1 kpc.

quotes.quote: However, in NGC 1672 and NGC 3627, velocity gradients are slightly offset towards to the peak of the CO emission. Velocity gradients are enhanced on the leading side of the bar in NGC 1672 and in the southern part of the NGC 3627. Interestingly, in the northern part of the bar in NGC 3627, it is the opposite.

NGC 3627 Enhanced velocity gradients along the dust lane are disconnected at R 19" ( 1 kpc) in both sides from the center (Figure fig:vg_set4 ). The elevated velocity gradients in the southern outer part (R > 19") are located at the leading side compared to the CO peaks (Figure fig:VG_CO_contour_set4 ), but in the inner part (R < 19''), they are located at the trailing side compared to the CO intensity. Along the northern part of the bar, enhanced velocity gradients are located along the trailing side.

sources.summary: The paper derives center estimates and harmonic-decomposition results for NGC 3627, describing its disk morphology, non-circular motions, and whether the inferred potential is consistent with being round.

quotes.quote: NGC 3627 NGC 3627 (Fig. fig:ngc-3627 ) a) Center estimates [ 1.5ex ] The 3.6 m IRAC image shows that NGC 3627 is a barred galaxy with two asymmetric spiral arms, which are also clearly visible in the total \ map. The central parts of NGC 3627 contain a nuclear point source which is visible in the IRAC image as well as in the radio continuum. Using the position of the point source ( =0 1 , =0 1 ) as input for an unconstrained \ fit results in a slightly different center estimate (see row 1 and 2 of Fig. fig:ngc-3627 ).

sources.summary: The paper detects bright polarized emission in NGC 3627, with a north-south fractional-polarization gradient, emission in spiral arms and interarm regions, and field orientation tracking optical structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 3627 (M66) Bright polarized emission is detected this galaxy, with a conspicuous north-south gradient of the fractional polarization. It originates in both optical spiral arm and in interarm regions. The polarized fraction at is less than 1 in the bright optical bar and inner disk of the galaxy, and increases in the regions outside of the spiral arms to as much as 15 on the eastern minor axis. The lowest brightness of polarized emission occurs near the receding major axis (PA = 173 ^ , as tabulated in Table table:summary ). The magnetic field orientation closely follows the optical spiral structure, except at the largest radii where it generally becomes more radial.

sources.summary: The paper reports [NeV] emission from NGC 3627, treats it as a mid-infrared AGN missed optically, and argues it is not strongly host-extincted.

quotes.quote: Of the 18 galaxies in common between D09 and our sample, D09 identified [ NeV ] 14.32 in three systems (NGC 3621; NGC 5033; NGC 5194), all of which we also identify here; however, we also identified [ NeV ] emission in two systems where D09 quote [ NeV ] upper limits (NGC 3627; NGC 5195), which could be due to dilution of the [ NeV ] emission by the host galaxy in the large apertures used by D09. Ten of our mid-IR classified AGNs are not unambiguously identified as AGNs at optical wavelengths: five are classified as HII galaxies (NGC 0613, NGC 1448, NGC 1792, NGC 4945, and NGC 5128), four are classified as LINERS (NGC 0660, NGC 3627, NGC 3628, and NGC 5195), and one does not have good-quality optical spectroscopic data (ESO121-G006). Of the 38 galaxies present in both our sample and Ho97, we find that eight are mid-IR identified AGNs (NGC 0660, NGC 1068, NGC 3627, NGC 3628, NGC 4051, NGC 5033, NGC 5194 and NGC 5195), only four of which are unambiguously identified as AGN at optical wavelengths. In Fig. fig_oiii_nev a, we show that three of the optically unidentified AGNs (NGC 0613, NGC 3627 and NGC 5195) are within the intrinsic scatter of the [ OIII ] [ NeV ] relation (shaded region), and consequently do not appear to be deficient in [ OIII ] flux. Furthermore, three of the optically unidentified AGNs are found to be hosted in relatively face-on galaxies (NGC 0613, NGC 3627 and NGC 5195), similar to that of the optically identified AGNs, suggesting there is little extinction from the host galaxy towards these AGNs.

sources.summary: The paper treats M66/NGC 3627 as an interacting Leo Triplet spiral with an asymmetric HI disk, recent star formation, and nearby newly detected HI supporting its role in the plume-forming collision.

quotes.quote: Another tidal encounter in Leo I is thought to have occurred between the large spirals NGC 3628 and M66 (NGC 3627) to create one of the largest known tidal tails extending 100 kpc off of NGC 3628 tripmodel, maptrip, tripobs . In M66, an asymmetric HI disk and a recent (less than 1 Gyr ago) burst of star formation coinciding with the time of the suspected encounter both suggest that an interaction between NGC 3628 and M66 led to NGC 3628's extensive gas loss tripmodel2, tripother .

Subsequent 21-cm observations found an associated neutral hydrogen plume roughly 100 kpc in length, an HI bridge connecting NGC 3628 to NGC 3623, and a distortion in the HI disk of NGC 3627 tripmodel, maptrip . Despite the appearance of a bridge of gas connecting NGC 3623 to the perturbed NGC 3628, simulations of a collision that is prograde for NGC 3627 and retrograde for NGC 3628 but not involving NGC 3623 match best with the observations of the plume toomre, tripmodel, maptrip .
This newly-detected HI and its spatial and spectral proximity to NGC 3627 furthers the hypothesis that NGC 3627 was responsible for the collision that led to the plume.

sources.summary: The paper describes 3627 as a strongly barred Leo Triplet galaxy with possible low-luminosity AGN activity, dust lanes, nuclear CO, and extended warm molecular gas structures.

quotes.quote: 3627 (M66) is a high-inclination strongly barred galaxy. It belongs to the Leo Triplet group of galaxies, showing some signatures of past interaction with its neighbour galaxy 3628. Optical emission-line ratios are inconclusive with respect to the type of nucleus this galaxy harbours. Whether it is a LINER/H II composite or Seyfert 2 like nucleus Ho1997 , the presence of a variable, flat-spectrum nuclear radio source is consistent with a low-luminosity AGN LLAGN, Filho2000 . Broad-band optical images of 3627 reveal a pronounced spiral pattern and a central region dominated by several straight dust lanes that run parallel to the host galaxy semi-major axis Martini2003a .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3627 as a tidally interacting barred spiral with unusual morphology, a perturbed magnetic field, detected radio continuum and polarization, and a measured spectral index.

quotes.quote: The group consists of three tidally interacting spiral galaxies, NGC 3623, NGC 3627, and NGC 3628. The galaxies are connected by bridges of neutral gas (Stierwalt et al. , Haynes et al. ). The most prominent one is the tidal tail that extends eastwards from NGC 3628, which is visible also in the infrared at 60 and 100 microns (Hughes et al. ). Since NGC 3627 possesses a perturbed magnetic field (see Soida et al. , for details), the question arises if the field is being stripped from galaxies into the intergalactic medium. As a possible explanation of the unusual morphology of NGC 3627, including its magnetic field, a collision with a dwarf galaxy was recently suggested by We zgowiec et al. ( ).
The second-most luminous object is the barred spiral NGC 3627, with an integrated flux of 299 14 mJy.
The value of 0.69 0.14 derived for NGC 3627 agrees well with the mean spectral index given by Soida et. al (0.64 0.04).

sources.summary: NGC 3627 was analyzed with XMM-Newton and Chandra, but the paper treats its long-term variability cautiously because surrounding nuclear emission strongly contaminates the XMM-Newton aperture.

quotes.quote: NGC 3627 We used one XMM Newton observation in May 2001 and another Chandra observation in March 2008 (see Table obs ). We recall that different apertures (8 for Chandra and 25 for XMM Newton) were used for the extraction of the nuclear spectrum. Since observations were obtained with different instruments, comparisons of HR were avoided. According to ^2_r , both spectra were individually best-fitted with a MEPL model. The best simultaneous fit implied variations in N_ H2 (Figure bestfig ), from no absorption to N_ H2 =1.28 10^ 22 cm^ 2 in a 7 year period (i.e. SMF1, Table bestfit ).

sources.summary: The paper presents 3627 as an interacting barred galaxy with distorted spiral arms, a buckled bar, uncertain orientation, and molecular gas associated with spiral arms and the bar.

quotes.quote: 3627 3627 is a barred galaxy deVaucouleurs1991 SAB(s)b, de Vaucouleurs et al. 1991 which shows signatures of interaction with its neighbour 3628 e.g. Zhang1993, Afanasiev2005 . Panel (a) of Fig. f_n3627_im shows Spitzer IRAC1 isophotes of the galaxy; the asymmetry between the western and eastern spiral arms is evidence of the distortions in the outer disc. Also visible is the strong bar, oriented almost vertically in the figure (we measure a PA = 161 \ for the outer part of the bar); the deprojected semi-major axis of the bar is 60 arcsec Erwin2013 . The combination of boxy inner isophotes and narrow, slightly offset spurs in the outer part of the bar are indications that the bar has vertically buckled : the boxy isophotes in panel (b) of Fig. f_n3627_im are thus projections of the thick, `box/peanut structure', while the isophotes in the interior ( 5 arcsec) become rounder, which is evidence for a more axisymmetric central component (a bulge or pseudobulge; see Afanasiev2005 Afanasiev Sil'chenko 2005 and Erwin et al. in preparation). Because of the interaction and the presence of a bar, the orientation of 3627 is somewhat uncertain, as the outer disc may not be intrinsically circular, and the strong bar may produce non-circular motions, even outside the bar itself.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3627 as the systemic-velocity outlier, attributing the mismatch to kinematic asymmetries and excess gas at the trailing edge of the velocity distribution.

quotes.quote: With exception of the outlier at about -14 km s ^ 1 , which is NGC 3627, the spread in differences is centred on zero with a standard deviation on the same order as the channel width for these galaxies, which was about 5.2 km s ^ 1 for 12 out of the 19, half that for the rest. De Blok et al. found that a V_ sys calculated from the global line wings of NGC 3627 returned values in the range 717 to 720 km s ^ 1 , which fall much closer to our value of 720.6. NGC 3627 appears to have kinematic asymmetries, such as have been described and discussed by swaters_1999 .

In contrast to this, for NGC 3627, Fig. 79 of deblok_2008 shows a significant excess of gas at velocities 10 km s ^ 1 or more greater than their tilted-ring fit, right at the trailing edge of the velocity distribution. The present model is sensitive to such distortions, so this is arguably the reason for the inconsistency between our value of V_ sys for this galaxy and that of deblok_2008 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3627 as a barred Leo Triplet galaxy with strong non-circular motions, lopsided CO, central HI-CO differences, and kinematics too complex for mass-model fitting.

quotes.quote: NGC 3627 NGC 3627 is an SABb galaxy and is part of the Leo Triplet. Notable studies of the molecular gas content of NGC 3627 are 1983ApJ...269..136Y and 2007PASJ...59..117K . Studies of the kinematics of the ionized gas 2003A&A...405...89C and the H i TRACHTERNACH:2008FK show strong signs of non-circular motions, which are due to the bar streaming motions as well as tidal interactions within the group. The comparison of the H i and CO data is shown in Figure fig:3627-atlas . The HERACLES data shows that the CO distribution is lopsided and although the CO velocity fields shows a general velocity gradient, the iso-velocity contours and the pV diagram show signs of non-circular motions.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 3627 as a nearby grand-design late-type spiral, comparing its gas and star-formation surface-density data, resolution, and lower metallicity to two similar galaxies.

quotes.quote: All three galaxies are nearby late-type galaxies with grand design (NGC 3627 and M 83) or flocculent (NGC 5055) spiral structures Elmegreen:1987kq . They belong to a similar population of galaxies in terms of stellar mass and SFR plane (Figure fig:MSFR ) but NGC 3627 seems to have lower metallicity than the other two galaxies (Figure fig:MZ ). We use archival data to estimate gas and SFR surface densities. The spatial resolutions of the data are 15 arcsec for NGC 3627 (700 pc at the distance of the galaxy) and NGC 5055 (570 pc), and 25 arcsec for M 83 (550 pc). The inclination corrected spatial resolutions are 892 pc for NGC 3627, 819 pc for NGC 5055, and 575 pc for M 83, which are larger than the boundary spatial resolutions at which the KS relation breaks 80-250 pc, Momose:2010fj,Verley:2010rt,Onodera:2010uq .

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 3627 differs from other targets at high CO intensity, with lower central CO-to-H2 conversion factors that could explain intergalaxy offsets.

quotes.quote: NGC 3627 and NGC 4321 in the EMPIRE data (Figure fig:density-density-empire ) also exhibit different behavior at high and low I_ CO (2-1) . For these galaxies, the slope relating HCN/CO to I_ CO(2-1) steepens near _ 10 I_ CO(2-1) 1.75 ( 55 K km s ^ 1 350 M _ ) and these galaxies show higher _ 10 I_ CO(2-1) and lower HCN/CO compared to the other two targets. Though we do not find similar curvature, the same two targets show a similar offset from NGC4254 to high _ 10 I_ CO(2-1) and lower HCN/CO in the ALMA observations (Figure fig:density-density-alma ).
Specifically, they found 2 3 times lower _ CO in the center of NGC 3627 and NGC 4321 compared to the disks.
Adjusting the inner (high I_ CO (2-1) ) NGC 3627 and NGC 4321 points in this way would bring the different galaxies into better agreement.

sources.summary: The paper evaluates PHANGS-HST and existing TRGB distances for NGC 3627, adopting the CMDs/TRGB catalog distance despite a close new measurement.

quotes.quote: NGC 3627 is the brightest member of a group colloquially known as the Leo Triplet, though there are fifteen likely group members 2017ApJ...843...16K . There are several existing HST observations that allow for the determination of a TRGB distance to this target, due to its nature as a host to SN1989B, a type Ia supernova. The most ideal data is from the Carnegie-Chicago Hubble Programme (CCHP, GO-13691, PI: W. Freedman). The CCHP group, with their distinct methodology, has determined a distance to NGC 3627 of 11.06 0.30 Mpc 2019ApJ...882..150H, 2019ApJ...882...34F . The existing determination on the CMDs/TRGB catalog from this same CCHP field is 11.32 0.48 Mpc, implying the two measurements are consistent to within 2 of each other, though 8 offset from the Cepheid determination of ( D = 10.05 0.37 Mpc). The PHANGS-HST field partially overlaps with the southern edge of the main disk, introducing some contamination from Pop I stars. We select the outer 30 of the observed field for our analysis. Within this region, we find m_ TRGB = 26.28 0.02 mag, from which we determine a distance of 11.07 0.44 Mpc. We choose to adopt the existing CMDs/TRGB catalog distance over the new PHANGS-HST determination due to the greater number of detected stars in the selected regions of the field in the former, though we note that the difference between the adopted measurement and the PHANGS-HST determination is quite small ( 2 ).

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3627 as a dense-gas/infrared outlier, notes HCO+ non-detection, and links its offsets to low nuclear star-formation efficiency relative to off-nuclear regions.

quotes.quote: MRK 331 and NGC 3627 have non-detections of .

NGC 6240 and NGC 3627 are two outliers deviated from the relationship of IR and dense gas tracer luminosity. This makes their dense-gas star formation efficiencies, which are traced by infrared to dense gas luminosity ratio, about three times lower than the average value of other galaxies.

Our observation of NGC 3627 is consistent with , who found that star formation efficiency in the nuclear region of NGC 3627 is several times lower than that in off-nuclear star-forming region. This could explain why NGC 3627 also shows offsets from both HCN and \ correlations.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3627 as an interacting weak-bar spiral whose warp, X-ray emission, molecular gas, and radio continuum trace disturbed bar and arm structures.

quotes.quote: NGC 3627 . This is an interacting spiral galaxy with a weak bar. A warp is present in the optical image and is probably caused by tidal interaction with the other member of the Leo Triplet (NGC 3628). X-ray observations with Beppo SAX and Chandra suggest that the bulk of X-ray emission has a star-forming origin georgantopoulos02 . The molecular gas is observed in a central concentration along the leading edges of the bar, at the end of the bar, and extending over nearly 4 in spiral arms that extend from the ends of the bar regan01 . The same unusual distribution is present in the RC emission at high resolution (see Fig. figura1a ).

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 3627 in CO along its major axis, finding exponential CO and 13CO profiles, central-deficient molecular-ring structure, and similar CO/13CO kinematics.
