quotes.quote: We have conducted half-beam spacing mapping of M82 over an area of 4' 2.5' and major axis mapping of NGC 3627, NGC 3628, NGC 4631, and M51. The radial distributions of \ and ^ 13 CO in NGC 3627, NGC 3628, and M51 can be well fitted by an exponential profile.
For the barred spiral galaxies NGC 3627 and NGC 4631, however, both \ and ^ 13 CO intensities peak at radius of 1 kpc ( 0.5') rather than at the nuclei.
In NGC 3627, the scale lengths are 2.8 kpc and 3.9 kpc for the \ and ^ 13 CO emission, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 3627 across spiral and bar regions, noting little central H emission and near/far-side geometry effects visible in extinction comparisons.

quotes.quote: We give positions for each pointing, including those mosaicked across the spiral and bar regions of NGC 3627 and NGC 4321.
Blanked regions indicate locations with low signal to noise, as in NGC 3627 and NGC 7331 where there is little H emission detected in the center.

A slight effect from the galaxy geometry may be seen between the near and far sides of NGC 3627 and NGC 4321, where the H ii regions in the south-east arm and north-west arm (respectively) at the connection with the bar (Figure fig:optical ) exhibit the same extinction in the stellar continuum as in the Balmer decrement. NGC 4321 unfortunately has the stellar continuum for only one side, however NGC 3627 is somewhat clearer.

sources.summary: The paper highlights NGC 3627 as a LINER/Seyfert system whose molecular-hydrogen fits prefer a warm-gas cutoff, indicating directly detected warm molecular gas excess.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:norm_warm illustrates this phenomenon in the galaxy NGC 3627, evaluating ^2 along the ridge line of best-fitting indices n for each T_ , and contrasting this galaxy with the more typical case of an indefinite minimum. Evident in the fits to NGC 3627 is a single best-fitting lower cutoff temperature of T_ 120 K. In such cases, a continuation of the power-law distribution to temperatures below this best-fitting value is counter-indicated, as it degrades the fit. This indicates that in these cases, the bulk of \ is directly detected via rotational emission in a warm gas component excess.

sources.summary: The paper reports the first tentative H2CO detection in M 66, noting weak 2-1 emission and no prior Costagliola or Lindberg observations.

quotes.quote: ( J =2-1) ( = 18.196 GHz) was observed with the 100-m telescope in 20 galaxies and only three galaxies are detected ( > 3 ): IC 342 , M 66 and NGC 660 . This is the first measurements of \ 2-1 reported in external galaxies, and the first \ detection in M 66.
M 66 : In M 66 \ 2-1 is only detected on about 2 level, but this is the first tentative detection of \ in M 66 . It was not observed by Costagliola2011 nor Lindberg2011 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3627 as a strongly barred interacting EMPIRE galaxy whose HCN is prominent along the bar and whose HCN/CO ratio shows a large radial decline.

quotes.quote: It also covers strongly barred galaxies (NGC 2903, NGC 3627), flocculent disks (NGC 5055, NGC 6946), strong nuclear bursts (NGC 2903, NGC 4321, NGC 6946), Virgo cluster members (NGC 4254, NGC 4321), and interacting galaxies (NGC 3627, NGC 5194).
The HCN intensity peaks at the center of each target and then appears prominent along the spiral arms (e.g., NGC 5055, NGC 5194 and NGC 6946) and central bars (e.g., NGC 2903 and NGC 3627).

We observe the largest HCN/CO declines in NGC 3627 ( 0.60dex), NGC 4254 ( 0.60dex), NGC 5194 ( 0.60dex) and NGC 6946 ( 0.55dex). Again, we see no strong morphological divide, NGC 3627 and NGC 6946 are strongly barred galaxies with prominent dense gas emission in their centers and bars, while NGC 4254 and NGC 5194 are unbarred galaxies rich in molecular gas.

sources.summary: NGC 3627 is used as a position-velocity example whose anomalous red- and blue-shifted central emission satisfies most criteria for an outflow candidate.

quotes.quote: The S-shaped rotation curve along the major kinematic axis (left panel) as well as the constant velocity along the minor kinematic axis (right panel) of NGC 3627 (upper) and NGC 4654 (lower) can be seen. In NGC 3627, multiple features suggest an outflow, such as, faint red-shifted emission at roughly +16 arcsec along the major axis extending almost to 150 \ and faint red- and blue-shifted emission at +5 and +14 arcsec along the minor axis with a velocity extent of 100 and 150 , respectively, possibly moving against the galaxy's primary sense of rotation. These velocities are impossible to reach for gas in a purely rotating disk. The features found in NGC 3627 fulfill most of the criteria listed above making it one of our outflow candidates (see Section sec:Results:OCLs ).

sources.summary: The paper reports K-band SRT measurements for NGC 3627, finding its 18.6 GHz flux compatible with existing fits but its 24.6 GHz flux in possible excess pending higher-frequency confirmation.

quotes.quote: For NGC 3627 and NGC 5055, the 18.6 GHz flux density is still compatible with the fit in ; the 24.6 GHz flux density, instead, is in excess of the fit extrapolation and larger than that at 18.6 GHz (see Fig. fig:sed for NGC 3627). With the current data, it is difficult to asses if the 24.6 GHz excess seen in NGC 3627 and NGC 5055 is due to an additional component, such as AME, or if it is just a statistical fluctuation. Observations at larger frequencies, like those that will be possible with the forthcoming Q-band receiver for SRT, are needed to confirm this finding. In fact, a model including synchrotron and free-free only can be fit to the entire 24.6 Ghz range, resulting in minor modifications with respect to the fit for 10 GHz.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC3627 as one of the three brightest ELVES central galaxies, hosting 27 confirmed satellites and being excluded from the main MW-analog analysis as part of a higher-mass small-group subset.

quotes.quote: The three brightest central galaxies are NGC3379, NGC3627, and M81, which are also the first three in the K_s band magnitude-ranked figure legend. They host the largest number of confirmed satellites -- 57, 27, and 24, respectively. Interestingly, these three groups have the largest number of massive satellites and as mentioned in Carlsten:2022 , their group luminosity is significantly larger than that of the primary host. As explained below, we exclude these three `small groups' from the analysis as their higher mass, and likely different formation history from bonafide MW-analogs may bias the results.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3627 as a Leo Triplet member with a flat mass-weighted age profile, continuous central star formation, and spiral arms possibly disrupted by interactions.

quotes.quote: Another exception is NGC 3627, which also shows a nearly flat MassW age radial profile. NGC 3627 is a member of the interacting group Leo Triplet Zhang1993 , interactions with nearby galaxies could have triggered episodes of star formation e.g., Renaud2019 that might explain its peculiar radial assembly history of stellar mass.
On the other hand, NGC 3627 seems to have been forming stars in its center continuously (this is also reflected in its inner radial bin, in Fig. fig:radial_inside_out ).

In the particular case of NGC 3627, the spiral arms-disk contrast is very low in the third age bin, which could be indicating a more recent origin of its spiral arms, possibly connected to a disruption driven by interactions with other galaxies from the Leo triplet (NGC 3623, and NGC 3628).

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 3627 as a late-type spiral in the Leo Triplet, with weak bar features, possible warping, and kinematics affected by elongated structures.

quotes.quote: NGC 3627 NGC 3627 is a late-type spiral galaxy with weak bar features and loosely wound arms forming an asymmetric spiral structure. It is also interacting with other galaxies in the Leo Triplet. The TRM detects that the inclination angle shows significant changes with radius, indicating that the galaxy maybe affected by a significant warp: however in that case the warp would be present inside the optical radius R_ 25 310'' . The P.A. shows a radial oscillation of moderate amplitude.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3627 as an interacting Sb spiral whose dust-to-gas ratio rises with radius, unlike most sample galaxies, while using a global luminosity-based CO conversion.

quotes.quote: Note also that since no metallicity gradients are available for NGC 4826 (Sab) and NGC 3627 (Sb), we used the CO-to-H _ 2 conversion factor corresponding to the global H band luminosity of each object (Boselli et al . 2002). NGC 3627 constitutes a notable exception, for its dust-to-gas ratio increases with radius. It is an interacting Sb spiral in the Leo triplet that gets redder in the outer regions, possibly because of SF quenching as a result of gas removal or exhaustion.

sources.summary: The paper flags NGC3627 as an extreme-sample LINER whose unusually steep measured metallicity gradient strongly shifts the sample median, while including its bulge spectrum makes the gradient less extreme.

quotes.quote: It should be noted here that the final figure changes dramatically (to --0.5742) with the inclusion of NGC3627, which falls into our `extreme' sample but shows an extremely, and probably unphysically, steep metallicity gradient (--2.880) according to our measurements. It is not known why this galaxy displays such a steep gradient. However, it may also be interesting to note that were we to include the bulge spectrum (initially excluded as the galaxy is classified as a LINER) the gradient is reduced to --2.203, leading to an average within the `extreme' sample of --0.4895.

sources.summary: The review identifies NGC 3627 as a strong m=2 spiral but reports no consistent phase evidence supporting long-lived spiral density waves.

quotes.quote: Only one of the objects (NGC 3627) shows the contrary evidence.
The results are shown in figure  fig1 , where we can see that only three objects (NGC 628, NGC 3627 and NGC 5194) show signs of strong m=2 spiral patterns.
No consistent phases were found for NGC 3627.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 3627's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 3627 is a grand-design spiral galaxy in the Leo Triplet at a distance of D= 10.1 1.7 Mpc (NED redshift-independent median) with an active nucleus either classified as a transition/Sy 2 ho_search_1997-1 or a LINER veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 . It is in tidal interaction with NGC 3628 zhang_high-resolution_1993 . The first N band photometry was taken by and .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3627 as a KINGFISH host, placing SN 1989B in a bright spiral arm and deriving dust extinction from its dust-mass map.

quotes.quote: 1 determined using Cepheid data 1999ApJ...522..802S 2 derived from redshift in Benetti2004MNRAS.348..261B 3 determined using Cepheid data WangSN2006X2008ApJ...677.1060W 4 determined using Cepheid data Wang2008ApJ...675..626W SN 1989B in NGC 3627 SN 1989B is a normal SNe Ia which occurred in a bright spiral arm of NGC 3627, 15''W and 50''N of its nucleus.
Figure figHSTSN1989B shows the position of the SN in the dust mass surface density map, compared to optical SDSS and Hubble Space Telescope images.

The total dust extinction, calculated from the KINGFISH dust mass column density map of NGC 3627, in V band at the position of SN 1989B is A_V = 3.0 mag, in case of ext = 1, when all of dust is taken into account ( _ dust = 9.667 10 ^ 5 g cm^ 2 ).

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3627 as the most inclined sample galaxy, dominated by overlapping CO and H-alpha sightlines and affected by group interaction.

quotes.quote: NGC 3627 -- the most inclined galaxy in our sample with i 55^ - is our only target where sightlines with overlapping CO and \ emission are the most common.
In NGC 3627 particular, the phenomenon is harder to identify because of the brightness of star-forming complexes at the bar ends e.g., see beuther17 .
The one galaxy where overlap sightlines dominate (NGC 3627) exhibits on average low SFR and sSFR values and is also part of a group currently undergoing a large-scale interaction.

sources.summary: NGC3627 shows bar-related variation: a second-bin cloud-lifetime outlier, bar-end star formation, and an especially short cloud lifetime caused by two bright CO and star-forming regions.

quotes.quote: A small number of bins in Figure fig:radprof exhibit very high or very low values of the molecular cloud lifetime (e.g. \ the second bin of NGC3627 or the inner bin of NGC4535), which is plausibly caused by the presence of a bar in these galaxies.
In NGC3627, Beuther2017 suggest that the interaction between the end of the bar and the spiral arms might induce strong star formation events.

The particularly short cloud lifetime ( t_ CO =3.8_ 0.8 ^ +2.6 Myr) measured in the second bin of NGC3627 (as well as a short t_ fb =0.8_ 0.4 ^ +0.7 Myr) is due to the fact that two very bright regions both in and in CO dominate this bin.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3627 as an interacting, barred galaxy with high velocity dispersion, notes uncertainty in the turbulent contribution, and says it is better matched by the model.

quotes.quote: On the other hand, NGC 3627 is also interacting Haynes1979 , showing high velocity dispersion and hosting a strong stellar bar. It is unclear how much of the observed dispersion is actually turbulent (and hence would be expected to contribute to mixing) and how much is due to imperfect modelling of the bulk velocity on our resolution scale -- given the high velocities associated with the bar and the substantial inclination. NGC 3627 is, however, better matched by the model.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC3627 as a barred, interacting galaxy with complex molecular gas, potentially variable recent star formation, a special metallicity-gradient calibration, and feedback-time upper-limit issues.

quotes.quote: Another possible exception to a constant star formation history is the galaxy NGC3627, which has a pronounced bar Beuther2017 , shows complex molecular gas motions (Be s li et al. \ in prep.) and is in interaction with the neighbouring galaxy NGC3628, and for which the star formation history may have varied recently e.g. Rots1978, Haynes1979 .

For NGC3627, we use the metallicity gradient determined by Kreckel2019 , scaling the absolute value to compensate the different calibration method used in these two studies, based on the average metallicity of galaxies present in both samples.

We note that in most cases these same bins are also affected by blending (e.g. NGC3627, NGC4535), which means that only an upper limit on the feedback time-scale can be estimated (see point (viii) of Section sec:requirements and Figure fig:blending ).

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3627 as strongly barred, fitting inner bar flow despite sparse velocity sampling and finding broad agreement with the outer rotation curve.

quotes.quote: NGC 3627 As this galaxy is strongly barred, we have included a bar flow in the inner part of our fitted model, even though that part of velocity map is rather sparsely sampled. The resulting rotation curve is shown in Figure  fig.N3627 , with the fitted radial and azimuthal bar flow velocities SS07 indicated, respectively, by the green and blue points with error bars. dB08 did not attempt to fit the barred region, but their outer rotation curve is in reasonable agreement with ours.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 3627 among the barred galaxies; its cloud masses reach higher scales than NGC 5068, and its catalogued ISM emission fraction remains radially steady.

quotes.quote: Bar - Six of our 10 galaxies have stellar bars (NGC 1637, NGC 2903, NGC 3627, NGC 5068, NGC 5643, NGC 6300).
In the homogenized data, the maximum mass of clouds reaches much higher scales in some galaxies (NGC 3627) than others (NGC 5068).
For example, NGC 3627 and NGC 5068 consistently show f_ cat 0.9 and 0.4 , respectively, implying the ISM emission is not changing in structure radially.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3627 as a strongly barred spiral with bar-arm starburst signatures, likely interaction with NGC 3628, perturbed western morphology, and single-pointing LEGUS coverage.

quotes.quote: NGC 3627 NGC 3627 is a strongly barred spiral galaxy Buta_2015 that has been studied in large atomic and molecular gas surveys Walter_2008,Leroy_2009,Kennicutt_2011 and exhibits strong burst signatures at the two interfaces of bar and arm in the north and south Kennicutt_2011 , making it a prime candidate for the study of bar-arm interactions Beuther_2017 . In addition, NGC 3627 appears to be interacting with the neighbouring galaxy NGC 3628 see, e.g., Soida_2001 , which is expected to be the cause of a perturbed morphology of its western arm, and a higher H _2 /H _ I mass ratio relative to other local star-forming galaxies e.g., Saintonge_2011 . LEGUS observed this galaxy in a single-pointing.

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 3627 has one of the largest KT18 rescalings, good semivariogram-shape agreement, and O3N2 behavior resembling the Scal diagnostic.

quotes.quote: The best-fit constants for rescaling are found using the python package scipy.optimize , and range from a factor of 606 for NGC 4254, to 1.48 10^ 4 for NGC 3627.

While the total amount of variance predicted by the KT18 model is far less than what is observed, the scale over which metallicities are correlated and the overall shape of the predicted semivariograms show very good agreement to the data, especially for NGC 1087, NGC 1672, and NGC 3627.

For NGC 1672, NGC 2835, and to some extent NGC 3627, the shape of the empirical semivariograms computed using the O3N2 diagnostic matches the shape of the semivariogram computed using the Scal diagnostic, with the overall values of the semivariogram translated upwards by a constant factor (typically 4 10^ 4 8 10^ 4 ).

sources.summary: For NGC 3627, the paper reports a high model value, unsatisfactory fits to star-formation and H i profiles, and a gas Toomre parameter decreasing from the center outward.

quotes.quote: Whereas most of these galaxies have a including between 200 and 400 , the of NGC 3627 and NGC 6946 are 1053 and 2102 , respectively.
In NGC 3627, the star formation and H i radial profiles could not be fitted in a satisfactory way.
In NGC 3627 the Toomre parameter decreases from Q_ gas =4 6 in the galaxy center to Q_ gas =1.5 2 at R=5 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3627 as a barred galaxy with radio-polarization studies showing a regular bar-aligned field strongest at the leading edges and an anomalous magnetic arm east of the bar.

quotes.quote: Another barred galaxy which has been studied in detail in radio polarization is NGC 3627 (Soida et al. \ ). The regular field in the bar region is again aligned parallel to the bar's major axis, being strongest at the leading edges of the bar. However, east of the bar the field behaves anomalously, forming a `magnetic arm' crossing the gaseous arm.

gmw_00000193.name: NGC 3628
gmw_00000193.overview: NGC 3628 is an edge-on Sb spiral galaxy [arXiv:2305.12526, arXiv:1601.06155] featuring a dust lane [arXiv:2008.06039] and a central starburst nucleus [arXiv:2008.06039, arXiv:1202.2713]. As a member of the interacting Leo Triplet [arXiv:2305.12526, arXiv:1202.2713], it displays a warped stellar disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0610114] and an extensive 140-kiloparsec tidal stream [arXiv:1509.04710, arXiv:2302.10948].

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 3628 as a nearby edge-on starburst galaxy, resolving compact central radio sources as supernova remnants and connecting radio/X-ray emission to star-formation and SN-driven activity.

quotes.quote: We present radio very long baseline interferometry (VLBI) and X-ray studies of the starburst galaxy . The VLBI observation at 1.5 GHz reveals seven compact (0.7 7 parsec) radio sources in the central 250 parsec region of . Based on their morphology, high radio brightness temperatures ( 10^5-10^7 K), and steep radio spectra, none of these seven sources can be associated with active galactic nuclei (AGNs); instead, they can be identified as supernova remnants (SNRs), with three of them appearing consistent with partial shells. Notably, one of them (W2) is likely a nascent radio supernova and appears to be consistent with the star formation rate of \ when assuming a canonical initial mass function.

\ is a nearby D = 10.6 Mpc or z=0.0028 , 2015MNRAS.450.3289G , edge-on Sbc galaxy 1990ApJ...362..434C . The source is also a member of the interacting group the Leo Triplet, together with the other two galaxies NGC 3623 and NGC 3627. \ is a sub-LIRG (sub-luminous infrared galaxy, 10^ 10 L_ <=L_ IR <10^ 11 L_ ) with infrared luminosity L_ IR =1.7 0.4 10^ 10 L_ 2021A&A...649A.105F . In particular, no evidence of an obscured AGN was found in \ 2021A&A...649A.105F .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3628 as an Sb Leo Triplet galaxy with a 140 kpc stellar stream, revisiting whether the stream came from interaction with NGC 3627 or a dwarf-galaxy minor merger.

quotes.quote: In this work, we identify and describe a star cluster, which we call NGC 3628-UCD1 (hereafter UCD1), embedded in a stellar stream around the nearby spiral galaxy NGC 3628. NGC 3628 is an Sb galaxy with M _ V = -21.37 . It is located in the Leo Triplet, a loose group with two other large companions, NGC 3623 and NGC 3627. An obvious stellar stream extends 140 kpc away from the galaxy, shown in Fig. 1. UCD1 is located within the plume nearest to NGC 3628.

In the literature, this stellar stream has often been considered the result of an interaction between NGC 3628 and another galaxy in the group, typically NGC 3627. However, the properties of UCD1 give us cause to consider a potential dwarf galaxy minor-merger scenario as the source of the stream.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 3628, an edge-on Sb galaxy in the Leo Triplet, and argues that its extended UV emission is dust-scattered light from diffuse extraplanar dust.

quotes.quote: We test whether such an additional thick dust disc agrees with the observed infrared emission in NGC 3628, an edge-on galaxy with a clear signature of a thick dust disc. We extend the far-ultraviolet radiative transfer models to full-scale panchromatic models. Our model, which contains no fine-tuning, can almost perfectly reproduce the observed spectral energy distribution from UV to mm wavelengths. These results corroborate the interpretation of the extended UV emission in NGC 3628 as scattering off dust grains, and hence of the presence of a substantial amount of diffuse extra-planar dust.

NGC 3628 is a well-known edge-on Sb galaxy at a distance of 10.95 Mpc. We adopt the same distance to NGC 3628 as 2015arXiv151103789S , who used the mean of a suite of different redshift-independent distance estimates from the literature, mainly based on the Tully-Fisher relation. It forms the so-called Leo Triplet together with NGC 3623 and NGC 3627.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 3628 among dusty interacting ULIRG-like sources, but says its X-ray measurements are background-compatible and unsuitable for the analysis.

quotes.quote: The ULIRG objects are very interesting on their own. Next to the standard two-bump spectrum, these objects have a very bright emission in the infrared-UV range of the spectrum. These objects (ARP 220, NGC 3628, NGC 3690), with ARP 220 as the most prominent ULIRG, are interacting galaxies for which a large amount of dust is observed, indicated by the very bright infrared-UV emission. Besides the infrared-UV peak, the radio emission as well as the emission in the X-ray and gamma-ray bands for our selected objects show similar behavior as found for the FSRQ objects.

NGC 3628 is a well known object, which has been studied in detail. The X-ray measurements given in for this object are within errors compatible with background, which makes the data not suitable for our analysis. It should be noted however, that since the X-ray measurements for this object are compatible with background a very strong dust component is expected to block the high-energy emission which makes this object very interesting to study on its own.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3628 in the Leo Triplet, separates likely cirrus from asymmetrical outer light, and notes possible intragroup-light contamination.

quotes.quote: NGC 3628 NGC 3628 is a member of the Leo Triplet along with M65 and M66. There are a few structures of note in the field that can be attributed to cirrus. An elongated linear feature northwest of M65 and a blob southeast of M66 seen in Dragonfly imaging are both found in 100 m IRAS/IRIS imaging. Cirrus structure in the southeast corner of the field also matches well between Dragonfly and IRAS/IRIS.

The north-south asymmetry about the midplane of NGC 3628 is not likely to be confused with cirrus. The outskirts extend southward from both sides of the disc towards the other massive group members. These (relatively) bright lobes are also accompanied by more extended and fainter light.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3628 as a Leo Triplet spiral with a long tidal plume, extensive gas loss, disturbed HI, and ALFALFA-detected extensions tied to interaction with M66.

quotes.quote: Another tidal encounter in Leo I is thought to have occurred between the large spirals NGC 3628 and M66 (NGC 3627) to create one of the largest known tidal tails extending 100 kpc off of NGC 3628 tripmodel, maptrip, tripobs . In M66, an asymmetric HI disk and a recent (less than 1 Gyr ago) burst of star formation coinciding with the time of the suspected encounter both suggest that an interaction between NGC 3628 and M66 led to NGC 3628's extensive gas loss tripmodel2, tripother .

noted a faint optical plume extending from NGC 3628 directed eastward away from the other two galaxies. Subsequent 21-cm observations found an associated neutral hydrogen plume roughly 100 kpc in length, an HI bridge connecting NGC 3628 to NGC 3623, and a distortion in the HI disk of NGC 3627 tripmodel, maptrip . Despite the appearance of a bridge of gas connecting NGC 3623 to the perturbed NGC 3628, simulations of a collision that is prograde for NGC 3627 and retrograde for NGC 3628 but not involving NGC 3623 match best with the observations of the plume toomre, tripmodel, maptrip .
Additional HI located outside the area covered by previous HI observations is found by ALFALFA to extend south of NGC 3627 and northward from NGC 3628.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3628 as an interacting Leo Triplet spiral with an eastward H I tidal tail, peculiar magnetic field, strong 2.64 GHz emission, and measured radio properties.

quotes.quote: The Leo Triplet group of galaxies is best known for the impressive bridges of neutral gas that connect its members. One of the bridges forms a large tidal tail extending eastwards from NGC 3628 that hosts several H i plumes and carries the material from this galaxy to the intergalactic space. The magnetic fields of the member galaxies NGC 3628 and NGC 3627 show morphological peculiarities, suggesting that interactions within the group may be caused by stripping of the magnetic field.

However, the highest signal comes from NGC 3628. The total intensity of that galaxy is equal to 364 17 mJy. Owing to the high signal level, emission in the vicinity of NGC 3628 is affected by the sidelobes of the telescope beam.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 3628 as the Leo Triplet galaxy whose tidal tail hosts Leo-TDG, using its distance, plume, encounter with NGC 3627, and progenitor properties.

quotes.quote: One of the best examples of such objects is the Leo Triplet, a nearby group of galaxies, known for a large tidal plume extending eastwards from NGC 3628. Originally described by Zwicky , the plume was later confirmed by photographic observations by Kormendy Bahcall .

Assuming the distance to NGC 3628 of 12.15 Mpc (median distance calculated from the Tully-Fisher-determined values from the NASA Extragalactic Database), this yields the linear resolution of our maps of some 4.7 kpc. This structure is also visible in a widefield image showing the tail of NGC 3628, provided to us by S. Mandel reproduced in Fig.

As estimated by Rots , the closest encounter between NGC 3627 and NGC 3628 may have happened 8 10^ 8 yrs ago. It is rather bluer than its supposed progenitor 0.65 compared to 0.8 for NGC 3628, Paturel et al. hyperleda , it is located exactly at the tip of the tidal tail, and has mass of some 10^ 8 M_ , typical for such objects.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3628-UCD1 as a compact star cluster in NGC 3628's stellar stream, with UCD-like size, old metal-poor population, and possible origin as a stripped dwarf-galaxy nucleus.

quotes.quote: Using Subaru/Suprime-Cam wide-field imaging and both Keck/ESI and LBT/MODS spectroscopy, we identify and characterize a compact star cluster, which we term NGC 3628-UCD1, embedded in a stellar stream around the spiral galaxy NGC 3628. The size and luminosity of UCD1 are similar to Cen, the most luminous Milky Way globular cluster, which has long been suspected to be the stripped remnant of an accreted dwarf galaxy. The object has a magnitude of i=19.3 mag ( L _ i =1.4 10^ 6 ). UCD1 is marginally resolved in our ground-based imaging, with a half-light radius of 10 pc.

Spectral analysis reveals that UCD1 has an age of 6.6 Gyr , [ Z / H ] =-0.75 , and [ / Fe ] =-0.10 . We propose that UCD1 is an example of an Cen-like star cluster possibly forming from the nucleus of an infalling dwarf galaxy, demonstrating that at least some of the massive star cluster population may be created through tidal stripping.

sources.summary: The paper finds 267 compact 8 micron cores in interacting NGC 3628, measuring a thick 438 pc core disk affected by warping and noting coherent loop and arc structures.

quotes.quote: To study the vertical distribution of the earliest stages of star formation in galaxies, three edge-on spirals, NGC 891, NGC 3628, and IC 5052 observed by the Spitzer Space Telescope InfraRed Array Camera (IRAC) were examined for compact 8 m cores using an unsharp mask technique; 173, 267, and 60 cores were distinguished, respectively.

The rms dispersion of the midplane distance, considered to be the half-thickness of the disk distribution of 8 m cores, is 105 pc for NGC 891, 438 pc for NGC 3628, and 74 pc for IC 5052. For NGC 891 and IC 5052, these are good representations of the disk half-thickness, but NGC 3628 is warped as a result of a recent interaction it is part of the Leo triplet, Arp 317, arp66 , and it is much thicker on the western side.

The half-thickness is 438 pc for NGC 3628, but that includes a broad region affected by a galaxy interaction. There is also a loop of several cores above the disk in NGC 891 and possibly coherent loops of cores in the thick part of NGC 3628. Some of the 8 m cores show coherent structures suggestive of kpc-scale corrugations, shingles, or spiral arms. For example, NGC 3628, which is slightly less inclined than the other galaxies, has arcs composed of several cores each that could be spiral arms viewed tangentially.

sources.summary: The paper tests its method on NGC 3628, recovering the known tidal tail in processed SDSS data, though with lower spatial detail because of noise.

quotes.quote: NGC 3628 As a next test target, we chose the well known tidal Tail of NGC 3628. Once again, we see the feature in the processed SDSS data. Figure 3628comp shows a direct comparison between a deep image, made by Steve Mandel http://www.galaxyimages.com , using a 6.3" refractor telescope. In this case, the entire feature seems to be visible, though not as spatially detailed as in the comparison image. This is caused by noise, since these images still have a rather low SNR.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3628 as a nearby edge-on HII LINER with a central starburst, possible small AGN, Leo Triplet interaction, and disturbed HI disk.

quotes.quote: NGC 3628 is a near-by ( 12.8 Mpc) HII LINER galaxy. It is of morphological type Sb pec and is seen edge-on. Although this galaxy's nucleus is dominated by a central starburst, it may also be host to a small AGN 2006A&A...460...45G,2001MNRAS.324..737R . NGC 3628 is a member of the Leo triplet, a group of 3 closely interacting galaxies. Observations of the HI disk reveal a very disturbed distribution, pointing to the interaction between NGC 3628 and its near-by companion NGC 3627 1978AJ.....83..219R, 1979ApJ...229...83H .

sources.summary: The paper compares the GBT H2CO absorption properties for NGC 3628 with earlier measurements and cites CO and continuum imaging that constrain its source size.

quotes.quote: NGC 3628: The 1_ 10 1_ 11 transition was detected in absorption by Baan1986,Baan1990 and confirmed by Araya2004 , while the 2_ 11 2_ 12 transition was not detected by Baan1990 . The GBT 1_ 10 1_ 11 line properties show good agreement with those measured by both S = 2.5 mJy Baan1986 and S = 2.97 0.21 mJy Araya2004 to within the calibration uncertainties (Figure fig:NGC3628IC860M83IR15107FormSpec ). Israel2009 measured the ^ 12 CO J= 1-0 , J= 2-1 , J= 3-2 , J= 4-3 and ^ 13 CO J= 1-0 , J= 2-1 , and J= 3-2 emission from this galaxy. From their CO J= 3-2 map one notes that the source size is <15^ . 450 m and 850 m continuum imaging Stevens2005 expose a 30^ core.

sources.summary: The paper searched NGC 3628 for gamma-ray emission, found no evidence above 100 MeV, and derived flux and luminosity upper limits while noting possible starburst activity.

quotes.quote: Our original aim was to search for gamma-ray emission from NGC 3628 (Arp 317), one of the three galaxies called the Leo Triplet', for which possible starburst activity has been reported based on XMM observations Tullman2006 .

For NGC 3628 (Arp 317), the test statistic, TS , returned by gtlike is consistent with zero, indicating there is no evidence of gamma-ray emission. Thus we calculated upper limits to gamma-ray flux from NGC 3628 of 1.4 (1.3) 10^ 9 cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 , at 95 C.L., above 100 MeV for the period 2008 August 4 to 2010 July 31 (2010 July 31 to 2012 October 27). This is translated to gamma-ray luminosity upper limit of 2.5 (2.3) 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 assuming the distance of 12 Mpc which is derived as the median of 8 measurements, which range from 6.7 to 14.2 Mpc NED .
A search for gamma-rays from NGC 3628 (Arp 317), for which possible starburst activity has been reported, found no evidence for > 100 MeV emission.

sources.summary: The paper interprets NGC 3628 as a CHANG-ES circular-polarization source whose steep band-to-band index favors Faraday conversion despite an inverted in-band Stokes-V spectrum.

quotes.quote: NGC 3628, like the other galaxies, has a steep band-to-band spectral index (Table table:spectral_indices ), arguing for the Faraday conversion interpretation. However, its Stokes V in-band spectral index is positive ( _V _L = + 2.7 ) which is highly discordant in comparison to the declining spectral indices seen for the other sources. It may be that absorption effects can turn over the spectral index, making it positive. Indeed, the measurement of _V _L is based on splitting a weak signal into 4 parts, as described in Sect. sec:alpha_measurements and is prone to errors. Nevertheless, this is a good example to show how the spectrum can not only be flattened (as for NGC 660) but can actually become inverted.

sources.summary: The atlas describes NGC 3628 as dust-obscured but showing large-scale and sub-kpc outflows in H-alpha, CO, and soft X-rays, with molecular gas expanding north of the disk.

quotes.quote: NGC 3628 (see Fig. Figure_AB3 ): The nuclear emission in this galaxy is classified as Dusty given the large amount of dust that prevents from seen the galactic centre. At larger scales (up to 100 ) reported for the first time the presence of an outflow in this galaxy, based on H observations, reporting that it is coincident with an X-ray plume. This work was latter supported by several other studies in other wavelength bands. Specifically, reported the existence of is a sub-kpc scale outflow north of the disc, detected with CO(1-0) emission, expanding at 50 km s ^ 1 outwards of the galaxy. This was confirmed by using also molecular gas observations, and they mention that this outflow is spatially coincident with a large scale plasma outflow ( 10 kpc) that was detected with soft X-ray observations with Chandra.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 3628's mid-plane asymmetry map strongly affected by quadrupolar signal; visual inspection identifies a warped disc with distortion around 100 arcsec and signal near 75 arcsec.

quotes.quote: For NGC 3628, NGC 4710, NGC 5073, and NGC 5529 the mid-plane asymmetry maps are strongly affected by a quadrupolar signal (which affects at least half the extent of the galaxy itself). This signal implies that we were unable to correctly identify the disc PA, despite our careful analysis. Visual inspection of the S ^4 G images of these galaxies and corresponding unsharp masked images (Fig.  fig:sample , middle panels) identifies strong and intrinsically asymmetric features in the discs (such as warps and/or lopsided discs) which may have affected the measurement of the disc PA. In particular, NGC 3628 hosts a warped disc. The distortion is visible in the image at |x| 100 arcsec, while a strong quadrupolar signal appears at |x| 75 arcsec.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 3628 has apparent soft X-ray filamentary emission, but modeling its halo makes the connection only marginally significant.

quotes.quote: The starburst galaxy NGC 3628 was observed by Dahlem \ (1996) and shows a striking filamentary structure, extending south from the galactic nucleus to a point source 2.7 arcmin away, in images in the 0.75 keV band (0.44--1.21 keV) and 1.5 keV band (0.73--2.04 keV); this point source itself appears connected to another point source a short distance to the SE, which Flesch Arp (1999) identify with a z=2.15 quasar. The contour levels selected by Dahlem \ correspond quite well to the 3 levels derived from simulation; after master veto rate filtering to match theirs and binning in 15-arcsec pixels I find a background count rate away from NGC 3628 in the 0.75-keV band of 0.23 counts pixel ^ 1 , corresponding to a 3 level of 0.73 counts pixel ^ 1 , which is actually below their adopted 2.5 level. The filament is thus apparent in contours at better than the 3 level.

However, this does not take account of the extended X-ray halo found by Dahlem \ (1996) around NGC 3628 (and clearly visible as excess emission in their figure 2). If we model this crudely as an extra uniform contribution to the background --- taking the average in a region between NGC 3628 and the southern source, the background is pushed up to 0.59 0.04 counts pixel ^ 1 -- then the connection between NGC 3628 and the point source can occur by chance in a few per cent of cases, so the feature is only marginally significant.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3628 with Chandra imaging that locates the nucleus, asymmetric disk-related soft X-ray emission and absorption, plus a variable off-nuclear ultraluminous X-ray source.

quotes.quote: NGC 3628 The true-color, smoothed Chandra image of NGC 3628 is shown in Figure 12 b . The blue dot at the center of the image represents the galaxy nucleus. The soft X-ray emission to the north is located on the top of the disk while there is strong absorption to the south. The white circle is a well-known, variable ULX (or IXO) with an X-ray luminosity of 1 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 . It is located 900 25 pc from the nucleus in projection (Strickland et al. \ 2001).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3628 as a starburst/low-level AGN with neutral-hydrogen plumes, apparent minor-axis ejection, and multiple X-ray/quasar sources at ULX luminosities.

quotes.quote: NGC 3628: This starburst/low level AGN exhibits extensive outgassed plumes of neutral hydrogen and is apparently ejecting X-ray material along its minor axis. Point X-ray sources might be associated with this ejected material and a number have already turned out to be quasars (Arp et al. 2002). We lists 7 of these quasars which have L_X between 38.8 and 39.6 erg s ^ 1 . Column 10 shows that six of these quasars fall within the same radial distances as most of the ULX's in this Table. The seventh, which has the strongest, L_X = 39.6 erg s ^ 1 , falls at a greater distance, very similar to the radial distance of the bright BL Lac from NGC 1365.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3628 as a nearby edge-on peculiar galaxy with an apparent excess of surrounding QSOs, concluding that gravitational lensing cannot explain the concentration.

quotes.quote: In section 3, we apply this microlensing effect to NGC 3628 which is a well-studied nearby edge-on Sbc peculiar galaxy and find that the concentration of QSOs in the fields of NGC 3628 could not be caused by gravitational lensing.

NGC 3628 is a well-studied nearby edge-on Sbc peculiar galaxy in the Leo Triplet. Dahlem et al. (1996) have listed many X-ray sources in the ROSAT-detected hot gaseous halo of NGC 3628, and they found this number density is higher than background with a 1.5 deviation. They also pointed out that most of these X-ray sources in the halo of NGC 3628 are probably background AGNs.
The number density of QSOs in the fields of NGC 3628 is shown in Fig.3, which is about three time the number density of background.

sources.summary: NGC 3628 is described as an ongoing merger in the Leo Triplet with a warped stellar disk, multiwavelength activity, extraplanar diffuse radio emission, and H i absorption at the nucleus.

quotes.quote: This on-going merger galaxy has been the target of numerous studies. As a member of the Leo triplet, it appears to have suffered most from the mutual interaction, its stellar disk being strongly warped. Its activity is manifest over the entire electromagnetic spectrum, from radio (Reuter et al. ) through X-rays (Dahlem et al. ). Despite the strongly elongated synthesised beam of the WSRT at this declination, significant diffuse radio continuum emission is seen to extend in the z-direction on either side of the disk, with some bright protusions marking the footpoints of the most active regions. Strong absorption in the H i map marks the location of the active nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 3628 along its major axis, reporting CO detections, an exponential radial CO profile with a 3.5 kpc scale length, and regular position-velocity behavior.

quotes.quote: We have conducted half-beam spacing mapping of M82 over an area of 4' 2.5' and major axis mapping of NGC 3627, NGC 3628, NGC 4631, and M51. The radial distributions of \ and ^ 13 CO in NGC 3627, NGC 3628, and M51 can be well fitted by an exponential profile.

Similar to the stellar luminosity profiles, the observed \ radial distributions in NGC 3627, NGC 3628 and M51 could also be well fitted by an exponential fit in R I(R) = I_ 0 \ exp \ (-R/R_ 0 ), where R is the galactocentric distance, R_ 0 is the disk scale length, and I_ 0 is the central integrated intensity.

The solid curves in Fig. Fig3 show the least-squares fit to the data excluding the center point as the nuclear gas could be a distinct component, yielding I_ 0 = 32.6 K km s ^ 1 and R_ 0 = 3.5 kpc for the \ emission in NGC 3628

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3628 as an interacting Leo Triplet outlier with an extended component, retains it in the edge-on sample, excludes it from mean disk ratios, and notes overestimated thick-disc light.

quotes.quote: The only one of these outliers remaining is NGC 3628, an interacting galaxy in the Leo Triplet which we visually identified to have an extended component which is faint enough to go through our sample selection criteria. If we were making our selection criteria more restrictive, we would be excluding galaxies which do not have an extended component other than a thin and a thick disc, so we decided to keep NGC 3628 in the sample.
For this range of velocities, the mean value of the stellar disc mass ratio is M_ T /M_ t =0.48 0.03 once the two outliers, NGC 3628 and NGC 5084 have been excluded.
In four galaxies (NGC 1596, NGC 3628, NGC 4081, and UGC 08737) our thick disc brightness estimate at 0<z<0.5z_ s overestimates the actual light emission.

sources.summary: The paper searched Fermi-LAT data for gamma-ray emission from NGC 3628/Arp 317, found no >100 MeV detection, and set flux and luminosity upper limits while noting possible starburst activity.

quotes.quote: Our original aim was to search for gamma-ray emission from NGC 3628 (Arp 317), one of the three galaxies called the Leo Triplet', for which possible starburst activity has been reported based on XMM observations .

For NGC 3628 (Arp 317), the test statistic, TS , returned by gtlike is consistent with zero, indicating there is no evidence of gamma-ray emission. Thus we calculated upper limits to gamma-ray flux from NGC 3628 of 1.4 (1.3) 10^ 9 cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 , at 95 C.L., above 100 MeV for the period 2008 August 4 to 2010 July 31 (2010 July 31 to 2012 October 27). This is translated to gamma-ray luminosity upper limit of 2.5 (2.3) 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 assuming the distance of 12 Mpc which is derived as the median of 8 measurements, which range from 6.7 to 14.2 Mpc .

sources.summary: The paper finds no nuclear X-ray point source in NGC 3628, only diffuse star-formation emission, and sets MIR-X-ray limits on any putative AGN.

quotes.quote: NGC 3628 analysed the deepest of the observations of the AGN candidate NGC 3628 and does not detect a nuclear point source but only diffuse emission due to star formation. They derive an upper limit on the observed X-ray luminosity of = 37.1 . Owing to the a number of relatively bright circumnuclear sources, the data are not usable to determine the properties of the putative AGN. The MIR--X-ray relation puts an upper limit on the intrinsic X-ray emission of any AGN to = 40.67 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3628 as hosting a CO-traced molecular outflow whose size, mass, velocity, and kinetic energy are compared with superbubble model predictions.

quotes.quote: These shells likely outline a larger shell around the central starburst region. observed a molecular outflow in NGC 3628 with the CO (J=1-0) line. The outflow shows almost a structureless morphology with a very weak bubble breaking through in the north part of the central outflow. Its size of 370 - 450 pc, inferred molecular mass of 2.8 10^7 M _ , and outflow speed 90 10 km s ^ 1 , suggest a total kinetic energy of molecular gas of 3 10^ 54 erg.
The corresponding predicted molecular masses are of order several times 10^6 - 10^7 M _ , also consistent with observations of NGC 253 and NGC 3628.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3628 as a starburst with FUV halo filaments, radio and ionized-gas halos but no extraplanar thermal dust, H I/UV filament coincidence, and decreasing FUV-NUV color.

quotes.quote: Filaments are most easily visible in the FUV, and they are seen in most starburst galaxies (M82, NGC 253, NGC 3079, NGC 3628, NGC 4666, and NGC 5775), as well as two normal galaxies (NGC 4522, NGC 4330). The filaments in the latter two are associated with gas stripped by ram pressure in the Virgo cluster.

The starburst galaxies all have eDIG, soft X-ray halos, extraplanar thermal dust emission (except for NGC 3628), and radio halos. H 1 emission is also seen around all galaxies in the sample with high resolution maps, although in some cases the beamsize makes it unclear how much of the emission is truly extraplanar.

The UV and H 1 filaments do coincide near the disk in the starburst galaxies NGC 253, NGC 3628, NGC 4666, NGC 5775, and NGC 3079 (not all shown in Figure figure.multiwave_sb ). The UV halo is also brighter and more vertically extended on the side of the galaxy with the large H 1 filament in NGC 891, and a similar phenomenon is seen in NGC 3044.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3628 as a starburst galaxy with molecular-outflow and X-ray plasma connections, a luminous off-nuclear X-ray source, and a modeled complex spectrum.

quotes.quote: NGC 3628: 2012ApJ...752...38T studied this starburst galaxy and found connection between MO and emission line plasma in X-rays. A study of the source was carried out by 2001ApJ...560..707S using Chandra data, where they find a luminous X-ray source 20'' away from the nucleus. The spectrum required one thermal component ( APEC ), neutral intrinsic absorption and a high energy absorption in the Fe K band which was modeled using an inverted Gaussian. The powerlaw slope is pegged at = 1.5 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3628 among low-accretion AGN lacking reflection, noting X-ray and mid-infrared studies find no reflection component, weak FeK constraints, low obscuration, and small torus contribution.

quotes.quote: We find that 12 objects do not present any signs of reflection (namely UGC 5101, NGC 3628, NGC 3718, M 106, NGC 7331, NGC 612, 2MXJ 1105+5856, NGC 3621, NGC 3786, M 88, NGC 5252 and 2MXJ 2325-3826).

NGC 3628 has been studied at both X-rays and Mid-IR with no indications for a reflection component. For instance, Gonzalez-09 found that this object, although presenting ionized lines, does not constrain the FeK line, with an EW <77 \ keV . Additionally, found that this object has a low obscuration, with a column density below 10^ 22 \ cm^ 2 , and Gonzalez-17 through a Mid-IR analysis found that this object presents 10 of torus contribution in the spectrum.

sources.summary: Using Herschel profiles, the paper finds that NGC 3628 requires an additional thicker dust component: the vertical emission dominates above the model at 1-2 kpc, making it an outlier from single-component fits.

quotes.quote: The vertical profiles for 6 galaxies (ESO 209-009, NGC 0891, NGC 3628, NGC 4013, NGC 4437, and NGC 4631) show the presence of another (thicker) dust emission component in all or almost all wavebands. This component clearly dominates over the model at larger vertical distances above the galaxy mid-plane, usually starting at z=1-2 kpc. For these galaxies, the average ^2=19.8 10.1 compared to ^2=1.04 0.45 for the whole sample without these outliers. This proves that another dust component is required to better describe the observed profiles of these six galaxies.

sources.summary: NGC 3628 is discussed as a highly inclined, high-optical-depth case not selected by PAH or HCN-vib criteria, illustrating possible inclination bias.

quotes.quote: In Fig. fig:EWRatios there is one object, NGC 3628, with a _N > 3.5 but is not selected as a CON by either the PAH EW ratios or the HCN-vib surface brightness. This is a highly inclined galaxy which suggests that the nuclear optical depth obtained from our spectral decomposition fitting may be sensitive to such galaxies with strong dust lanes obscuring the line of sight, which may bias our estimates of the number of CONs.

The case of NGC 3628 is one such example however this case also shows the strength of the PAH EW method to select CON candidates. While there may be a bias falsely selecting highly inclined sources as CON candidates, this bias may actually counteract the exclusion of the most diluted CONs by their host galaxy ( < 0.4 ) and so while individual objects may be miss-classified, the total number of CONs likely remains accurate.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3628 as a distorted M66-group spiral with a very faint 135 kpc tidal tail containing a likely tidally destroyed dwarf component.

quotes.quote: In the M 66 group, the second brightest spiral galaxy NGC 3628 exhibits strong distortions at the periphery. A tidal tail of very low surface brightness stretches to the east of NGC 3628 jennings2015 , the length of which reaches 42 or 135 kpc. This structure is well seen also in the HI line stierwalt2009 . The dwarf galaxy dw 1120+1337= NGC 3628, UCD 1 is the brightest part of this tail and was probably destroyed by the tidal influence of NGC 3628 .

sources.summary: The paper finds no central point source in NGC 3628, but a central diffuse patch and multiple point sources, suggesting starburst-related stellar processes power the LINER emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 3628 (T2, Sb) has five point sources in its central kiloparsec including a bright point source 1 kpc away from the center. There is no point source at the center of the galaxy, but a central patch of diffuse emission. The spectrum of the diffuse emission is well fitted by a variety of models with a two-temperature plasma or a power-law giving the best fits. The resulting luminosity is ( L_ 0.5-10 keV =4.6 10^ 38 erg s ^ 1 ).

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 3628's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 3628 is a nearby disturbed edge-on spiral galaxy in the Leo Triplet at distance of D= 12.2 2.8 Mpc (NED redshift-independent median) with a prominent dust lane covering an active starburst nucleus, which is optically classified as an AGN/H II transition object ho_search_1997 . Its association with a compact radio and X-ray source is controversial filho_nature_2000,nagar_radio_2005,yaqoob_hard_1995,gonzalez-martin_x-ray_2006,flohic_central_2006 . In addition, the detection of the AGN-indicative is also controversial dudik_mid-infrared_2007,goulding_towards_2009 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3628 as one of three galaxies with clear extraplanar dust, sharing the highest thick-dust scale height and showing UV halo excess beyond PSF-wing effects.

quotes.quote: Three galaxies (NGC 891, NGC 3628, and UGC 11794) show clear evidence for the existence of extraplanar dust layer.
NGC 891 has the highest z_d^ thick as does NGC 3628.
The vertical profiles of NGC 891, NGC 3628, and UGC 11794 clearly show the halo emission excess which cannot be explained with the PSF wing.

sources.summary: The paper produces a single NGC 3628 Swift spectrum, finding a neutral-absorbed power law consistent with previous spectral studies.

quotes.quote: NGC 3628 Only a single summed spectrum of NGC 3628 could be produced from the Swift data. We found a power law with a neutral absorber to be best-fitting to the Swift data. Previous studies have found the same spectral model to be best-fitting Dahlem1995,Gonzalez-Martin2009 , with a similarly low photon index ( ^2_R = 1.36 ) to that found in our best-fitting model.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3628 as a disturbed edge-on disk whose known tidal tail, thick buckled disk, and boxy outer isophotes trace disk-dominated low-surface-brightness light.

quotes.quote: Fig.  n3628range shows the unusual case of NGC 3628. Although the tidal tail has long been noted, the buckled and disturbed disk is thick, with the deepest isophotes showing a boxy 2:1 structure. One could safely assume that all light contributing to the low surface brightness components of this galaxy belongs to the disk.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3628 as a dust-lane Sb starburst with LINER emission, ambiguous AGN status, and radio emission matching optical disk protrusions.

quotes.quote: NGC 3628 NGC 3628 is a Sb spiral galaxy which has a starburst nucleus and a prominent dust lane liu2005 . NGC 3628 hosts a LINER nuclei and classify it as an AGN from the spectral fitting of the X-ray data, whereas suggested that the LINER emission in NGC 3628 is starburst-related rather than AGN related due to the absence of an X-ray point source at the center of the galaxy. In our image, we see radio emission overlapping features in the optical image with finger-like protrusions at several locations across the disk.

gmw_00000194.name: NGC 3660
gmw_00000194.overview: NGC 3660 is a face-on spiral galaxy containing an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1310.2770]. It is classified as an unabsorbed Seyfert 2 candidate [arXiv:0808.2385, arXiv:1601.02702] that exhibits rapid X-ray variability [arXiv:0808.2385, arXiv:1601.02702] and may host a nuclear starburst [arXiv:1508.05065, arXiv:1310.2770].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3660 as an X-ray-unabsorbed Seyfert 2 with ASCA variability, concluding the nucleus is probably viewed directly and may lack a broad-line region.

quotes.quote: NGC 3660 As there have been no observations of NGC 3660 with XMM-Newton or Chandra , we use the ASCA observation and its documentation in the Tartarus catalogue. The ASCA spectrum is fitted well by a power-law with no absorption above the Galactic column. Fig. ngc3660lc shows the light-curve for the 26 ks observation which shows significant variability on short time-scales.

NGC 3660 The X-ray variability of this source on short time-scales and unabsorbed profile of its X-ray spectrum leads us to the conclusion that we are genuinely viewing the nucleus of NGC 3660 directly. This leads us to believe that NGC 3660 also lacks a broad line region as we believe with NGC 3147. However, the origin of the X-ray variability is not necessarily nuclear as variability was recently discovered in the ultra-luminous X-ray (ULX) source, M82 X-1 mucciarelli06 . A Chandra observation of NGC 3660 should be able to resolve any ULXs as we have shown for NGC 4501.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 3660 as a controversial true Seyfert 2 candidate whose MIR-X-ray position supports AGN activity but whose nuclear MIR may be starburst contaminated.

quotes.quote: NGC 3660 Based on observations which show an unabsorbed and variable powerful source in NGC 3660, classified this objects as a true Seyfert 2 candidate. This finding was supported by new data presented in . However, in , we conservatively classified NGC 3660 only as candidate AGN because of the controversial optical classifications spanning everything between H II region and Sy 1.8 (see also . This controversy is likely caused by a nuclear starburst that dilutes the optical spectra.

Its position directly on the MIR--X-ray correlation supports the AGN nature but would favour the existence of a dusty nuclear structure s well. However, as pointed out in , the nuclear MIR emission is likely contaminated or even dominated by a nuclear starburst. While this could mean the absence of a torus in NGC 3660, argue that broad optical emission lines are actually present and thus NGC 3660 would not be a true Seyfert 2 in any case.

sources.summary: The paper confirms NGC 3660 as an unabsorbed Seyfert 2, finding weak broad lines, no X-ray obscuration, normal reflection, and no evidence for a Compton-thick hidden nucleus.

quotes.quote: NGC 3660 has been shown to have weak broad optical/near infrared lines, no obscuration in the soft X-ray band, and no indication of ``changing look'' behavior. The only previous hard X-ray detection of this source by Beppo-SAX seemed to indicate that the source might harbor a heavily obscured nucleus. However, our analysis of a long-look Suzaku observation of this source shows that this is not the case, and that this source has a typical power law X-ray continuum with normal reflection and no obscuration. We conclude that NGC 3660 is confirmed to have no unidentified obscuration and that the anomolously high Beppo-SAX measurement must be due to source confusion or similar, being inconsistent with our Suzaku measurements as well as non-detections from textsl Swift-BAT and RXTE .

NGC 3660 was presented as a true Seyfert 2 candidate in the Brightman Nandra (2008) sample. This source has very weak measured broad lines and no evidence of polarized broad optical lines (Tran 2001; Shi 2010; Tran 2011). From an ASCA observation, this source displayed rapid X-ray variability on kilosecond scales, strong evidence that the source is viewed directly.

sources.summary: The paper highlights NGC 3660 as a rare high-accretion AGN lacking polarized broad lines, using its luminosity, black-hole mass, and Eddington ratio as a test of transient BLR models.

quotes.quote: One object of particular interest in their sample is NGC 3660, which shows no polarized broad lines (Tran 2001). Its \ luminosity is estimated to 6.5 10^ 41 (Kollatschny et al. 1983). With the bolometric luminosity of L_ Bol 10^ 45 , along with the SMBH mass 10^7 estimated from the Magorrian relation (Magorrian et al. 1998), we find an Eddington ratio of about unity. NGC 3660 is the only such object in the Wang Zhang (2007) sample.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 3660's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 3660 is a face-on spiral galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0123 ( D 60.8 Mpc) with an active nucleus, which is a ``true''-Seyfert 2 candidate ( brightman_nature_2008,bianchi_simultaneous_2012 ; but see shi_unobscured_2010 . The optical classification of this AGN varies throughout the literature: first classified it as a Seyfert/H II transition object, while later it was classified either as borderline LINER/H II contini_starbursts_1998 , Sy 1.8 veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 , or Sy 2.0 moran_classification_1996, bianchi_simultaneous_2012 . This controversy might be related to the possible existence of a nuclear starburst on the one hand imanishi_compact_2003 , and very weak broad emission lines on the other (see discussion in shi_unobscured_2010 and bianchi_simultaneous_2012 ).

gmw_00000195.name: NGC 3690
gmw_00000195.overview: NGC 3690 is a luminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:1801.04328] undergoing a late-stage merger with IC 694 [arXiv:1801.04328, arXiv:1101.0132]. The system hosts heavily obscured active galactic nuclei [arXiv:astro-ph/0306375, arXiv:1508.05065] alongside vigorous, dust-enshrouded starburst activity [arXiv:astro-ph/0306375, arXiv:astro-ph/0311154].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3690 as Arp 299B+C in the Arp 299 merger, noting AGN/starburst activity, complicated velocity components, and stronger HCO+ and C2H peak temperatures than IC 694.

quotes.quote: Arp 299, a well-known merging system, is composed of two galaxies: IC 694 (Arp 299A) and NGC 3690 (Arp 299B+C, 45'' to the west of Arp 299A). Here Arp 299C refers to the overlapping interface of the two merging galaxies, which is also a starburst HII region e.g., gehrz83, aalto97, casoli99 . It is believed that IC 694 and NGC 3690 both host an AGN with strong starburst ballo04,gm06 , although for IC 694 there is still some controversy gallais04 . Previous interferometry observations have revealed that the ^ 12 CO(1-0), ^ 13 CO(1-0), and HCN(1-0) emission peak mainly at these three components Arp 299A, B, and C aalto97,casoli99 .

Of our observation towards NGC 3690, the 29'' beam only yielded to us a spectrum of Arp 299B and Arp 299C together. The spectrum shows complicated velocity components. No signal of H ^ 13 CN line appears, and the velocity components of HC ^ 15 N are coincident with those of HCN, even if the spectrum was less smoothed(see Figure spec-hcn ). However, considering the low S/N of these two isotopes and the high N/ ^ 15 N abundance ratio ( 200 - 400 ), these features could be artificial.

We also find that the HCO ^+ and C _2 H lines in the spectrum of NGC 3690 have higher peak temperatures (T _R^* , 12 mK for HCO ^+ and 4.0 mK for C _2 H, respectively) than the corresponding lines in the spectrum of IC 694 (T _R^* 9 mK for HCO ^+ and 3.5 mK for C _2 H), but both lines of IC 694 have higher integrated intensities.

sources.summary: The paper places the obscured AGN in NGC 3690, notes neutral iron emission and elevated Fe K flux, and describes it as a possible Compton-thick Seyfert 2.

quotes.quote: The hard X-ray spectrum of the nucleus of NGC 3690 indicates that the obscured AGN is probably located there Zezas2003 . Ballo2004 detected the \ emission feature in both system components NGC 3690 and IC 694. They found that the energy of the emission line in NGC 3690 is consistent with neutral iron, however in our fit the energy of the emission line is not well constrained (Table tbl_feka ). The measured 6.4 keV \ flux is 10 times larger than that expected from star-formation suggesting the presence of an AGN in NGC 3690.

The other 3 Seyfert 2s in our LIRGs sample (NGC 3690, NGC 5135, and NGC 7130) might be Compton thick AGN. In fact, NGC 5135 and NGC 7130 have been classified as Compton thick based on their large \ EW (see Levenson2004, Levenson2005 ). The \ EW of NGC 3690 is 0.93 keV (see Table tbl_feka ). It is slightly less than the typical values of Compton thick AGN ( > 1 keV). However the star-formation contribution to the hard X-ray continuum is 30 in NGC 3690 (see Sect. ss:hard_sfr ) and thus it decreases the observed EW of the \ emission line.

sources.summary: The paper presents panoramic spectroscopy of Mrk 171, resolving two components with H II-region condensations and finding star formation rather than AGN activity.

quotes.quote: Observations of Mrk 171, aimed at conduction of panoramic spectroscopy, were undertaken with the Byurakan 2.6-m telescope using spectrograph "VAGR". Within the two components of the galaxy, Mrk171W and Mrk171E, there were differentiated eight condensations of starforming activiity, i.e. HII-regions, and no sign of AGN activity was revealed inspite of existing suggestions. Mrk 171 is one of the unique nearest galaxies with complicated morphology and richness in physical characteristics. The main part of the galaxy is "pressed" by two big zones of radiation in the radio ( ). The double structure of the galaxy is interpreted as an initial stage of merging processes (e.g., ).

For the two components of the galaxy we use designations Mrk171E and Mrk171W, according to their mutual disposition. Along with these, their other designations as components of double galaxy NGC 3690, triple system ARP 299, and fivefold system VV118, are correctly placed on Fig.1a, to avoid more cases of an incorrect usage in the literature. In any case, the structures differentiated in the two components of the galaxy Mrk 171 are composed of HII-regions, i.e the regions exposed to processes only of starforming activity.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3690 as the Arp 299 merger with two AGN, where the western nucleus dominates hard X-rays and both nuclei may suffer strong obscuration.

quotes.quote: NGC 3690 -- Arp 299 NGC 3690 is a merger system with two AGN, NGC 3690E (Arp 299A; sometimes wrongly called IC 694; see NED and yamaoka_supernova_1998 ) and NGC 3690W (Arp 299B). First evidence for the presence of an highly obscured AGN came from observations della_ceca_enshrouded_2002 , while the presence of a double AGN was then later postulated on the basis of and observations zezas_chandra_2003,ballo_arp_2004 . Recently, NGC 3690 was observed with , where ony NGC 3690W was detected above 10 keV and thus contributes at least 90 per cent of the observed hard X-rays ptak_focused_2015 . Its intrinsic luminosity is estimated to be = 43.26 with an obscuring column density of = 24.6 .

NGC 3690E, on the other hand, has to be much fainter or even more obscured (see also alonso-herrero_uncovering_2013 ). The predicted X-ray properties from the MIR--X-ray correlation ( = 43.45 0.43 ; = 24.46 0.49 ) for NGC 3690W match very well the direct estimates from , while those for NGC 3690E ( = 43.02 0.43 ; = 25.37 0.49 ) agree with the highly CT scenario. However, as pointed out in , the nuclear MIR fluxes of both nuclei might still be significantly contaminated by star formation. In particular, NGC 3690E might be completely star formation dominated.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3690 + IC 694 as a five-nucleus interacting system, using MIR photometry and optical spectra to estimate dust-to-gas ratios despite sparse line constraints.

quotes.quote: NGC 3690 + IC 694 ^1 from Armus et al. (1989) NGC 3690 + IC 694 show a system of five nuclei. The MIR photometry has been performed by S01 in single regions and with different apertures. It is thus possible to determine the d/g ratios for each nucleus. The optical spectra (Armus et al. 1989) are available in the A, B, and C regions (see S01 for nomenclature). The number of lines is very small allowing only a rough modelling.

The SED of the continuum is shown in Fig. 9 for the summed spectrum. Notice that most of the data in the radio-to-X-ray range are well explained by a model with \ = 500 \ and t=3.3 Myr (solid lines). However, lower velocities are also important to explain the FIR bump (dash-dot lines).

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC3690 as an earlier-stage merging system with IC694, also called Arp 299 or Mrk 171, whose infrared luminosity and emission lines indicate starburst activity.

quotes.quote: NGC3690 is also a merging system, but in contrast to NGC3310 it is in an earlier merger phase. The system is composed of NGC3690, which is the western part of the merger and IC694, the eastern component. The whole system is also known as Arp 299 or Mrk 171.

The infrared surface brightness of NGC3690 is at least twice that of IC694 (Friedman 1987). The total mass of gas calculated from radio observations is about 2 10^ 11 M_ (Casoli . 1989) The high IR luminosity (L_ FIR =1.2 10^ 12 L_ ) (Soifer 1987), results from re-radiating dust heated by the starburst activity. This is supported by multi-wavelength studies (Gehrz 1983, Friedman 1987, Nakagawa 1989). Detailed analysis of emission line ratios using the diagnostic diagrams of Veilleux and Osterbrock (1987) clearly indicate a stellar origin of the ionizing continuum (Friedman 1987).

sources.summary: The paper separates NGC 3690 East from the western galaxy, treats it as part of the hard-X-ray-quiet sample, and tests its known Fe K emission contribution.

quotes.quote: In NGC 3690, the western and eastern galaxies are treated separately due to the difference in classification, and their FIR luminosity ratio is assumed to be 1:2 based on the 38 m study (Charmandaris et al. 2002). The X-ray luminosity is as observed in the rest-frame 2-10 keV band, corrected only for Galactic absorption.

Although detection of the Fe K line has been reported for the XMM-Newton spectra of NGC 3690 East (Ballo et al. 2004) and Arp 220 (Iwasawa et al. 2005), the line does not have sufficient counts in the Chandra data for a significant detection (see e.g., Clements et al. 2002). These two objects are included in the integrated hard-band spectrum and their contribution to the total spectrum is also investigated.

However, given our stacking method, which is biased for sources that are bright and/or with a long exposure, it is at least worth examining whether Arp 220 and NGC 3690 E, the two known Fe K emitters from their XMM-Newton spectra, dominate the line detection. These two objects and the rest of the HXQ sample were integrated separately, and their spectra are presented in Fig. 2b and 2c. Both spectra show a 2 excess in the Fe xxv band with comparable line fluxes.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3690 as a dusty interacting ULIRG-like source kept because of strong flat-spectrum synchrotron radio emission and possible neutrino relevance.

quotes.quote: The ULIRG objects are very interesting on their own. Next to the standard two-bump spectrum, these objects have a very bright emission in the infrared-UV range of the spectrum. These objects (ARP 220, NGC 3628, NGC 3690), with ARP 220 as the most prominent ULIRG, are interacting galaxies for which a large amount of dust is observed, indicated by the very bright infrared-UV emission. Besides the infrared-UV peak, the radio emission as well as the emission in the X-ray and gamma-ray bands for our selected objects show similar behavior as found for the FSRQ objects.

Even though it is not determined whether or not there is an AGN located inside ARP 220 and NGC 3690, we decided to keep them in our selection based on the fact that strong (flat spectrum) radio emission has been observed which is most likely due to synchrotron emission. Hence particle acceleration takes place at these sources. This in combination with the very strong dust component, makes them very interesting candidates for high-energy neutrino production through the proton-matter interaction channel.

sources.summary: NGC 3690 is included as an Arp 299 LIRG merger with hundreds of clusters in clumps, B-band color measurements, and HCO+, HCN, and other dense gas tracers.

quotes.quote: For this paper, we used multi-wavelength observations of 7 merging systems in the survey: Arp 220, ESO 185--IG13, Haro 11 (ESO 350--IG038), NGC 1614, NGC 2623, NGC 3256, and NGC 3690. Arp 220 is an Ultraluminous Infrared Galaxy (ULIRG), NGC 1614, NGC 2623, NGC 3256, and NGC 3690 (Arp 299) are LIRGS, and ESO 185--IG13 and Haro 11 are blue compact galaxies. Their distances range from 38 to 90 Mpc.

Arp 220 is anomalous in having very few clusters within the clumps, whereas NGC 3256 and NGC 3690 have hundreds. As the figure illustrates, the distributions of clusters within the clumps are fairly uniform, although the concentration of clusters increases in regions within a clump where there are brighter arcs and substructure, such as in NGC 3256 and NGC 3690.
Dense gas tracer isotopes of CO and CN were detected in NGC 1614 konig , isotopes of CO in NGC 2623 brown , and HCO ^+ , HCN, and other dense gas tracers in NGC 3690 jiang .

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 3690 as an obscured AGN host whose nuclear mid-infrared spectrum arises from hot dust in a high-covering-factor clumpy torus.

quotes.quote: The GTC/CC nuclear spectrum of NGC 3690 was observed under excellent seeing conditions, FWHM _ 8.7 m =0.3 . Thus the unresolved MIR emission originates from a region of 60 pc in size. The nuclear spectrum shows dust continuum emission likely produced by the obscured AGN and a moderately deep 9.7 m silicate feature.

We fitted the IR emission of NGC 3690 with the clumpy models. The dust is located in a high covering factor torus and heated by an AGN with L_ bol ( AGN )=3.2 0.6 10^ 44 erg s ^ 1 . The torus high extinction along the line of sight dominates over the foreground extinction.

sources.summary: The paper monitored NGC 3690, found no UV transients, and detected powerful variable X-ray emission inside the galaxy consistent with multiple Chandra/NuSTAR sources including AGNs and possible ULXs.

quotes.quote: NGC 3690 The weekly monitoring of the galaxy NGC 3690 ranged from the second week of January 2014 to the last week of September 2014. Good data have been collected for 35 by the XRT and for 34 epochs by the UVOT (see Table observations_NGC3690 ). We identified no transient events in the UVOT data collected during our observations. In the XRT data we identified a powerful and significantly variable emission generated inside the target galaxy NGC 3690 at coordinates RA = 11:28:31.7, Dec = +58:33:46.4 (NGC3690-I1, see Fig. NGC3690_X_center ).

Several X--ray sources are present within NGC 3690 as observed by Chandra Zezas2003 and NuSTAR Ptak2015 at a position consistent with our variable X--ray source. Two of them have been identified as AGNs, some others might be classified as ultra-luminous X--ray sources (ULX).

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC3690 as the earliest-stage sample merger, with two traceable galaxies, nuclear cluster formation, an optically hidden compact starburst, and future overlap-region cluster formation.

quotes.quote: NGC3690 is the only system in our sample, where the two galaxies are still traceable, similarly to that observed in the more famous Antennae galaxies. In Figure fig:agemass2 we see that the most massive clusters are currently forming in the peak of the H luminosities of each component, which is close to the nuclear regions of NGC3690A and B. However, it is important to notice that also in this system, our H tracer and optical study of the cluster population is not able to provide information about the extreme compact nuclear starburst of NGC3690A which coincides with the peak emission in dense gas and radio continuum aalto1997 detected in the galaxy. This starburst is almost completely extincted in the optical as is revealed by an H depression where the CO emission peaks on the nucleus.

With a gas fraction of 30 larson16 , it is very likely that cluster formation will drift towards the overlapping region of the NGC3690 system in its future evolution. This phase is also predicted by numerical simulations kruijssen12a, renaud15 , which find enhancement in star and cluster formation in the overlapping region because of the high gas pressure exerted by the merger during the final coalescing phase.

sources.summary: The paper links NGC 3690's B1 nucleus to the obscured AGN in Arp 299 and finds separate hard diffuse emission and a compact starburst component near B2.

quotes.quote: Turning now to the case of X-ray Source 6, which we identify with the nuclear component B1 in NGC 3690, we find that it has a very hard X-ray spectrum typical of a heavily obscured AGN, indicating that this component is the nucleus of NGC 3690. In the light of these spectroscopic results we associate the recently discovered highly obscured AGN in Arp 299 (Della-Ceca 2002) with the nucleus of NGC 3690. The observed luminosity of this nucleus is 6.3 10^ 40 (0.1-10 keV), and contributes 2 and 15 of the overall X-ray emission of Arp 299 in the 0.1-2.0 keV and 2.0-10.0 keV X-ray bands respectively.

Given the presence of diffuse hard X-ray emission (extending up to 8 keV) in the region of NGC 3690, some fraction of this luminosity might be associated with a starburst component. However, from Fig. 1, we do see a clear displacement between the location of the AGN and the peak of the diffuse emission (coincident with Src 4), indicating that the most vigorous star-formation is not spatially coincident with the AGN. This extended X-ray source (Src 4) with a luminosity of 6.6 10^ 39 (0.1-10.0 keV band) is associated with the second brightest near IR peak in NGC3690 (component B2).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3690 as a disturbed Arp 299 component with strong mid-infrared continuum, hard radiation, extraordinary star formation, and evidence favoring a Compton-thick AGN in source B.

quotes.quote: The central region of NGC 3690 displays a hard radiation field characterized by a [ Ne iii ] / [ Ne ii ] ratio 1.8 . It also hosts a strong continuum from 5 to 16 m which can be explained as thermal emission from a deeply embedded ( A_ V 60 mag) compact source, consistent with the mid-infrared signature of an active galactic nucleus (AGN), and in agreement with recent X-ray findings.

Our observations are actually consistent with all these possibilities if we consider that IC 694 contains the larger fraction of molecular gas and cold dust and dominates the IR luminosity of Arp 299. This implies that the starburst in source A has been taking place for a longer period of time than in sources B and C since the former seems to be closer to dynamical equilibrium than the two sources in NGC 3690 which are even more disturbed and obviously still strongly interacting Casoli99 .

A best fit with an A_ V of 60 mag is derived in the central region of NGC 3690 (source B1) using a homogeneous mixture of dust with radiating sources. The nuclear region of IC 694 harbors enshrouded massive star forming activity. No evidence for an active galactic nucleus is found in our mid-IR spectra.
