We confirm that C and C ^ are sites of recent starburst events based on the elevated values of the [ Ne iii ] 15.6 m flux and the high [ Ne iii ] / [ Ne ii ] ratio, which is similar to those of classical young starbursts. Source B is characterized by a unique mid-IR spectrum which is dominated by a hot dust continuum extending shortward of 5 m, and which is the strongest of the Arp 299 system. Given the ample presence of dust and the strength of the radiation field, this continuum is consistent with the recent Chandra/Beppo-SAX results which favor the presence of a Compton thick AGN at this location.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3690 as Arp 299’s western component, containing an obscured AGN and a likely H2O maser hotspot near its core.

quotes.quote: Arp 299 is a merging system at D 40 Mpc, composed of two main sources, IC 694 and NGC 3690 (for an alternative nomenclature, see Sect. 4.2.3), that are separated by 22 \ in east-west direction (e.g. Sargent Scoville 1991). A FIR luminosity of several 10 ^ 11 L _ (Casoli et al. 1992) places Arp 299 near the boundary between luminous (LIRGs) and ultraluminous (ULIRGs) infrared galaxies. Supporting the merging scenario, two highly extended \ tails have been identified by Hibbard Yun (1999). Radio and infrared observations reveal three main regions of activity (e.g. Gehrz et al. 1983; Aalto et al. 1997; Casoli et al. 1999), the nuclear regions of IC 694 and NGC 3690 and an interface where IC 694 and NGC 3690 overlap. NGC 3690 contains a deeply enshrouded active galactic nucleus (AGN), while the situation with respect to the similarly obscured nuclear region of IC 694 is less clear (e.g. Della Ceca et al. 2002; Ballo et al. 2004; Gallais et al. 2004).

Although the location of the western hotspot is close to the core of NGC 3690, the velocities of the maser emission, the CO lines and the systemic velocity of NGC 3690 do not match perfectly ( 3100 km s ^ 1 from CO, z_ sys = 3121 km s ^ 1 (NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database (NED)), versus 3150 km s ^ 1 from H _2 O). Interestingly, velocities near 3150 km s ^ 1 as seen in H _2 O are consistent with those of the overlap region. A comparison of the profile shown in Fig. arp299a with the central one in Fig. arp299b suggests a slight offset in position (a few arcsec in east-west direction) and weaker peak emission in the latter case. This is within the uncertainties of pointing and calibration, but maser variability can also explain the differences. With the H _2 O emission likely originating from IC 694 and NGC 3690, Arp 299 is the fourth extragalactic system beyond the Magellanic Clouds that is known to exhibit = 18 cm OH and = 1.3 cm H _2 O maser emission (in NGC 253, NGC 1068 and M 82, such masers are also observed; see Weliachew et al. 1984; Turner 1985; Baudry Brouillet 1996; Gallimore et al. 1996; Henkel et al. 2004).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3690 as a merging AGN/LIRG system with IC 694, possible emission contamination, anomalously large HCO+/HCN, and chemistry requiring abundance enhancement.

quotes.quote: Finally we note that NGC 3690 is in the process of merging with IC 694 (the system is called Arp 299 or Mrk 171). The nuclei of NGC 3690 and IC 694 are separated by only 20 arcseconds and both nuclei have an AGN gar06 . The \ and HCN emission from the IC 694 nucleus is much stronger than that from the NGC 3690 nucleus aal97 ; thus, although we are centered on NGC 3690, the emission may have a contribution from IC 694.

We will address the role density plays in these ratio trends later, however, NGC 3690, NGC 4258 and NGC 6240 are quite anomalous in having unusually large ratios of HCO ^+ /HCN, and variations in chemistry are almost certainly needed to explain these ratios. NGC 3690 and NGC 6240 were both part of the jun09 study of ULIRGS, and these galaxies were among those with the largest HCO ^+ /HCN ratio.

sources.summary: The paper says the eastern component of NGC 3690 has a complex radio structure with a central bulge-associated component classified as AGN-like and other regions resembling starbursts.

quotes.quote: NGC 3690 (E) The east radio component of this interacting system has a complex radio structure, and the map shows four discrete areas. The central radio component, the one associated with the bulge, has flat values of in the centre steepening rapidly outwards and getting higher than 1.1 suggesting a radio-AGN. The other areas have the characteristics of SB regions. The wide range of values in the histogram are due to the fact the radio source is composed of smaller radio components at 8.44 GHz. We classify this object as AGN/SB in the sense that it contains both AGN and SB.

sources.summary: The paper says the western component of NGC 3690 has a complex merger radio structure, including one AGN-like steepening region and other flatter starburst-like regions, so it is classified AGN/SB.

quotes.quote: NGC 3690 (W) The west radio companion of this galaxy merger also has a complex radio structure, and in the map we see three discrete areas. The NE area displays steep values of getting larger than 1.1 going outwards, suggesting an AGN. The other two regions show a flat centre, steepening outwards slightly, probably from a SB. The wide range of values in the histogram are due to the fact the radio source is composed of smaller radio components at 8.44 GHz. We classify the west radio companion of the galaxy pair as AGN/SB in the sense that it contains both AGN and SB.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3690 as a clearly interacting, multi-component system with progenitor nuclei, off-nuclear starbursts, VLBI point sources, and a potential AGN.

quotes.quote: NGC 3690 : This clearly interacting system consists of multiple components. Two of the components them are associated with the nuclei of the progenitors, NGC 3690E (east) and NGC 3690W (west), while the others are a combination of off nuclear starbursts. The strongest nucleus (NGC 3690E) has been observed with VLBI. At least 30 point sources have been found plus a potential AGN e.g., Neff04 . Due to the proximity of this system and its spatial extent, the 33 GHz emission is resolved at the D configuration resolution ( 2"), clearly showing 5 components (see white crosses in map from Figure fig:fig1 ).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3690 as the reference sample’s only LIRG and a late-stage merger, with composite LINER and star-forming activity plus red SED evidence for an AGN.

quotes.quote: NGC 3690 : NGC 3690 is the most IR-luminous galaxy in the Reference Sample and its only LIRG ros14 . This galaxy is nearing final pass; although not at coalescence, it is still classified as a late-stage merger. We measure the AGN contribution at 30 . var15 have shown it to be a composite galaxy, containing a LINER-type nucleus while also undergoing SF. L13 described the SF but did not mention an AGN. dal14 characterized the AGN contribution to the SED at 50 . The red 3.6--24 \ SED suggests an AGN is present.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3690/Arp 299 as an interacting two-galaxy system where e-MERLIN detects Arp 299-A, B, and C; A and B host compact VLBI components.

quotes.quote: NGC 3690, an interacting system also known as Arp 299 (or Mrk 171), is an interesting case, composed of two galaxies: the eastern, brightest member NGC 3690A, and the western member NGC 3690B. We use the names Arp 299-A and Arp 299-B to refer to NGC 3690A and NGC 3690B, respectively, in agreement with the nomenclature used in Fig. 1 from romero11 . In our e- MERLIN maps we detected three sources, which correspond to i) Arp 299-A, ii) Arp 299-B, and iii) Arp 299-C which could be a satellite galaxy taking part in the merger event or a vigorous off-nuclear star-forming region related to the merging system tarchi11 .

Specifically, Arp299-A was resolved into several components with VLBI: an extremely prolific SN factory neff04,perez09,ulvestad09 , and a LLAGN perez10 . In addition, Arp 299-B has been resolved into two main components: B1, which includes another SN factory, although less extreme than the one in Arp-299A ulvestad09,romero11,sliwa12 , an AGN ( detected in hard X-rays, ptak15 ) and a bright transient source (Arp299-B AT1; mattila18 ); and B2 neff04,alonso00 , a weaker component with no reported compact radio sources in it.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3690 as an aperture-effect outlier: its COS and IUE spectra may not be co-spatial, and the COS pointing samples an off-center star-forming knot that yields a different UV slope.

quotes.quote: NGC 3690 is an exception: it is unclear whether the COS and IUE spectra are co-spatial. We decided to keep this galaxy in our sample to maximize statistics.
The COS spectra for both (5) IRAS 08339+651 and (15) NGC 3690 show bluer slopes.

As for NGC 3690, the COS pointing is only in one of the star-forming knots outside of the other bright regions in the center of this galaxy. This offset of the COS aperture towards the edge of NGC 3690 may explain the different -slope we obtain for this galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 3690's dust reradiation with an AGN-like component, notes free-free absorption, and identifies it as a peculiar merger with starburst clouds near the center.

quotes.quote: Fig. 5 shows that the IR reradiation by dust of NGC 253, IC 342, NGC 3256, NGC 3690, NGC 5236, and NGC 7552 can be also modelled by a unique model corresponding to a AGN with \ = 1000 , 0 \ = 1000 3, and a power-law flux with log \ = 11 (model m7) as was found in Arp 220 (Contini et al. 2004, Fig. A1).
Free-free continuum absorption is evident in a few objects (NGC 3256, NGC 3690, NGC 7552).

The luminous IR galaxies of the Verma et al sample, with L _ IR > 10^ 11 L _ , NGC 3256 and NGC 3690 which are identified as a colliding pair and a peculiar merger, respectively, show starburst cloud distances from the centre 100 pc.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 3690's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 3690 is a pair of merging late-type galaxies at a distance of D= 45 Mpc. The two nuclei are separated by 20 ( 4.3 kpc) and have been designated with various names, leading to confusion in the literature. Therefore, we refer to the western nucleus as NGC 3690W, it is also called NGC 3690A, Arp 299B, UGC 6472 and sometimes just NGC 3690.

sources.summary: The paper uses CanariCam data to model the nuclear infrared emission of NGC 3690, finding AGN-heated dust in a compact, obscured clumpy torus.

quotes.quote: Fig. fig2 (left) shows the best fit to the IR SED and the GTC/CC mid-IR spectrum of the nuclear region of NGC 3690, one of the components of the interacting LIRG Arp 299. The high angular resolution (0.3 arcsec) of the CC data allowed us to probe nuclear physical scales of approximately 60 pc, which is a factor of 10 improvement over previous mid-IR spectroscopy of this system. From the fit done using the torus models we inferred that the IR emission of this nucleus comes from AGN-heated dust in a clumpy torus with both a high covering factor and high extinction along the line of sight .

sources.summary: The paper notes NGC 3690 as a local Compton-thick AGN in the parent sample; its Fe K line was detected but fell below the selection confidence threshold.

quotes.quote: Only one AGN from the parent sample is included within the local CT AGN reported in bright11 , NGC 3690, which was classified as a CT AGN because of a high EW Fe K emission line. The automated analysis detected an Fe K emission line with an EW > 500 eV, but consistent with being lower than that value at the 90 confidence level. Therefore, it was not flagged as a highly absorbed candidate by our selection criteria.

gmw_00000196.name: NGC 3718
gmw_00000196.overview: NGC 3718 is a peculiar spiral galaxy [arXiv:1310.2770, arXiv:1503.01836] classified as a type 1.9 LINER [arXiv:2210.15376, arXiv:2006.09463] that hosts a compact radio jet [arXiv:1201.5660, arXiv:1310.2770]. It features a strongly warped gas and dust disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0310419, arXiv:1201.5660] and forms an interacting pair with its companion, NGC 3729 [arXiv:0902.2492, arXiv:astro-ph/0310419].

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 3718 as a nearby peculiar galaxy with a dust lane, diffuse arms, and a twisted H I disk whose gas orbits are nearly polar inside.

quotes.quote: We have mapped NGC 3718, a nearby bright galaxy in a loose group, and its companion NGC 3729 in the 21 \ line of neutral hydrogen. NGC 3718 is a strikingly unusual galaxy with a strong straight dust lane across the center, peculiar diffuse spiral arms, and an extended disk of neutral hydrogen. Earlier work showed the gas disk to be strongly twisted, warping through edge-on where we see the straight dust lane; stars formed in this gas appear to make up the `spiral arms'. Our improved maps show a twisted but bisymmetric disk of gas extending to 7 \ or 35 , where the orbital period is roughly 1 .

The apparent major axis swings through 100 \ from the innermost gas orbits at 30 \ radius to the outer edge. When viewed in the reference frame of the galaxy's stellar disk, the innermost gas orbits are nearly polar, while the outer rings of gas are tilted at 30 -40 . The line of nodes, where the gas orbits pass through the plane of the stellar disk, twists by roughly 90 \ about the pole. We do not see gas orbiting in the plane of the stellar disk.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3718 as a type 1.9 LINER whose weak Fe K reflection can be fit by either Compton-thin torus or ionized disk models.

quotes.quote: NGC 3718 NGC 3718 was optically classified as a type 1.9 LINER 1997Ho . showed that its broad H line is produced in the BLR rather than an outflow. The X-ray spectrum shows a small Fe K line, indicative of a reflection component 2011younes,2014Lore,2020diaz . Modelling the reflection with PEXMON we found R _ f <0.24 . This low reflection signature can be produced by a torus like neutral reflector ( borus02 ), if it is Compton thin ( (N_ H,refl )=22.78_ 22.38 ^ 23.98 ), with an unconstrained covering factor (CF<0.99 ^ 1.00 _ 0.05 ).

Fitting a disk like reflector instead, using XILLVER requires high levels of ionization with =3.12_ 1.35 ^ 3.61 and a R _ f >0.86 . A ( MEKAL ) component was necessary to improve the fit at low energies in all the models. The XILLVER and borus02 are indistinguishable. All the models are consistent with a Compton-thin absorber in the line of sight to the primary coronal emission with (N_ H, los ) 22 .

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 3718 as a peculiar active galaxy with a strongly warped gas and dust disk, low molecular gas content, polar-ring-like warp, and possible recent accretion of a gas-rich spiral.

quotes.quote: We present the first observations of molecular line emission in NGC 3718 with the IRAM 30m and the Plateau de Bure Interferometer. This galaxy is an impressive example for a strongly warped gas disk harboring an active galactic nucleus (AGN). An impressive dust lane is crossing the nucleus and a warp is developing into a polar ring. The molecular gas content is found to be typical of an elliptical galaxy with a relatively low molecular gas mass content ( 4 10^8 ). The molecular gas distribution is found to warp from the inner disk together with the HI distribution.

NGC 3718 is a peculiar galaxy at a distance of 13 Mpc. The most prominent features of this galaxy are its large, warped dust lane running across the entire stellar bulge of the galaxy. The galaxy NGC 3718 (Arp 214) and its companion NGC 3729 form a galaxy pair, at a distance of about 13 Mpc Throughout this work we assume H _0 =75 km s ^ 1  Mpc ^ 1 as part of the Ursa Major Galaxy Cluster.

sources.summary: The paper uses deep NuSTAR, XMM-Newton, and Swift/BAT spectra of NGC 3718 to constrain weak reflection, Compton-thin torus geometry, and LLAGN coronal parameters.

quotes.quote: Here we focus on NGC 3718 ( L/L_ Edd 10^ 5 ) combining observations from XMM--Newton and the deepest to date NuSTAR (0.5--79 keV) spectrum of a LLAGN, to constrain potential reflectors, and analyze how the fitted coronal parameters depend on the reflection model. We test models representing both an accretion disc (Relxill) and a torus-like (MYTorus and Borus) neutral reflector. From a statistical point of view, reflection is not required, but its inclusion allows to place strong constraints on the geometry and physical features of the surroundings: both neutral reflectors (torus) tested should be Compton thin ( N_H<10^ 23.2 cm ^ 2 ) and preferentially cover a large fraction of the sky.

In this work, we use simultaneous observations by XMM-Newton and NuSTAR to study in detail the LLAGN NGC 3718, a nearby galaxy at redshift https://ned.ipac.caltech.edu/ z =0.003. This galaxy is classified as a type 1.9 LINER (optical classification, e.g., 1997Ho, 2018cazzoli ), with a black-hole mass (M_ BH )=7.85 1.42 given by 2014Lore and determined using the correlation between stellar velocity dispersion and black-hole mass of tremaine2002 , where the stellar velocity dispersion used was =169.9 km s ^ 1 2009bho . It has a low accretion rate R_ Edd 4 10^ 5 , estimated from its mass, its 2--10 keV X-ray luminosity (log [ L_ X /(erg s ^ 1 ) ] =40.4, Satyapal2005 ) and bolometric correction factor ( L_ Bol /L_ 2.0-10.0 keV =15 ) appropriate for low-luminosity AGN lusso2012 .
We found that NGC 3718 is not variable at 3 confidence level.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3718 as a candidate lacking torus-related reflection, citing no FeK indication, a low Eddington rate, large covering factor, and no statistically required reflection component.

quotes.quote: We find that 12 objects do not present any signs of reflection (namely UGC 5101, NGC 3628, NGC 3718, M 106, NGC 7331, NGC 612, 2MXJ 1105+5856, NGC 3621, NGC 3786, M 88, NGC 5252 and 2MXJ 2325-3826).

NGC 3718 has also been studied with no indications of a reflection component. For instance, studied this object with a model comparable to ours and found no indication of the FeK line which may be linked to the torus. Similarly, Diaz-20 found that this object has an Eddington rate _ Edd 10^ 5 , with a large covering factor but with no indications of a reflection component. Indeed, they claim that the reflection component is not statistically required, a result comparable to ours.

sources.summary: The paper fits NGC 3718 X-ray spectra with an absorbed power law, finding an intrinsic column density around 10^22 cm^-2, spectral softening, and declining hard-band flux.

quotes.quote: satyapalapj05:sfVSliner studied the snapshot \ observation and fit the spectrum with an absorbed ( N_ H 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 ) power-law ( 1.5 ) in excellent agreement with our fit results to the same observation. We studied two \ observations of NGC 3718, being in the field of view of the observations of the heavily absorbed Seyfert 2 galaxy UGC 6527 . We fit the spectra with an absorbed power-law and found an intrinsic hydrogen column density similar to the one derived for the \ observation of 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 but a somewhat softer power-law with 1.8 . This softening is accompanied with a 2-10 keV flux decrease from 3.3 10^ 12 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 to 1.6 10^ 12 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 .

sources.summary: NGC 3718 is described with a compact MERLIN radio jet, high-brightness core detected by VLA/VLBA, and an unusual strongly warped optical gas disk.

quotes.quote: At 18 cm, with subarcsecond resolution of the MERLIN radio telescope, NGC 3718 shows a compact jet extending to the northwest direction (0.5 ), which is weakly present at 6 cm krips07AA:radlin . The core has a very high surface brightness temperature with a lower limit of 3 10^ 8 K. It is detected with the VLA and VLBA at subarcsecond and milliarcsecond scales nagar02aap . In the optical band, NGC 3718 shows an unusual strongly-warped gas disk pott04A&A:ngc3718, krips05A&A:ngc3718, sparke09AJ:ngc3718 .

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 3718 needs a varying normalization in joint Chandra/XMM fitting, implying soft and hard luminosity changes, while its UV nucleus is undetected.

quotes.quote: NGC 3718 : We jointly fit Chandra and XMM -Newton data since emission from the annular region is negligible. The best representation of the data needs Norm_2 vary (37 ), i.e., SMF1 is used. This implies a change in luminosity of 35 (29 ) at soft (hard) energy in one years period. In the UV data, the nucleus of the galaxy is not detected.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 3718's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 3718 is a peculiar early-type spiral galaxy at a distance of D= 17.0 3.4 Mpc tully_nearby_1988 with a broad-line LINER nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 , covered by a prominent dust lane, which completely absorbs the nuclear UV signature barth_search_1998 . A compact radio source with a possible jet extending 0.5 40 pc to the north-west was detected nagar_radio_2002,krips_nuclei_2007 . Also only very weak extended NLR emission was detected pogge_narrow-line_2000 .

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 3718 as a dusty SBa LINER with warped gas, compact jet, dwarf companion, and possible tidal interaction and gas accretion.

quotes.quote: NGC 3718 (L1.9) Morphologically, NGC 3718 is an SBa galaxy with a prominent dust lane crossing its central bulge. On kpc scales it is also known for its strongly warped atomic and molecular gas disk Sparke09 . A type 1.9 LINER nucleus FWHM _ H = 2350 km s ^ 1 ; Ho97 lies behind this dust lane, with a compact jet extending to the northwest Krips07,Nagar02 .

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 3718 is well fit by the baseline X-ray model, with modest intrinsic absorption, photon index around 1.86, and no significant iron-K line.

quotes.quote: The base-line model well reproduces the X-ray spectra of NGC 3718, yielding ^2/d.o.f = 214.9/206 with N_ H = 1.29^ +0.12 _ 0.11 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 and = 1.86^ +0.13 _ 0.09 . No significant iron-K line is detected. also reported a similar result by analyzing the Chandra and XMM-Newton spectra.
